The latter is the random-cluster representation of an Ashkin--Teller model (ATRC), and is related to the Potts model via a chain of couplings going through the six-vertex model. In the current work, we extend the analysis to a pair of interacting order-disorder interfaces forming the separation between the two ordered phases, and couple them to a pair of well-behaved random walks conditioned not to intersect. The construction of the coupling is based on rigorously deriving entropic repulsion between the two interfaces. We also prove convergence of interfaces in the FK-percolation model at $p_c(q)$ when $q>4$.

title: Privacy-Preserving Distributed Stochastic Optimization with Homomorphic Encryption and Heterogeneous Stepsizes
authors: Haoqiang Zhou, Chi Chen, Yongfeng Zhi, Huan Gao
abstract: Distributed stochastic optimization enables multi-agent collaboration in applications such as distributed learning and sensor networks, but also raises critical privacy concerns due to the involvement of sensitive data. While existing privacy-preserving approaches often face limitations in balancing accuracy with efficiency, we propose a novel distributed stochastic gradient descent algorithm that integrates Paillier homomorphic encryption with heterogeneous and time-varying random stepsizes. The proposed algorithm provides inherent privacy protection against both internal honest-but-curious agents and external eavesdroppers, without relying on any trusted neighbors. Furthermore, we incorporate an attenuation factor to effectively mitigate quantization error induced by the encryption process, ensuring almost sure convergence to the optimal solution while maintaining privacy preservation. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed approach.

title: Tailoring Germanium Heterostructures for Quantum Devices with Machine Learning
authors: Patrick Del Vecchio, Kevin Rossi, Giordano Scappucci, Stefano Bosco
abstract: Germanium (Ge) quantum wells are emerging as versatile platforms for quantum devices, supporting high-quality spin qubits and integration with superconducting leads. These applications benefit from strong intrinsic spin-orbit interaction (SOI), enabling efficient electrical control and engineering of spin degrees of freedom. The most advanced Ge/SiGe heterostructures to date, based on compressively strained Ge channels within strain-relaxed silicon-germanium (SiGe) barriers, exhibit weak SOI due to the heavy-hole character of the wave function, posing challenges for spin-based quantum devices and requiring complex device designs for fast qubit manipulation. In this work, we demonstrate that concrete heterostructure modifications can overcome these limitations, enhancing SOI by up to three orders of magnitude. Specifically, we propose to enrich unstrained Ge channels by localized, strained silicon spikes. Leveraging a multi-objective Bayesian optimization, we optimize the spike profile to maximize SOI, while ensuring compatibility with current epitaxial growth processes and robustness against realistic variations of growth parameters. Our heterostructure substantially enhances device performance, yielding up to two orders of magnitude higher quantum-dot spin qubit quality factors than state-of-the-art materials. We also predict GHz-scale spin splittings for hybrid superconducting Andreev spin qubits. These novel Ge heterostructures with engineered Si concentration profiles can open pathways to scalable quantum and spintronic applications.

title: Consecutive non-square non-primitive pairs in a finite field
authors: Stephen D. Cohen
abstract: Let $q$ be an odd prime power and write \[ θ_q := \frac{φ(q-1)}{q-1}. \] If $θ_q < \tfrac{1}{3}$, or if $θ_q = \tfrac{1}{3}$ and $q \notin \{7,13,19,25,37\}$, then the finite field $\F$ contains a pair of consecutive elements that are both non-square and non-primitive. This extends a result of Jarso and Trudgian for prime fields $\Fp$, where the same conclusion was obtained under the stronger condition $θ_p \le \tfrac{1}{4}$. More generally, let $\ell$ be the least odd prime divisor of $q-1$. If $θ_q \le \tfrac{1}{3}$, then $\F$ contains a pair of consecutive elements that are non-squares and $\ell$th powers, with the sole exceptions $q \in \{7,13,19,25,37,43\}$.

title: NEST: Network Enforced Session Types (Technical Report)
authors: Jens Kanstrup Larsen, Alceste Scalas, Guy Amir, Jules Jacobs, Jana Wagemaker, Nate Foster
abstract: This paper introduces NEST (Network-Enforced Session Types), a runtime verification framework that moves application-level protocol monitoring into the network fabric. Unlike prior work that instruments or wraps application code, we synthesize packet-level monitors that enforce protocols directly in the data plane. We develop algorithms to generate network-level monitors from session types and extend them to handle packet loss and reordering. We implement NEST in P4 and evaluate it on applications including microservice and network-function models, showing that network-level monitors can enforce realistic non-trivial protocols.

title: Projective Chromatic Numbers
authors: Adrian Rettich, Luke Serafin
abstract: We extend classical notions of definable colourability of graphs to the general projective setting and investigate whether known results, mainly about the $G_0$ dichotomy and the $2n + 1$ conjecture, hold in the context of higher projective pointclasses. We establish that for $n \ge 2$, the presence of a $\mathbfΔ^1_n$-definable well-order of the reals implies $χ_{\mathbf{Δ^1_n}}(G) = χ(G)$ for all locally countable $\mathbf{Δ^1_n}$-definable graphs $G$, and that the presence of a $\mathbf{Δ^1_2}$-definable well-order of the reals implies $χ_{\mathbf{Δ^1_2}}(G) = χ(G)$ for all locally countable Borel graphs $G$.

title: Spectral Diffusion Mitigation with a Laser Pulse Sequence
authors: Kilian Unterguggenberger, Alok Gokhale, Aleksei Tsarapkin, Wentao Zhang, Katja Höflich, Herbert Fotso, Tommaso Pregnolato, Laura Orphal-Kobin, Tim Schröder
abstract: The optical spectrum of a quantum system is jointly determined by the properties of the emitter and the driving field. All-optical spectral control can hence be a promising method to engineer the properties of single photon emitters for quantum technological applications. It was proposed that driving a two-level system with a periodic sequence of optical pi-pulses during the excited state lifetime shifts the emission and absorption maximum to an arbitrarily detuned pulse carrier frequency, enabling the mitigation of spectral diffusion in noisy emitters. In this article, we report on the first experimental observation of this effect. We implement the protocol on a solid-state emitter and reduce its inhomogeneously broadened optical linewidth close to the lifetime limit. By detuning the excitation laser, we are able to concentrate approximately half of the absorption to a freely selectable target frequency. Our approach is solely based on properties of coherently evolving quantum systems, rendering it applicable to a wide range of individual and ensembles of quantum emitters.

authors: S. Nirmal Sam, Thirupurasundari D. R
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are regarded as essential components of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), as communication between vehicles and infrastructure is facilitated by them. However, due to the inherent characteristics of mobile and hoc networks and the high mobility of VANETs, they are made susceptible to malicious node attacks, which adversely affect road safety and network stability. Traditional detection frameworks are not optimised for real-time performance and are often associated with high computational cost and a high false positive rate. To address these limitations, a novel Greatest Cane Rat Algorithm-based Deep Learning Intrusion System (GRADE-IDS) technique is proposed in this paper. Initially, the data from the VANET environment is collected by the proposed model, and it is subsequently preprocessed through techniques such as data cleaning, normalization, and transformation. The features from the preprocessed data will be extracted using a variational autoencoder (VAE), and selected using the Greatest Cane Rat Optimization (GCRO) algorithm. Then, the selected features will be provided to the Deep Operator Network (DON), which will classify the intrusions into Botnets, Sybil, DoS, Wormholes, and normal. The performance of the proposed model has been evaluated using accuracy (AC), precision (PR), recall (RE), f1-score (FS), and detection rate. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves a higher accuracy of 99.24%, whereas the previous techniques, such as VAN-FED-IDS, 2PT-CIDS, and Light Bioptimum, achieve a lesser accuracy of 98.35%, 98% and 97% respectively.

title: High Performance Slotted Rectangular Textile UWB Antenna With Modified CPW Configuration for Wban Applications
authors: Nanditha. D, Mohan. C, Vidhushi. B
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: This work describes a small Ultra-Wideband (UWB) antenna constructed on a cotton fabric substrate for usage in wearable and body-centric wireless systems. To enhance impedance characteristics and bandwidth, the antenna uses a redesigned radiating patch with square, semicircular, and rectangular parts as well as carefully positioned horizontal and vertical slots. To enable flexible integration on textile materials, a CPW-fed architecture is used. With a reflection coefficient of less than - 10 dB, the optimized construction achieves a broad operating range from 3 GHz to 10.8 GHz. The design retains radiation efficiency beyond 81% and offers stable gain levels between 2.5 and 3.4 dB despite its small dimension of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17 \times 17 \times 1 ~\text{mm}^{3}$</tex>. These findings demonstrate the antenna's applicability for wearable and implanted WBAN applications, when wideband performance, comfort, and compactness are significant.

title: Valuation Model Optimization in ERP Using Genetic Algorithms and Deep Learning
authors: Sukumar Reddy Beereddy, Rubani Singla
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The current paper approaches towards a possible means of how the use of genetic algorithms and deep learning would be used to the advantage of improving the valuation models used in ERP (Enterprise resource planner) systems. Although genetic algorithm can be applied to analyze this model and optimize it through automatic optimization tools, the sophisticated trends falling in the range of cost, productivity and systems performances are located by deep-learn models. The researchers use the actual ERP data to the test concerning the hybrid models, concluding that diagramming of the optimized hybrid models was found to increase the effectiveness of prediction in 25-30 percent improvement over the conventional models. This was also discovered to have resulted into reduced processing time and enthusiasm by the users. This information corresponds to the point that smart optimization and deep learning are methods that are not only used to promote the level of valuation but also enable organizations to make better financial and operation-related decisions. The future system of ERP innovation and smart business decisions is well-grounded on the proposed approach

title: Online Time-Series Contrastive Learning for Soft Sensing of Biopharmaceutical Processes_supp1-3679614.pdf
authors: Xinggao Liu
abstract: Biopharmaceutical processes typically generate abundant high-frequency online sensor data but suffer from sparse and delayed offline quality measurements, creating a “data-rich but label-poor” dilemma that hinders effective process monitoring. Furthermore, these processes are subject to significant distribution shifts due to batch-to-batch variability and time-varying metabolic states. To address these challenges, this article proposes a novel online time-series contrastive learning framework for soft sensing, validated on an industrial erythromycin fermentation process. We introduce a mechanism-informed soft contrastive learning strategy that utilizes information derived from domain knowledge to construct instance-level and temporal similarity matrices, guiding the model to learn physically meaningful representations from unlabeled data. In addition, we develop a dual-timescale online learning architecture comprising a slow branch for robust representation learning and a fast branch for real-time adaptation. Experimental results on a large-scale industrial dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms state-of-the-art time-series contrastive learning baselines, particularly in predicting complex rheological indicators like broth viscosity under temporal distribution shifts.

title: ROR: Using Reads for Fast and Efficient Data Repair in Erasure-Coded Storage Systems
authors: Marc Tranzer, Shadi Ibrahim
publish: Proceedings of the 6th Workshop on Challenges and Opportunities of Efficient and Performant Storage Systems
abstract: None

title: Optimising Large Language Models for Lung Cancer-Related Clinical Text for a Scalable Fine-Tuning Pipeline
authors: Neha Gautam, Minakshi Ramteke, Megha Gautam, Jitendra Kumar Jaiswal, Princy Randhawa, Sumit Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable potential in interpreting complex clinical data and advancing healthcare informatics. However, automated interpretation faces challenges as domain-specific diagnostic and prognostic processes, often documented in unstructured clinical narratives, heavily influence cancer management. This study presents a robust and scalable pipeline for fine-tuning LLMs on cancer-specific clinical data, emphasising information extraction from pathology reports within the Pan-Lung Cancer dataset. The BioBERT model serves as the foundation, enhanced through named entity recognition and domain-specific fine-tuning. The pipeline includes key stages, such as data cleaning, tokenisation, and entity annotation, to enhance the model’s understanding of oncology-specific terminology. BioBERT was evaluated on over 1,000 lung cancer pathology reports and 191 paediatric osteosarcoma reports, with outputs validated against expert-curated structured data. Fine-tuning targeted the extraction of essential tumour features, including regional lymph node involvement (pN), overall tumour stage, and histological diagnosis. The model achieved 91.48% accuracy for pN, 91.66% for tumour stage, 84.12% for histological diagnosis, and an overall average accuracy of 91.78%. These findings confirm BioBERT’s effectiveness in structuring complex oncology narratives and its potential to support scalable, automated clinical decision-making in cancer care.

title: Chiplet Interconnect Repair for Clustered Defects with Minimal Propagation Delay
authors: Po-Yao Chuang, Erik Jan Marinissen
abstract: Chips with multiple interconnected dies offer significant advantages over those consisting of only a single monolithic die, driving the rapid adoption of multi-die packages in the market. Die-to-die interconnects are typically realized as large, dense arrays of fine-pitch micro-bumps or hybrid bonds, prone to manufacturing defects like shorts and opens. To address this, typically spare interconnects are included to “repair” defective ones. Repairing is implemented by avoiding the defective interconnects and detouring the functional signals over spare interconnects. This paper proposes a repair scheme for chiplet interconnects with minimal propagation delay for those chiplets that communicate at high speed and do not tolerate too much delay on their interface. Compared to the default UCIe repair scheme, our proposed solution also demonstrates an improved repair rate in case of clustered defects.

title: Interactive Object Removal via Probabilistic Image Inpainting: Integrating Classical and Deep Learning Approaches for Semantic Content Elimination
authors: Newton Shahi Thakuri, Bhuma Devi Acharya, Hari Bhakta Shahi
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This paper presents an interactive image inpainting system for semantic object removal that integrates real-time object detection with both deterministic and probabilistic inpainting techniques. Unlike existing systems that focus exclusively on either speed or visual quality, the proposed hybrid framework combines YOLOv8 for object detection, LaMa for fast deterministic inpainting, and RePaint for diffusion-based probabilistic reconstruction. The system enables users to interactively select objects for removal through a desktop interface and produces either a single coherent output or multiple diverse restorations. Experimental observations show that LaMa achieves real-time performance with visually consistent results, while RePaint generates higher-quality and diverse outputs at increased computational cost. The proposed approach effectively balances interactivity, speed, and realism, making it suitable for applications such as privacy protection, image restoration, and creative media editing.

title: Low-Cost Teflon Substrate for Enhanced Performance of 60 GHz Patch Antenna for 5G Wireless Applications
authors: Talha Anwar, Anum Afroz, Arjuna Marzuki, Ikechi Augustne Ukaegbu
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a low-cost 60GHz millimeter wave frequency microstrip patch antenna for 5G wireless communication using a Teflon substrate. The design of the presented antenna utilizes a dielectric substrate with a relative permittivity of 2, thickness of 0.254 mm and a loss tangent of 0.0009. There are two antenna designs studied, single element microstrip patch antenna and the other is a four-element antenna array. In both designs, a patch of copper is printed on the top of the substrate, and a slotted ground plane is used on the lower layer to enhance the radiation characteristics. The effect of antenna size and antenna array structure on important performance parameters, such as impedance bandwidth, gain, and directivity were analyzed through CST Microwave Studio. The simulation results indicate that the array design offers better gain and directivity than the single-element design with acceptable bandwidth of 5G short range communication. The four-element array has a reflection coefficient of -22.048 dB at 60 GHz, and it has stable VSWR characteristics. The performance of the antennas is further confirmed by three-dimensional radiation patterns and electric fields distributions. The findings confirm the Teflon substrates are an effective and affordable way of using the millimeter-wave microstrip antennas in 5G Wireless Communication applications.

title: Accelerating Privacy Implementations with GenAI at Meta
authors: Rituraj Kirti, Daniel Bloomfield Ramagem
abstract: At Meta, purpose limitation requirements specify how certain types of data can or cannot be processed. While the concept is simple, its practical implementation at scale presents opportunities for optimization. This paper presents how we leverage generative AI to accelerate deployment of purpose limitation technology. We describe the architecture of key privacy workflows spanning asset annotation, data flow analysis, regression prevention, and verification. Our GenAI-powered solutions automate complex privacy tasks, enhance consistency across reviews, and enable efficient enforcement of purpose limitations across Meta’s diverse products and services. We present key takeaways and best practices learned from deploying these GenAI solutions at scale, including strategies for ground truth validation, performance optimizations via pre-computed caches, and integration with developer experiences.

title: Review and Comparative Evaluation of Chaotic Image Encryption Approaches Incorporating a Hybrid Logistic-Fibonacci Q Model
authors: Akanksha Pandey, Dhiraj Kapila, Rachit Garg
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Image encryption has become a crucial component of data security due to the increasing reliance on digital communication. Chaotic systems, known for their unpredictable behavior and strong sensitivity to initial conditions, have been widely applied in cryptography. This paper explores the potential of integrating chaotic maps with matrix transformations to develop a more secure image encryption method. Specifically, it highlights the hybridisation of the Fibonacci Q Matrix and the Logistic Map, leveraging their unique properties to enhance encryption performance. The study begins with a detailed review of existing encryption techniques, outlining their advantages as well as their limitations. It also identifies key issues such as limited key space, statistical weaknesses, and susceptibility to cryptanalysis. By combining the randomness of the Logistic Map with the structural complexity of the Fibonacci Q Matrix, the proposed hybrid approach introduces a robust framework for secure image encryption.

title: Secure Aggregation-Enabled Federated Vision Transformer Framework for Tissue Image Analysis
authors: Poreddy Jayaraju, Narendra B Mustare, K Kiran, Rohit Agarwal, Keerthika J, A M Viswa Bharathy, S. Siva Shankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Medical image analysis employing deep learning has made incredible strides, but the joint training of such model architectures in multiple healthcare institutions is still restricted because of data privacy, regulation limitations and ethical concerns. Conventional centralized training by necessity involves pooling data, leaving patient information prone to breaches. To overcome this difficulty, this paper proposes a Secure Aggregation-Enabled Federated Vision Transformer (SecureAgg-FedViT) Framework for the analysis of tissue images that allows several institutions to jointly train Vision Transformers (ViTs) without having to share raw data. The proposed method combines secure aggregation protocols for privacy preservation of the model updates, differential privacy for limiting the leakage of data into the model, and multi-scale Vision Transformer architectures for histopathology image representations. Experiments built on the CAMELYON16 public data set prove that the classification performance of SecureAgg-FedViT (AUC: 93.8%, Accuracy: 90.2%, F1-score: 89.5%) is better than that of 5 state-of-the-art methods, while providing good privacy guarantees. The framework enables the trade-off of model utility, computational efficiency and privacy in an effective manner; demonstrating robustness against the heterogeneity of inter-institutional heterogeneity. Overall, the results of this study set the stage for a scalable and secure paradigm of collaborative analysis of tissue images and will lead to privacy compliant AI-driven pathology systems in the clinical environment.

title: Two-Step Transition Zeroization Techniques for MLC STTMRAM
authors: Shyue-Kung Lu, Tzu-En Huang, Kohei Miyase
abstract: Spin-transfer torque magnetic RAM (STTMRAM) is a promising candidate to replace SRAM due to its beneficial features of non-volatility, higher density, and zero leakage power. To further increase the data density, multi-level cell (MLC) STT-MRAM, which can store two bits per cell has been proposed. However, it has to conquer the two-step transition (TT) threats for some state transitions because writing the hard domain of a cell also writes the soft domain to the same value. The main drawbacks of TT are higher power consumption and a performance penalty. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel two-step transition zeroization (TSTZ) technique that can completely eliminate two-step transitions. TSTZ is basically a ternary encoding technique where each MLC cell stores a ternary digit. The ternary digits 0,1, and 2 are encoded into cell states 00, 01/10, and 11, respectively. Since only three states of an MLC cell are used at a time, TSTZ eliminates TTs and significantly reduces the read error rate. Corresponding hardware architectures for encoding and decoding have also been proposed. The space overhead incurred is about 33%, the lowest among existing techniques. Power consumption and lifetime can be improved by 12% and 22.1% respectively. Moreover, the read error rate can be reduced from the original 8.24% to 0.75%.

title: Intelligent Automation in Equity Research: A LangChain–FAISS–LLM Framework for Financial News Understanding and Insight Generation
authors: C P Pavan Kumar Hota, Vinod Babu Polinati, Prasanthi Gottumukkala, Surya Pavan Kumar Gudla, S Murali Krishnamachari, P Neelima
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Today, equity research is heavily reliant on the analysis of large amounts of unstructured financial data, such as news reports, earnings releases, and market updates. Traditional approaches are frequently slow, inconsistent, and influenced by human judgment, necessitating greater automation. This study introduces a financial news analysis tool built with modern Natural Language Processing (NLP) methods and tools such as LangChain, FAISS, and Streamlit, as well as Large Language Models. Initially based on OpenAI's GPT models, the tool later shifted to open-source models from Hugging Face to reduce costs and improve scalability. Semantic search with vector embeddings, automatic summarization, and a simple interface for financial queries are among the key features. FAISS allows for quick and relevant information retrieval, whereas LangChain improves response accuracy through effective prompt management. Testing revealed that the system was capable of producing results in milliseconds while extracting financial insights with greater than 80% accuracy. This work presents a practical, efficient, and cost-effective solution for using AI in equity research, providing better support for financial decision-making.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Predictive Maintenance in IoT-Enabled Smart Grids
authors: Amanullah Quamer, Harshita, Kode Sai Harshitha
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid deep learning model for predictive maintenance in Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled smart grids. It uses Convolutional Neural Networks , Recurrent Neural Networks, and Graph Neural Networks to find the topological, temporal, and spatial relationships between grid assets. IoT sensor data, including voltage, current, temperature, and vibration, are preprocessed to predict equipment failure probability and Remaining Useful Life (RUL). The proposed model surpassed the existing CNN–LSTM and Random Forest benchmarks, achieving an accuracy of 98.6%, an F1-score of 97.9%, and a ROC-AUC of 0.982. The hybrid approach helps us understand how faults spread and reduces false alarms by using GNN to model asset connections more efficiently. An edge–cloud deployment architecture ensures real-time, low-latency inference suitable for operational environments. The results indicate that the fusion of IoT data and hybrid deep learning allows predictive maintenance based on big data, which enhances grid reliability and decreases downtime and ultimately leads to intelligent self-healing smart grid systems.

title: Sustainable Machine Learning for Fraud Detection in E-commerce: A Lightweight Pipeline with SHAP-Guided Feature Selection
authors: Aldrin Manon, Sankara Reddy Thamma, Suveg Moudgil, Navdeep Kumar Chopra, Rajiv Kumar, Kawerinder Singh Sidhu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: We present a carbon-aware fraud detection pipeline for historical fraud benchmarks using efficient tabular models (logistic regression, LightGBM, XGBoost) with SHAPguided feature selection and interpretability. Experiments on IEEE-CIS (2017–2018) and CreditCard (2013) datasets employ CodeCarbon emissions tracking with stratified cross-validation as the primary validation protocol. LightGBM achieves strong performance–emissions trade-offs: IEEE-CIS PR-AUC 0.740±0.002 (2 seeds), CreditCard PR-AUC 0.876 (single seed); emissions 8.6±0.6 mg CO₂e (IEEE) and 0.48 mg CO₂e (CreditCard). Runtime profiling reveals SHAP overhead dominates (96% of total emissions). We provide methodological frameworks for time-stratified validation, cost-sensitive optimization, SHAP-free deployment (projected 96% emissions reduction), and early stopping strategies. Comprehensive reproducibility artifacts, carbon-aware + SHAP-free + early stopping and production deployment extrapolations support sustainable fraud detection research.

title: Sky Scout Modern Weather Software with Air Quality Information and Voice Interaction
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Blessings Mthaphwi, Himani Tyagi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Sky Scout is a state of the art weather software intended to give users real time weather forecasts and air quality information, with a clean desktop interface. It was developed using Python and CustomTkinter. This software uses the OpenWeatherMap APIs to provide live data on weather and air quality. The user interface presents this data in an easy to use and attractive design. The Sky Scout software provides unique opportunities for using a 7 day weather forecast, live Air Quality Index, and a hands free city search using voice. The design of the application was created in a responsive manner and optimized with creative and dynamic backgrounds and hover tooltips to make the program easier to use. This research section will cover the program architecture, workflow development, and data presentation for streaming data in order to visualize data that would be of interest to users in some manner; namely, live, accurate, relevant, useful, and actionable environmental information. User surveys indicated that prior to seeing and using the interface that all users were very supportive of the long term use of the Sky Scout interface, the clarity of information presented, and the ease of interaction. Sky Scout could be a viable idea that is useful for the user interacting with Sky Scout to gain a better understanding of the environment in which they live for the day or the long term environment quality.

title: Vox Civitas: AI-Powered Civic Action
authors: S. Biruntha, Nanditha Noble, A.L. Sudarrshana, R. Sureshkumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Although artificial intelligence has advanced rapidly, there are still low participation levels, biases, and inability to scale up with civic participation and policy discussion, most digital participation platforms have less than 35 percent response rates, and fewer incorporate citizen views into practical policy results. This paper is a research proposal of an AI-based civic action system, incorporating large language models (LLMs), natural language processing (NLP), and computational social choice algorithms to enable civic deliberation that is large and inclusive, and data-driven. The framework was experimented with 3,000 simulated citizens profile based on the knowledge of participatory design, AI governance, and algorithmic fairness by applying sentiment analysis, topic clustering, and evaluation of the quality of the argument to mediate online discussion. In all stages of simulation and alignment, we used the GPT-3.5-Turbo model as our base LLM. The 3,000 synthetic citizen profiles were generated by conditioning GPT-3.5-Turbo on demographic, policy-interest, and sentiment constraints, and cross-validated with accuracy of 90% using a publicly available participatory-budgeting dataset (OpenPB-2022) to guarantee plausible distribution." The experimental findings show that the accuracy of consensus detection was improved by 27%, the moderate time was diminished by 41%, and the F1-score was 0.89 to detect a common-ground statement, as well as 22% of fairness and inclusivity when using the tool compared to the standard deliberation tools. Our improvements over a RoBERTa-large baseline for moderation and a TF-IDF consensus voter were statistically significant (paired t-test, p<0.05) across 5 runs. These results affirm that it is true that AI-human facilitation can be effectively scaled up to deliberative democracy, without impairing the level of representation or transparency. Expanding on it, the paper presents a new concept, the CivicGPT, the dynamic deliberation engine, that combines the work of the LLM and participatory budgeting with fairness optimization, which is a radical change in the system of ethical and transparent and participatory decisions made by the population.

title: MobileNetV3Large-Based Deep Learning Framework for High-Accuracy Cotton Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Manam Vamsi Krishna, Geetha Gorile, Divya Bharti, Shatakshi Dudala, Eega Tejaswini, Mahalaxmi Dhumala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Every year, cotton leaf diseases cause enormous economic losses to global cotton production. In this paper, we propose a new deep learning framework based on MobileNetV3Large for the automated classification of seven cotton leaf diseases. Utilizing neural architecture search (NAS) and hardware-aware design, our method strikes an excellent trade-off between strong performance and efficient computation. Our model adopts a two-stage transfer learning process, including the initial stage to extract features and the upper layers to fine-tune locally. On the whole SAR-CLD-2024 dataset, the experimental results show that it achieves the state-of-the-art performance for the first time, achieving 99.29% validation accuracy with 4.65 million parameters. The model achieves almost 100% classification for all the disease categories and the macro-average precision, recall, and F1-score are found to be 99.29%, 99.32%, and 99.30%, respectively. Aside from achieving new state-of-the-art performance for cotton disease classification, this research also offers a lightweight solution that can be easily integrated into precision agriculture applications with real-time constraints.

title: A Design of Secured Communication for IoT-Supported Fog-Cloud Computing Network Through Graph Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Govindaraj Ramkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: An Internet of Things (IoT)-based Fog-Cloud Computing Network device arranges a hierarchical system where IoT devices generate real-time data, fog nodes process and store data locally, and cloud servers perform heavy computation and manage data. The Smart Cloud–IoT–Fog Computing with Graph Neural Network (SCIFCG) model is a multi-layered structure that seeks to optimise data processing, resource allocation, and decision-making in IoT-based agricultural scenarios. The three layers of the model are interdependent layers: IoT devices for the collection of environmental data in real-time, fog nodes for localised computations, and cloud infrastructure for in-depth analytics and storage. Resources are directed by a central broker using a FIFO scheduling system, which is modified for execution time and workload balancing. Data redundancy is guaranteed by duplicating data among fog nodes for fault tolerance, and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are introduced to enable more efficient resource allocation based on the cross-relations between resources. The performance metrics achieved by throughput of 12 KB/s, bandwidth of 1.76 GB/s, latency of 0.53 ms, response time of 1770 ms, and CPU utilisation 2.5 Seconds when compared to existing models IMCQE, SCIEC, and NIAGM.

title: Multi Modal Graph Fusion Framework for Equitable and Sustainable Content Discovery in Digital Streaming Platforms
authors: Hari Kumar Palani, K. Gunasekaran, C. Sivasankar, Loganayagi S, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, G. Vasanthi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper presents a fusion-based recommendation framework in which several embedding techniques were integrated to improve content discovery in streaming media platforms, while support for sustainable digital infrastructure and equitable information access was also facilitated. TF-IDF vectorization, sentence transformer embeddings, heterogeneous graph-based meta-path representations, and adjacency-based sparse matrix decomposition were combined to produce unified feature representations. An experimental evaluation carried out on the Netflix dataset showed that the fusion strategy achieved a Precision@5 of 0.68, a MAP@10 of 0.72, and an NDCG@10 of 0.85, with these values surpassing individual baseline methods by 6.25 percent, 9.09 percent, and 8.97 percent. Enhanced diversity was additionally demonstrated by the fusion model, supported by an entropy score of 3.42 and a coverage measure of 0.78, indicating improved content variety and reduced imbalance in content exposure. Classification experiments using Random Forest classifiers produced 92 percent accuracy. Through this integrated methodology, challenges related to sustainable digital content delivery, efficient resource utilization, responsible technological advancement, and inclusive information access were effectively addressed, with a slight elaboration highlighting the framework’s strengthened capability for broad user engagement.

title: GNN-SUIT: Topology-Aware Virtual Machine Placement using Graph Neural Suitability Scoring
authors: Advithiya Duddu, Shreya L, Panchadip Bhattacharjee, Nishanth Shet, Somyajeet Arukh, Dayananda P
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: In order to meet service-level goals, virtual machine (VM) placement in contemporary cloud datacenters must simultaneously optimize usage of resources, energy consumption, network locality, and fragmentation. Under realistic, time-varying workloads, existing heuristic policies like Best Fit, First Fit, and Worst Fit often optimize a single objective and treat hosts independently, resulting in frequent utilization target violations and suboptimal consolidation. This work presents GNN-SUIT, a framework based on graph neural networks that learns a continuous suitability score for every host-VM pair by modeling the entire datacenter as a resource graph. In order to capture both local and topological effects, the model is trained on multi-objective labels that reflect memory and computation space, energy-aware allocation targets, and distance from the network penalties. GNN-SUIT attains 93.5% Top-1 and 100% Top-3 placement accuracy on a realistic fat-tree arrangement with 60 hosts and an average degree of 29. This represents a 92% relative improvement over the Best Fit and a 47 percentage point gain over Worst Fit. Qualitative analysis reveals that GNN-SUIT maintains consistent accuracy across small, medium, and large VM sizes, avoids overloads, and favors underutilized, well-connected hosts, indicating robust generalization. With just 20% of the training set, the model achieves 84% Top-1 accuracy. It is also data-efficient, supporting efficient implementation with sub-2 ms estimation latency per placement on standard GPUs. Additionally, ablation studies show that using GraphSAGE layers and graph structure results in accuracy that is up to 61 percentage points higher than non-graph and vanilla GCN baselines.

title: Edugate AI: An AI-Powered Interactive Learning Platform for GATE Data Science and AI Preparation
authors: K. Raju, K. Sriram, S. Vijaya Venkatesh, A. S. Jenita Anjali
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: For GATE Data Science and Artificial Intelligence exam preparation, it is necessary to have knowledge mastery in mathematically intensive concepts and learn to overcome individual learning gaps, which cannot be efficiently supported by the conventional learning platforms. This paper proposes EDUGATE AI, an interactive learning platform that combines generative AI tutoring, explainable reasoning, visualization-driven learning, and online adaptive learning for exam preparation. The proposed learning platform combines concept explanation, visualization-driven learning for basic mathematical concepts, and adaptive quizzes with dynamic difficulty adjustment in real-time using a contextual bandit-based adaptive learning strategy. The platform also supports the functionality of recording user interactions and learning assessment results for continuous personalization and learning analytics support. The adaptive learning strategy is evaluated using multi-user interaction traces, which show progressive improvement in cumulative rewards and converged difficulty levels for all users. By leveraging explainable AI and online adaptation and visualization-driven learning, EDUGATE AI illustrates the feasibility of developing a learner-centric and transparent learning platform for competitive exam preparation.

title: Impact of AI-Driven Recruitment on Hiring Quality and Efficiency in the Construction-Real Estate-infrastructure-Project Management (CRIP) Sector: An Empirical Analysis in Delhi-NCR
authors: Ameet Sao, Garima Vijh, Manuha Nagpal, Shresth Kumar, Surbhi Vijh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The increasing significance of AI in the CRIP sector has profoundly transformed recruiting efficiency and the acquisition of high-quality people, hence fostering a competitive advantage in the market. This study, grounded on the Resource-Based View (RBV) and Dynamic Capabilities (DC) paradigm, sought to elucidate the role of AI in recruiting proficient human resources and enhancing candidate quality inside the organization. To achieve the research objectives, a meticulously designed questionnaire was developed with structured inputs from HR professionals in the sector. The questionnaire was structured using a Likert scale from 1 to 5, where 1 represents extreme disagreement and 5 indicates strong agreement. Stratified sample and snowball sampling approaches were employed to gather data from the respondents. A total of 238 respondents were contacted, of which 212 genuine replies were obtained. The replies were analyzed utilizing SPSS-25 and SEM to evaluate the success of the recruiting process in relation to Pre-AI and Post-AI. Descriptive analysis and factor analysis utilizing SPSS-25 were conducted to provide diverse insights into AI and the difficulties impacting organizational adoption of AI in the CRIP sector. Factor analysis identified four important factors accounting for 71.2% of the variation influencing AI challenges in adoption. The identified factors include Bias & Ethical Issues (23.46%), Legal & Regulatory Issues (19.01%), Technical Issues (14.74%), and Integration Issues (13.99%). Furthermore, the mediating effect had shown that hiring efficiency partially mediates the relationship between AI Adoption and Candidate quality. Moreover, researchers suggest that the usefulness of the output can be examined by assessing additional sectors to assess the impact of AI-driven recruiting and by comparing the results with the crip sector to generalize the significance of AI in recruitment.

title: Transmission Line Micro-Motion Detection and Intelligent Construction Process Optimization Integrating Machine Learning and Sensing Technologies
authors: Xiaogang Li, Wangxin Cai, Xingyun Chen, Yifan Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The construction of transmission lines faces challenges such as unclear underground geological conditions and inaccurate pile foundation bearing capacity assessment, which leads to high construction safety risks and difficult cost control. This study proposes a new micro-motion detection method that integrates distributed fiber optic sensing (DFOS) and background noise cross-correlation technology. By deploying a high-density fiber optic sensor array to capture natural field micro-motion signals, the three-dimensional underground shear wave velocity (Vs) structure along the transmission line is inverted using a machine learning algorithm. On this basis, a construction optimization framework based on spatiotemporal feature fusion is developed: the graph convolutional network (GCN) is used to extract the topological correlation features of the tower node displacement, and the long short-term memory network (LSTM) is combined to predict the deformation trend of key sections; deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is then used to construct a real-time decision model for construction parameters. Experiments show that the average absolute error of the underground interface depth obtained by micro-motion inversion is 2.295 meters, and the stratum identification accuracy reaches 95%; the DRL optimization scheme based on stratum prediction improves the compliance rate of pile foundation bearing capacity and significantly reduces the rework costs caused by unknown geological conditions.

title: IoT-Enabled Coronary Heart Health Monitoring System Using ESP8266 with Real-Time Cloud Integration
authors: Akshat Mishra, Priyanka Garg, Nookala Venu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: IoT technologies have seen enormous growth and have made it possible for low-cost, continuous, and remote monitoring and measurement of vital physiological signals. The current research work designs and develops a low-cost, compact coronary heart health monitoring system using an ESP8266 microcontroller with pulse and LM35 temperature sensor modules. Continuous pulse rate and temperature values are measured and then communicated online to the cloud-based ThingSpeak platform. The result obtained shows that the sensor works continuously with high accuracy and low-latency cloud updates, ensuring that the system functions effectively and properly. Numerical simulations were conducted, and it shows that the designed model maintains acceptable physiological signal values with high data transmission accuracy. The outcome of this work clearly confirms that IoT-based technologies have enormous capabilities and scope in effectively assisting with early health anomaly detection and preventive healthcare.

title: A Sub-0.3 ppm/°C and High-PSRR Current-Mode Bandgap Reference in SCL 180nm CMOS for High-Performance LDOs
authors: Banothu Bapuji, Venkata Krishna Odugu, Harish Babu Gade
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a sub-0.3 ppm/°C high-precision current-mode Bandgap Reference (BGR) in SCL 180 nm CMOS technology, targeted for high-performance low-dropout (LDO) regulators. The proposed BGR employs current-mode proportional-to-absolute-temperature (PTAT) and complementary-to-absolute-temperature (CTAT) current generation with optimized first-order and residual second-order temperature compensation, enabling ultra-low temperature drift over a wide operating range. To achieve high power supply rejection ratio (PSRR), cascoded biasing and supply-independent current mirrors are incorporated, effectively suppressing supply noise coupling into the reference node. The circuit generates a stable reference voltage of approximately 1.2 V while consuming only a few µW of power, making it highly suitable for energy-constrained and noise-sensitive systems. Post-layout simulations in SCL 180 nm CMOS confirm a temperature coefficient as low as 0.27 ppm/°C across –40 °C to 125 °C, PSRR exceeding 150 dB at low frequencies, and robust operation under process–voltage–temperature (PVT) variations. The combination of sub-ppm/°C temperature stability, high PSRR, and ultra-low power establishes the proposed design as an efficient and reliable reference solution for advanced analog, mixed-signal, and power-management applications.

title: An Inductive Wireless Power Transfer System with Frequency Domain Analysis and Photovoltaic Design in Electric Vehicles
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Recharging an Electric Vehicle (EV) battery with electricity that has been "put back" into the grid is essentially replenishing the battery with power, which is most of the time done by means of cables or through systems that allow power to be sent without wires. We came up with an inductive wireless power and feedback-driven photovoltaic (IWPFDP) system that facilitates renewable energy-based wireless EV charging to be more efficient. The unit generates power through a Photovoltaic (PV) module that is made energy-efficient by using a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) method that takes different environmental factors into account. A high- frequency inverter changes the direct current output for wireless sending to another coil by using inductive power transfer. This setup implements an Active Load Impedance Matching (ALIM) scheme to power flow more efficiently and lessen the conversion losses. Balanced pulsed-width modulation makes sure charging stays steady, even when load conditions change. The performance metrics are efficiency, voltage & power, power loss and load. It achieves 70% at 250 W to 80% at 700W, over 77% at 550W, where the 310V range achieves 80V at 9A, and decreases by 40V at 1A, later 650W to 800W was achieved in 250 mins duration, the output from 262W to 725W we achieve 264.9W in HF - inerter of (149.5W, in 56%), IPT of (99.1W, in 37%), and HF - rectifier of (16.3W, in 6%).

title: Multimodal Hybrid Deep Learning for Fetal Health Prognosis: Integrating CTG Signals and Clinical Records
authors: Niranjani K, Senthil K, Ayyappan G
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Whether the baby is healthy or not is paramount because hypoxia, developmental lag and stillbirth are some of the undesired consequences of fetal health monitoring. With traditional cardiotocography (CTG) interpretation, clinical expertise and handcrafted features are used extensively, which tends to be subjective and can lead to points of inter-observer variability. In this work, we introduce a hybrid deep learning model based on the combination of CNNs(Convolutional Neural Networks) and LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) networks, Rhythms of the Womb, to automatically decode CTG signals to predict the fetal health. Raw fetal heart rate and uterine contraction signals were pre-processed, normalized and converted to spectrograms to allow time and frequency-based feature extraction. The CNN layers learn spatial features of the spectrograms, whereas LSTM layers learn the time duration dependencies. Fully connected layers with SoftMax activation output predict fetal states as normal, suspicious, and pathological states. This model was tested on benchmark CTG dataset and shows overall 95.0 accuracy, surpassing both traditional machine learning approaches like Random Forest and SVM, as well as the current single-architecture CNN or LSTM models. The findings indicate the effectiveness of the automated spatiotemporal feature extraction in the detection of pathophysiological fetal patterns that represents a scalable, reliable, and clinically relevant tool in prenatal care.

title: Multi-Layer Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Phishing Detection with Explainability
authors: Amrutiya Urvish, Dhruv Loriya, Govinda NB, Khush Loriya, Shriniwas Maheshwari, Minal Moharir
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Phishing attacks are among the biggest and growing cybersecurity risks, and fraudsters use social engineering. URL obfuscation, and use of Unicode homograph to circumvent the old email security systems. Available methods of detection are frequently not particularly accurate and have high false-positive rates in dealing with such sophisticated attacks. This paper presents Email Guard - A multi-layer phishing detection system, based on deep learning, machine learning, and rule-based analysis, that provides higher detection accuracy and interpretability of the system classification of features by URL using Gradient Boosting and classification of features by Logistic Regression at the decision level. Using a dataset of about 500 real-world emails consisting of legitimate, spam, and phishing emails, the system was trained and tested with a training set, validation set, and testing set. The experiments have shown that Email Guard has a total accuracy of 94.2%, precision and recall of 93.8% and 94.6% respectively and an average processing time of 0.23 seconds per email making it almost real time detection. Ablation analysis demonstrates a 12% accuracy improvement of the multi-layer architecture to a single layer method. Also, the system creates confidence scores and human-readable explanations, which make false-positive complaints 15% lower in user assessments.

title: Real time Face Feeling Recognition using YOLO-based Face Detection and Deep Feature-Driven Machine Learning Classification
authors: Vimala P, R. Selvakani, R. Kavitha, Raaghav. R.S.T, Logeshwaran. T, Jyotheesh. S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Emotional computing, intelligent surveillance systems, and human computer interaction all depend heavily on facial emotion recognition (FER). For real time applications, however, maintaining low computational complexity while obtaining high identification accuracy continues to a major difficulty. An optimised convolutional CNN based FER framework is presented in this study along with a performance analysis that compares it to other approaches, such as lightweight CNNs, landmark based attention models, transformer based FER architectures, and hybrid deep feature methods. Through effective parameter optimisation and task specific architectural modification, the suggested optimised CNN architecture improves discriminative feature learning. The suggested process outperforms state of FER techniques transversely conventional assessment measures. The robustness of the suggested approach in reducing misclassification mistakes is confirmed by the balanced improvement in precision and recall. These findings suggest that effective trade off between computational efficiency then recognition presentation can be achieved with optimised CNN-based designs. As a result, the suggested FER architecture is a viable option for useful affect-aware intelligent systems since it works well in real time and resource-constrained settings.

title: Adaptive Dynamic Live-Line Work Bypass Load-Bearing Platform Leveling Kit
authors: Changlin Leng, Mingjie Luo, Zhuqin Luo, Haitao Wang, Wu Pu, Yi Zhang, Erjie Lu, Shanshan Li, Biao Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the continuous increase in requirements for power system operational reliability and supply continuity, liveline bypass construction has become increasingly widespread in transmission and distribution maintenance. During bypass operations, the load-bearing platform is susceptible to factors such as changes in conductor tension, shifting operational loads, and external environmental disturbances under complex working conditions. These factors can lead to platform attitude imbalance, adversely affecting operational safety and construction efficiency. Addressing the limitations of existing bypass platform leveling methods-specifically their inadequate adaptability and restricted dynamic response capabilities-this paper proposes an adaptive dynamic leveling system for live-line bypass platforms. By integrating attitude sensing, dynamic leveling actuators, and adaptive control units, the system enables real-time monitoring and rapid adjustment of platform posture changes, maintaining stability and horizontal alignment across diverse operational conditions. Structural design analysis, operational principle evaluation, and field application verification demonstrate that this integrated system outperforms conventional leveling solutions in precision, response speed, and operational stability. It significantly enhances the safety and reliability of live-line bypass construction. These findings provide valuable insights for advancing the intelligent and integrated development of live-line work equipment.

title: Modeling and Optimization of Particle Beam of Linear Accelerator Machines Using AI for Health Application
authors: Zairov Temirlan, Ilyas Muhammad, Abdullah Aamir Hayat, Shawana Noor
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This work investigates the application of artificial intelligence for improving patient lateral positioning accuracy in external beam radiotherapy using a linear accelerator. A predictive setup optimization framework based on a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is proposed to compensate for patient motion, particularly respiratoryinduced displacement. The system is evaluated using a programmable QA phantom tool and a motorized treatment couch, with parameters calibrated from real measurements acquired on a Varian TrueBeam STX linear accelerator. Simulation results demonstrate a median post-correction residual positioning error of 0.6 mm, with 95% of cases remaining below the 2 mm clinical tolerance threshold. The results demonstrate that the proposed AI-based prediction framework can improve patient positioning accuracy and may support real-time adaptive radiotherapy workflows.

title: ISBDAS 2026 Preface

title: WordCon: Word-level Typography Control in Visual Text Rendering
authors: Wenda Shi, Yiren Song, Zihan Rao, Dengming Zhang, Jiaming Liu, Xingxing Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Visual text rendering represents a fundamental capability of large-scale text-to-image (T2I) models, yet achieving precise word-level controllability remains a significant challenge in this domain. While existing approaches primarily focus on text content accuracy, they often fail to provide fine-grained control over typographic attributes at the word level. To address this limitation, we introduce a comprehensive solution comprising three key components: (1) a novel word-level controlled scene text dataset and benchmark, (2) the Text-Image Alignment (TIA) framework that leverages cross-modal correspondence between textual queries and local image regions through grounding models, and (3) WordCon, a hybrid parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) method that employs selective parameter reparameterization to enhance both computational efficiency and model portability. The proposed framework incorporates additive supervision mechanisms: a masked loss at the latent level to focus on text regions, and a joint-attention loss at the feature level to promote disentanglement between different words. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both qualitative and quantitative metrics. The proposed method exhibits remarkable versatility, enabling seamless integration with diverse pipelines, including artistic text rendering and image-conditioned text generation. Our datasets, source code, and models will be available for academic research.

title: Delay-Doppler Domain Channel Measurements and Modeling in High-Speed Railways
authors: Hao Zhou, Yiyan Ma, Dan Fei, Weirong Liu, Zhengyu Zhang, Mi Yang, Guoyu Ma, Yunlong Lu, Ruisi He, Guoyu Wang, Cheng Li, Zhaohui Song, Bo Ai
abstract: As next-generation wireless communication systems need to be able to operate in high-frequency bands and high-mobility scenarios, delay-Doppler (DD) domain multicarrier (DDMC) modulation schemes, such as orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS), demonstrate superior reliability over orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM). Accurate DD domain channel modeling is essential for DDMC system design. However, since traditional channel modeling approaches are mainly confined to time, frequency, and space domains, the principles of DD domain channel modeling remain poorly studied. To address this issue, we propose a systematic DD domain channel measurement and modeling methodology in high-speed railway (HSR) scenarios. First, we design a DD domain channel measurement method based on the long-term evolution for railway (LTE-R) system. Second, for DD domain channel modeling, we investigate quasi-stationary interval, statistical power modeling of multipath components, and particularly, the quasi-invariant intervals of DD domain channel fading coefficients. Third, via LTE-R measurements at 371 km/h, taking the quasi-stationary interval as the decision criterion, we establish DD domain channel models under different channel time-varying conditions in HSR scenarios. Fourth, the accuracy of proposed DD domain channel models is validated via bit error rate comparison of OTFS transmission. In addition, simulation verifies that in HSR scenario, the quasi-invariant interval of DD domain channel fading coefficient is on millisecond (ms) order of magnitude, which is much smaller than the quasi-stationary interval length on 100 ms order of magnitude. This study could provide theoretical guidance for DD domain modeling in high-mobility environments, supporting future DDMC and integrated sensing and communication designs for 6G and beyond.

title: A Consortium Blockchain Scheme for Secure Collaboration in BIM
authors: Sihan Li, Min Wang, Guoxin Huang, Yuna Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Blockchain technology, with its traceability and tamper-proof features, provides a viable solution for defining responsibilities and enabling traceability in Building Information Modeling (BIM) design processes. To address the limitations of existing BIM collaboration platforms in terms of compatibility, storage efficiency, and traceability convenience, we propose a secure BIM collaboration scheme based on enterprise consortium blockchain. The scheme treats a whole BIM model file as the fundamental on-chain unit, a single file is stored for each block instead of incremental data storage. Therefore, it is compatible with multiple BIM model formats, significantly reducing the number of generated blocks, and decreasing on-chain latency. Based on the scheme, we design and implement a BIM collaboration and traceability platform to ensure reliable data collaboration while maintaining compatibility and usability. Our scheme boosts blockchain storage and traceability efficiency in BIM Collaboration by 85.7%, while provide reference for BIM information in intelligent construction. It also demonstrates a concrete engineering case where blockchain empowers trustworthy computing and decentralized data sharing.

title: MegaScale-Data: Scaling DataLoader for Multisource Large Foundation Model Training

title: Chimera: Transparent and High-Performance ISAX Heterogeneous Computing via Binary Rewriting
authors: Jiatai He, Qinglin Pan, Ruilin Zhao, Ji Qi, Kaiwen Liang, Jiahao Xu, Zhiyuan Li, Yuexiang Wang, Jiageng Yu, Yanjun Wu
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems
