Results from model evaluation indicated clinically meaningful prediction patterns with 3 predictors with the highest influence including the presence of metabolic biomarkers, status with diabetic retinopathy and age (Table 1). The model performed reasonably well for overall accuracy and exceedingly well for false positive rates among all age groups, but high false negative rates suggested the inability to correctly identify true disease cases, even if the disease was present, particularly in younger populations. Calibration analysis showed that the model was very well calibrated for predictions with high confidence but it overstated risk on predictions with mid-probability ranges. The accuracy continued to improve with age peaked at individuals 80+ where disease markers are more apparent. In conclusion, these results emphasize that metabolic and demographic data can facilitate eye disease prediction while demonstrating the need for increased sensitivity and better probability calibration for the early-screening application of these models.

title: Design and Implementation of 32-bit Pipelined RISC Processor with VLSI optimized ALU and AI Acceleration : Efficient Pipeline Architecture with High Performance ALU Optimization
authors: Nithin V Y, Pramod Raj T, Shraddha Kalmath, Srushti R, Hariprasad T L
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Designing and implementing high-performance processors requires a trade-off between speed, area, and energy efficiency, increasingly relevant in modern computing environments where artificial intelligence (AI) workloads comprise a growing share of the total workloads. This paper presents the architecture and implementation of a 32-bit pipelined RISC processor with a VLSI-optimized Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), coupled with the processor’s specific AI acceleration. The processor uses a five-stage instruction pipeline—fetch, decode, execute, memory access, and write-back—to boost instruction throughput while reducing structural and data hazards. The ALU has been optimized at the transistor and gate levels to use low power while minimizing critical path delay for energy-efficient silicon real estate. Furthermore, an AI acceleration unit has been added to perform computationally heavy tasks, like machine learning inference, based on combining specialized multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations with parallel datapath structure. The processor was implemented in Verilog HDL, verified in ModelSim simulation, and shows statistically significant improvement in execution performance over a baseline RISC implementation, while minimizing hardware complexity. We further synthesised the design onto FPGA and explored area, timing, and power trade-offs to confirm viability for System-on-Chip (SoC) for embedded platforms. This work enhances capabilities in the development of energy-efficiend SoCs.

title: Federated Continual Digital Twins for Industrial Edge Systems
authors: Abishek Ragav J, Aldan Dallas D, Priya S B
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The proliferation of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems demands adaptive digital twin models that can learn continuously from distributed edge devices while respecting privacy constraints and communication budgets. Existing approaches either centralize data (violating privacy), employ static models (failing to adapt), or ignore the unique challenges of edge deployments (intermittent connectivity, resource constraints). We present FedCoDiT, a federated continual learning framework for digital twins that operates under stringent edge constraints. Our approach combines experience replay with elastic weight consolidation for local continual learning, implements differential privacy guarantees during federated aggregation, and employs adaptive device selection for asynchronous updates. Experimental validation on NASA C-MAPSS turbofan degradation data, UCI industrial datasets, and synthetic manufacturing scenarios demonstrates that FedCoDiT achieves 87.3% average accuracy across sequential tasks while consuming only 12.4 MB communication per device and maintaining (ϵ = 0.1, δ = 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-5</sup>)- differential privacy—outperforming baseline federated learning methods by 23.6% in catastrophic forgetting mitigation while reducing communication costs by 89%.

title: Neuro-Emotion Recognition for Enhanced Driver Safety using Electroencephalography
authors: Yeshaboina Kumara Swamy, Duggirala Mouli, Ch. Raga Madhuri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The present work is a real-time system that monitors driver emotions and identifies them using Electroencephalogram (EEG) signals to enhance road safety. Driver emotions such as anger, fatigue, happiness, and neutrality are identified with EEG data obtained from an EEG sensor paired with an Arduino Uno. The states are identified using the application of simple threshold method due to the simplicity and real-time operation of the system. Upon identification of a state, the system gives feedback using sound with a DFPlayer Mini and speaker setup, and also shows visual feedback on Serial Monitor. With the provision of two types of feedback, drivers have instant situational awareness of the emotional state using minimal computational power. The prototype is not only technically simple, but offers a low-cost, lightweight, and hardware-based solution that can potentially minimize the number of motor vehicle accidents that can be caused by unsafe states of emotional conditions. Future iterations of this work can further enhance this work with the application of machine learning algorithms to improve the validity of classifications and variations between drivers.

title: Automated Quality Inspection in Robotic Assembly Lines Using Logistic Regression and Deep Feature
authors: P. Murugan, M. Manickam, B. Karthik, S Pradeksha, M Tharani.
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Robotic assembly lines require automated quality inspection to ensure precision and productivity in industrial production. It introduces a smart inspection model that combines deep feature mining and logistic regression classification to improve the level of defect detection. The model makes use of convolutional layers to learn features and logistic regression in the binary classification of defective and non-defective components. The testing accuracy of 97.9%, precision of 98.1%, recall of 97.6%, and F1-score of 97.8% obtained experimental validation with an inference time of 14.9 ms and a processing speed of 33 FPS, which allows the operation to be real-time. The system also saved the energy use by 23%, and the reliability of decision of the system improved by 4% as compared to the current approaches. The proposed solution has a better performance in terms of inspection and computational power, which has created a strong base to be used in the future in industrial applications in the fully automated robotic assembly setting.

title: IoT-Enabled Remote Restoration and Enhancement of Mammogram Images adapted to Poisson Noise using Deep Residual CNN
authors: Sanjeev Singh Rawat, R.B. Yadav, Manoj Kumar, Tara
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Poisson noise poses significant challenges in medical imaging, particularly in mammography, where accurate representation is crucial for reliable diagnosis. To denoise mammography images affected by Poisson noise, this paper introduces a novel method utilizing a deep residual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). By employing a residual learning framework, the proposed approach enables the network to effectively learn residual mappings. This enhanced denoising capability is vital for the rapidly evolving field of IoT-enabled medical imaging, where devices may capture and transmit images from diverse locations, often leveraging cloud infrastructure for storage and computational power, necessitating robust preprocessing to maintain data integrity and ensure diagnostic accuracy in real-time or near real-time. Comprehensive testing and comparison with standard Poisson denoising methods demonstrate that the deep residual CNN outperforms others. The evaluation considers various noise levels and reflects scenarios typical of real-world medical imaging, including data sourced from multiple IoT-connected sensors and imaging units. The deep residual CNN significantly enhances denoising performance, as shown by the results, indicating its ability to extract intricate structures and features from mammograms. This study identifies the deep residual CNN as a powerful and superior option, offering promising enhancements for mammogram images that are Poisson-corrupted, with the potential to improve diagnostic accuracy in breast cancer detection and characterization, ultimately bolstering the reliability of IoT-driven healthcare diagnostic workflows.

title: Proactive Safety Management Decision-Making Model for Power Transmission and Transformation Projects Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Guokai Weng, Huazhong Chen, Cheng Lin, Damiao Yang, Yi Huang, Jingwei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Addressing bottlenecks in traditional safety management models for power transmission and transformation projects, such as delayed risk perception and passive decision-making, this paper proposes a proactive safety decision-making model based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL). This approach constructs a state space that integrates multi-source IoT data and operational information, designs a hierarchical safety intervention action space, and quantifies safety performance through a reward function based on domain knowledge. The Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm is then used to train an intelligent agent to autonomously learn optimal risk management strategies within a digital twin environment. Experimental results show that the PPO model's average warning time is 7.31 seconds faster than that of the expert system; the proactive detection rate of hidden dangers in unknown and complex working conditions reaches 90%; and the average investment cost of the PPO model's safety solutions in terms of safety resource input-output efficiency is 92,300 yuan. These experimental results verify the superiority of the PPO-based proactive decision-making model in risk perception, decision reliability, and optimal resource allocation, demonstrating its ability to effectively enhance the intelligent level of safety management in power transmission and transformation projects.

title: A Vision Language Model for Automated Pancreatic Cancer Diagnosis and Radiology Report Generation using CT and MRI
authors: Bharath Kumara J, Vinopriya Vijayaboopathy, Kumarasenthil Muthuvel, Prabhu Krishnaswamy, Dharmeesh Kondaveeti, Amsa Selvaraj
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Pancreatic cancer is the cause of one of the most lethal malignancies due to late detection and complex radiological analysis. Nowadays, detection and staging are greatly dependent on CECT and MRI; however, it is time consuming to perform manual analysis and generate radiology reports, accompanied by inter observer variability. In this paper, a vision language framework for pancreatic cancer diagnosis and radiology report generation using CT and MRI data is developed. In particular, the architecture combines modality specific vision encoders, a transformer based language model and a cross attention based fusion module under a multi task learning paradigm. Experimental evaluation on a retrospective multimodal dataset proves that the proposed model achieves the best diagnostic accuracy of 92.6%, sensitivity of 91.8%, specificity of 93.1% and an AUC of 0.962, clearly outperforming the unimodal and vision only baselines. On radiology report generation, the end to end multimodal vision language model reaches BLEU 0.384, ROUGE L 0.497 and METEOR 0.351, achieving better clinical coherence and completeness. Results from ablation and sensitivity analyses further validate the importance of balanced CT–MRI fusion and language supervision.

title: EduMind: AI Powered Interactive Study Companion
authors: G. Charles Babu, Bingi Jayanth, Padala Pranavsai, Tholakoppula Sai Kumar, Guduru Adeep Reddy, Inaganti Sandeep
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: EduMind: Providing a study aid via AI to revolutionize the involvement of students in learning. The study aid tool integrates vector search retrieval algorithms, online quiz generation mechanisms, and personal language models on a framework that maintains user privacy. The tool has a FastAPI-run backing application programming interface and a React Native-developed mobile app user interface. The study aid tool has real-time QA from learning materials, automatic topic summarization of study materials, as well as online quiz generation from study documents submitted by users/typed content. The study aid application promises secured learning information without resorting to cloud computing services using inference mechanisms from Ollama and LLMs such as Mistral 7B. The study will cover the architecture, approach/methodology, implementation process of EduMind, as well as its critical review with a focus on how the technology can help improve study outcomes while upholding user privacy.

title: Spatial Metrics based evaluation of urban growth: A case of Jaipur
authors: Deeksha Pendharker, Asfa Siddiqui, Manoj Panwar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The spatial urbanization patterns show deviation from the developments proposed in master plans and highlights the timely interventions through informed decision making. Spatial metrics have been very helpful in examining the urban growth dynamics and its growth directions. Authors used well established Shannon Entropy and Area Weighted Mean Expansion Index (AWMEI) to analyze compactness and dispersal of urban growth; and Landscape Expansion Index (LEI) analyses urban growth type as infill, edge expansion or outlying growth. The analysis is done on opensource QGIS 3.24 by taking case of Jaipur urban area using data from Global Human Settlement Layer program of European Commission Joint Research Center from the year 1990 to year 2020. The results of Shannon Entropy and AWMEI are highly correlated and inform that urbanization rate is high in 2010-2020 decade in comparison to previous decades. The results of LEI apprise that the city maintains urban core and grows along transportation corridors while increased outgrowth is observed near satellite towns. These findings are significant for urban planners and policymakers as a base to formulate and establish long term strategies, and timely interventions in masterplan for managing Jaipur’s urbanization through informed decisions.

title: LLM-Powered Conversational Therapy System with Journaling and Cognitive Reframing Capabilities
authors: Shaik Aabid Farhan, E Udhayadhanush, R Poonkuzhali
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Global mental health services continue to struggle with workforce limitations, barriers in access, and treatment delays. The current study introduces a domain-adapted Large Language Model platform that fuses conversational support, structured journaling with embedded cognitive pattern detection, and dynamic affirmations into a monitoring framework. Fine-tuning the LLaMA architecture with Unsloth on a synthetic dataset of roughly 88,000 conversation samples derived from publicly available mental health dialogue corpora provides state-of-the-art performance in context-sensitive emotional understanding for multiple dialogue sessions. A novelty contribution is found in the shared computational memory layer enabling cross-modal information exchange across its conversational and journaling components. Comparative in silico assessment against rule-based, retrieval-based, and hybrid transformer baselines returns 62.3%, 66.8%, and 71.4% for response coherence score, respectively, against 87.2%, with 84.6% emotional recognition accuracy. Longitudinal trajectory analysis using this data identifies repeated stressors and emerging risk indicators, visualized in an interpretable format by the monitoring dashboard. This system does not constitute diagnostic clinical work or treatment, representing only a research tool. Supported cold-start adaptation enables appropriate contextual responses from the very first interactions based on a very short onboarding assessment. This integrated approach delivers some of the building blocks to underpin scalable digital mental health support research.

title: AI-Powered Document Analyzer
authors: Lavanya S, Madhan S, Aravindha T K, Dheepanraj M, HariKarthik S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The explosion of unstructured digital documents has rendered manual document processing not only inefficient but also hardly scalable. Different types of documents with diverse structures and qualities continue to come from organizations, making it even more challenging for them to accurately classify and extract information from these documents. To solve this issue, the paper proposes an AI- Powered Document Analyzer that combines Optical Character Recognition (OCR) with transformer, based Natural Language Processing (NLP) models to perform end, to, end document analysis. The suggested solution is tested through a mix of public and custom datasets of academic papers, invoices, and reports. The study of performance is done through accuracy, precision, recall, F1, score, and processing latency. Experimental data show that the LayoutLM model has reached a classification accuracy of 97.4% and an F1 score of 96.9%, thereby surpassing traditional machine learning methods as well as text-only transformer models. The results suggest that integrating layout and textual information enhances document comprehension and thus can be effectively used at scale for digital archiving, enterprise document management, and document verification applications.

title: Deep Learning for Digital Mental Health: A Cross-Social-Media Analysis using BERT
authors: Pramod Mehra, Om Prakash Pal, Pawan Kumar Mishra, Vartika Agarwal, Rahul Kumar, Jyotir Moy Chatterjee
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Mental health has emerged as a pressing global concern, with increasing numbers of individuals using social media platforms to share their emotional experiences and psychological states. This project explores and analyzes mental health-related discourse across Reddit, Tumblr, and YouTube. By leveraging Natural Language Processing (NLP) and transformer-based models such as BERT, we classify user-generated content into various mental health categories such as depression, anxiety, PTSD, and stress. Our system enables cross-platform analysis to assess the emotional tone, severity, and distribution of psychological distress. Results contribute toward scalable, real-time mental health monitoring and digital well-being research.

title: Pose-Guided Multi-Scale Vision Transformer for Robust and Accurate Person Re-Identification in Visual Surveillance
authors: Shimja M, Anupriya Ramachandran, Krishna Priya, Saiqa Khan N, Abhilash K S, Sreekanth S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Person Re-Identification (Re-ID) is the task of matching images of the same individual captured across multiple non-overlapping surveillance cameras. It plays a crucial role in intelligent video surveillance, public safety, and criminal investigation by enabling automated tracking of individuals across different locations. However, challenges such as pose variation, occlusion, illumination changes, and inconsistent image resolution make accurate re-identification difficult. Traditional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and attention-based models have achieved moderate success but often fall short in capturing long-range dependencies and semantic understanding. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Pose-Guided Multi-Scale ViT-ReID model with Part Tokens and an Attribute Head, which integrates a Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone for global spatial reasoning, pose-aware token weighting to emphasize visible body parts, and multi-scale patch embedding for resolution robustness. An attribute prediction head is further employed to incorporate semantic-level identity cues. The model is trained and evaluated on the DukeMTMC-reID dataset, comprising 36,411 images across 1,812 identities from 8 cameras. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves 99.00% Rank-1 accuracy, 99.50% Rank-5 accuracy, 99.80% Rank-10 accuracy, and a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 85.12%, outperforming existing approaches. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of combining spatial, pose, and semantic cues in enhancing person re-identification performance under real-world surveillance conditions.

title: State-Wise Sports Participation Analysis based on Gender in India using Machine Learning
authors: A. Tamilarasi, M. Jagadeesan, Jayalakshmi S, Janisha Flavy Florina J, Iswarya M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Sports participation is not the same across states, nor are the participation levels same for men or women, confirmed government data and visualizations in Power BI were utilized in this study to understand participation, gender ratio and regional gaps. There are states that seem to show a nearly gender balanced participation rate, and some have a higher participation for females, while other states illustrate disparities, and do so significantly with some states having alarming disparity rates, in part due to culture, and in part due to infrastructure or education. These findings are meant to support policymakers make informed choices to strengthen access to the sport and physical activity opportunities proportionally across the country.

title: Enhancing Factual Reliability in Large Language Models Through Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Shreya S, K R Sai Harsha, Nagaraj S, Shivakumar Rejental
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable progress in natural language understanding and generation. Despite their success, hallucination—where models generate fluent but factually incorrect or unsupported information—remains a major challenge that limits their reliability in real-world applications. This study aims to develop and evaluate a hybrid framework that improves factual consistency by combining Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Milvus-based vector search and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) fine-tuning. The proposed system retrieves semantically relevant documents using Sentence-BERT embeddings, integrates the retrieved evidence through structured prompt engineering, and employs parameter-efficient fine-tuning to guide the model toward grounded and evidence-based responses. The hybrid model is evaluated against a baseline LLaMA-2-7B model using Semantic Similarity, Hallucination Rate, Fact Score, and Accuracy metrics. Experimental results show a significant reduction in hallucination rate (51.00% to 20.00%) and notable improvements in factual accuracy (24.30% to 60.67%). These results demonstrate that integrating vector-based retrieval with efficient fine-tuning provides a practical and scalable approach for enhancing the trustworthiness of LLM systems.

title: Intelligent Field Monitoring For Real-Time Animal Movement Detection using A Yolo-based Deep Learning Approach
authors: D. Dhanasekaran, P. Kumar, Poovendan R, Sabari S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: One of the biggest problems in agricultural production is the intrusion of animals in crops which can result in loss of huge quantity and economic harm. Traditional methods such as manual surveillance, physical fencing and simple sensor-triggered alarms are labor intensive, expensive and likely to raise false alarms. To overcome these weaknesses, the current paper introduces a smart field monitoring system that will be used to detect movements of animals in real-time using a YOLOv11-based deep learning model. The suggested system is used to capture images and video streams in live fields, use preprocessing tools based on OpenCV, including noise reduction, resizing, and normalization, and locate animals with high precision in relation to changing lighting and environmental factors. In order to have real-time performance, feature optimization and dimensionality reduction techniques are used to reduce computational overhead and still have good detection accuracy. When an intrusion is detected, the system starts a multi-level response, which involves automatic logging of events to the system, real time email notification with details of the intrusion, and the activation of deterrence devices like buzzers and LED floodlights, particularly at night. A specific web and mobile interface makes farmers keep track of events remotely and regulate warning systems. As illustrated by experimental assessment, the system is characterized by high detection rate, low latency, and lower false positive rates and provides a cost-effective, scalable, and efficient system towards smart agriculture, crop protection, farm security, and sustainable food production.

title: LSTM Autoencoder-based Anomaly Detection for Network Traffic Analysis
authors: Reshma Jebashree S R, Anusha Bamini A M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Traditional signature-based intrusion detection systems are ineffective against evolving cyber threats as complex temporal and behavioral characteristics of modern network traffic. An LSTM autoencoder-based anomaly detection framework for network traffic analysis is demonstrated in this paper. It learns normal traffic patterns from unlabeled data and uses reconstruction errors to detect anomalies. The suggested method captures traffic frequency, behavioral entropy, session dynamics, and error patterns on comprehensive feature engineering at the request, session, and IP levels. A sequence-to-sequence By modeling long-term temporal dependencies in network traffic, the LSTM autoencoder makes it possible to identify minute and time-dependent anomalies. The LSTM model is integrated into an ensemble architecture combining Isolation Forest, K-Means clustering, and a dense autoencoder to increase detection robustness. Final anomaly predictions generate using an adaptive thresholding strategy optimized on validation data. The proposed model effectively identifies anomalous traffic behaviors like sudden request bursts, irregular access patterns, and temporal deviations, according to experimental evaluation on real-world network traffic logs. The concluded method is suitable for practical cybersecurity monitoring applications since the results indicate increased detection accuracy and accuracy when compared to individual models.

title: A Cloud-based LLM-Powered RAG and SQL Agent Framework for Reliable Personal Finance Assistance
authors: L Kartheesan, Syed Faheez Ahamad, Muvvala Sai Puneeth
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Accelerated advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) has enable smarter financial assistant systems. This paper presents an in-depth development roadmap for a cloud-native personal finance chatbot that will be built upon Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in tandem with SQL agents to provide accurate and financially relevant guidance. The envisioned system aims to address the existing critical challenges of AI hallucination, data staleness, and the need for personalized financial advice, by incorporating external knowledge retrieval as well as structured database querying. It explore the theoretical underpinnings of RAG systems, analyze multi-agent SQL generation techniques and propose an architecture that unifies these technologies to build a robust personal finance assistant. Consequently, the system will be able to handle complex financial queries, while also ensuring production-grade scalability and reliability through a centralized cloud-based reasoning engine.

title: DA-TSK-PLR-FS: Domain Adaptive Takagi–Sugeno–Kang Fuzzy System via Pseudolabel Refinement for CCTA-Based Vulnerable Coronary Plaques Recognition
authors: Yuanpeng Zhang, Wei Zhang, Xinyun Zhang, Zhaoheng Huang, Sheng Huang, Saikit Lam, Ge Ren, Shitong Wang, Jing Cai
abstract: Artificial intelligence has shown great promise in noninvasive recognition of vulnerable coronary plaques. However, practical data issues in multicenter studies, such as inconsistent data distribution and insufficient or missing data labels, could significantly affect the recognition accuracy. Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) can be introduced to address this challenge, but several limits still remain. First, many existing UDA models are black boxes, hindering healthcare professionals' ability to interpret and trust the model's decision. Second, some methods use pseudolabel to enhance performance, but often overlook the quality assessment of these pseudolabels, potentially leading to negative knowledge transfer. To this end, based on the interpretable Takagi–Sugeno–Kang fuzzy system (TSK-FS), a novel domain adaptive method is proposed to improve model generalizability for vulnerable coronary plaques recognition in multicenter data. First of all, TSK-FS is employed to construct a shared fuzzy feature space for the source domain and the target domain, aiming to better align data distribution. To make full use of the information of unlabeled target domain data and further reduce the negative knowledge transfer, the enhanced pseudolabel learning mechanism is further introduced by combining the graph-based random walking and label filtering. Moreover, Multicenter data of 910 patients with suspected or diagnosed coronary artery disease were collected from three hospitals for experiments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed DA-TSK-PLR-FS achieves the promising generalizability across multicenter datasets

title: Microwave Probe Based on an Inverted Microstrip Line for Broadband Electron Paramagnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
authors: Selina Eckel, Matthias Oliver Beck, Ahmet Çağrı Ulusoy
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a broadband inverted microstrip line (IMSL) as a microwave probe for electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) spectroscopy, a technique used to detect unpaired electron spins in biological and chemical samples. The IMSL geometry enables sample placement in the most homogeneous region of the microwave magnetic field, overcoming limitations of standard coplanar waveguide (CPW) and microstrip line (MSL) probes. Simulated and measured S-parameters confirm broadband operation up to 67 GHz. Improved field homogeneity relative to MSLs is demonstrated by a reduced coefficient of variation of the simulated magnetic field and by EPR measurements showing signal amplitudes independent of sample orientation. The IMSL geometry offers a compact microwave probe with straightforward fabrication for broadband EPR sensing in biological and healthcare applications.

title: Interconnected Observer-Based SMC for DC–DC Converters With Disturbances and Noises
authors: Peng Liu, Zuo Wang, Jinya Su, Ji Wang, Shihua Li
abstract: The sliding mode controller (SMC) is inherently sensitive to noise due to its discontinuous control, especially under mismatched disturbances. To improve the noise robustness and disturbance rejection of SMC, this work proposes a tightly integrated framework for DC–DC converters. Rather than simple combinations of separate modules, it seamlessly unifies a filter, disturbance observer (DOB), and SMC. A novel discrete-time interconnected observer, fusing a DOB and an adaptive Kalman filter, simultaneously reconstructs disturbances and suppresses noise. By leveraging filtered states and disturbance estimates, compensation for both mismatched disturbance and input discretization dynamics is explicitly embedded into an improved sliding mode surface. Rigorous stability analysis of the closed-loop system is provided, and comparative simulations and experiments on DC–DC converter are conducted against three benchmark control methods.

title: Resource Allocation Strategy for Multiple Maneuvering Target Tracking in Networked Radar System
authors: Jieyu Huang, Junwei Xie, Haowei Zhang, Weike Feng, Kun Wang
abstract: Networked collocated multiple-input multiple-output (C-MIMO) radar system has attracted more and more attention in recent years. Networked radar system can offer a higher tracking accuracy for multiple maneuvering targets tracking (MTT). This article proposes a joint resource allocation (JRA) strategy in networked C-MIMO radar system. Specifically, the power, bandwidth and beam resources for each radar are optimized by the JRA strategy to improve the MTT accuracy. The predicted conditional Cramér–Rao lower bound is utilized as the tracking evaluation criterion. A fast three-stage solution algorithm is proposed to solve the nonconvex and mixed-integer problem. The simulation results show that the strategy is effective and efficient.

title: Guest Editorial Introduction to the Special Section on the 2025 RFIC Symposium
authors: Omeed Momeni

title: Towards Well-Connected Retina Segmentation: A Fully Differentiable Endpoint Connectivity Loss (DECL)
authors: Jannik Sobisch, Jaden Myers, Mirco Fuchs, Žiga Bizjak
abstract: Despite achieving high volumetric overlap, pixel-based deep learning segmentation approaches do not ensure true vascular connectivity, leading to fragmentations structurally analogous to Branch Retinal Artery Occlusions. This fundamental deficiency means that even segmentations achieving high pixel-wise overlap metrics (such as the Dice Score) do not guarantee or improve true vascular connectivity, rendering them unreliable for quantitative analysis and subsequent clinical risk stratification of high-risk systemic events, such as stroke. To resolve this, we introduce the Differentiable Endpoint Connectivity Loss (DECL), a novel, fully differentiable loss function that directly optimizes vascular continuity by precisely targeting errors in vessel endpoints. DECL leverages a differentiable soft-skeletonization module to impose two specialized penalties: a Normalized Count Penalty (L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub>), which rigorously penalizes the discrete difference in the number of predicted versus true endpoints (ΔN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p</sub>), and a Normalized Distance Loss (L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>), which penalizes the Euclidean distance between the weighted centers of the true and predicted endpoints. In comparative experiments across four datasets (DRIVE, CHASE DB1, STARE, and OCTA-500), we tested DECL against established methods including Dice CE, clDice, cbDice, and SAC Loss. DECL and its hybrid variant, DECL<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">clDice</sub>, consistently recorded the highest mean Dice Score and clDice scores, specifically, DECL<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">clDice</sub>d improved the mean clDice score from 0.839 to 0.851 on CHASE DB1 and from 0.836 to 0.842 on DRIVE, while minimizing Betti Number Error (β-Error) andVariation of Information. The DECL method establishes a robust, mathematically justified framework for generating high-fidelity vascular maps, essential for accurate biomarker quantification and reliable clinical assessment.

title: SUM4SOFT: A process model to build and update software samples
authors: Juan Andrés Carruthers, Andrea Lezcano Airaldi, Jorge Andrés Diaz-Pace, Emanuel Irrazábal
abstract: The validity of research findings critically depends on the selection of software samples. However, Mining Software Repositories studies often rely on outdated datasets and lack systematic, transparent procedures for repository selection and dataset construction, which hinder their replicability and undermine their findings. In this article, we propose SUM4SOFT, a process model to provide a structured, transparent, and replicable approach for building and maintaining software samples in Mining Software Repositories studies. We designed SUM4SOFT following a four-step methodology based on established sampling protocols from the literature. We validated the model in two empirical studies: (i) a Mining Software Repositories study and (ii) a replication case study conducted with external researchers to test both its effectiveness and clarity. The mining study showed that datasets collected and updated with the model produced consistent distributions across key code metrics. Furthermore, the case study confirmed these findings and provided feedback that led to refinements in the model’s roles, tasks, and work products. Our validation studies demonstrated the effectiveness and practical utility of SUM4SOFT, although further studies in other contexts are needed to generalize its applicability.

title: A Fine-Tuned Fuzzy-Optimized UNet++ for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Kumar JanardanPatra, Jibitesh Mishra, Sanjit Kumar Dash, Dipak Kumar Nidhi, Sudhir Kumar Mohapatra, Jukka Heikkonen, Rajeev Kanth
abstract: The human eye is a vital organ responsible for vision, and the health of the retina is crucial for preserving sight. Retinal vessel segmentation plays a key role in the early detection of ophthalmic diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and hypertension, where vascular abnormalities indicate disease progression. This study introduces a novel hybrid framework that enhances vessel segmentation performance using Fuzzy-Optimized UNet++ architecture, demonstrating a 2% improvement over the baseline UNet++ (95.3% accuracy). The proposed method is trained and validated on five benchmark datasets DRIVE, HRF, IOSTAR, ARIA, and CHASE_DB1offering diversity in image resolution, pathology, and vessel morphology to evaluate cross-dataset generalization potential. To address limitations such as class imbalance, noise sensitivity, and poor micro-vessel continuity, we incorporate fuzzy logic for enhanced boundary refinement and Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) for robust parameter tuning and convergence acceleration. Additionally, a synthetic vessel generation module, VesselGAN, is used to expand dataset diversity, achieving an SSIM score of 0.89 while preserving anatomical accuracy. Comprehensive evaluation is performed using 10-fold cross-validation and external testing on five independent datasets RETA, IDRiD, IOSTAR (external), Kaggle, and clinical-grade images. The integrated approach achieves superior performance across all key metrics, including Dice Coefficient, IoU, SSIM, and F1-score, especially under noisy, low-contrast, and ultra-thin vessel conditions. This research presents a unified, end-to-end system that advances the state-of-the-art in retinal vessel segmentation. Its superior accuracy, resilience to data variability indicates robustness across unseen clinical domains and suggest suitability for real-world deployment in ophthalmic diagnostic systems.

title: Multi-Connectivity for Industrial Automation: Achieving Deterministic Reliability through Private 5G, Public 5G/LTE, and Wi-Fi Integration
authors: William O’Brien, Adam Dooley, Mihai Penica, Sean McGrath, Eoin O’Connell
abstract: The transition toward fully wireless industrial automation under Industry 4.0 requires communication systems capable of deterministic and ultra-reliable performance. This study experimentally evaluates the potential of multi-connectivity, specifically the simultaneous use of private 5G, public 5G/LTE, and Wi-Fi in order to meet the 99.9% reliability target defined in 3GPP TS 22.104 for mission-critical applications. Using a mobile robotic platform within the Confirm Smart Manufacturing Centre, four experimental scenarios were conducted under stationary and mobile conditions with 64 byte and 25000 byte payloads. Results show that no individual interface achieved the 99.9% reliability threshold independently, however composite multi-path configurations employing packet duplication across interfaces consistently met or exceeded this benchmark, achieving up to 100% reliability. Dual private 5G + Wi-Fi and triple-interface configurations eliminated single-path deficiencies, while private 5G demonstrated superior stability under mobility due to its single cell architecture. Although packet duplication increases overall network load, it ensures mission-critical communication with a minimum reliability of 99.98%, fulfilling deterministic performance requirements for industrial control and robotic systems. These findings confirm that multi-connectivity offers a practical and robust approach to achieving ultra-reliable wireless communication in industrial environments.

title: Development of Novel Neural Network Architecture for Automated Pharmaceutical and Vitamin Identification Using Image Classification
authors: Ilyas Rashitov, Ualikhan Sadyk, Baglan Tolebay
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Automated identification of pharmaceutical drugs and vitamins is critical for improving medication safety and reducing human errors in healthcare systems. This study proposes a novel multi-scale, attention-enhanced convolutional neural network designed for fine-grained classification of pharmaceutical images. The model integrates multi-scale feature extraction to capture both local visual details (e.g., imprints, edges) and global characteristics (e.g., shape, color distribution), while an attention mechanism emphasizes discriminative regions, enabling accurate differentiation of visually similar classes. Transfer learning with ImageNet-pretrained weights accelerates convergence and improves generalization. The model was evaluated on the Pharmaceutical Drugs and Vitamins Synthetic Images dataset, comprising 100,000 images across ten classes. Experimental results demonstrate high classification performance, achieving a macro-averaged F1-score of 0.897, with per-class F1-scores ranging from 0.766 to 0.934. ROC analysis and confusion matrices confirm robust discriminative capability, although challenges remain for classes with minimal visual differences. Compared to conventional CNN architectures, the proposed approach shows improved precision, recall, and F1-scores. The study contributes a scalable, accurate framework for automated pharmaceutical identification, offering a foundation for integrating synthetic data augmentation, multimodal features, and few-shot learning in future research.

title: Research on Perspective Transformation Correction Combined with YOLOv8’s Intelligent Arm Vibration Detection Technology
authors: Haoyu Lan, Yunting Liang, Jiamin Lei, Turui Ning, Ziyang Zeng, Huanchao Liu, Tianyu Liang, Yingxiang Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Arm swing parameters serve as critical indicators for assessing mechanical equipment operation. Traditional detection methods relying on manual measurements or contact sensors suffer from low efficiency and significant measurement errors. This paper presents a computer vision-based non-contact detection strategy for robotic arms, which employs perspective transformation to eliminate image distortion and integrates the YOLOv8 deep learning model for intelligent positioning of vibration angular points. This approach enhances detection accuracy and automation levels, providing a reliable solution for industrial equipment condition monitoring.

title: Bridging Hardware and Semantics: An LLM-Enhanced Ontology Framework for Explainable Anomaly Detection in IoMT Systems
authors: Thrity Golzari, Zhangying He, Hossein Sayadi
abstract: This paper presents an LLM-enhanced ontology framework for cybersecurity in Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) environments, integrating hardware performance counter (HPC) features, device metadata, and known vulnerabilities to enable reasoning-driven, explainable anomaly detection. The study introduces a hybrid human-LLM ontology development workflow that accelerates ontology construction and refinement while preserving conceptual rigor and semantic consistency. It further investigates how large language models (LLMs) can augment ontology engineering through a comparative evaluation of three state-of-the-art models: ChatGPT-o3, ChatGPT-4o, and Gemini 2.5 Pro, assessed across structural and semantic dimensions. Using the proposed LLM-enhanced ontology, we achieve 84.29% anomaly (e.g., malware) classification accuracy from HPC data while mapping detected anomalies to relevant vulnerabilities and device properties. Experimental results demonstrate that LLMs significantly improve ontology quality and reasoning coherence, with Gemini 2.5 Pro achieving the best balance between expansion and consistency.

title: Cross-scale Color Rendering Mechanism and High Fidelity Reproduction Model of Color 3D Printing
authors: Wei Yu, Chunwei Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This article focuses on the cross-scale color rendering and high-fidelity reproduction problems of color 3D printing. In this article, by deeply analyzing the basic theory of cross-scale color rendering in color 3D printing, the influence of material characteristics, nozzle accuracy and model structure on color rendering is clarified, a high-fidelity reproduction model based on this mechanism is constructed, and color correction, optimization and cross-scale color consistency algorithms are designed. Photosensitive resin, nylon, PLA (polylactic acid) and other materials were selected to print model samples with different complexity. The results show that the color correction algorithm can effectively reduce the color deviation of the red channel of the standard color card, and the corrected data points are more concentrated on the ideal value. After several generations of evolution, the fitness value of the optimization algorithm increased significantly, and better color parameters were found. Cross-scale color consistency algorithm ensures the smooth change of cross-scale color parameters of complex model samples. The comprehensive assessment shows that the high-fidelity reproduction model and algorithm significantly improve the similarity between the actual color and the design color. The research proves that the model and algorithm can effectively solve the problems related to color 3D printing, and provide strong support for improving printing quality.

title: Dual-Branch RGB-TIR Network for PV Module Fault Detection: Training on UAV Imagery from Multiple Sites
authors: Tolulope Jayeola, Ilyas Muhammad, Michal Kačmařík
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Dual-branch RGB-thermal infrared (TIR) deep networks are increasingly being utilized for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-based photovoltaic (PV) module fault detection, but most published evidence remains single-site and does not rigorously demonstrate cross-installation robustness under varying panel types, layouts, and environmental conditions. To address this, this paper presents a two-phase research pathway toward a robust multi-site PV fault detection using a dual-branch RGB-TIR framework analyzed across heterogeneous datasets. The phase 1 of this study comprises of assessments conducted on RGB and TIR datasets from public repositories to characterize model behavior, training dynamics, and dataset-driven limitations before deployment on proprietary, higher quality self-acquired site imagery. This study identifies practical data-centric requirements for stable training which involves allocating approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 6 0 \%}$</tex> background/null imagery to reduce false positives and mitigate overfitting towards defect-only images. This enforces distinct and sufficiently represented defect classes while excluding rare or visually confusable categories, and finally, adopting oriented bounding boxes (YOLOv8-OBB) for RGB imagery to accommodate the rotated geometry of PV modules and defect extents in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery. Phase 2 of this study will transfer these insights into the assessment of our self-acquired imagery, with the goal of improving cross-site generalization and operational readiness for scalable PV operations and maintenance.

title: Research on Safety Management and Control of Power Operation Sites Based on YOLOv Algorithm
authors: Zhen Niu, Chengzhang Wang, Li Min Li, Zhonghua Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In power operation sites, only directly acquired on-site images can be used, resulting in low accuracy of safety management and control. Therefore, a safety management and control method for power operation sites based on the YOLOv algorithm is proposed. To address the problem of frequent interference with on-site images, a preprocessing method based on multi-scale fusion and adaptive enhancement is proposed to improve image quality. The YOLOv algorithm is introduced to accurately extract dangerous features from aspects such as cluster center selection and feature dimension processing. A safety management and control system is constructed, using the soft-max function to evaluate the probability of dangerous features, defining information entropy to quantify safety risks, formulating management and control strategies by optimizing model parameters at the cloud and edge, and using vector cross product to determine illegal intrusion behavior, thus achieving safety management and control of power operation sites. Experimental results show that the initialization of the 3-layer convolutional layer has a relatively high detection frame rate at various iteration counts, reaching 47 frames per second after 600 iterations, which can promptly warn of potential hazards. In safety management and control tests, the design method has significant advantages in indoor and outdoor scenarios, accurately capturing dangerous behaviors. Regarding the false alarm rate of violations, with 500 samples, the false alarm rate of the method in references [1]–[4] was 31 %-36 %, while that of the design method was only 19%, with an increase of 8-9 percentage points less than other methods. It can more comprehensively and accurately identify violations and has excellent performance and strong practicality in the safety management of power operation sites.

title: A Precise Prediction Method for Competitive Athletes’ Specialized Performance Based on MultiDimensional Training Data Fusion
authors: Yang Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of sports science and intelligent training technologies, precise prediction of athletes’ specialized sports performance has become essential for scientific training optimization, injury prevention, and competitive strategy formulation. However, existing performance prediction methods are often limited by single data sources, inefficient data fusion, and insufficient prediction accuracy. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a precise prediction method for athletes’ specialized sports performance based on multi-dimensional training data fusion. First, multi-dimensional training dataincluding physiological indicators, kinematic parameters, training load, and historical performance records-are collected and preprocessed to reduce noise and redundancy. Then, an improved attention-based multi-modal fusion module is designed to effectively integrate heterogeneous data while emphasizing key performance-related features. Finally, a hybrid prediction framework combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) is constructed to capture both temporal dependencies and nonlinear feature relationships. Experiments conducted on real-world training data from track and field sprinters demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional singlemodel approaches (LSTM, GBDT, and Support Vector Machine). Specifically, the mean absolute error (MAE) is reduced by 12.3% 27.8%, and the root mean square error (RMSE) is reduced by $\mathbf{1 0. 7 \% - 2 5. 9 \%}$, with improved prediction stability. The proposed method provides an effective technical solution for precise performance prediction and promotes the development of intelligent sports training systems.

title: Research on Dynamic Execution Optimization of Smart Contracts Based on GRPO Algorithm
authors: Ruixue Xu, Sijia Lian, Suhua Liu, Yuanjie Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Aiming at the core pain points such as low execution efficiency, high resource consumption, and insufficient dynamic adaptability caused by the "deploy-and-freeze" characteristic of traditional blockchain smart contracts, this paper proposes a dynamic execution optimization scheme for smart contracts based on the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) algorithm [1]. Specifically, the "group sampling + relative advantage" mechanism, which is the core of the GRPO algorithm, is implemented through two key modules: for the group sampling module, the algorithm first divides the smart contract execution state space into multiple sub-scenarios based on key feature dimensions such as transaction type, data volume, and network congestion degree, then randomly selects 3–5 candidate execution actions from each sub-scenario and forms a candidate action group by fusing actions from different sub-scenarios; for the relative advantage calculation module, instead of adopting the absolute advantage evaluation method of the traditional Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm [2], it introduces a relative advantage function that takes the average execution effect of the candidate action group as the reference benchmark, quantifies the advantage of each candidate action relative to other actions in the group through indicators such as Gas cost saving rate, execution delay reduction rate, and task completion rate, and weights the relative advantage values to determine the optimal execution action. With the GRPO reinforcement learning algorithm as the core driving force, the scheme constructs a three-layer collaborative architecture consisting of an off-chain Artificial Intelligence (AI) decision-making layer, an oracle data layer, and an on-chain contract execution layer. Through real-time state perception, group sampling action generation, and scenario-based reward function design, it realizes the dynamic adaptive adjustment of smart contract execution strategies. Experimental results show that in typical application scenarios such as Decentralized Finance (DeFi) lending and supply chain finance, compared with traditional static contracts and optimization schemes based on the mainstream PPO algorithm, this scheme can reduce the average Gas fee by 22%~25%, lower the non-performing loan rate from 3.2% to 1.1%, and control the execution response delay within 500ms, significantly improving the execution efficiency, resource utilization, and dynamic adaptation capability of smart contracts. This research provides a new technical path for solving the problem of dynamic execution of smart contracts and has important theoretical and practical significance for promoting the efficient and trusted operation of the Web3 ecosystem.

title: Integrating Neural Machine Translation and Large Language Models for Multilingual Semantic Search
authors: Pavol Batalik, Roman Kosik, Jakub Dubec, Jan Lang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper presents a multilingual semantic search system that integrates neural machine translation (NMT) with large language models (LLMs) to enable efficient, languageagnostic document retrieval. The proposed approach leverages Meta’s M2M-100 translation model alongside advanced embedding generators—BGE, E5, and Sentence-BERT—to unify multilingual inputs within a dense vector space. A custom synthetic dataset of multilingual queries and summaries, generated using Google’s Gemini API, was used to evaluate retrieval accuracy and resource efficiency across English, German, and Slovak. Experimental results show that while BGE and E5 achieve high recall (up to 0.98), Sentence-BERT consistently attains the highest precision across languages. Comparative analyses of GPU, CPU, and memory utilization reveal practical trade-offs between embedding quality and computational cost. The findings highlight that combining neural translation with LLM-based embeddings enables robust cross-lingual semantic retrieval, offering a scalable framework for multilingual digital libraries and information systems.

title: LLM-based Text-to-SQL: A Case Study of Geospatial Information Retrieval
authors: Temitayo Olamilekan Ogunsusi, Jose Angel Nguema Azeme Mangue, Weishan Bai, Xinyue Ye, Lijun Qian, Xishuang Dong
abstract: Text-to-SQL aims to convert natural language questions into executable SQL queries for retrieving information from databases. Despite significant advancements, a key limitation in current research is the lack of focus on geospatial databases. This paper addresses that gap by exploring LLM-based Text-to-SQL for geospatial information retrieval. We first construct a new benchmark using a PostGIS spatial database, specifically designed for Text-to-SQL tasks in the geospatial domain. Specifically, we use LLMs to generate additional user-SQL query pairs, enhancing the benchmark’s size and diversity. We also provide a statistical analysis of its characteristics, including query length, SQL complexity, and difficulty levels. Afterwards, we implement a Text-to-SQL pipeline leveraging state-of-theart (SOTA) large language models, including GPT-4, Llama, and Google’s Gemma. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed pipeline effectively retrieves geospatial information from the spatial database based on natural language queries, as evaluated using Execution Accuracy (EX) and Valid Efficiency Score (VES).

title: MetaGen Blended RAG: Unlocking Zero-Shot Precision for Specialized Domain Question-Answering
authors: Kunal Sawarkar, Shivam Raj Solanki, Abhilasha Mangal
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) struggles with domain-specific enterprise datasets, often isolated behind firewalls and rich in complex, specialized terminology unseen by LLMs during pre-training. Semantic variability across domains like medicine, networking, or law hampers RAG’s context precision, while fine-tuning solutions are costly, slow, and lack generalization as new data emerges. Achieving zero-shot precision with retrievers without fine tuning still remains a key challenge. We introduce ‘MetaGen Blended RAG’, a novel enterprise search approach that enhances semantic retrievers through a metadata generation pipeline and hybrid query indexes using dense and sparse vectors. By leveraging key concepts, topics, and acronyms, our method creates metadata-enriched semantic indexes and boosted hybrid queries, delivering robust, scalable performance without fine-tuning. On biomedical PubMedQA dataset, MetaGen Blended RAG achieves 82% retrieval accuracy and 77% RAG accuracy, surpassing all prior zero-shot RAG benchmarks and even rivaling fine-tuned models on that dataset, while also excelling on datasets like SQuAD and NQ. This approach redefines enterprise search using new approach to building semantic retrievers with unmatched generalization across specialized domains.

title: A Fake News Detection Based on Cross-Social Media Communication Characteristics
authors: Jiaxu Wu, Yuan Gao, Ke Ji
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address existing fake news detection limitations-overreliance on single-platform offline datasets, ignorance of cross-platform expression variations, and insufficient consideration of dissemination characteristics-this paper proposes DFAS (Cross-social media Dissemination Feature-based Fake News Detection Model), a dissemination feature-driven model. It integrates news content and dissemination information: web crawling collects multi-source data (reports, timelines, comments) from diverse platforms; eight benchmark neural networks generate content feature vectors, while statistical methods extract propagation features; the two are fused with postheuristic error correction. Experiments confirm DFAS significantly improves detection accuracy and efficiency.

title: Construction of a Task-Driven Model for BIM Course Cost Training Based on OBE Concept
authors: Zhenzhen Geng, Xiaojiang Du
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Traditional Building Information Modeling cost estimation training often suffers from fragmented task design, weak linkage between skills and knowledge, and unbalanced assessment mechanisms, resulting in inefficient learning and insufficient competency formation. To address these problems, this study develops a task-driven training model grounded in the Outcome-Based Education philosophy. The model integrates task decomposition, knowledge–skill mapping, and closed-loop feedback, constructing a dynamic and data-driven learning process that continuously aligns performance indicators with instructional goals. Using data from 56 students in a comprehensive BIM cost estimation course, experimental results show significant improvements: task completion rate increased by 10.0%, knowledge mastery by 12.4%, feedback response time reduced by 42.6%, and learning engagement enhanced by 18.1% compared with traditional teaching. These findings verify the model’s effectiveness in strengthening precision, collaboration, and adaptability. The study offers a replicable framework for intelligent, measurable, and outcome-oriented BIM education within engineering training.

title: Research on Automatic Target Localization Method of Train Hook Picking Robot Based on Binocular Vision
authors: Zhirong Zhao, Dalong Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Hook-picking robots often use monocular vision system for target localization, which needs to rely on pre-precisely calibrated parameters to convert two-dimensional image coordinates into three-dimensional spatial coordinates, resulting in low localization efficiency, so study the automatic target localization method of train hook picking robot based on binocular vision. The robot uses binocular vision system to determine the hook area synchronously. In the image of the hook area, the points with significant changes in gray scale are defined as feature points, and the feature points are extracted by Gaussian filtering, and the feature point matching is completed by the Euclidean distance measure of similarity to generate the correct matching point set. Based on the successfully matched feature point pairs, the three-dimensional coordinates of the hitch are restored using the principle of stereo triangulation, and the depth, horizontal and vertical coordinates of the feature points are calculated to realize the relative three-dimensional localization. The experimental results show that the method has a total relative error of only 2.76% in terms of localization accuracy, which is significantly better than that of the comparison methods of $6.67 \%, 8.56 \%$, and 7.37%, while the average time consumed in terms of localization speed is only 0.8 to 1.1 s, which demonstrates an excellent real-time performance in target localization of train-hook picking robots.

title: Research on Lifetime Prediction Method for Electrochemical Energy Storage Systems Based on Multi-model Fusion
authors: Weiwei Li, Huijuan Huo, Cheng Xin, Tianqiong Chen, Shuo Wang, Jing Duan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the advancement of energy structure transformation and dual carbon goals, electrochemical energy storage systems play a crucial role in renewable energy integration and grid operation. Accurate remaining useful life (RUL) prediction is vital for maintenance strategy formulation and risk reduction. However, existing single-model approaches suffer from limited generalization and robustness. This paper proposes a multi-model fusion-based lifetime prediction method for electrochemical energy storage systems. It constructs a heterogeneous model pool with BP neural network, SVM, and LSTM, and develops a two-layer ensemble strategy of “performance threshold + weighted fusion + linear stacking”, excluding ELM to avoid instability. Experimental results show that the Weighted Fusion Model achieves the lowest RMSE (128.8 cycles) and highest $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{0. 7 9 8 8} \boldsymbol{)}$, outperforming single models. This method enhances adaptability to complex degradation patterns and robustness under uncertainties, providing theoretical and technical support for precise RUL prediction. Future work will integrate advanced algorithms and physical models, and extend to multi-scenario applications.

title: Study of Early Weak Fault Injection of Broken Rotor Bar in Traction Motor
authors: Li Liu, Junfei Nie, Wu Shao, Jintian Yin, Xinbo Liu, Zhaoying Zeng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Analyze the early weak faults of the rotor broken bars in high-speed train traction motors, and the relationship between fault damage factor and phase resistance is obtained; the simulation platform of traction drive system is constructed, and the simulation injection of early weak fault of traction motor is realized by the method based on model through the fault injection module, using the relationship between fault damage factor and phase resistance change. Experimental results demonstrate the rationality and effectiveness of the injection method.

title: Finite Element Analysis of Microstrip Feeding Antenna and its Parametric Management
authors: Qianyue Nie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: For the microstrip feeding antenna, finite element analysis of the original model and associated structures are conducted, resulting in some numerical results such as electric field intensity, S-parameter, 3D and 2D radiation patterns. Then, a parametric management App is constructed based on the results of finite element analysis, by changing the structural parameters and (or) working parameters of the microstrip feeding antenna, the solid model, grid model, nephograms of electric field intensity, S-parameter and radiation patterns can be obtained in real time.

title: EM³R: An Explainable Modular Multi-LLM Reasoning Framework for Question Answering System
authors: Siwen Zhao, Sen Lin, Wei Wang, Yujun Li
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Entailment tree generation provides a structured paradigm for explainable question answering by explicitly modeling multi-step logical inference. However, most existing large language model (LLM)-based approaches rely on a single model and treat reasoning as an unconstrained generation process, resulting in limited controllability over intermediate inference steps. As a result, early reasoning errors readily propagate across steps, while large search spaces induced by distractor facts hinder reliable entailment tree construction. To address these challenges, we propose the Explainable Modular Multi-LLM Reasoning framework (EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R), which reframes entailment tree construction as an interactive, verifier-guided reasoning process. EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R follows a stepwise Select–Decompose–Propose–Verify workflow that explicitly separates evidence selection, goal planning, step generation, and entailment validation. Specifically, a Semantic Selector filters hypothesis-relevant facts, a Goal Decomposer derives intermediate subgoals, a Step Proposer generates executable entailment steps, and an Entailment Verifier validates each step before it is incorporated into the proof. Verified steps are then assembled into a structured and human-interpretable entailment tree. Experiments on the EntailmentBank benchmark show that EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R produces more coherent and structurally consistent entailment trees than strong baselines. Under the strict AllCorrect evaluation, EM<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>R achieves a 44.1% improvement in overall accuracy, indicating substantially improved global reasoning correctness. These results demonstrate that explicitly controlling search and validating intermediate reasoning steps is critical for reliable and interpretable multi-step question answering.
