The framework extracts utterance-level acoustic and textual embeddings via end-to-end fine-tuning, then dynamically weights modality contributions to capture cross-modal emotional correlations. Experiments on KEMDY-19 and AIHub datasets show the model achieves 73.3% test accuracy and $\mathbf{8 5. 5 2 \%}$ validation unweighted average recall, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and confirming effectiveness for both majority and minority emotion categories.

title: AI-based Custom Web Application Scanner and Bug Detection in Website using Docker
authors: Ezhumalai P, Sharmila V, Christopher A, Makashkumar V, Aswinkumar M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Web application security is now a very important issue because of the increasing number of user-driven websites and dynamic sites that are susceptible to cyber attacks. Conventional scanners are dependent on high-level testing of static payload and rule-based detection and they most of the time fail to detect emerging or complicated threats. The project introduces an AI-powered Custom Web Application Scanner as the combination of manual test payloads and a Large Language Model (LLM) and Docker-based studying of the sandbox to improve the vulnerability detection and prevention. This system uses Python Flask as the backend and Flutter as the frontend to use to interact and analyze target URLs in real time. Manual testing makes use of payload-based scripts to detect vulnerabilities which include SQL Injection, Cross-site Scripting, CSRF, and Command Line Injection, whereas the LLM can automatically analyze the responses that will be analyzed through the use of a parser in order to identify any hidden or advanced threats and recommend remediation measures. Also, an environment of Docker container is employed to test suspicious or potentially malicious files in a safe way, and it is isolated and executed without any risk. Experimental analysis indicates that the suggested hybrid model has a better detection rate, a lower number of false positive and actionable information to the developers, and it is a strong and smart approach to a secure web application evaluation.

title: Static Voltage Stability Analysis Based on Adaptive Model Prediction Algorithm
authors: Xiang Li, Dongxu Zhou, Zun Yang, Guoshan Fu, Yewei Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The scale of the power system is expanding, and new energies are constantly integrated. The existing static voltage stability assessment is difficult to meet practical needs. This article uses model predictive control (MPC) to construct a mathematical model of the power system, optimize control decisions in real time, and predict and prevent possible voltage instability situations. In terms of mathematical model construction, the state variables of the power system is defined, including node voltage amplitude and phase angle, and a discrete-time state space model is established to describe the dynamic behavior of the power system. Through a series of experiments, this article tests the performance of MPC algorithm in response time, accuracy, voltage stability margin, and active power loss. The experimental results indicate that the average response time of the MPC algorithm is 204.8ms, and the accuracy remains between 95.1% and 99.9%. Its active power loss remains in a low range of 20.4kw to 40kw. In contrast, the PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control method has lower voltage stability margin and higher active power loss. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of MPC algorithm in static voltage stability analysis of power systems.

title: A Bionic Manipulator Design based on Multi-Sensor Data Fusion
authors: Yingxing Lan, Yang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Based on multi-sensor data fusion analysis, this paper improves the design of bionic manipulator and improves the visual and touch perception ability. In this study, the bionic manipulator design based on multi-sensor data fusion is constructed by using the recognition model construction method and based on effective evolution and improvement, and the bionic design based on intelligent perception and analysis, sensory recognition evaluation, and feedback mechanism improvement is based on the data-driven multi-sensor data fusion method. The experimental results show that the improved design model shortens the operation time by 18.51%, increases the accuracy of perception signal recognition by 44.22%, and increases the sensitivity of the soft robot by 16.02%, which verifies the effectiveness of the bionic manipulator design method in the field of industrial automation. It can be seen that the bionic manipulator based on multi-sensor data fusion is involved, which can make the bionic manipulator design more visual-tactile and highly sensitive.

title: AEOF: Adaptive Evolving Online Firewall with Hybrid Drift-Aware Anomaly Detection for Cloud-Native Network Security
authors: Jaswanth Kumar N, N H Shrinandh, Bhavana V
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Traditional firewalls and batch-applied machine learning approaches are unable to identify state-of-the-art cloud network attacks because they cannot react to changes in the distribution of network traffic in real-time. This work proposes an adaptive streaming firewall that copes with concept drift in cloud network security through resorting to hybrid anomaly detection and validated deployment. A new system, AEOF, is proposed that uses hybrid anomaly detection, consisting of an online isolation forest algorithm and z-score detection using a weighted α value of 0.8, source behavioral tracking, and an ADWIN algorithm-based adaptive percentage threshold, and is implemented using Python and AWS Elastic Beanstalk, CloudFront, and S3. Evaluation with synthetic three-phase drift streams and actual datasets (UNSW-NB15, CICIDS) reported 100% precision, 82.2% F1 score, and 99.2% ROC AUC. It improved on baseline statistical approaches with 77.1% ROC AUC, also recording 87.6% F1 score for port scan, 82.4% for DDOS, and 76.3% F1 score for data exfiltration. Production deployment on AWS validated sub-second processing latency with horizontal scalability, advancing cloud security through validated adaptive online learning mechanisms for enterprise operations.

title: Spatio-Temporal Assessment of Vegetation Change for Sustainable Ecosystem Monitoring using Remote Sensing and NDVI-based Change Detection
authors: T.Poovizhi, S.Christy, T.Nalini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper is a detailed spatio-temporal analysis of vegetation dynamics through the NDVI-based change detection with high-resolution Sentinel-2 data and cloud-based geospatial processing. The study is a comparative study of vegetation conditions in two different time frames to assess the changes in ecological health, landscape productivity and long-term environmental resilience. The patterns of vegetation density were mapped with the help of NDVI classification to determine the areas where degradation or regeneration process was evident. Development of remote sensing and automated processing of Google Earth Engine allows scalable, climate-adaptive monitoring that can be used in the sustainable management of land-use and conservation planning. The results of this research are relevant to the research on vegetation transition that is critical to the protection of biodiversity, sustainability of the ecosystem, and man-responsive resources.

title: Intelligent Edge Computing Architectures for 5G-Enabled Real-Time Multimedia Streaming in Cloud Environments
authors: Moti Ranjan Tandi, Archana Mishra
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Real-time multimedia applications have seen a booming market in ultra-high-definition video streaming and immersive, interactive services, placing high demands on networks for latency, bandwidth efficiency, and quality of service. Communication systems, Fifth-generation (5G) systems, and cloud computing. When combined with centralized cloud computing, it can provide high data rates and scalable resource capacity. Still, centralized cloud design alone may not be able to support the real-time demands of multimedia workloads with latency constraints. Intelligent edge computing has emerged as an attractive paradigm for overcoming these limitations by moving computation, storage, and analytics to the edge. This paper presents a detailed description of intelligent edge computing architectures for real-time multimedia streaming in 5G clouds. It examines architectural designs that incorporate edge nodes, cloud enclaves, and a 5G core network, and their roles in adaptive resource management, traffic forecasting, caching, and quality-of-experience optimization, leveraging the power of artificial intelligence and machine learning. Mobility management, dynamic workload orchestration, energy efficiency, and security are the main challenges discussed systematically. Additionally, the paper evaluates how edge intelligence improves scalability and resilience, while minimizing end-to-end latency and network congestion. By synthesizing recent developments and architectural approaches, this paper provides an overview of how effective, smart edge-cloud architectures can be designed to deliver next-generation real-time multimedia services over 5G networks.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of PI-PD Controller for Robust Load Frequency Control of Power System
authors: Aman Kumar Yadav, Maneesh Kumar Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Load frequency control (LFC) is a critical issue that significantly affects the quality of power generation. Therefore, the design of a LFC is essential for efficient power transfer, mitigating unknown external disturbances, and addressing variations in the power system's parameters. This paper presents LFC using a PI-PD controller for a single-area system, which demonstrates resilience to parameter changes and effective disturbance suppression capabilities. We have used the differentiation method to analyze the stability of the boundary locus in the closed-loop control system, aiming to derive the parameters for the PI-PD controller. This method was systematically applied for both the inner and outer loops of the system structure. The resilience and performance of the proposed PI-PD control system were evaluated using some integral error criteria. These evaluations were conducted with nominal values, variations in system parameters of ±50%, disturbances and noise. The simulation results indicate that the PI-PD controller effectively mitigates the impacts of load disturbances and variations in system parameters.

title: Design and Verification of AHB to I2C Protocol
authors: Krithika A, Ravikumar M, Nagesh K N, Veerappa Chikkagoudar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Transfer of data between low-speed external devices and high-speed system buses is frequently necessary for modern System-on-Chip (SoC) platforms. The Advanced High-performance Bus (AHB) provides parallel, high-throughput data transfers suitable for processors and memory units; However, the Inter Integrated Circuit (I2C) network protocol provides a straightforward two-wire serial interface that is frequently utilized for control devices, sensors, and memories. Direct data exchange between these protocols is not possible due to their architectural differences. The implementation and validation of an AHB-to I2C networking connection using Verilog HDL are presented in this paper. The suggested design functions as an AHB slave to record parallel transactions and transform them into serial data that is compatible with I2C for peripheral communication. The simulation and implementation in the Synopsys environment were used to validate the design, which was written in Verilog HDL. The findings accurately verify that the AHB to I2C protocol interface is a good fit for SoC applications requiring communication between low-speed peripheral modules and high-speed cores

title: Lightweight Deep Neural Networks Outperform Complex Architectures for Chronic Kidney Disease Prediction
authors: Narendhar Mulugu, Gorityala Yashashwini, Dwaram Akshitha, Gopuvaram Reddy Likhitha, Gugulothu Niharika, Endurthi Vaishnavi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Predicting Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) at early stage is really important for early treatment so that risk of later possible outcomes may be avoided. In this study we develop a deep learning–based framework that through domain-driven feature engineering, feature importance analysis and ensemble of multiple neural architectures, drives robust CKD classification. With an artificial dataset of 400 patient records, we created clinically relevant interaction and polynomial features and ranked the 15 most informative predictors through Random Forest ranking; the most dominant features were hemoglobin-related measures and serum creatinine. Basline_DNN, Advanced_DNN, Residual_DNN and Ensemble_DNN were the four deep-learning models trained and evaluated using stratified splits and all of them were optimised with early stopping and learning-rate scheduling. Of them, Baseline_DNN performed the best, with a balanced accuracy of 0.9733, precision and recall of 0.98, F1-score of 0.98 and an AUC–ROC of 0.998. Only two cases were misclassified (1 false positive, and 1 false negative) demonstrating excellent sensitivity (0.98) and sensitivity (0.9667) for the model. Cross-validation validated robustness with a mean accuracy of 0.98 ± 0.0127. The results of this work show that simple neural architectures with targeted feature engineering may provide a competitive advantage over more complicated models whilst remaining fully clinically interpretable conclusion. The approach provides a solid basis for the final design of pragmatic screening tests for CKD; future studies will concentrate on external validation and probability calibration for implementation in everyday clinical practice.

title: Next-Gen Intelligent Waste Sorting and Monitoring Framework using EfficientNet
authors: A. Syed Musthafa, S. Meivel, M. Varshini, R. Vedhasya, D. Subhika, A. L. Vedhika
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The amount of waste in cities is getting bigger every day. This is a problem for the environment and for keeping our cities clean. When we do not throw away waste properly it makes our surroundings dirty and not safe. To deal with this problem this project is going to use a waste segregation system. This system will automatically find out what kind of waste it is and sort it. The smart waste segregation system uses a camera to take pictures of the waste. Then a program that uses intelligence looks at the pictures to figure out if the waste is biodegradable or non-biodegradable. The smart waste segregation system is really good, at sorting waste. The result shows where the waste goes. It gets sent to the place by a machine. This means people do not have to touch the waste. The machine helps keep everything clean. The waste goes to the compartment through this machine so people are not in contact, with the waste and this helps to keep the sanitation good. The waste is directed to the compartment and this is done automatically by the machine. The smart bin has sensors that can find out if there is a fire or bad gas, inside it. These sensors check for problems that can happen when waste is breaking down. The smart bin also keeps an eye on how full it's and tells the people in charge when it is full. They get a message so they can empty the bin. If nobody empties the smart bin people can complain about it on the internet. This helps to get the problem fixed faster. The smart bin also has a GPS so we can see where it is all the time. It is connected to the cloud so we can check on it always. The smart bin is a way to keep track of waste and make sure everything is working properly. Overall, this smart waste management solution helps keep cities cleaner, safer, and more environmentally friendly.

title: AI Model for Crop and Fertilizer Recommendation using Advanced Reasoning Techniques
authors: Mariappan P, Harshan A, Biravin Kumar S, Hariharasudhan D
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Precision agriculture requires a smart system that provides localized crop and fertilizer recommendations using integrated multi modal data sources. This paper describes an AI model that provides crop and fertilizer recommendations incorporating sophisticated reasoning. The architecture is based on three main components, the Artificial Neural Network which provides a high-level approach to explain the crop recommendations using a Rule-Based Chain of Thought with transparent outputs and A Random Forest classifier that is composed of multiple path approaches based on a Tree of Thought approach to determine fertilizer recommendations and a Convolutional Neural Network for crop health diagnosis with images. The model was validated with real-life agricultural datasets with soil nutrient concentrations, climate features, and crop images. The experimental results exhibited solid results across all modules: a crop recommendation module demonstrated a 93.0 percent accuracy in classifying six crop types and had all balanced precision and recall a fertilizer recommendation module demonstrated a 91.0 percent accuracy in determining the best fertilizers from seven types and the crop health diagnosis pipeline demonstrated validation accuracy of 92 percent and was capable to determine diseases and nutrient deficiencies by analysing leaf images.

title: A Multi-Objective Optimization Allocation Model for Inter-Regional Transmission Capacity Taking into Account Green Electricity Consumption Responsibility under the "Dual Carbon" Goals
authors: Gang Shentu, Fei Du, Min Wu, Xiaogang Li, Yue Gu, Qi Xie, Gaowei Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: To address the coordinated optimization of green power consumption responsibility allocation and transmission capacity in interregional power grids under the "dual carbon" goals, this study proposes a multi-objective optimization allocation model for interregional transmission capacity that takes green power consumption responsibility into account. By constructing a three-dimensional objective system encompassing minimization of total system operating costs, total carbon emissions, and absorption responsibility weight deviation, a processing strategy combining a linear weighting method with an ε constraint method is employed, and the Benders decomposition algorithm is employed for efficient solution. After the interregional transmission capacity is increased to 1600MW, the model achieves a total system operating cost lower than that of the traditional economic dispatch model; the wind curtailment rate is reduced from 12.8% to 2.0%, carbon emissions are reduced by a maximum of 279,000 tons, and the absorption responsibility weight deviation is reduced from 4.2% to 0.4%. This study provides a decision-making tool for interregional transmission capacity allocation that balances economic efficiency, low carbon emissions, and policy compliance.

title: An AI-Enhanced Machine Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection in U.S. Election Systems
authors: Khadija Hasta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Election infrastructure cybersecurity has become a critical priority for democratic nations, particularly in the United States, where voter registration databases, electronic poll books, and voting machines face increasingly cyber threats. Conventional intrusion detection systems often fall short in identifying zero-day attacks and adapting to rapidly evolving threat patterns. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an AI-enhanced intrusion detection system for U.S. election infrastructure, leveraging machine learning techniques such as XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and Neural Networks (NN). Experimental evaluation on simulated election network traffic demonstrates a detection accuracy of 97% with a low false positive rate of 3%. The proposed framework further integrates real-time anomaly detection, feature importance analysis, and automated risk assessment, offering a robust and adaptive solution for safeguarding the integrity of election systems. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance compared to traditional signature-based approaches, with detection latency under 1 second. The system successfully identifies DDoS attacks, privilege escalation attempts, and data exfiltration patterns specific to election systems. This research addresses critical gaps in election cybersecurity by providing an adaptive, intelligent defense mechanism capable of protecting the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of democratic processes.

title: LLM-Powered Real-Time Sign Language Recognition and Translation using OpenCV and MediaPipe
authors: Mouna G S, Harsha Vardhana G M, Anil Kumar Reddy M, Manoj Kumar B, Shyam Sundar V
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper introduces HandTalk, a real-time system for recognizing sign language and translating multiple languages, aimed at bridging communication gaps between hearing individuals and those who are deaf or hard of hearing. The system utilizes a multi-user dataset that captures differences in hand shapes, skin tones, and signing styles to enhance its ability to recognize signs from various users. By employing OpenCV and MediaPipe for hand landmark extraction, a compact CNN performs gesture classification, achieving operational speed even on less powerful devices at a resolution of 640×480 and at lower frame rates with only slight accuracy degradation. Multilingual translation is realized through a hybrid method, integrating rule-based mappings for frequently used phrases alongside external translation APIs to enhance language coverage. Results from experiments show impressive performance, with an accuracy of 97.6%, precision of 96.8%, recall of 96.5%, and an F1-score of 96.6%. Although the present system emphasizes spatial modeling, future work is suggested to include temporal models like LSTM or Transformer architectures to better handle continuous dynamic gestures. The manuscript clearly delineates the problem, outlines a structured workflow, and presents validated results, thus reinforcing its scientific relevance.

title: AI-Driven Integration of Ayurveda and Yoga for Mental Health Support in Students and Working Professionals: A Comprehensive Framework
authors: Vikas K Kolekar, Yashwant V Dongre, Namrata G Kharate, Pranali G Chavhan, Disha S Wankhede
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The lives of students and working professionals have been derailed by stress, anxiety, and depression to the extent that mental health has become the issues of concern. There is a proven fact that traditional holistic practices like Yoga and Ayurveda have a good efficiency in the maintenance of mental health. The principal reasons why these methods have not been more widely used has various reasons. Like these methods are not real-time responsive and trackable and because the methods are not as generally applicable as they should be their privilege is not as wide-ranging. Now it is possible to overcome above reasons with assistance of artificial intelligence (AI). An AI by examining the physiological, psychological, and lifestyle data, can give personalized and dynamic interventions, which, in their turn, bring the recommendations of personalized yoga poses, Ayurvedic treatment, and lifestyle changes. This research paper presented an approach of AI, Ayurveda, and yoga converging towards creating a unified system that can be applied to bring specific mental health improvement to students and working professionals. This research paper reviews the literature on the related topic, presents the recent developments of the implementation of AI in holistic mental health, and discusses the ethical issues such as data protection and the cultural sensitivity. The paper ends with recommendations on how the future research on scalable, interpretable AI systems which complement traditional knowledge can be carried out.

title: Mapping Consumer Discourse on Circular Fashion in Emerging Economies: An Exploratory Machine Learning Analysis of Social Media in India
authors: S.Sivapriya, P.Suganthi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The environmental impact of the fashion business has prompted widespread interest in circular fashion. This study examines Indian consumers’ perspectives of regenerative and circular fashion through shifting attitudes and quantifiable behaviours. This study leverages machine learning methods and social media analytics for 4,413 YouTube comments concerning sustainable apparel. Sentiment analysis and topic modelling, using Similarity Index and BERTopic, show that most sustainability themes have been received positively, yet skepticism and neutrality. Topic heat map and clustering highlight key thematic stages of consumer debates like environmental awareness, product quality and innovation discourses. Instead of making adoption predictions, this study maps the sentiment and thematic structures of online conversations. Data-driven insights into current consumer attitudes and current narrative trends for small retailers. Limitations include support on a single social media source and potential demographic bias. The framework provides a basic foundation for future research using an expanded dataset and platforms and informs retail strategies toward sustainability goals in emerging market contexts.

title: Self-Powered E-Waste Collection Bin Using Energy-Scavenging Technologies
authors: Komal Chopra, Sapna Sharma, Bhoop Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: With the rising levels of electronic waste, we require a concrete, environmentally friendly strategy to manage it. This paper outlines a conceptual design of a ewaste collection locker that can power itself using renewable energy sources, ensuring that it keeps running, independent of any power source, thanks to energy harvesting technologies such as solar, piezoelectric, and thermoelectric materials. Such collection points would be IoT-enabled, allowing them to monitor the quantity of e-waste available, which would then aid real-time or more efficient scheduling of collection van visits. Also, with reduced fuel consumption and CO2 emission, it hopes to contribute less to environmental pollution, keeping these locker points running through renewable energy sources. Preliminary implementations have proven significant improvements, with approximately a 30% reduction in collection vehicle visits, a 29% reduction in fuel consumption per visit, and an absence of any additional energy requirement to power the collection

title: Integrating Long Term Price Predictions with Portfolio Optimization using LSTM
authors: Jyotirmoy Bhatt, Chinmay Singhal, Nandani Giri, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Predicting stock prices is a demanding trend owing to nonstationary behavior, volatility clustering, and speed of information assimilation in effective financial markets. Most deep learning based methods only consider the price dynamics and do not explicitly consider time varying market risk, which makes performance unstable at volatile times. This article suggests a hybrid model, which is a statistical volatility model and deep temporal learning. Volatility is determined by the use of a generalized autoregressive conditional heteroskedasticity model and is incorporated as an extra input feature with price based technical indicators. The predictive part uses pyramidal long short term memory network with attention mechanism to learn the hierarchical temporal dependencies. Evidence of the model is based on day to day stock data of fortune five hundred corporations between two thousand fifteen and two thousand twenty five. The rolling walk forward validation strategy is used to guarantee realistic and leakage free evaluation. Directional accuracy is a metric of decision that is relevant in financial forecasting as it is used to measure model performance. As an experimental result, the proposed hybrid approach is always at a better performance relative to the conventional long short term memory, volatility only and standalone deep learning baselines in terms of directional prediction, which were proved more robust in the efficient market setting.

title: Solar Irradiance Forecasting Using LSTM and Conventional ML Models: A Performance-Based Comparison
authors: Suraj Singh, Aman Ganesh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: A strong increase in energy demand is a driver that pushes solar energy into the electric grid. Accurate prediction of information is crucial to the maximization of energy PV systems performance. Proper estimate of changes in solar irradiance is vital for service quality improvement. It is possible to undertake efficient planning and allocation of energy through incorporation of solar power with the reliable forecast. In this paper, a comprehensive study of the methods for predictions of solar irradiance currently adopted is done. Such methods make it possible to select the most appropriate forecasting method custom made for particular needs or requirements. The paper performs comparisons and assessments of four predictive models namely: Random Forest, Linear Regression, Support Vector Regression, and Long Short-term Memory in terms of its prescription of hourly solar irradiance prediction, seasonally and time-limited, at the city of Halifax, Nova Scotia. This research compares the accuracy of different forecasting models according to the main error metrics, i.e. MAE, RMSE, nMAE and nRMSE. Findings show that the LSTM was immensely more effective than any other model with the minimal RMSE (14.01) and MAE (11.59) and the best consistency in winter, spring, summer, and autumn. LSTM algorithms were better than traditional and hybrid models for solar irradiance prediction across the entire study period. The results demonstrate the successes of LSTM at recognizing temporal dynamics and maintaining reliable forecasts in varying situations.

title: Federated Machine Learning Approach for Multi-Protocol IoT Intrusion Detection for Healthcare
authors: Ashwin S, Shreenithi M, Roshni Rangarajan, Ganesan M
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: This paper aims to present an Adaptive Federated Multi-Protocol Intrusion Detection System (AFM-IDS) to protect Internet of Things (IoT) environments which is used in many smart healthcare systems through a privacy-preserving and decentralised learning approach. Unlike conventional intrusion detection systems, instead of depending on centralised data and focusing on a single protocol, AFM-IDS uses Federated Learning (FL). This enables collaborative model development across heterogeneous IoT nodes or clients. A publicly available dataset, “ACI-IoT-2023”, was preprocessed and distributed among multiple virtual devices for federated training. Each device trained a local neural network on protocol-specific traffic and sent encrypted model parameters to a central server for aggregation. The global model was evaluated with accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score metrics. AFM-IDS provides low latency, scalability and robust detection. Hence, this is a practical and adaptable framework for healthcare systems, which nowadays are very crucial to be handled with proper and safe usage.

title: Optimal Sensor Placement for Fall Detection: A Dual-Sensor Framework with Multi-Mode Motion Analysis
authors: Poojasaai Gokulnath, G. A. Jayaashri, M. Mohamed Fargik, S. Poonguzhali
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Reliable fall detection remains a fundamentally unsolved challenge in wearable safety systems due to the complex biomechanics of human impact events and the difficulty of separating falls from high-variance normal walking patterns. This work introduces and experimentally validates a fall-detection algorithm that was developed, which leverages dynamic acceleration-magnitude profiling and adaptive thresholding for real-time impact characterization. To rigorously assess the algorithm's biomechanical sensitivity, we employed high-precision Xsens MTw Awinda IMUs as a reference instrumentation system. Quantitative evaluation using various features demonstrates a clear dominance of central torso placements, with hip and navel yielding the strongest fall signatures and earliest impact localization. The results provide high-fidelity experimental validation of the proposed algorithm and decisively establish torso-mounted sensing as the optimal configuration for robust, real-time fall-detection architectures.

title: Confidence-Aware and Explainable Deep Learning for Ovarian Cancer Subtype and Risk Mapping
authors: Harshini J, Fathimathu Liya PP, Hepsiba D
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Ovarian cancer is one of the most aggressive gynecological malignancies and this is mainly attributed to late diagnosis, recurrence and strong histopathological heterogeneity. Accurate detection of ovarian cancer subtypes important in determining the treatment plans and enhancing patient survival. Traditional diagnostic procedures rely on manual histopathological examination, which is subjective, time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variability, inspiring the process of adopting automated and effective computer-aided diagnostic systems. In this paper, the authors suggest a Confidence-Aware and Explainable Deep Learning model that can be used to classify ovarian cancer subtypes and map risks based on histopathological images automatically. A tuned EfficientNet-B0 model is used and compared with MobileNetV2, a lightweight baseline model. The framework combines the entropy-based confidence estimation and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) through Grad-CAM to increase the reliability and interpretability of predictions. The experimental findings indicate that the proposed model has a validation accuracy of 91.01% with test-time augmentation (TTA), outperforming the baseline while providing higher transparency and clinical applicability of computational pathology.

title: Investigation of EEGNet and its variants for ADHD Detection: Joint Spatial, Temporal, and Frequency Domain Learning
authors: Kuricheti Prerana, Niharika Sharma, Diya Dinesh, Pavani Satwika, Amrutha Veluppal
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Early and reliable detection of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) through EEG analysis holds much promise for developing objective and accessible neurodiagnostics. However, the conventional EEG- based ADHD detection approaches usually process the spatial, temporal, and frequency information separately, which leads to fragmented representations and limit the model's ability to learn representations from EEG data. In the study, we propose an end to end ADHD detection framework that uses EEGNet and modified EEGNet which are both compact convolutional networks used to classify different EEG-based brain computer interface applications. The models learns frequency, temporal and spatial domain representations together which enables the model to extract EEG patterns which reflect ADHD related neural activity. To avoid data leakage and evaluate the model's performance, the dataset was evaluated using subject-wise splitting through Group KFold cross validation. The model achieved an accuracy of 97 % when it was evaluated using sample-wise splitting. However, this setup introduces data leakage due to overlap of subject specific EEG characteristics across training and testing sets. These results highlight EEGNet as an efficient approach for ADHD detection from EEG signals.

title: Design and Development of a Smart Augmented Reality Vision Glass Usage in Healthcare Monitoring, Enhanced Learning and Navigation Purpose
authors: Maheswari E, Saisubramani H S, Sarvesvar S, Sabarish K S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The Smart AR Vision Glass is a low-cost and portable wearable Augmented Reality (AR) device based on NodeMCU (ESP8266) technology, which can be applied in health care, primary education, and navigation support. The proposed Smart AR Vision Glass design integrates biomedical sensors for the measurement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SpO}_{2}$</tex>, blood glucose level, body temperature, and blood pressure, with vital signs information presented using an OLED-based head-up display for continuous and unobtrusive hospital monitoring. In an educational environment, the proposed Smart AR Vision Glass design enhances learning by presenting interactive visual information to primary school children. For delivery and mobility service providers, the proposed Smart AR Vision Glass design offers real-time navigation support using wireless connectivity and smartphone integration. The proposed design emphasizes energy efficiency, portability, and seamless IoT communication. Experimental results confirm the accuracy of sensor data, robust wireless communication, and clear visual information display. The Smart AR Vision Glass is a scalable and affordable wearable AR platform for diverse real-world applications.

title: Development of a Bernoulli Suction-Type Non-Contact Manipulator Capable of Controlling Object Position and Orientation During Holding_supp1-3685899.mp4
authors: Takeshi Takaki
abstract: In this study, we propose a Bernoulli suction-type non-contact manipulator that can attract an object and simultaneously change its in-plane position and orientation while holding it. The proposed manipulator consists of a suction plate equipped with six compressed-air nozzles. By ejecting compressed air from the nozzles toward the object surface, a low-pressure region is generated, which allows the object to be adsorbed on the suction plate. Furthermore, by independently adjusting the airflow rate of each nozzle, thrust forces are applied to the object surface, enabling translational motion with two degrees of freedom and rotational motion with one degree of freedom on the plane while the object is held. A high-speed camera is used to capture and calculate the position and orientation of the object, and feedback control is implemented to achieve precise control of its motion and posture. This paper describes the structure and working principle of the Bernoulli suction manipulator and experimentally verifies the feasibility of simultaneously controlling translational and rotational motion while maintaining suction.

title: Partial UDF Inlining
authors: Samuel Arch, Yuchen Liu, Todd C. Mowry, Jignesh M. Pate, Andrew Pavlo
abstract: None

title: An AI-Driven Framework for Personalized Dietary Recommendation and Meal Planning
authors: Varun Harinath Rudravally, You E, Dinesh Jackson Samuel Ravindran
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Personalized dietary guidance has become an essential requirement in modern healthcare due to increasing lifestyle-related health issues. This paper presents NutriSmart, an intelligent web-based nutrition advisory system that delivers personalized dietary recommendations by integrating machine learning and generative artificial intelligence. The proposed solution employs a three-tier architecture consisting of a dynamic, conversational user interface and a backend processing layer powered by machine learning model and a large language model (LLM). Nutritional data are sourced from the USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods database, which provides comprehensive information on food composition and nutrient profiles. A nutrient KNN-cluster scoring mechanism is used to evaluate and assign food recommendations based on individual user requirements. The machine learning model generates initial dietary predictions, which are subsequently combined with contextual prompts and passed to Google Generative AI to produce real-time, personalized meal recommendations. Designed with a conversational interaction paradigm, NutriSmart enables users to query dietary needs naturally and receive tailored advice in an intuitive and user-friendly manner. By combining data-driven prediction with generative reasoning, the proposed system demonstrates potential as a scalable and intelligent nutrition guidance platform for personalized health and diet management.

title: A Multi-Port Thévenin-Consistency Integrity Guard for Impedance-Based Voltage-Stability Monitoring in PV-Rich Distribution Feeders
authors: Mohammed Alshalfouh, Nizar Zorba, Ahmed Massoud
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Impedance-based voltage-stability monitoring provides fast situational awareness in photovoltaic (PV)-rich distribution feeders, but it can be misled if synchronized voltage/current telemetry at PV points of interconnection (POIs) is falsified. This paper proposes a lightweight physics-based integrity guard that tests whether PV-port phasor measurements remain consistent with a feeder-wide multi-port Thévenin (port-reduced) representation. For each PV port, a local impedance estimate computed from measured phasors is contrasted with a network-consistent multi-port estimate derived from the reduced model and trusted reference-bus voltages. The resulting per-port residuals are aggregated into a compact consistency indicator and evaluated online using a threshold with short persistence. On an 8-bus PV-rich feeder case study, the guard detects a targeted phasor manipulation that biases impedance inference, with zero pre-attack alarms in the demonstrated run.

title: A Modular Framework for Multi-Speaker Speech Recognition in Noisy Environments
authors: Khai Chean How, Venkata Durga Kumar Burra, Sim Kuan Goh, Mohammed N. M. Ali
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: OpenAI Whisper models have several serious drawbacks when applied to multi-speaker scenarios in noisy conditions such as inconsistency of long text transcription, accent bias and low accuracy of low-resource languages. The conventional fused multimodal systems cannot be optimized and validated module-specification. This study proposes a non-fusion modular architecture that combines three independent modules: GPU-optimized Audio Targeted Extraction (based on Guided Source Separation, GSS), 3D CNN-based Lip Recognition, and BERT-LSTM Contextual Embedding (based on teacher-student architecture). These modules can evaluate each upgrade separately and are called either separately or with seven combinations. This modular system is intended to reduce Word Error Rate (WER) by 10%-20% in noisy-infested settings and support inference in real-time on edge devices (e.g. personal laptop).

title: Computer Vision-Driven Product Recommendation Interface for E-Commerce Personalization
authors: B R Celia, Parveen Banu S, D. Sethuraman, C. Sathiyamoorthy, R. Santhanakrishnan, V. O. Kavitha
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The revolutionary contribution that Artificial Intelligence has had to the personalization practice is examined through this approach as the technologies of customer experience enhancement become more and more topical thanks to the increasing significance of online purchasing through the development of the acclimatized relationships in which online purchasing turns out to develop. The main objective of this research is to investigate the analysis of the preferences of stoners towards gestures and buying history based on artificial intelligence-based algorithms that allow offering individual product suggestions, targeted notifications, as well as dynamic content offers. E-commerce companies will then be able to make much more personalized shopping gestures with the assistance of machine literacy and data analysis. Such movements are able to boost the degree of customer satisfaction and persuade customers to purchase goods. Like the data sequestration companies and algorithmic actions, the paper contains a discussion of the challenges and ethical issues that are relevant to the use of the artificial intelligence in personalization. The exploration educates the business on the way it can effectively integrate these technologies to capitalize on its personalization ideas and stay competitive in the digital business environment. This is made possible through case studies, which identify its benefits and proxy threats of artificial intelligence in the e-commerce.

title: Fault-Aware Repair Scheme for Inter-Die Interconnects of Chiplet-Based Chips
authors: Yi-Chun Huang, Jin-Fu Li, Hong-Siang Fu, Yung-Ping Lee
abstract: In the chiplet-based chip, multiple dies are communicated through a large amount of inter-die interconnects. The bonding interfaces, e.g., microbumps (ubumps), of the interdie interconnects are prone to defects during the assembly process. Effective yield-enhancement techniques for inter-die interconnects thus are needed to ensure the yield of chiplet-based ICs. In this paper, we propose a fault-aware repair technique for inter-die interconnects. A test and diagnosis method is proposed to distinguish single-net faults from multi-net faults and identify the interconnects of a multi-net fault. Then, a shift and broadcast reconfiguration method is used to repair two interconnects with a short defect using one spare interconnect. Simulation and analysis results show that the proposed fault-aware repair technique can effectively increase the repair rate.

title: Multi-Label Telecom Complaint Categorization Using BERT
authors: Sarmila Upreti, Aman Shakya, Basanta Joshi, Anku Jaiswal, Nischal Acharya
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Complaint handling in companies is traditionally done by agents, though some may have moved towards semi-automation, but the process has been mostly manual. Thus, complaint handling has been a laborious task that requires significant time and resources to categorize complaints. This manual process comes with discrepancies and inaccuracy due to the diverse nature of complaints. This paper introduces an applied natural language processing study on automated categorization of real world telecom complaints data. This paper evaluates the use of transformer-based model that can be fine-tuned on complaint datasets to enhance contextual understanding and improve classification precision. Using the complaints dataset from Nepal Telecom, this study aims to compare the effectiveness of Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) over traditional machine learning (ML) models such as Term Frequency–Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) with Support Vector Machine (SVM) and Naïve Bayes (NB).

title: A Schema-Driven Prompt Engineering Approach for Data-Centric LLM Tasks
authors: Emanuele Cavalleri, Marco Mesiti
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: General-purpose LLMs are increasingly employed in a variety of data-centric tasks, ranging from entity and relationship extraction from plain text to schema-aware data integration and analysis. Their large number of parameters and extensive training corpora enable strong generalization across application domains. However, LLMs lack access to up-to-date knowledge and are prone to hallucinations, limiting their reliability in data management scenarios. To address these issues, prompt engineering techniques have been proposed to specify the context in which a task should be performed. In this paper, we explore the use of conceptual schemas as a foundation for schema-driven prompt engineering, providing structured and reusable contexts for grounding LLM behavior in data-centric applications. We present SchemaLink, an intelligent web-based system for the graphical design and enhancement of conceptual schemas and their exploitation for LLM-based tasks. We demonstrate the applicability of our approach to knowledge extraction from plain text and to the discovery of joinable columns in data lakes, showing how schema-driven prompting improves grounding and consistency across heterogeneous data sources.

title: Multilevel Analysis for Predictors of Household Solid Biofuel Use in Nepal
authors: Yash Raj Lamsal, Prabal Sapkota, Madhav Prasad Pandey, Shailendra Kumar Jha, Durga Prasad Dhakal
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This study investigates the multilevel determinants of solid fuel in Nepal, a major public health concern linked to household air pollution. The data in this study was used from Nepal Demographic and Health Survey conducted in 2022 (n=13,780 households; 476 communities). This study uses, a multilevel logistic regression analysis to separate household and community-level determinants. The findings shows that solid fuel use is mainly driven by community framework, with 70% due to variation between clusters. The strongest predictor was community-level poverty, which increased the odds of solid fuel use by over 19-fold (OR 19.37). Key household-level factors included extreme poverty (aOR 0.03 for rich vs. poor), larger household size (aOR 3.34 for >5 members), and lack of higher education. The results underscore that transitioning to cleaner fuels requires interventions that address both individual socioeconomic barriers and the structural poverty of communities.

title: Responsible AI Framework for Ethical and Inclusive Adoption of Artificial Intelligence in Indian Education Systems
authors: Sivakumar Dhakshinamoorthy, Karthikeyan P, V Balaji
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: The increased use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) within the Indian education systems such as adaptive learning systems, automated tests, and learning analytics introduces a massive potential of enhancing the learning outcomes, but also increases some severe ethical, governance, and inclusion issues. The threat of unequal access, data privacy risks and lack of transparency poses a threat to equitable educational outcomes due to the problem of algorithmic bias. The proposed model on Responsible Artificial Intelligence (RAI) in this paper agrees with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 that embraces ethical and inclusive uses of AI in education. The framework integrates data reported by AI-enabled learning systems and the views of the involved parties and evaluates AI usage through six principles, namely, fairness, transparency, accountability, explainability, privacy and human oversight. Empirical validation was done with data of 420 respondents in schools, colleges, and universities in Karnataka. The results have shown that AI-enabled systems perform better in learning (81.7% vs. 68.4%), assessment accuracy (88.3% vs. 72.1%), and the intermediate RAI compliance, transparency, and explainability are the main points of drawbacks. The suggested framework provides realistic governance recommendations on equitable and learner-centered AI application.

title: A Novel Zero-Trust Identity Framework for Agentic AI: Decentralized Authentication and Fine-Grained Access Control
authors: Ken Huang, Vineeth Sai Narajala, John Yeoh, Jason Ross, Mahesh Lambe, Ramesh Raskar, Youssef Harkati, Jerry Huang, Idan Habler, Chris Hughes
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Traditional Identity and Access Management (IAM) systems, primarily designed for human users or static machine identities via protocols such as OAuth, OpenID Connect (OIDC), and SAML, prove fundamentally inadequate for the dynamic, interdependent, and often ephemeral nature of AI agents operating at scale within Multi Agent Systems (MAS) - a computational system composed of multiple interacting intelligent agents that work collectively.This paper posits the imperative for a novel Agentic AI - IAM framework: We deconstruct the limitations of existing protocols when applied to MAS, illustrating with concrete examples why their coarse-grained controls, single-entity focus, and lack of context-awareness falter. We then propose a comprehensive framework built upon rich, verifiable Agent Identities (IDs), leveraging Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs), that encapsulate an agent’s capabilities, provenance, behavioral scope, and security posture.Our framework includes an Agent Naming Service (ANS) for secure and capability-aware discovery, dynamic fine-grained access control mechanisms, and critically, a unified global session management and policy enforcement layer for real-time control and consistent revocation across heterogeneous agent communication protocols. We also explore how Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) enable privacy-preserving attribute disclosure and verifiable policy compliance.We outline the architecture, operational lifecycle, innovative contributions, and security considerations of this new IAM paradigm, aiming to establish the foundational trust, accountability, and security necessary for the burgeoning field of agentic AI and the complex ecosystems they will inhabit.

title: A Study on the Flexible Scheduling Strategy for the Computing Power Load of Data Centers Based on Multi-algorithm Integration
authors: Yang Bai, Fan Wang, Hongliang Chu, Kang Huang, Qian Peng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Under the dual drive of the East-to-West Computing Resource Allocation and Dual Carbon strategies, the contradiction between the rigid growth of computing power load in data centers and the pressure of new energy consumption in the power grid has become increasingly prominent. This paper proposes and practices a complete theoretical and technical system for the collaborative optimization of computing and power, aiming to transform data centers from traditional rigid loads into flexible resources that support the construction of a new power system. This paper mainly makes the following three innovations: First, a closed-loop system engineering framework of monitoring dispatching evaluation has been constructed, achieving fullprocess management from panoramic monitoring of electricity and carbon, intelligent integrated dispatching to real-time feedback of effects. Secondly, a Power Computing Power Intraday Fusion Scheduling Optimization Model integrating multiple algorithms was proposed. This model adopts dual time scales of a minute level and a 15-minute level, aiming to minimize the total cost. It innovatively combines greedy algorithms, simulated annealing, and task exchange strategies to find an efficient solution, guiding the computing power load curve to actively follow the photovoltaic output curve. Thirdly, by integrating multimodal carbon data-driven and AI mechanismdriven hybrid modeling, a carbon emission knowledge graph and causal reasoning model have been constructed, enhancing the interdependence and strategy adaptation capabilities of carbon management. An empirical study taking the State Grid Jiangxi Electric Power Data Center as the object shows that this system can achieve cost savings in operation and effectively improve the utilization rate of clean energy. This research provides a replicable and scalable systematic solution for solving the problem of spatiotemporal mismatch of computer resources, which is of great theoretical and practical significance for promoting the green and low-carbon coordinated development of energy and digital infrastructure.

title: Support Vector Machines: An Inclusive Survey in Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Ermir Balliu, Bilal Saoud, Ibraheem Shayea, Timur Grigoryev
publish: 2025 IEEE/ACIS 29th International Conference on Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Networking and Parallel/Distributed Computing (SNPD)
abstract: In the financial industry, using of several Machine Learning (ML) algorithms and artificial intelligence (AI) models has simplified the solution and detection of main problems related to its wide landscape. One of the most broadly used techniques of ML is Support Vector Machine (SVM) which owns its specific algorithms appropriated to detect frauds in financial applications. The reason why it is widely used in this field is that it can work well with High Dimensional Data (HDD) in applications where the number of features is greater than number of observations. Also, this technique is less susceptible to overfitting of HDD which makes it suitable for use in these cases.

title: Abelian Group Codes for Classical-Quantum Channels: One-shot and Asymptotic Rate Bounds
authors: James Pang, S. Sandeep Pradhan, Hessam Mahdavifar
abstract: We study the problem of transmission of information over classical-quantum (CQ) channels in the one-shot regime where the underlying codes are constrained to be shifted group codes. Given a group G, a group code of length n is a subgroup of Gn. In the achievability part, we introduce a new collection of input probability distributions that incorporates the encoding homomorphism and the underlying channel law. Using a random coding argument, we characterize the performance of group codes in terms of hypothesis testing relative-entropic quantities. In the converse part, we establish bounds by leveraging a hypothesis testing-based approach. Furthermore, we apply the one-shot result to the asymptotic stationary memoryless setting, and establish a single-letter lower bound on the group capacity of a CQ channel. Moreover, we derive a matching upper bound on the asymptotic group capacity.

title: TMVS: Threshold-based Majority Voting Scheme for Robust SRAM PUFs
authors: Sara Faour, Filip Maksimovic, David C. Burnett, Paul Muhlethaler, Thomas Watteyne, Kristofer Pister, Mališa Vučinić
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: SRAM Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) derive secret keys from start-up values for inherent security benefits but suffer from reliability issues due to bit flipping. We introduce the Threshold-based Majority Voting Scheme (TMVS), a lightweight method that eliminates noise and mitigates bias in SRAM PUFs while retaining the simplicity of majority voting decoders used by repetition codes, without the significant entropy loss that repetition codes incur under biased responses. TMVS runs entirely in software, requires no cell-level bit-error rate qualification or SRAM redesign, and avoids the complex decoders of heavy error correcting codes. We derive closed-form expressions for decoding-error probability and expected memory, validate them on experimental data, and present a security analysis that provides exact formulas for min-entropy and secrecy leakage due to helper data and bias, identifying conditions under which TMVS achieves zero secrecy leakage. On a large public dataset, TMVS shows near-zero cross-chip secrecy leakage and preserves average conditional min-entropy above 1 bit despite biased, spatially correlated SRAM statistics. Compared with prior work, TMVS offers the smallest decoding complexity at the cost of a larger PUF size. In a representative configuration, TMVS generates a 128-bit key with failure probability 9.15 · 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> and zero secrecy leakage at a bit-flip probability of 10%, requiring only ∼ 248k clock cycles on a 32-bit ARM Cortex-M0. These results show that TMVS is practical and implementation-friendly for resource-constrained, low-power devices.

title: A Novel Active End-Effector Type Dynamic Arm Support System With Twisted String Actuator
authors: Sohyun Moon, Yeji Hwang, Suncheol Kwon, Jonghyun Kim
abstract: Dynamic arm supports (DAS) help individuals with impaired upper limb function perform activities of daily living. However, most existing systems lack accurate gravity compensation based on user posture, and few studies have explored design methods to reduce required torque. In this study, we propose a novel end-effector-type active DAS (A-DAS) system using a spring joint mechanism driven by a deflected twisted string actuator. The design was optimized for maximum transmission ratio, compactness, and safety. We implemented an assistive force control strategy providing precise assistance based on the user's body weight and desired assistance level. To validate the system, we conducted a simulated eating task with 12 healthy participants. Results showed that the A-DAS provided superior control accuracy (0.48 versus 13.57 mm root-mean-squared error) compared to conventional passive systems. This led to significantly improved muscle activation patterns, showing both reduced activation range (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\kappa$</tex-math></inline-formula>) in the anterior deltoid (33.2% versus 60.9% reference voluntary contraction) and more stable activity (coefficient of variation) in the biceps (32.0% versus 49.0%). Future research will focus on expanding clinical applications to wider patient populations, including those with tremors.

title: QoS Aware Physical Layer Security Design for MISO-NOMA system with Untrusted Users
authors: Prerna Devi, Deepak Mishra, Ravikant Saini
abstract: Multiple Input Single Output (MISO) Non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), combining the benefits of spatial multiplexing with higher spectral efficiency, has recently attained a substantial research interest in 5G and upcoming mobile communication networks. This paper proposes an optimal power control algorithm for downlink secure MISO-NOMA communication involving untrusted users. Sum secure rate maximization of both single antenna users has been considered, while satisfying their quality of service requirements, numerical method is used for optimal power sharing between users, and optimal beamforming vectors have been derived. Simulations validate the analysis, demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed design, and offer design insights into secure MISO-NOMA communications.

title: Double-RISs Aided Receive Polarization-Combined Space Shift Keying and Spatial Modulation Systems with SPTnet-Detector
authors: Yuyan Liu, Chaorong Zhang, Benjamin K. Ng, Chan-Tong Lam
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) have recently attracted significant attention for improving wireless performance, yet single-RIS deployments remain limited in terms of coverage and design flexibility. To further enhance transmission reliability and link quality, we propose double RISs receive polarization and spatial shift keying (D-RPSSK) and receive polarization and spatial modulation (D-RPSM) systems over Nakagami-m fading channels, introducing a new paradigm for next-generation wireless communications. By jointly exploiting polarization and spatial diversity, the proposed architecture improves spectral utilization and enhances link robustness. Building upon the system models, the novel theoretical average bit error probability (ABEP) expressions are derived using maximum likelihood (ML) detector. Moreover, a spatial and polarization Transformer-based (SPTnet) detector is developed to capture long-range dependencies, enabling accurate symbol recovery under complex propagation environments. This integration of D RISs-assisted index modulation and deep learning (DL)–based detector effectively overcomes challenges related to spectral efficiency (SE) and bit error rate (BER) under time-varying channels. Simulation results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed D-RISs schemes, showing significant improvements in both reliability and efficiency over conventional designs and show that the proposed SPTNet achieves near-ML detector accuracy and surpasses LSTM-based detector under complex channel conditions.

title: Toward a Stealth-Capable 5G-AKA: Secure Key Distribution with Covert Channel Integration for Future-Sensitive Military Applications
authors: Yongho Ko, Seonghan Shin, Hoseok Kwon, Ilsun You
abstract: Secure and covert key establishment is increasingly important in tactical and intermittently connected military 5G environments, where authentication traffic may be observable and captured traffic may remain valuable after transmission. However, the standard 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) 5G Authentication and Key Agreement (5G-AKA) protocol provides neither covert key establishment nor forward secrecy beyond its conventional key hierarchy. To address this limitation, this paper proposes Stealth-5G-AKA, a compact enhancement that embeds a fragmented stealth Elliptic-Curve Diffie–Hellman (ECDH) exchange into standard 5G-AKA challenge–response signaling without modifying the visible message flow or the 5G core authentication architecture. The Home Network splits a 256-bit ephemeral ECDH public value into two 128-bit fragments and conceals them across successive random challenges. After receiving both fragments, the User Equipment reconstructs the hidden public value, derives a covert shared secret, and generates a stealth anchor key K′SEAF alongside the conventional KSEAF. Formal verification in ProVerif under the Dolev–Yao adversarial model shows that Stealth-5G-AKA preserves secrecy and mutual authentication, achieves indistinguishability of stealth executions, and provides forward secrecy for K′SEAF even if long-term subscriber credentials are compromised after protocol completion. Experimental results show that the stealth embedding introduces no additional messages or signaling fields and adds less than 10% computational overhead over the nearest forward-secrecy baseline while additionally achieving covert activation, indicating that Stealth-5G-AKA is a practical approach to secure and covert key establishment for next-generation military 5G deployments.

title: Research on Live Detection and Localization of Localized Insulation Defects in Distribution Cables Using FMCW
authors: Dewen Zhang, Ruihui Han, Xiaolong Xie, Jie Sheng, Zhongyu Wang, Chao Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the acceleration of urbanization, the large number of distribution cables has become a core component of modern distribution networks. Insulation defects such as localized aging, moisture, and damage to the outer sheath have become one of the main causes of power system failures, seriously affecting the safe operation of the grid. Frequency Modulated Continuous Wave (FMCW)-based method for locating localized cable defects offers higher sensitivity and detection range, showing broad application prospects in the cable field. However, this method is currently limited primarily to offline applications and cannot achieve online detection of cable defects. Building upon this method, this paper conducts research on the live detection and localization of partial insulation defects in power distribution cables. It discovers that the interval length of notch peaks can characterize the severity of defects and proposes the use of a single-cycle signal mixing processing method to avoid harmonic interference introduced by cycle superposition. This approach results in smoother spectrum curves and makes the notch positions more distinguishable.

title: AI-Enhanced Intelligent Interfaces for Secure Biometric Authentication in IoT Ecosystems: Recent Advances and Future Directions
authors: Sofia Marilina Glorioso, Giuseppe Pappalardo, Salvatore Sorce, Vincenzo Conti
abstract: The ubiquitous integration of devices from the Internet of Things (IoT) into daily life demands authentication mechanisms that are secure, continuous, and unobtrusive. Traditional static credentials are increasingly inadequate for dynamic IoT environments. This work explores the paradigm shift towards AI-enhanced intelligent interfaces that leverage biometric authentication as an intrinsic component of user interaction. We analyse the convergence of three critical dimensions: (1) AI-driven intelligent interaction modalities, ranging from contactless RF sensing to wearable neuromuscular interpretation; (2) Advanced biometric authentication paradigms, focusing on behavioural traits and multimodal fusion strategies; and (3) Security and privacy constraints specific to IoT ecosystems, including edge intelligence and cancellable biometrics. By synthesising a selection of recent literature, this paper highlights how Artificial Intelligence is transforming interfaces from passive input points into active, security-aware agents capable of robust user identification in resource-constrained environments.

title: Electric Vehicle Charging Scheduling Optimization Model Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Peijun Li, Wen Wang, Xiangliang Fang, Ye Yang, Xiaoqing Zhang, Shun Li, Yi Long, Xiaorui Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: As electric vehicle (EV) becomes more widespread, the power grid is encountering significant issues such as load fluctuations and rising charging costs for users. To address these challenges, this paper introduces an optimization method for EV charging scheduling based on the Q-learning algorithm, which aims to lower both user charging costs and grid load fluctuations. Initially, a charging scheduling model is created with user charging costs and grid load standard deviation as the optimization targets, and the charging process is framed as a Markov decision process. Next, the Q-learning algorithm is employed to enhance the charging behavior of EVs, allowing for dynamic adjustments to charging times, avoiding peak grid loads, and achieving peak shaving and valley filling. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method can decrease the average user charging cost by 8.56% and the standard deviation of grid load by 9.4%. It also effectively balances load and manages costs even as the number of EVs increases, with particularly notable results during winter and summer. Compared to traditional methods, this approach offers benefits such as a shorter training period, faster convergence, and significant optimization results, providing an effective solution for EV charging scheduling.

title: QML-360°: Permutation-Equivariant Quantum Graph Learning for 360° Image Classification with Distributed Training
authors: Himanshu Sahu, Hari Prabhat Gupta
abstract: Processing 360◦ images for machine vision tasks can significantly enhance various applications in augmented and virtual reality, autonomous driving, and drone surveillance. However, two main challenges arise in this area: the large feature space and angular distortions. To address this, we propose QML-360◦, a hybrid quantum–classical framework for 360◦ image classification that learns on spherical graphs. It uses a permutation-equivariant quantum head to preserve symmetry under node permutations. To address memory and runtime constraints on near-term quantum devices (and in classical simulation), we adopt a distributed training strategy. Gradient evaluations are parallelized across workers, and split-shot estimation reduces per-device load while maintaining estimator quality. Across datasets, our method achieves competitive performance relative to the baselines under large rotation perturbations while reducing per-device compute and memory. In addition, we establish theoretical formulations for equivariance, gradient variance, and cost scaling, and present empirical results that demonstrate their effectiveness.

title: Control Oriented Real-Time Nonlinear Dynamic Pseudo-Inverse for Non-minimum Phase Systems
authors: Miguel Sempertegui Sosa, J. Jim Zhu, Douglas A. Lawrence
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: This paper presents a novel algorithm for the dynamic inversion of nonlinear systems within the output tracking control framework, wherein the inverse serves as an open-loop nominal controller to facilitate feedback stabilization of the tracking error dynamics along a nominal trajectory. The desired dynamic response for the open-loop controlled system is treated as a pseudo identity from which the causal and stable inverse is defined. For non-minimum phase systems, the Fliess normal form is used to enable a practical pseudo-inverse design without the need for internal dynamics stabilization or reverse-time previewing. Unlike exact inversion, which can be unstable or anti-causal, the proposed pseudo-inverse is stable and implementable in real time. A nontrivial application example illustrates the algorithm.

title: Anatomy-Guided Radiology Report Generation with Pathology-Aware Regional Prompts
authors: Yijian Gao, Dominic Marshall, Xiaodan Xing, Junzhi Ning, Congren Dai, Giorgos Papanastasiou, Guang Yang, Matthieu Komorowski
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Engineering in Medicine and Biology
abstract: Goal: Radiology report generation holds significant potential to alleviate clinical workloads and streamline medical care. However, achieving high clinical accuracy remains challenging, as radiographs often feature intricate structures and subtle pathologies. Methods: To address these challenges, this work introduces an innovative approach that explicitly integrates anatomical and pathological information into report decoding by leveraging pathology-aware regional prompts. Specifically, we develop an anatomical region detector that extracts structured visual features from distinct anatomical areas, coupled with a novel multi-label pathology detector that identifies global abnormalities. Results: Our model demonstrates superior report generation performance in natural language generation and clinical efficacy, surpassing previous state-of-the-art methods. It achieved scores of 0.394 in BLEU-1, 0.302 in ROUGE-L, and 0.470 in F1, reflecting substantial improvements in both linguistic fluency and medical accuracy. Formal expert evaluations further affirmed the model's potential to elevate radiology practice. Conclusion: By integrating anatomical and pathological insights to emulate radiologists' workflow, our model achieves superior accuracy and clinical coherence of radiology reporting. It offers remarkable promise to support clinical decision-making and transform patient management.

title: Data-Driven Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Markov Multiplayer Tidal Turbine Systems
authors: Jiacheng Wu, Bosen Lian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper designs data-driven inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) for Markov jump tidal turbine control systems, where the stochastic tidal flow is modeled as a finite-state Markov process, and the pitch and generator speed controllers are treated as players in a nonzero-sum differential game. By exploiting demonstrated optimal state and control input trajectories, the cost function weights can be reconstructed without requiring knowledge of the system dynamics. First, a model-based IRL algorithm with three components is developed: (i) policy evaluation based on a modified Lyapunov function, (ii) policy improvement using the optimal control, and (iii) weight updates via inverse optimal control. An equivalent data-driven IRL algorithm is designed, which incorporates transition rate identification into an integral off-policy method that uncovers cost and control parameters without prior knowledge of system dynamics. Rigorous convergence analysis and simulation studies validate the proposed approach.

title: A Multi-Stage Time-Variant Motion Planner for Agile Autonomous Driving Maneuvers
authors: Alexander Langmann, Stefan Kohl, Levent Ögretmen, Mattia Piccinini, Johannes Betz
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Agile, dynamic, and safe motion planning in interactive environments is a key challenge for autonomous vehicles. Most research has focused on enhancing established planning algorithms, often aiming to improve optimality or computational efficiency within fixed planning horizons. This paper introduces a novel sampling-based planner that employs a multi-stage approach to generate a tree of maneuvers by concatenating trajectory segments of different time horizons and selecting the best maneuver. To enable real-time performance, we reduce computational complexity through several strategies such as limiting the number of planning stages and using informed sampling. This allows us to combine short agile maneuvers with longer-horizon trajectories, enhancing recursive feasibility while respecting dynamical constraints. We evaluate our algorithm in simulation using high-speed, highly agile autonomous racing scenarios, and compare it against a state-of-the-art trajectory planner. Our experiments show that the approach can perform static and dynamic obstacle avoidance and succeed in challenging overtaking scenarios. These findings indicate that our method improves the agility and maneuverability of autonomous vehicles, expanding their operational domain under challenging conditions.
