title: Classifying Post-COVID “Brain Fog” Patients and Identifying Key ROIs via Graph Neural Network Model
authors: Yuzhe Li, Yubo Zhang, Zhaomin Dong, Li Ma, Yonggui Yuan, Lili Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Brain fog has raised significant public health concerns as a common neurocognitive impairment in the post-COVID-19 condition, involving memory loss, poor concentration, and language difficulties. However, their neural mechanisms remain unclear, and objective resting-state fMRI–based diagnostic tools are still lacking. To address these challenges, we first recruited 72 patients who experienced persistent brain fog symptoms following COVID-19 infection, along with 68 post-COVID participants without brain fog (PC-noBF), and collected resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging (rs-fMRI) data from all participants. The interpretable graph neural network model BrainGNN was employed to model and classify individual brain networks, utilizing functional connectivity graphs constructed from the Automated Anatomical Labeling (AAL) atlas. Using out-of-fold predictions from 5-fold cross-validation, BrainGNN achieved an accuracy of 75.71% (Bootstrap 95% CI: 68.57%–82.86%) and an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 76.07% (Bootstrap 95% CI: 73.93%–88.48%). Furthermore, on an independent test set, BrainGNN outperformed traditional machine learning methods and other GNN models, achieving an accuracy of 82.14% and an AUC of 82.82% (classification threshold: 0.5). Moreover, the model identified several key brain regions—bilateral insula, bilateral Heschl's gyri, and the left superior temporal gyrus—as potential neurobiological markers. Notably, in post-hoc analyses, the ALFF and ReHo values of the left insula were significantly associated with scores related to language and memory symptoms. These findings collectively underscore the effectiveness and interpretability of the proposed approach in identifying functional markers of brain fog. This study not only demonstrates the potential of individual-level identification of brain fog using resting-state fMRI empowered by interpretable GNN, but also reveals its capacity to provide novel insights into the neurobiological mechanisms underlying COVID-19-related cognitive impairment.

title: Quantum Overflow Detection for Reliable Bioinspired Image Enhancement
authors: Neeraj Kumar Misra, Nirupma Pathak
abstract: In quantum information science, fundamental principles of quantum mechanics, particularly quantum parallelism are leveraged to enable computational capabilities that exceed the performance limits of classical computing systems. This paper proposes a novel overflow detection circuit (ODC) within a Binary-Coded Decimal (BCD) framework, aimed at improving biomedical image enhancement. In this research, quantum-inspired methodologies for biomedical image enhancement have not been extensively investigated. The proposed ODC quantum circuit is transpiler and executed on Ibm_Kingston quantum hardware, where measured qubit frequencies are compared with ideal simulation results to characterize hardware-induced errors. The ODC circuit is decomposed into a Clifford+T gate set, and a noise-aware Quantum Equivalent Design (QED) is developed within the IBM Quantum Experience platform. Various quantum noise models, including depolarizing, amplitude damping, phase damping, and bit-flip noise, are quantitatively analyzed using the core IBM Qiskit Ignis framework. The real-time biomedical images enhancement performed by ODC circuit with the help of code simulated on IBM-Qiskit. A case study is conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed overflow detection approach in biomedical imaging applications. Circuit testability is rigorously evaluated through missing-target-gate fault simulations. Comparative analysis indicates that the proposed ODC achieves a 66.66% reduction in gate count, a 33.33% reduction in garbage outputs, a 66.66% reduction in ancilla inputs, and a 50% reduction in quantum cost. These findings underscore the efficiency, robustness, and practical viability of the proposed quantum ODC for quantum-enhanced biomedical imaging and noise-resilient quantum information processing systems.

title: A HV and LV Motor Combined Electric Powertrain for Battery Modular Balancing and Efficiency Improvement
authors: Henry Grasman, Ian Gibson, Sujan Thapa, Michael Jimenez, Nathan Cahilig, Chen Duan
abstract: this paper proposes an electric powertrain for battery module state-of-charge (SOC) balancing and powertrain efficiency optimization through using high-voltage (HV) and low-voltage (LV) motors. This hardware architecture presents the first battery balancing scheme that leverages the traction motor as a balancer. If all of the modules are balanced, then the Auxiliary Power Module (APM) is connected to the LV motor. A motor rule-based power-split algorithm is implemented in both simulation and experimental studies to validate the balancing performance and energy savings, and to facilitate future optimization. Simulations were conducted using a Mercedes ML 350 vehicle model equipped with eight 48 V battery modules and one 48 V APM, demonstrating a 1.2% improvement in powertrain efficiency over a combined UDDS + HWFET drive cycle and a 2.4% increase in full-discharge driving range. The battery balancing method was implemented on a go-kart for experimental validation using two unbalanced 48V battery modules, showing that the LV motor was capable of balancing both battery modules.

title: Enhancing the Reliability of Fault Diagnosis in Lithium-Ion Batteries Using a Hybrid Model-Based and Machine Learning Approach
authors: Taha Mohamed Abdelatif Maaradji, Said Alem, Emanuele Gravante, Matilde D’Arpino, Giorgio Rizzoni
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIB) are essential for electric vehicles (EV) and renewable energy storage, and their safe, reliable, and efficient operation is critical. As their use grows, robust fault diagnosis becomes key to maintaining availability and performance. A major challenge is that many battery faults exhibit similar patterns, making it difficult to distinguish and isolate specific fault types. This paper presents a hybrid approach that combines model-based methods with data-driven techniques to improve the safety and reliability of LIB systems. The proposed diagnostic architecture uses structural analysis (SA) to identify analytical redundancy for fault detection and isolation (FDI), while Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) algorithms generate residuals that capture inconsistencies between model predictions and measurements. These residuals are processed into statistical features and passed to a machine learning (ML) classifier for accurate fault detection and classification. In this series-hybrid structure, using SA–EKF residuals as classifier inputs significantly improves diagnostic performance compared to ML-only features, and among the tested classifiers, Residual-based RF achieves the highest accuracy. The method achieves 98.73% accuracy in distinguishing fault types, demonstrating high sensitivity across all faults and robustness to varying noise levels. Overall, the proposed hybrid methodology enhances fault diagnosis effectiveness and supports the safe operation of LIB systems, contributing to the transition toward clean and sustainable energy solutions.

title: Resilient Data-Driven Security Platoon Control for Heterogeneous Vehicle Systems Under Saturation and External Disturbances
authors: Xiaomiao Xie, Ying Wan, Liang Hua, Jinde Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper proposed a model-free adaptive security control (MFASC) method designed for nonlinear vehicle systems, simultaneously tackling controller saturation, external disturbances, and aperiodic Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. To achieve a desired platoon control objective with only input-output data, the dynamics are reconstructed into a differential form with pseudo partial derivative parameters. Under saturation constraints, a de-saturation factor is introduced to ensure the controller’s state remains within permissible limits. Additionally, a disturbance observer is included to estimate external disturbances and mitigate their negative effects. For aperiodic DoS attacks, an attack compensation mechanism is developed. Comprehensive stability analysis demonstrates the ultimate boundedness of the formation error of vehicle systems under aperiodic DoS attacks. The validity of the theoretical results is supported by numerical simulations.

title: Tunable Spatial Power Splitter Using Single Layer Cylindrical Plasma Discharges
authors: Mohammad G. H. Alijani, Alessio Monti, Stefano Vellucci, Mirko Barbuto, Alessandro Toscano, Filiberto Bilotti
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This work presents a novel single-layer, reconfigurable spatial power splitter based on plasma discharges. Each unit cell features two adjacent core–shell cylinders, composed of a tunable plasma core surrounded by a high-permittivity dielectric shell. The plasma core is modeled using a free-electron plasma permittivity with variable plasma frequency. Numerical simulation demonstrates that the power-splitting ratio can be continuously varied between 30% and 70% by independently controlling the plasma frequencies of the two cylinders. This reconfigurability enables precise manipulation of the transmitted power distribution while limiting the total reflected, absorbed, and undesired diffraction power to less than 10% of the incident energy, corresponding to an overall efficiency exceeding 90%.

title: Clinical Knowledge-Guided PET/CT Lesion Segmentation with Interpretable Fusion of Metabolic and Structural Cues
authors: Song Zhang, Jiajin Zhang, Liheng Qiu, Wei Liu, Dakai Jin, Wenpei Jiao, Le Lu, Tzu-Chen Yen, Shenmiao Yang, Ke Yan
abstract: Automated whole-body lesion segmentation in <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG PET/CT images marks a pivotal breakthrough in oncological diagnostics, substantially improving the accuracy and efficiency of tumor burden assessment. Manual segmentation is often plagued by significant interobserver variability, underscoring the necessity for automated solutions. The synergistic combination of PET’s exceptional sensitivity for detecting metabolic activity with CT’s anatomical precision renders accurate segmentation crucial for achieving quantitative and reproducible clinical workflows. However, current methodologies frequently grapple with challenges such as over-segmentation or under-segmentation, inadvertently delineating normal tissues with elevated uptake or neglecting lesions characterized by subtle intensity variations, primarily due to a lack of integrated metabolic and anatomical insights. To address these limitations, we present a novel framework that adeptly integrates clinical expertise regarding anatomical and metabolic cues to refine PET/CT lesion segmentation. Our innovative mixture-of-experts (MoE) based interpretable fusion module skillfully merges complementary modality information while explicitly elucidating the pixel-level contributions of each modality to the final segmentation outcome. Rigorous evaluations across three in-domain benchmarks and two external datasets demonstrate our model’s superior segmentation performance and generalizability. Furthermore, our visualizations provide compelling insights into the pivotal role each modality plays in the decision-making process, highlighting our approach’s transformative potential in enhancing PET/CT lesion segmentation. Building on this foundation, we further validated the prognostic significance of the features extracted from our proposed framework in the context of PET/CT-based prognosis predictions.

title: Distributed Random Space-Time Coding for Unmanned Ground Vehicles in Barrage Relay Networks
authors: Meng Wang, Fengye Hu, Zhuang Ling, Yayun Tan
abstract: We propose a distributed random space-time coding based on rotated Alamouti code to address the loss of orthogonality in Alamouti codes and the resulting inter-symbol interference (ISI) caused by link asymmetry in distributed cooperation of high-density unmanned ground vehicles (UGVs) barrage relay networks (BRNs) under complex tactical environments. This approach incorporates rotation coding techniques, applying specific angular rotations to the coding matrix to circumvent ISI without increasing relay communication overhead, thereby restoring system diversity gains. Combined with random space-time coding (RSTC), it utilizes a randomly weighted coefficient matrix to enhance spatial diversity capabilities and physical layer security in multipath fading channels. Furthermore, a composite forest channel model incorporating path loss, vegetation attenuation, and shadow fading is constructed, with end-to-end outage probability derived. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) gain over the conventional phase rotation (PR)-based BRN, while significantly reducing outage probability. This validates its effectiveness and reliability in tactical environments.

title: Explaining Performance Issues of Cloud Applications from Logs using Rule Induction and Dempster-Shafer Theory
authors: Ashot Harutyunyan, Arnak Poghosyan, Edgar Davtyan, Karen Petrosyan, Nelson Baloian
abstract: This study focuses on the explainability of AI operations for diagnosing degradations in cloud applications. Log data can provide essential contextual information on misbehaving applications in monitoring, evaluated against a key performance indicator (KPI). We describe an approach to learning conditions that lead to unacceptable KPI states using tree-based and rule-induction algorithms, including a rule-validation framework based on the Dempster-Shafer theory of evidence. The latter also provides a "what-if" analysis mechanism to test user or expert hypotheses about the application’s performance. These algorithms produce specific rules that explain anomalous behavior as disturbances in log event-type distributions. Event types (clusters of similar log messages) significantly reduce log volume and enable efficient analytics (e.g., anomaly and change detection) on these structures. Our methods infer event types exhibiting frequency imbalances, which can degrade application performance, and then trace those types back to raw log messages for complete interpretation and root-cause analysis (RCA). We present experimental insights from Elastic Sky X Integrated (ESXi) hosts in a private cloud environment that demonstrate the viability of the proposed framework for explainable cloud administration. This combines time-series and unstructured data from data center resources. Experimental validation across three distinct failure scenarios (compute thrashing, network congestion, and storage saturation) demonstrated the model’s robustness. The approach achieved a true positive rate of 98% for compute anomalies, 97% for network failures, and 99% for storage bottlenecks. Compared to traditional threshold-based monitoring, the proposed method significantly reduces false positives by distinguishing benign high-latency states from true system failures. In particular, event-log-only models achieved low recall (0.118), while the proposed multi-modal fusion substantially improved detection performance and interpretability.

title: Design of a Multi-Object Wearable Recognition and Tracking Algorithm Based on PHSM-YOLO
authors: Hao Wu, Mingyao Wang, Bo Liang, Jinyang Zhang, Dingbang Ren, Chuanlan Wu
abstract: To address issues such as missed detections and false positives in safety equipment wear detection tasks caused by complex backgrounds, severe occlusions, and small target sizes, as well as frequent ID switching and low tracking accuracy during personnel tracking due to occlusions and drastic changes in target scale, this paper proposes a multi-object wear detection and tracking algorithm based on an improved YOLOv11 and Byte Track. During the detection phase, a novel C3k2_PPA architecture is constructed by introducing a parallel patch-aware module. This architecture leverages a multi-branch parallel structure to enhance feature representation capabilities for multi-scale objects and improve the fusion of local and global information. It effectively suppresses background interference while enhancing small object features. The lightweight downsampling module HWD is introduced to reduce redundant model parameters while preserving more useful information, further boosting the model's feature expression capabilities. At the backbone network's terminal layer, the MPCA attention mechanism captures cross-scale contextual information through multi-scale attention, improving small object spatial localization. Finally, a new detection head incorporating a separation and enhancement attention module is introduced to boost feature extraction capabilities. During the tracking phase, the aspect ratio in the state variables is replaced with width w, and the state update and observation models are redesigned to better align with target motion characteristics. The covariance matrix is adjusted, and noise estimation is unified using height h as the scale. Finally, DIOU replaces the traditional IOU for data association. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved YOLOv11 model achieves a 2.62% increase in mAP50, a 10.5% reduction in parameters, and a 7.9% decrease in computational load, exhibiting enhanced robustness and detection accuracy. The enhanced Byte Track algorithm achieves an average MOTA of 71.69%, average MOTP of 76.89%, and an average of 5 ID jumps per test data point, validating the superiority of this algorithm and its practical applicability.

title: Collaborative Neural Networks significantly Improve Global Aerosol Retrievals From Multi-Angle Polarimeters_supp1-3685881.pdf
authors: Guangliang Fu
abstract: We present the RemoTAP-Hybrid algorithm for global aerosol products retrieved from Multi-Angle Polarimeters (MAP), and apply it to one-year aerosol retrievals from the SPEXone MAP instrument. For the first time, Multiple Collaborated Neural Networks (MCNN) are used to perform global aerosol MAP retrievals over land and ocean. The combined use of MCNN with a full physics inversion in RemoTAP-Hybrid, significantly improves accuracy and computational speed of SPEXone aerosol retrievals. We validate the retrieved Aerosol Optical Depth (AOD), Angstrom Exponent (AE), Single Scattering Albedo (SSA), Absorption AOD (AAOD), Fine mode AOD (FAOD), Coarse mode AOD (CAOD), and fine mode effective radius (reff_fine) with AERONET data. The validation suggests that aerosol retrievals can be further improved when extending the aerosol description to include a fine non-spherical mode. For AOD, the Root-Mean-Square Error (RMSE) is 0.051 (0.037) over land (ocean), while 78.3% (80.8%) of the retrievals are within the requirement formulated by the Global Climate Observing System (GCOS). The RMSE is 0.186 (0.207) for AE, 0.038 (0.044) for SSA, 0.023 (0.014) for AAOD, 0.047 (0.031) for FAOD, 0.043 (0.027) for CAOD, and 0.021 (0.026) for reff_fine over land (ocean). Compared to the current RemoTAP algorithm, RemoTAP-Hybrid shows improved agreement with AERONET for AOD, AE, and FAOD over land and ocean while the number of valid retrievals is increased by 4% (3%) over land (ocean) and the computational time is reduced by 43% (32%). Finally, we present the first global seasonal maps of aerosol optical and microphysical properties and chemical composition from SPEXone.

title: Mobile-Based Applications for Early Detection of Oral Cancer and Oral Potentially Malignant Disorders: A Systematic Review
authors: Rutuja Prasad Pujari, Sunil Surendraprasad Mishra, Prajakta Ravindra Patil, Akash Dattatray Bade, Anupama Anand Chavan, Aarati Panchbai
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Oral Cancer is a major challenge in remote populations, low and middle-income countries because of late diagnosis and limited access to specialized health care needs and screening. In this systematic review, assessment of mHealth applications for early detection of oral cancer and OPMDs, along with addressing the gaps in feasibility, diagnostic accuracy and implementation evidence. From the eight studies, applications using MobileNet, ResNet152V2, U-Net and DenseNet201 softwares showed sensitivity, specificity and accuracy of more than 90% with positive usability. Although these applications demonstrate promising performance and CHW acceptability, they also pose challenges such as image quality variability, connectivity needs and limited external validation. mHealth shows strong potential for scalable screening but for national integration we require multicentric trials with standardized protocols and cost effectiveness data.

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Performance Calibration and Optimization of Integrated mmW Power Amplifiers
authors: Valentin Coppola, Florent Cilici, Sylvain Bourdel, Estelle Lauga-Larroze, Salvador Mir, Florence Podevin, Manuel J. Barragan
abstract: This paper explores the application of reinforcement learning algorithms to the problem of calibrating and optimizing the performance of an integrated millimeter-wave (mmW) power amplifier (PA). The proposed calibration algorithm is aimed at compensating performance degradation while optimizing power efficiency, by finding the optimum values of a set of on-chip tuning knobs. The developed calibration solution is based on a multiagent deep reinforcement learning algorithm, in which a number of reinforcement learning agents learn to collaborate in order to optimize the complete set of specifications of the circuit under calibration. The proposed technique is validated based on post-layout simulation results of a tunable 60 GHz PA case study implemented in STMicroelectronics 55 nm CMOS technology.

title: Time-Frequency Attention Network for Interference Mitigation in FMCW Radar
authors: Zhijie Sun, Niraj Tamrakar, Yuxuan Gong, Subash T D, Shilong Pan, Bikash Nakarmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: FMCW radar faces severe mutual interference in multi-target scenarios, where interference overlaps with echoes of closely spaced targets, degrading detection reliability. To address this, we propose SpecUNet, a time-frequency attention network integrating STFT, CBAM attention, and dual-domain loss. By exploiting distinct time-frequency signatures of interference and targets, SpecUNet adaptively mitigates interference while preserving target features. The proposed method is validated on datasets with target objects separated within 10-25 cm with several multiple interferences. In the experiment, our proposed method reduces the noise floor, enhances Peak-to-Sidelobe Level (PSL) from 3.6 dB to 11.2 dB, and restores multi-target resolution in low-SINR environments.

title: Self-Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Models for Waste Collection Route Optimization in Smart Cities
authors: Koushik Chakraborty, M.Sughasiny, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Jamal K. Abbas, Hameed H. Khalaf, Manoranjani.R
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Smart waste management is key component of megacity systems that influences public health, environmental eco-sustainability, and the overall stability of the economy. Despite this importance, it is still a very challenging problem to solve for waste collecting route optimization for waste collection given the variations in waste quantities, business traffic, and rainfall conditions. In this paper, we propose a tone-Adaptive Neuro Fuzzy Model (SANFM) to help solve waste collection route optimization in smart cities. SANFM that combines neural networks and fuzzy logic, is able learn and adjust to the environment (from the readings of the sensors) and be able to adjust as the waste can be collected better and more effectively. SANFM is adaptive because it dynamically adjusts in real time to the waste quantity and the movements of the vehicle, so the routes the waste collection will operate in will be smart routes. This will allow for the decrease of the cost of collecting waste, the business traffic, the greenhouse emissions, and the overall impact of the waste collection on the environment. The simulation results confirm that SANFM lowers the cost of waste collection and improves the efficiency of the routes. The results from this study show that SANFM could lower waste collections costs from 10 - 20% compared to other conventional methods for route optimization. The study supports the development of smart city infrastructure by introducing a new framework for optimizing waste collection routes. The possibilities for SANFM extend well beyond waste management as it provides a viable framework for route optimization in many logistics and transportation systems.

title: Emotion Estimation Using Eye Gaze and Head Pose: A Temporal and Disentangled Representation Learning Approach
authors: A. Deepa, S. Ponmaniraj
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The recent innovations in affective computing have revealed a growing interest in non-verbal cues for emotion recognition. This study proposes an innovative emotion estimation framework that uses only eye gaze and head pose, excluding facial expressions and body posture. The proposed method leverages disentangled representation learning and temporal transformer models. This is aimed to precisely assess emotional states such as happiness, anxiety, confusion, and boredom. Our model demonstrates high broadness and performance using recent datasets. The research addresses the need for subtle, privacy- protecting and hardware-efficient emotion detection in real-world applications. The temporal and disentangled representation learning approach is a deep learning technique in emotion recognition. This focuses on learning representations - the internal features that are temporal- time-based and disentangled where emotion-related features are separated from irrelevant ones.

title: Empirical Analysis of Confidence and Semantic Fidelity in Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Understanding Hallucination Patterns Across Domains
authors: Diksha Khandelwal, Manvendra Singh Tanwar, Navya Nayer, Subhash Chand Gupta
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a prominent paradigm for enhancing the factual grounding of large language models (LLMs) by incorporating external knowledge into the generation process. Despite these advances, hallucinations remain a significant challenge, where models confidently produce information that is factually incorrect or unsupported. In this work, we conduct a comprehensive empirical analysis of the relationship between model confidence, semantic fidelity, and hallucination patterns across three diverse datasets: XSum, TruthfulQA, and SciFact. Through systematic evaluation of ROUGE, BERTScore, perplexity, entropy, and semantic similarity metrics, we reveal critical insights into how dataset characteristics influence hallucination risk. Our analysis demonstrates that low entropy paired with poor semantic alignment signals overconfident hallucinations, particularly in abstractive summarization tasks, while high entropy with strong semantic fidelity indicates cautious, faithful generation in retrieval-grounded scenarios. We identify significant effect sizes across datasets and provide comprehensive visualizations that illustrate metric interdependencies and hallucination patterns. Our findings establish that hallucination behavior is multifactorial, influenced by task abstraction level, retrieval grounding, and domain complexity. This work provides empirical foundations for understanding confidence-fidelity dynamics and informs the design of future adaptive hallucination mitigation strategies in trustworthy LLM deployment.

title: Smart Community Connect: ML-based Real-Time Urban Issue Detection and Prioritization using XGBoost
authors: Konijeti Sai Kalyan, Maddu Rakesh, Yamini B
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Smart Community Connect is an urban issue reporting platform that operates in real time and employs XGBoost to effectively classify and prioritize community challenges. Residents have the ability to upload photos, provide descriptions, and use geotagging to mark precise locations, while the system evaluates the severity through machine learning and community votes. The platform promotes transparency by displaying real-time status updates and priority rankings, enabling authorities to respond more swiftly and efficiently. This machine learning-based strategy enhances citizen engagement, accelerates problem resolution, and fosters more intelligent, data-informed urban management.

title: Design and Analysis of 18nm FinFET based Hybrid Adder
authors: M. BalaMurali Krishna, N. Ashok Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The ever-increasing need to use energy efficient and fast arithmetic circuits in the current digital systems has compelled it to create an optimized full adder architectures that would run successfully within the highly scaled technologies. CMOS adders of conventional design have higher switching capacitance and power absorption, and hybrid and pass-transistor designs have been found to have problems with signal degradation and variability. This paper shows an enhanced hybrid full-adder design that uses 18-nm FinFET technology to realize better power-delay performance with full-swing output operation. The pass-transistor-logic based design is proposed to allow use of CMOS-assisted restoration to control the number of transistor layers stacked and decrease the loading of critical signal paths by parasitics. The design and evaluation of that circuit were done in the Cadence Virtuoso Spectre simulation environment and with an industry-standard FinFET process design kit. Performance analysis involves transient and comparative analysis with hybrid adders that have already been reported in the past under normal operating conditions. The outcome of the simulation is a propagation delay of 28.03 ps and a power dissipation of 138.96 nW at a supply voltage of 0.8 V, and thus this is much less than the available designs. The suggested architecture has better switching performance, constant full-swing functionality, and stronger stability and is acceptable in low-power arithmetic datapaths of advanced VLSI systems.

title: Energy-Efficient Cooperative Attack Algorithms for UAV Swarms in Dynamic Environments
authors: Pradeep Kumar G, Teena S, Meenakshi R, Pooja K V, B. Sridevi, P.Suveetha Dhanaselvam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This research paper presents a Cooperative Attack Algorithm sketched for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarm systems, confronting the difficulties posed by spatiotemporal constraints in dynamic environments. The proposed algorithm allows UAVs to synergically plan and execute attack missions during the optimization of their trajectories ensuring efficient resource allocation. The algorithm helps the swarms to increase their flexibility in order to address dynamic combat-zone scenarios and targets by utilizing decentralized decision-making methods. Simulations show the efficiency of the algorithm therefore representing the maximized mission rate and lesser operational hazards when compared to conventional methods. This work forms a stronger layout for future applications in both military and non-military conditions.

title: Meta FOA: Classifying and Scheduling of IoT Tasks in Cloud Computing Environment
authors: C Manjunath, Keerthi Manchikanti, Johnson Kolluri, Raveendrababu Vempati, C Veena, Santhosh Kumar Medishetti
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Task scheduling in cloud computing plays a critical role in ensuring reliable and time-constrained execution of applications, especially for mission-critical services such as airline reservation systems (ARS). To address challenges related to low reliability, limited fault tolerance, and poor deadline adherence in existing schedulers, an efficient Metaheuristic Falcon Optimization Algorithm (FOA)-based scheduling framework is introduced for optimal task-to-virtual machine mapping. The proposed approach is implemented using the CloudSim simulation toolkit and evaluated under realistic ARS workload conditions, with comparative analysis against Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA). Experimental results demonstrate that the FOA-based scheduler achieves significant performance gains, improving system reliability by up to 18.6%, enhancing fault tolerance by 21.3%, and increasing deadline adherence by 19.8% compared to the benchmark methods. These improvements are attributed to the effective exploration-exploitation balance and adaptive search strategy of the FOA, which enables robust scheduling decisions under dynamic workload conditions. The findings confirm that the FOA-based scheduling framework provides a dependable and resilient solution for time-critical cloud applications, particularly in airline reservation environments where service continuity and timeliness are paramount.

title: RFID-Integrated Patient Monitoring System for Early Detection of Neurodegenerative Diseases
authors: R Subramanian Shashank, R Vikas Gowda, Rakshit Patil, Teja A N, Nagesh K N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Millions of individuals worldwide are affected by neurodegenerative disorders such as Parkinson’s disease and Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS), which often remain undiagnosed until symptoms become severe and irreversible. Their progressive nature often delays detection until irreversible symptoms manifest and cure doesn’t come easy. This paper presents a cost-effective and portable patient monitoring system designed for early-stage anomaly identification through multimodal biosensing. The suggested prototype combines RFID-based patient authentication with multi-sensor data acquisition, which includes MPU6050 motion detection and Myo Ware electromyography (EMG) analysis, all managed by a Raspberry Pi controller. We detail the signal acquisition, feature extraction, and anomaly detection framework that enables consistent real-time monitoring and supervising. Simulated experimental analysis demonstrates consistent sensor response and baseline deviation tracking with high reliability. This design seeks to bridge the gap between academic prototypes and deployable healthcare tools, assisting the medical community in translating innovative research concepts into practical, scalable, and user-friendly solutions that can be seamlessly integrated into real-world clinical settings to improve patient outcomes and optimize healthcare delivery.

title: A Review of Perovskite Solar Cells: Structure, Materials, and Fabrication
authors: Kamlesh Kumar Bharati, Pavan Chaudhari, Vijay Pratap Singh, Rajesh Kumar, Shilpi Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are among the most inspiring technologies regarding the future of solar energy due to their flexible design, low cost, and high-power conversion efficiency (PCE). This review highlights the structure, working principle, different perovskite materials, and fabrication methods used in PSCs. Materials such as hybrid organic-inorganic perovskites (like MAPbI₃, FAPbI₃), all-inorganic compounds (CsPbI₃), and lead-free alternatives (e.g., Sn and Bi-based perovskites) have been reviewed. Significant improvements have been made over the past decade in both material composition and device fabrication. Several low-cost fabrication methods like Solution processing, Thermal Evaporation and Chemical vapor deposition are also reviewed for their impact on device performance and scalability. With rapid progress in both materials and fabrication techniques, PSCs show great potential for next-generation solar energy solutions.

title: Localized Disaster Response Optimizer: An AI-Driven Geospatial Platform for Proactive Resource Allocation and Damage Assessment
authors: Karthikeyan S, Nizam Aashiq I, Varalakshmi K
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Nature disasters often impact on communication systems, block access to the affected areas and slow down damage assessment and allocation of resources. Disaster response systems currently being built on AI do not support real-time flexibility, understanding and performance-efficiency, particularly in low-connectivity settings, which represents a significant vulnerability of the state of disaster management technology. To overcome them, this work suggests the Localized Disaster Response Optimizer (LDRO), a comprehensive AI-based geospatial solution of the platform to improve preparedness, response, and recovery efforts. LDRO consolidates damage detection (based on deep-learning: YOLO, ResNet), reinforcement learning and graph neural networks based resource optimization, crowdsourced reporting with credibility scoring, and offline synchronization to field operability. The system is more efficient and accurate in resource allocation, with 89 percent and a damage detection accuracy of 92.3 percent, which beats the conventional and state-of-the-art solutions. Through the integration of perception, decision-making, and optimizing logistics into one framework, LDRO provides more responsive, quicker and more open disaster management. Although the plan is promising, LDRO can still be extended using more comprehensive multi-disaster datasets and more detailed explainability modules to make it meet the requirements of large-scale deployment in the real world.

title: Deep Learning and Multi-Access Edge Computing for 6G-Based Underwater Wireless Networks
authors: Ajay Krishna G, Kayalvizhi K R, Gokuleswaran S
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The rapid spread of 7G-enabled underwater wireless networks has continuing hurdles. These challenges arise from the unpredictable nature of acoustic–optical channels, high latency, restricted bandwidth, and the necessity for real-time, autonomous decision-making in changing underwater settings. These limitations impair reliable sensing, reduce communication efficiency, and make coordinated control more difficult for groups of autonomous underwater vehicles. To address this issue, the proposed system uses Vision Transformers for strong multimodal perception, Deep Q-Networks for policy learning that considers energy use and latency, and compact, edge-hosted Large Language Models for contextual reasoning. This is all done within a single reinforcement learning and Multi-Access Edge Computing architecture. This collaborative framework facilitates adaptive routing, the ability to transition between different modes, the allocation of tasks in a cooperative manner, and scalable online learning. These capabilities are underpinned by distributed MEC inference and orchestration using Python. The aim is to build systems that can withstand challenges, deliver better performance, and keep running longer in situations where failure isn't an option. The novelty stems from a unified, AI-driven architecture. It cleverly combines Vision Transformers (ViT), Deep Q-Networks (DQN), and large language models (LLMs) deployed at the edge. A programmable, self-optimizing communication network, specifically for underwater environments.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Using Deep Learning and Image Processing Techniques
authors: Pream Kumar Reddy, Girish R, Gowtham S R, Manjunath S, Nagesh K N
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Brain tumors are one of the most severe neurological disorders that affect both children and adults, as the manifestations of their symptoms include headache, regularly experienced seizures, nausea, and vision issues, cognitive and personality changes. Compared to all other forms of tumors, brain tumors are particularly perilous as they have the capacity to complicate critical brain functional activities. A normal classification of these tumors in four grades is usually based on the severity and speed of growth. The approach to treatment depends on the type, grade and location of the tumor in the brain. Unless it is detected at an early stage, a brain tumor may be a killer condition. The Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans give accurate and vivid images of the brain and hence doctors and neurosurgeons use it to diagnose and analyze brain tumors.

title: A Cloud-IoT Framework for Smart Farming and Sustainable Agri-Food Management
authors: Harish Reddy Gantla, Manvi Sharma, Anusha Preetham, Pooja Nayak S, Udit Mamodiya, Rakesh Dani
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Smart farming signifies a revolution in the sphere of agriculture, combining the data-based intelligence with the use of resources sustainably. The old farming system that is limited by fragmented sensing and poor interoperability cannot change dynamically to the climate variability and diverse soils. The current IoT-based platforms are not yet capable of providing low-latency analytics, safe interoperability, and cross-domain data incorporation among heterogeneous gadgets. To overcome such difficulties, this paper presents a Cloud-IoT Framework of Smart Farming and Sustainable Agri-Food Management, which can integrate cloud computation, intelligent sensing, and predictive analytics on a single adaptive framework. The model uses cloud-based federated intelligence and edge-layer pre-processing to monitor crops in real-time, predict soil-moisture of the soil, and optimize the yield sustainably to climate change. Multi-agent control layer will guarantee the data routing is energy-efficient, and a contextual AI module will provide the ability to explain the decisions and trust the recommendation. Experimental validation of multi- sensor IoT deployments reveals that there are 32 percent and 21-percent improvement in data transmission efficiency, 21-percent lowering in cloud processing latency and 19-percent better yield estimation accuracy than traditional approaches. The proposed system forms a platform to be scaled to incorporate cloud and IoT systems in sustainable agriculture, that would be able to connect local agro-data ecosystems with international agri-food supply systems. The work consequently leads to the development of sustainable, transparent, and resource conscious agricultural systems towards food security in emerging economies.

title: An Efficient Image Encryption with Feature Extraction and Cross-Sensor Feature Alignment and Fusion for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: S. Agnes Shifani, B Priyanka, Abla Suliman Hussein, Y Ravi Sankar, A Yadagiri, J Alekhya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Efficient Image Encryption (EIE) model by merging data from various sensors, like cameras, LiDAR, and radar, autonomous vehicles can have a better understanding of their surroundings. The EIFCFA model enhances self-driving cars' perception capabilities through a structured approach that involves encrypted image analysis, multi-modal feature extraction, and predictive modelling. Initial image processing converts data into encrypted 1D vectors for privacy. Object location determination involves analysing zero sequences and using image processing techniques like YOLO with DIoU loss for accurate bounding boxes. Prospective Generative Module (PGM) - LiDAR and camera data are utilized to extract relevant features while maintaining privacy. This processed data enables anticipating object interactions, creating a fast and accurate perception pipeline for future autonomous systems. The performance analysis was compared with F1 confidence curve, precision confidence curve, precision recall curve, recall confidence curve, and accuracy and loss calculation.

title: Intelligent Device Condition Scoring with YOLO and XGBoost for Sustainable Digital Redistribution
authors: D. Menaga, Sandhiya A, Rubini J S
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Due to the rapid rise in electronic waste and the limited availability of digital equipment in underdeveloped areas, more intelligent and sustainable redistribution mechanisms are urgently needed. An AI-based device reusability assessment methodology is proposed to automatically evaluate donated devices and enable fair and efficient digital access. Photos submitted by donors are paired with technical information such as device age, battery health, and hardware characteristics. In order to generate a reusability score that classifies devices as recyclable, repairable, or reusable, a hybrid learning technique is employed in which machine learning models such as Random Forest and XGBoost evaluate structured device data and a convolutional neural network analyzes physical damage from photographs. The model is integrated into a full-stack MERN platform to enable seamless device submission and verification. Experimental results show shorter evaluation times, higher accuracy, and more consistent judgments when compared to manual inspection. Future studies will focus on expanding datasets, smartphone integration, and real-time assessment.

title: Representing Multi-Relational Data via Statistical Relational Learning and Graph Convolutional Networks
