title: Resource-Efficient Adaptation of Large Multimodal Models in Medical Visual Question Answering
authors: Zahra Rezaei, Sara Safi Samghabadi, Mohammad Amin Amini, Yaser Mike Banad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques for large multimodal models in Medical Visual Question Answering (Med-VQA), focusing on optimizing performance while reducing computational demands in clinical applications. Using the VQARAD dataset, which comprises 2,244 question-answer pairs across 314 radiological images, we implemented Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA) techniques to fine-tune four state-of-the-art models: Idefics3-8B-Llama3, idefics2-8b, llava_hf_llava_1_5_7b_hf, and blip-vqa-large. Systematic hyperparameter optimization was conducted, including rank dimension, adaptation scaling, and 4bit quantization, to assess performance through accuracy metrics and computational efficiency. The Idefics3-8B-Llama3 model achieved 90% accuracy with minimal computational overhead (2,239 seconds training time), outperforming idefics28b, which reached 88% accuracy with a longer training duration of 5,145 seconds. Both models significantly outperformed llava_hf_llava_1_5_7b_hf (74% accuracy) and blip-vqa-large (55% accuracy). The integration of LoRA and QLoRA demonstrated substantial memory efficiency improvements while maintaining high accuracy, particularly in the Idefics models. These findings establish new performance benchmarks for Med-VQA tasks and provide a practical framework for deploying advanced AI models in resourceconstrained clinical settings, highlighting their potential to enhance medical image interpretation through efficient model optimization.

title: Semi-Supervised Open-Set Federated Learning for Heterogeneous Respiratory Sound Classification
authors: Won-Yang Cho, HyeSun Chang, Sangjun Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Deep learning-based classification study for the noninvasive diagnosis of respiratory diseases using auscultation sounds is actively progressing. Practical clinical applications face limitations arising from the high cost of medical data labeling, data heterogeneity across institutions, the existence of previously unseen classes, and stringent privacy constraints. This study proposes a Semi-Supervised Open-Set Heterogeneous Federated Learning (SOHFL) framework to address these multifaceted issues simultaneously. SOHFL effectively addresses label scarcity and open-set characteristics within a multi-institutional, heterogeneous environment. This is achieved by integrating prototypebased federated learning with GMM-based test-time adaptation, without sharing raw data. Experiments utilizing four distinct respiratory sound datasets confirm the superior performance of the proposed method, achieving an average score of $\mathbf{6 2. 2 - 6 2. 9 \%}$ compared to existing federated approaches. The framework demonstrates increased effectiveness in settings with a high proportion of previously unseen samples. This study presents a practical approach that addresses the complex challenges inherent in real-world clinical settings while preserving patient privacy.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Based Mobile Application for Pigeon Pea Disease Classification
authors: Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Ujjwal Kumar Karn, Deepika Neupane, Rajiv Kumar Yadav, Deepesh Prakash Guragain, Bijaya Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: About 65 percent of Nepal’s population depends on agriculture, with pigeon pea (‘Rahar’) being a key legume crop for traditional food products. Frequent disease outbreaks, coupled with limited access to agronomists and challenging terrain, force small-scale farmers to rely on manual visual inspection, often resulting in delayed or inaccurate disease identification and excessive pesticide use. This study presents an end-to-end mobile application for real-time pigeon pea disease classification. A self-collected dataset of 7,930 leaf images from multiple farms was used to train a VGG16-based deep learning model, achieving 98 percent accuracy and an F1-score of 94 percent. For deployment on resource-constrained devices, the model was optimized using TensorFlow Lite, with an average inference time of 18 ms per image. The results demonstrate the system’s feasibility and effectiveness for early field-level disease detection. Future work will expand the dataset, improve the interpretability of the model, and integrate additional features such as video-based diagnosis, agronomist guidance, and market insight.

title: LLM-Driven Stationarity-Aware Expert Demonstrations for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning in Mobile Systems_supp1-3682994.pdf
authors: Zongyuan Zhang
abstract: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) has been increasingly adopted in many real-world applications. While MARL enables decentralized deployment on resource-constrained edge devices, it suffers from severe non-stationarity due to the synchronous updates of agent policies. This non-stationarity results in unstable training and poor policy convergence, especially as the number of agents increases. In this paper, we propose Refinement-Enhanced LLM Expert Demonstrations (RELED), a scalable MARL framework that integrates large language model (LLM)-driven expert demonstrations with autonomous agent exploration. RELED incorporates a Stationarity-Aware Expert Demonstration module, which leverages theoretical non-stationarity bounds to enhance the quality of LLM-generated expert trajectories, thus providing high-reward and training-stable samples for each agent. Moreover, a Hybrid Expert-Agent Policy Optimization module adaptively balances each agent's learning from both expert-generated and agent-generated trajectories, accelerating policy convergence and improving generalization. Extensive experiments with real city networks based on OpenStreetMap demonstrate that RELED achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art MARL methods.

title: Multi-Robot Target Monitoring and Encirclement via Triggered Distributed Feedback Optimization_supp1-3686280.mp4
authors: Lorenzo Pichierri
abstract: We design a distributed feedback optimization strategy, embedded into a modular ROS 2 control architecture, which allows a team of heterogeneous robots to cooperatively monitor and encircle a target while patrolling points of interest. By relying on the aggregative feedback optimization framework, we handle multi-robot dynamics while minimizing a global performance index depending on both microscopic (e.g., the location of single robots) and macroscopic variables (e.g., the spatial distribution of the team). The proposed distributed policy allows the robots to cooperatively address the global problem by employing only local measurements and neighboring data exchanges. These exchanges are performed through an asynchronous communication protocol ruled by locally-verifiable triggering conditions. We formally prove that our strategy steers the robots to a set of configurations representing stationary points of the considered optimization problem. The effectiveness and scalability of the overall strategy are tested via Monte Carlo campaigns of realistic Webots ROS 2 virtual experiments. Finally, the applicability of our solution is shown with real experiments on ground and aerial robots.

title: Dual Age Estimation Framework Using PINN and Modified AlexNet with Grad-CAM Visualization: A Deep Learning Approach for Chronological and Biological(Apparent) Age Prediction
authors: Divilin Sweety D S, Abinaya K M, Ananthi S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Age estimation on the face has gained more relevance in healthcare, biometrics as well as human-computer interaction. The dual-AlexNet architecture learns parallel deep learning approximations that differentiate chronological age (calendar age) and biological indicators of physiological aging on parallel deep learning frameworks trained with fine-grained age annotations. Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) constraints are incorporated into the training loss to enforce biological plausibility of age predictions. Although the majority of available systems look at the chronological age; an increasing understanding is that biological age as a measure of physiological aging and not the time elapsed is of greater benefit in clinical decision-making. Our proposed dual-stream deep learning architecture concurrently predicts chronological and biological age based on faces. Two parallel variants of the AlexNet architecture are employed with the modification to age classification using discrete values (one as discrete) and predicting the biological age using weighted probability averaging (the other). In order to increase clinical trust and interpretability, we combine Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) visualization that identifies age-relevant parts of the face. The framework supports various age label formats by doing intelligent parsing and offers a REST API that can be production ready to enable easy integration with healthcare systems.

title: Design Optimization of a Dual-Cuff Supraglottic Airway for Enhanced Seal Performance
authors: Sudha S, Sushmitha Sree S, Shruthi Lashmi N, Sridharan P
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Supraglottic airway devices (SADs) are essential for maintaining the prolonged efficacy of a consistent airway, which is influenced by structural robustness and flow resistance, by preventing gastric reflux. Conventional (SADs) offer modest advancements in sealing dynamics, but they are limited by their inability to regulate downstream pressure and ensure proper sealing to prevent regurgitation. A previously designed external channel dual-cuff SAD enhanced sealing, but its disproportionate geometry induced displacement in stress-strain concentration and disruption of airflow. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> remodelled dual-cuff airway tube with an integrated gastric drainage channel (Design2) was evaluated through a computational analysis to compare the performance of the our previous design external-channel dual-cuff (Design1) and conventional SADs. The integrated channel model depicted significantly enhanced operational implications in functional outcomes, with almost an 85% reduction in peak pressure and 73% reduction in unstable laminar flow. Substantially, a 70% reduction in peak stress with improved uniform distribution of loads was observed compared to the external gastric channel dual-cuff approach. If implemented practically, these outcomes can help produce a significantly more resilient and clinically adaptive airway pathway. The outcomes depict that the revised geometry yields substantial improvements in mechanical endurance.

title: Embedded AI System Continuous Surveillance for Neurofibromatosis Using Wearable Bioimpedance and Nerve Signal Sensors
authors: Vadivelan M, Padmaja J, Bhuvaneswari R, Sajini Navab N
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Neurofibromatosis is a progressive neurological disorder that requires continuous observation to detect subtle changes in nerve and tissue characteristics at an early stage. The risks of neurofibromatosis range from cosmetic and functional impairments to potential malignant transformation of tumors, emphasizing the importance of timely diagnosis and intervention. Early identification of neurofibromatosis-related changes is crucial to prevent long-term complications and improve patient quality of life. This work proposes a wearable and non-invasive monitoring approach that focuses on tracking physiological variations over time and interpreting these patterns to assess potential risk levels. The proposed approach emphasizes intelligent signal interpretation and real-time assessment to support early abnormality identification without revealing underlying implementation details. Clinically meaningful outputs are provided to assist healthcare professionals in long-term monitoring and timely intervention, while prioritizing portability, patient comfort, and practical usability in everyday clinical environments.

title: Segmentation of Plasmodium Falciparum Infected Cell in Blood Smear Images Using Optimized U-Net
authors: R Saranya, A. Bakiya
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Malaria is a major global health concern, contributing to significant illness and mortality across many regions of the world. Every year a millions of cases reported, early detection and accurate diagnosis are critical for effective disease control and timely treatment. Malaria diagnosis has been regarded as the gold standard of the manual microscopic analysis of the blood smears. The manual microscopic examination of blood smears has been considered as the gold standard for malaria diagnosis. The identification of infected cell using microscope is highly challenging during mass screening in rural areas. Hence, the identification of infected cells in blood smear images requires the efficient computer assisted system. In this study, the camera was used to capture the images of the thin blood smear of Plasmodium Falciparum in microscope after staining the slides. The quality of collected blood smear images was enhanced by Histogram Equalization (HE) techniques. Further, the Honey Badger Optimization based U Net (HBA-UNet) was developed to segment the infected cells in thin blood smear images of Plasmodium Falciparum species. The performance of the developed segmentation model is compared with existing deep learning models such as Mobilenet, Efficientnet, and UNet. The results demonstrated that the dice coefficient of HBA based U Net model attains higher (96.07%) when compared to the deep learning models. Similarly, recall, precision, accuracy and IOU of HBA based UNet model achieves 90.93%, 91.26%, 98.77% and 91.24 % respectively, which are higher when compared to the other deep learning models. This finding reveals that the HBA-UNet model is efficient and accurate segmentation of Plasmodium Falciparum infected cell in the thin blood smear images.

title: A BERT-Enhanced Framework for Semantic-Aware Phishing Email Detection and Real-Time Analysis
authors: Piya Mondal, Sudakshina Mandal, Debrupa Pal, Rituparna Mondal, Sabari Kumar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The problem of phishing emails is still one of the most serious cyber threats. It calls for smarter and more reliable detection mechanisms. This paper proposes a deep learning-based approach using Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers for improving the understanding of contextual information within email text. The paper first constructs and preprocesses a phishing e-mail dataset, showing in detail the process of data cleaning and how to address class imbalance. In an attempt to establish a baseline, several traditional machine learning models, namely Logistic Regression, KNN, SVM, Random Forest and XGBoost were evaluated using TF-IDF features. Among these models, Logistic Regression and SVM achieved full precision, while Random Forest attained the maximum accuracy of 99.56%. Despite this strong performance, it is observed that TF-IDF cannot capture deeper semantic patterns which motivated shifting towards a transformer-based model. The BERT model demonstrated superior language understanding and achieved 99.47% accuracy and a precision score of 0.989. It was useful in practical real-time phishing e-mail detection to prepare the trained BERT model integrated into a RESTful API implemented in Flask and connected with a user-friendly web-based interface for live analysis of e-mails. Results show that though traditional models are effective, BERT’s contextual capabilities make it a more suitable choice for practical phishing email detection.

title: AI-Augmented FinTech Platforms for Real-Time Credit Risk and Supply Chain Financing in Smart Industries
authors: Swetha Sistla, Mohan Sankaran, Nagaraju Jooluri, Yahya Fikri, Shruthaalaxmi, Sonal Devesh
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: A combination of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and FinTech platforms has transformed the financial services sector, specifically, real-time credit risk evaluation and supply chain financing of smart industries. The conventional models of credit assessment, based on the use of fixed financial information and manual processing, are ineffective in capturing the dynamic nature of the modern-day industrial process. This paper conducts empirical research on AI-enhanced FinTech applications and utilizes machine learning (ML), natural language processing (NLP), and multi-modal industrial data, such as financial data, IoT sensor data, and supply chain data, to enable better predictive models and decision processes. The study compares several models, such as ensemble learning and deep neural networks, to predict credit risk and maximize the financing. Findings show that this is highly improved with AUC scores more than 0.88 and reduction in decision latency up to 70 percent showing quicker more information-oriented and context-sensitive risk management. It offers practical implications in the design of AI-based financial solutions, which will allow making smarter credit decisions and allocating working capital in intelligent industries more effectively, and it also notes that AI can transform industrial FinTech ecosystems.

title: Real-Time Patient Condition Prediction in Ambulances Using Explainable AI
authors: Prem Nanda Kishore, Mattaparthi Pardhasaradhi, Mallivalasa Jaideep, Kusumalatha Reddy, Padarthi Layateja, Ankur Saxena
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Emergency medical services must make high-stakes decisions under severe time pressure, often before a patient arrives at the hospital. Real-time prediction of patient condition in the ambulance can support earlier stabilization, resource allocation, and medication preparation. However, many high-performing machine learning models are opaque, which limits trust and adoption in safety-critical settings. This paper presents an end-to-end system for real-time patient condition prediction in ambulances using explainable AI (XAI). The pipeline ingests ambulance-collected data (demographics, vital signs, symptoms, medications, and attachments) via a Streamlit-based interface, processes it through a FastAPI backend, and serves risk predictions and explanations to a hospital dashboard. Gradient boosting (GBM) is used as the primary model, with L2-regularized logistic regression as an interpretable baseline. Models are trained and evaluated on publicly available critical-care datasets, primarily MIMIC-IV and eICU-CRD, after mapping ICU records to a prehospital schema. Explanations are generated using SHAP and LIME to provide both global and local interpretability. Experimental findings indicate that the GBM model provides superior discrimination and calibration compared with the logistic baseline, while remaining explainable through XAI techniques. We discuss system latency, domain shift from ICU to prehospital settings, and future directions for multimodal integration and federated learning.

title: Implementation of Artificial Intelligence Models for the Prediction of Plant Diseases
authors: Abhishek Pujari, Vinutha Asundi, Rashmi S
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Plant disease leads to major yield loss and compromises its quality, making early and accurate detection essential for sustainable farming. Conventional diagnostic methods such as ELISA and PCR are promising but not easily accessible to all farmers, because of its cost and time consuming. This study reviews how Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools—especially machine learning, deep learning, and image processing—can be used to predict and detect plant diseases earlier and more accurate. Tool like PlantVillage dataset analyses the captured leaf disease images, followed through multiple steps such as capturing, cleaning, segmenting, and extracting features. Theses gathered images were classified using techniques such as Support Vector Machines, K-means clustering, and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). Among these, newer CNN architectures like DenseNet-121, ResNet-50, and EfficientNetV2B2 showed, the highest accuracy, reaching above 98–99%. Complex image data are analysed effectively by deep learning, but yet challenges remain in handling unbalanced or limited datasets and variations in real field conditions. The findings of this study indicates that AI-based systems can support farmers by enabling quick and reliable disease diagnosis, achieving Zero Hunger, reducing pesticide use, and improving crop productivity. Further work should aim at developing larger, well-annotated datasets and simple mobile applications for practical use in the field.

title: An Optimization-Driven Framework for Refactoring Sequence Selection to Improve Software Maintainability
authors: Ritika Maini, Navdeep Kaur, Amandeep Kaur
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Most people concur that code smells indicate more serious issues with software system architecture. If these issues are not resolved, they accumulate throughout the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), increasing the complexity of the code and the expense of maintenance. Code smells can be eliminated through refactoring while maintaining the external functionality of the code. Its advantages, however, rely heavily on how effectively you select and arrange the refactoring procedures. In order to improve software maintainability, this study suggests an optimisation-based approach for proactively creating refactoring sequences. The suggested system predicts refactoring sequences using machine learning, meta-heuristic, and hybrid optimisation techniques after ranking smell-prone classes using a severity-driven index. This differs from conventional approaches, which only restructure when a chance arises. Extensive experimental evaluation of several open-source object-oriented systems shows notable improvements in maintainability and a notable reduction in refactoring work. Sequence-aware refactoring outperforms ad hoc and single-technique refactoring techniques, according to the results.

title: Ethical Hacking in the Age of Autonomous AI: A Governance-Centric Approach to Cyber Defense
authors: Swetha Sistla
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Autonomous and generative AI systems have turned cybersecurity into an adaptive environment in which attackers and defenders alike are becoming more algorithmic. Classical ethical hacking, which used to be based on human intuition and manual exploit trials, sees today’s artificially intelligent opponents able to learn and adapt in real time. In this paper, we introduce a Governance-Centric Ethical Hacking (GCEH) framework that bakes algorithmic accountability, explainability, and blockchain-enabled auditability into each phase of AI-driven penetration testing. Through governance encoded as code, the model allows red-team efforts to respect ethical and regulatory limits without sacrificing operational flexibility. Experiments conducted in simulated enterprise testbeds show that governance-integrated AI agents enhanced the vulnerability coverage by 40%, achieved a GCI value of 0.94, and elevated ETS from 0.62 to 0.76. Moreover, the number of non-compliance violations reduced by 70%, and the ability to reproduce across runs was 95% reproducible. These findings validate that integrating dynamic policy logic inside AI operations converts cybersecurity from a reactive defense to a proactive, explainable, and ethical discipline. The GCEH model then reframes the ethical hacking activity as reproducible, auditable, and governance-aligned responsible autonomy.

title: Hybrid Soft Computing Models for Real-Time Intrusion Detection in Industrial IoT Networks
authors: Shivendra Kumar Singh, Bassem Hassan Khader, Mouhamd Hashim, Ali Ihsan Alanssari, Riyam Adnan Hasan, Alaa Abdulahusin
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) has revolutionized manufacturing and critical infrastructure by enabling smart, connected, and automated operations. However, this increased connectivity also introduced significant cybersecurity challenges, and real-time, autonomous intrusion detection became a critical requirement. Traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) are unsuitable for heterogeneity, high velocity, and resource constraints commonly found in IIoT. This paper introduces the Hybrid Soft Computing Intrusion Detection System (HSC-IDS), which combines intelligent techniques to meet these requirements efficiently. The model includes a Genetic Algorithm (GA) to identify the best feature subsets, a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) model for accurate anomaly detection, and a Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) to handle uncertainty in decisions. GA enhances performance and decreases computational overhead through the selection of the most suitable features, while CNN-BiLSTM architecture tracks both spatial and temporal patterns across IIoT traffic. The fuzzy logic layer increases interpretability and resilience, as it allows the system to effectively cope with ambiguous and borderline instances. The suggested model is tested on various benchmark datasets, UNSW-NB15, ToN-IoT, and Edge-IIoTset. Experimental findings prove that HSC-IDS performs better than conventional and single-technique models in detection accuracy, false positive ratio, and processing delay. Furthermore, the light and modular architecture of HSC-IDS qualifies it for deployment in real-time IIoT settings, especially at the network edge or as part of fog computing infrastructure.

title: A Multimodal Artificial Intelligence Framework for predicting Periodontal Bone Loss and Inflammation using Imaging, Biomarkers and Behavioral Data
authors: Oorja Shanker, Santosh Martande, Swathi PV, Sharath Shetty, Anita Kulloli, Nomita Yein, Neha Nerkar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Microbial challenge, host response, systemic state, and behavioural factors like tobacco use all have an impact on periodontitis, a complex inflammatory disease. Conventional periodontal assessment techniques mainly analyse current tissue degradation and offer little information about how the disease may develop in the future. Artificial intelligence developments present chances to improve predicted, individualized periodontal care by integrating heterogeneous clinical data. A multimodal clinical decision support system based on artificial intelligence by combining radiographic imaging, intraoral photos, biochemical biomarkers, and patient-specific risk factors like tobacco use, oral hygiene habits, and metabolic disorders is proposed to predict periodontal bone loss and inflammation. This further contributes to individualized risk profiling. Longitudinal monitoring and early detection of patients at higher risk of disease progression are made possible by the integration of these many data sources. Such an approach maintains clinical judgment while supporting risk classification, individualized preventative interventions, and optimized maintenance routines.

title: A Study on Adaptive Deep Learning Models for Network Intrusion Detection in Heterogeneous Environments
authors: Tarak Hussain, Nara Sreekanth, K Kiran, Sandeep Kumar Rathore, Anandakumar Haldorai, C. Rajesh Babu
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: In modern heterogeneous network architectures composed of cloud, Internet of Things (IoT) and enterprise systems, the traditional IDS by virtue of the dynamic traffic, feature heterogeneity and unknown attacks cannot meet the high accuracy and adaptability. This study addresses the challenge by designing an Adaptive Deep Learning Model (AdaptiveDeepNIDS) which has heterogeneity-aware encoding, federated pretraining, and an uncertainty-based meta-controller that ensures the robustness of concept drift and distribution shift. The model causes the latent resolution of shared representations in several different feature spaces, adaptive parameters balancing to a new environment on a fly, the combination of supervised and unsupervised detection mechanisms. Experiments done on the CIC-IDS2017 dataset shows better performance with 99.12% accuracy, 99.0% precision, 99.3% recall and false positive rate of 0.40%, which outperforms 5 state of the art methods including federated and Transformer based baselines. The results of this paper confirm the usefulness of adaptive deep learning in a heterogeneous environment and offer a scalable, interpretable and privacy-preserving framework for the next generation of network intrusion detection.

title: Multi-Class Fault Type Classification Using Sequence Components and Wavelet Features with Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models and Neural Networks
authors: Santosh Kumar Chaudhary, Subash Khanal, Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Basanta Pancha, Jitesh Kumar Yadav
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: The growing complexity of modern power networks requires intelligent fault classification techniques capable of handling multiple fault conditions with high accuracy. Classifying faults in transmission lines with higher accuracy is very important to provide reliability to the power system along with a shorter fault clearance time. The presented work provides a reliable fault classification system in transmission lines using a hybrid approach in feature extraction, taking into consideration both the symmetrical components and time-frequency analysis using wavelet transform. The hybrid technique will be able to analyze imbalance in both transients. Some machine learning algorithms along with an artificial neural network are presented for their implementation in a unified platform. A comparative study will be performed based on standard parameters for fault classification accuracy. The simulation results show improved accuracy with lower misclassifications among related faults using ensemble machine learning algorithms.

title: Detecting Malicious Clients in Federated Learning
authors: Saikat Sinha Ray, Karthikeyan Periyasami
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Purpose: We look into a major problem in Federated Learning(FL): some devices, instead of helping, try to harm the system. These devices send misleading updates during training, which can weaken the overall model and thinking a real threat is safe. This is especially dangerous in areas like fraud detection or cybersecurity. Our aim is to find a way to spot and ignore these harmful updates so that the final model stays accurate and reliable.Survey: We explored different methods that researchers have suggested to spot bad actors in FL. We read about a few ideas like combining models in a smart way, spotting strange behavior using stats, and giving trust scores to each client. I wasn’t just looking at what they do, but also how well they work—especially when it comes to handling many clients and keeping data private.Key Findings of Research Gap: We noticed that most of the accurate detection methods are either too slow or can’t be used easily when there are many clients. On the flip side, faster methods tend to miss subtle or complex attacks.

title: Impact of AI-Assisted Online Compilers in Programming Education
authors: Amrit Poudel, Pratikshya Shrestha
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: In computer programming education, the choice of code compilers significantly influences student learning outcomes. This study compares legacy offline compilers, specifically Dev C++, with modern AI-assisted online compilers via the Process Feedback (PF) platform (processfeedback.org). A Randomized Controlled Trial (RCT) was conducted with first-semester undergraduates at Gandaki University, comprising a control group using Dev C++ and an experimental group using PF for lab sessions. The experimental group achieved substantially higher practical coding assessment score ((μ = 7.79) than the control group (μ = 6.29,p < 0.001), with a large effect size (Cohen’s d = 1.00), and demonstrated significantly greater theoretical improvement (Mann–WhitneyU =382.0,p =0.026). A strong positive correlation ((r =0.68,p < 0.001) was observed between coding proficiency and theoretical gains, suggesting that improved practical skills reinforce conceptual understanding. These findings support integrating AI-assisted compilation environments to enhance learning outcomes in C programming courses.

title: Next-Generation Smart Home System: A Research on AR-Enhanced Interfaces, Multimodal Interaction, and AI-Driven Personalization
authors: Angel, Saksham Malhotra, Priyanka Yadav, Sumit Kumar, Surbhi Vijh, Roop Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Smart home technology is changing quickly, and this has helped us understand the problems with current designs, such as fragmented system controls, lack of context, and static control which can’t adapt to different user settings and preferences. This study analyses at recent developments in three areas that overlap: augmented reality user interfaces, multimodal communication strategies, and AI-based personalisation. We look at 20 of these papers from the year 2022 to 2025 to find the most important ones and the problems that affect these fields. AR-based interfaces are 30–40% quicker than smartphone interfaces, and gesture recognition techniques that use the MediaPipe system are 88–95% accurate in the best conditions. Even with these changes, present systems are still limited by fixed learning models, sensitivity to the environment, and limited integration across domains. To solve these problems, we suggest a single framework that combines augmented reality overlays with spatial context, multiple modes of voice and gesture interaction for natural management, and a generative computing intelligent Multi-Armed Bandit (GenMAB) system for adaptive automation. GenMAB combines positive reinforcement training for making the best decisions with generative modelling for context-aware exploration. This lets you keep personalising without having to retrain the whole model. Our results show that user satisfaction and availability have improved significantly, energy optimisation has increased by 10–30%, and habit-tracking adherence has increased by more than 80%. This research not only provides a roadmap for the development of future-proof autonomous houses that are readily available, adaptable, and genuinely intelligent, but it also offers a thorough comprehension of current research trajectories.

title: Deployable CNN-Based System for Real-Time Aloe Vera Leaf Disease Monitoring
authors: Sakshi Koli, Anil Kumar Lamba, Puneet Banga, Nagendar Yamsani, Rohini Rohini
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The aloe vera plant leaf is renowned universal for its therapeutic possessions and finds applications in various industries, including pharmaceuticals and cosmetics, due to its numerous benefits. It is recognized for its soothing and healing properties, as well as its hydrating and rejuvenating effects. However, the quality and quantity of aloe vera plant leaves go down due to the various diseases present in the aloe vera leaf. So to detect these diseases deep learning approach is discussed in this article. The study indicates that CNN, despite having the fewest parameters ranging up to 278K as compared achieves the most efficiency in detecting the aloe vera plant leaf disease. The discussed CNN architecture outperformed the pre-trained named vgg16, inception, and Alexnet by providing an accuracy of 92.42 %, precision of 90.4%, recall of 88.32 and F1-score of 88.46. The work found that CNN is ideal for lightweight applications with lower computational demands and higher response time.

title: Implementation of Higher Order Sectorization Techniques for Efficient Resource Allocation in mmWave 6G Networks During Peak Demand
authors: Preeti Badhani, Nookala Venu, Prateek Srivastava, Deepak Upadhyay, Seema Sharma, Chetan Trivedi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: A study in this paper demonstrated a method using higher-order sectorization along with reinforcement learning (RL) for managing resource distribution in mmWave 6G networks under high-traffic conditions. By dividing a single cell into multiple subcells or "sectors" each with their own antenna array, higher order systems will improve frequency reuse and reduce co-channel interference. Additionally, RL will provide an intelligent way to determine how to distribute available bandwidth between sectors. Using simulations on synthetic traffic models to evaluate several metrics including throughput, fairness, delay and outage, it was found that the higher-order with RL systems performed significantly better than traditional three-sector systems at large user densities. Specifically, the average delay was reduced by approximately 25% and the outage probability was significantly decreased as well. In addition, the degree of fairness among users was also increased with the higher-order with RL system. Therefore, this study concludes that such resource management techniques are expected to be beneficial for supporting heavy traffics in dense environments in future 6G wireless networks.

title: Conversational Career Guidance System using LangChain and Agentic AI Framework
authors: Shivansh Pandey, Sandhya Avasthi, Aditya Tandan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The modern job market is changing incredibly fast due to new technology, and it's getting much harder for students and professionals to figure out what skills they need. The old way of giving career advice—like static lists of recommendations—just doesn't work well anymore because it can't keep up with complex career decisions. We designed and built an Intelligent Career Guidance System to solve this. It uses an agentic AI framework, essentially creating a smart, autonomous agent powered by the popular LangChain toolkit and the OpenAI API. This agent can chat naturally with a user to understand their specific skills, interests, and education, and then do a lot of sophisticated work behind the scenes. Our system can tap into live data and use a multi-step thinking process to come up with completely personalized career paths and pinpoint where a user's skills are lacking. We built the system using an agent-based setup, giving the AI specific "tools" (task-specific tools) so that it can provide high-quality, highly relevant advice. The result is an application that feels like a truly interactive and iterative counseling session. We found that the system is very effective: it gives users proactive, relevant guidance that significantly boosts their confidence in making smart career choices.

title:
Temperature-Invariant TMR in Fe
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Si/2L-WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /Fe
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Si Magnetic Tunnel Junctions from First-Principles Simulations
authors: Saisha Gandagatri, Shagufta Parveen Asif Akhtar, Sanskar Kumar Karn, Gadipalli Varun Sai Kumar, Aminul Islam, Md. Waseem Akram
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Magnetic Tunnel Junctions (MTJs) are among the most essential elements of modern spintronic devices and enable spin-polarization-controlled electron transport. In this work, we present a Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si/2L-WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si MTJ studied at the quantum level using QuantumATK. The major emphasis is put on TMR properties under different thermal conditions. The MTJ consists of a 2-layer WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> barrier placed between two ferromagnetic electrodes of Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si. We have calculated the spin-polarized transmission spectrum and conductance for parallel (P) and anti-parallel (AP) magnetic configurations at 50 K, 100 K, 200 K, and 300 K. The device demonstrates consistently high TMR values (up to 102.81%) that do not exhibit any noticeable variation throughout the investigated range of temperatures. This temperature-independent behavior proves the stability of the Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si/2L-WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si material stack. Conductance analysis demonstrates strong spin filtering due to strong spin polarization in Fe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si and pronounced spin-orbit coupling in WSe<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. Finally, some points have been discussed regarding realistic integration perspectives: fabrication compatibility, scalability, and thermal endurance. Based on these results, the proposed MTJ is suitable for stable and high-performance spintronic applications.

title: A Integrating Approach for Oral Cancer Detection using Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Salman Khan, Ranjeet Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Oral cancer is a prevalent and challenging disease characterized by a high mortality prevalence. Numerous diagnostic methods exist for oral cancer; yet, their efficacy in precisely detecting cancer cells is limited. This oral cancer presents a major and considerable concern to public health because to its substantial influence on death rates. Consequently, novel methodologies are necessary to enhance the identification of oral cancer. Deep learning algorithms provide an alternate technique that, via their advanced algorithms and image processing capabilities, plays a crucial role in cancer detection. The proposed study seeks to detect oral cancer at its onset, hence improving treatment outcomes and survival rates via deep learning methodologies. The goal of the research is to improve the accuracy of diagnosing oral cancer using deep learning architectures. The emphasis is on enhancing the efficacy of oral cancer detection by using innovative approach the Adaptive Class-Aware Feature Pruning (ACAFP) for feature selection algorithm and integrating algorithm with the deep learning classifier improve overall effectiveness. The study attains 97% accuracy in early oral cancer diagnosis via the sophisticated deep learning model VGG16, indicating enhanced patient outcomes and treatment efficacy.

title: A Lightweight Graph Contrastive Model for Cross Language Semantic Clone Detection
authors: M Jaithoon Bibi, M.Malathi, R.Subha, M.Suganthi, Vinitha Jeevanandham, R Gayathri
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The main challenge in cross language semantic code clone detection in software engineering exists owing to the syntax differences in various computer programming languages, resulting in difficulties in applying traditional code clone detection methods. Even though transformer models have shown promising results in code clone detection, they have a high computational overhead, thereby imposing a constraint on scalability for deployment in real world software development settings. In light of these requirements, a new approach named Lightweight Graph Contrastive Model (GCM) has been proposed for efficient semantic code clones across multiple computer programming languages. The proposed strategy models code using language independent program graphs constructed using Abstract Syntax Trees and control flow relationships to support robust semantic abstraction. A highly optimized Graph Neural Network with a small parameter set is employed to transform semantically similar code snippets from multiple computer programming languages into a common vector space using a contrastively learned objective to emphasize robust semantic abstraction with lower computational complexity. The proposed approach has demonstrated promising results on BigCloneBench, POJ 104, and the cross language CodeNet benchmarking dataset, with an overall F1 score of 96.1%, 92.3%, and 88.7%, respectively, with a significant reduction in computational latency of nearly 2x, while reducing overall parameters by 65% in the cross language semantic code clones to outperform the existing state of the art baseline GraphCodeBERT models.

title: IoT–Enabled Smart Pest Detection and Control System for Sustainable Agriculture
authors: Sridharan S, Dharani M, Esther Abraham, Deepthi Jenni Priya, Chemmozhi B.
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Agriculture continues to face serious problems with pest attacks that not only reduce the quality of crops but also re- duce the yield. Traditional pesticide-based control systems rely on chemical interventions, which negatively impact the soil fertility, human health and the ecological balance in the long term. The latest developments in Internet of Things (IoT) technologies have enabled low-cost environmental sensing and remote agricultural monitoring, thus providing new opportunities for automated pest detection. This paper presents an IoT-based Smart Pest Detection and Control System incorporating motion sensing, temperature- humidity sensor, wireless connectivity, cloud data recording and real-time ultrasonic repelling of pests. The suggested system is an autonomous system that constantly monitors the field conditions, detects the movement patterns of pests, and triggers a non-lethal ultrasonic repellent when needed. The system has been shown through experimental implementation in a controlled field setting to reduce considerably the amount of manual monitoring effort, enhance the accuracy of pest detection, and provide effective real- time response. The project aims to offer a low-cost, scalable, and ecologically sound substitute to the use of chemical pesticides by small and medium-sized farmers.

title: PVDSF: A Photovoltaic Generation Forecasting Network With Dynamic-Static Correlation Fusion on Endogenous and Exogenous Variables
authors: Tianze Deng, Zengni Zhang, Kai Zhang, Yuhan Dong
abstract: Predicting photovoltaic (PV) generation is essential for ensuring grid reliability, optimizing energy allocation, and promoting a green transition in the global energy structure. Previous PV power prediction models only focus on the temporal relationships within exogenous sequences (such as temperature, cloud cover, and humidity) or endogenous sequences (i.e., PV generation), neglecting the impact of exogenous factors on the vulnerable PV generation process. However, the complexity of the physical environment makes it challenging to accurately model the interaction between the two types of variables. To address this issue, we propose a PV generation forecasting network with dynamic-static correlation fusion between endogenous and exogenous variables to improve prediction accuracy. Specifically, we, respectively, encode the exogenous and endogenous variables into static and dynamic components, and accordingly design static and dynamic networks to learn the complex environment, fully capturing the impact of different exogenous components on PV generation. Subsequently, we integrate the dynamic-static enhanced representations through a dual network, achieving lightweight representation prediction. Our model significantly improves PV generation prediction accuracy, achieving state-of-the-art (SOTA) results across four real-world datasets, with average reductions of 12.87% in mean squared error (MSE) and 13.89% in mean absolute error (MAE) compared with competitive baselines. This advancement significantly enhances PV generation forecasting and fosters global sustainability.

title: Multiexpert Inference Model Based on Belief Rule Base Under Uncertainty for Complex System Performance Evaluation
authors: Qiangqiang He, Zhichao Feng, Zhijie Zhou, Changhua Hu, Zheng Lian
abstract: Performance evaluation of complex system (CS) is critical for ensuring their operational efficiency. Under limited monitoring data, incorporating expert knowledge becomes essential to achieve reliable evaluation results. However, the multiple uncertainties from the interfering monitoring data, subjectivity of expert judgments, and difficulty of quantifying critical performance states pose challenges to developing models integrating data with knowledge. To address these challenges, this article proposes a multiexpert inference model based on belief rule base (BRB-ME) for CS performance evaluation under uncertainty. In the proposed framework, multiple belief rule bases are embedded within a mixture-of-experts architecture to fuse multisource monitoring data, mitigating data uncertainty and avoiding rule explosion. Expert cognitive strength and prior knowledge are encoded into the beta distribution's shape parameters to obtain the reference values, enabling the probabilistic modeling of expert knowledge uncertainty. Moreover, single-proposition outputs are extended to the power set to explicitly represent both partial and complete ignorance, enhancing uncertainty characterization. An alternating iteration optimization strategy is further designed to optimize parameters across all BRB-ME components. Finally, by the case study on rocket structure performance inference, the BRB-ME model achieves an average root mean square error of 0.0065, validating the effectiveness of the BRB-ME evaluation results.

title: Research on Collaborative Optimization of Near-Field ISAC Systems Based on Multi-Waveguide Pinching Antenna Arrays
authors: Yuanhao Chang, Fenggan Zhang, Fengcaho Zhu, Jianwei Zhao, Ziwei Xiang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In this paper, a near-field integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) using a multi-waveguide pinching antenna system (PASS) is proposed, where discrete radiating particles are flexibly clamped along multiple dielectric waveguides to realize reconfigurable near-field beam focusing. To accurately characterize the underlying propagation mechanism, a spherical-wave channel model is established by jointly accounting for free-space distance-dependent attenuation, waveguide propagation loss, and phase accumulation. Based on this model, a constrained joint design problem is formulated to simultaneously optimize pinching antenna (PA) positions, communication-and-sensing power allocation, and quantized phase-only beamforming under total power, minimum power, minimum inter-element spacing, and finite-resolution hardware constraints. The resulting problem is a high-dimensional nonconvex mixed-integer optimization problem. To address it, a collaborative alternating particle swarm optimization-genetic algorithm (PSO-GA) framework is developed, where PSO is employed to optimize continuous variables, including PA positions and power allocation, while GA is used to search over discrete beamforming phases. In addition, feasibility is maintained through projection and power normalization, and a physics-informed initialization strategy based on MRT and weighted ISAC beamformers is introduced to accelerate convergence. Furthermore, exact channel gradients, a closed-form Fisher information matrix (FIM), and corresponding Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) expressions are derived to analytically characterize geometry-dependent sensing performance. Simulation results for an eight-waveguide, 64-element PASS operating at 28 GHz with 500 MHz bandwidth demonstrate that the proposed design achieves about 6.1 Gbps communication rate while maintaining about 0.01 m localization accuracy. The results also show that the proposed method attains a favorable communication-sensing tradeoff, converges faster than standalone heuristics, and remains robust under discrete configurability, waveguide attenuation, and imperfect channel state information (CSI).

title: Robust Model-Based Reinforcement Learning for Rocket Landing
authors: Donghe Chen, Di Wu, Lin Cheng, Shengping Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Autonomous vertical landing for reusable launch vehicles is a demanding control task constrained by nonlinear dynamics, strict precision requirements, and environmental uncertainties. Traditional numerical methods often face computational bottlenecks and reduced robustness under uncertainty, while current Model Based Reinforcement Learning (MBRL) approaches struggle with training instability caused by conflicting objectives. To address these issues, this paper proposes Harmony-TDMPC2, an MBRL frame work adapted for rocket landing. We introduce Harmony Loss, an adaptive weighting mechanism that balances conflicting optimization goals, effectively stabilizing the multi-objective learning process. Additionally, a multi-dimensional domain randomization strategy is integrated to improve generalization across physical parameter variations. All simulations are conducted using a 1:10 scale model of a reusable launch vehicle. Simulation results show that Harmony TDMPC2achieves an 82.1% landing success rate under randomized conditions, outperforming TDMPC2 and DreamerV3 by 23.4% and 28.7% respectively, with a computational efficiency 3.5 times faster than standard NMPC that supports real-time onboard deployment.

title: Machine Learning Based Early Warning Model for Mild Cognitive Impairment Risk in Elderly Population Using a Gradient Boosting Approach with Korean Longitudinal Study of Aging Data
authors: Ronghui Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Early detection of mild cognitive impairment represents a critical challenge in geriatric healthcare and smart elderly care systems. This study develops and validates a machine learning based risk prediction model for cognitive impairment using longitudinal data from the Korean Longitudinal Study of Aging. We analyzed 29,176 observations from 9,048 unique individuals with 177 features encompassing demographic, socioeconomic, health, and functional status indicators. Four machine learning algorithms were systematically compared, with XGBoost achieving the highest area under the receiver operating characteristic curve of 0.9661 and LightGBM achieving 0.9656. Feature importance analysis revealed that temporal orientation, educational attainment, and physical function measures were the most predictive variables. The proposed model provides a reliable tool for early identification of cognitive impairment risk in elderly populations, supporting proactive intervention strategies in smart elderly care frameworks.

title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Kazakh Question Generation: A Comparative Study of ChatGPT and Gemma
authors: Mukhtar Amirkumar, Meraryslan Meraliyev, Kamila Orynbekova, Assem Talasbek, Andrey Bogdanchikov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study compares two well-known big language models, ChatGPT and Gemma, to assess the quality of autonomously generated multiple-choice questions in Kazakh. We created questions using eight different configurations that varied by model, prompt language (English vs. Kazakh), source text language, and generation technique (direct vs. translation), all based on the historical book The Nomads by Ilyas Esenberlin. A 5-point Likert scale measuring fluency, clarity, and syntax was used to evaluate 48 questions from 577 Kazakh-speaking respondents in three surveys. The findings show that while ChatGPT slightly outperformed Gemma (3.95 vs. 3.86, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.15$</tex>), Kazakh-prompted questions scored much higher than Englishprompted questions (4.00 vs. 3.81, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p} \leq 0.01$</tex>). Translation was inferior to direct generation (3.96 vs. 3.85, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.10$</tex>). These results have significant implications for the development of educational technologies in the Kazakh language as well as lowresource language NLP applications.

title: Research on Mountainous Area Path Optimization Problem Based on Ant Colony Algorithm
authors: Yangyang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The optimization of the shortest path in mountainous areas represents a significant extension of traditional path-planning problems in complex mountainous terrains. Its objective is to identify the shortest path in a multidimensional path space. However, the task of finding the shortest path in mountainous regions is notably complex, and traditional algorithms often struggle when dealing with highly intricate environments. In light of the unique characteristics of mountain paths, this paper proposes a path optimization method based on the ant colony algorithm. The ant colony algorithm is capable of conducting a global search within complex mountainous terrains to determine the shortest path. Therefore, this study employs the ant colony algorithm for optimizing the shortest path in complex mountainous areas, and experimental validation has been conducted. The experimental results demonstrate that the ant colony algorithm can provide an effective solution for finding the shortest path in complex mountainous regions. This method holds significant implications for various applications, such as apprehending criminal suspects in mountainous areas, determining tourist routes, and facilitating travel for mountain residents.

title: Looking into Black Box Code Language Models
authors: Muhammad Umair Haider, Umar Farooq, A. B. Siddique, Mark Marron
abstract: Language Models (LMs) have shown applications for code-related tasks, leading to numerous code LMs being proposed recently. However, most works focus solely on benchmark performance, treating LMs as black boxes. A handful of studies explore attention mechanisms in code LMs, yet feed-forward layers — which contain two-thirds of parameters — remain underexplored. In this work, we investigate feed-forward layers in two state-of-the-art code LMs, Codegen-Mono and Polycoder, across three programming languages: Java, Go, and Python. We examine concept organization, editability, and layer-wise behavior under varying input contexts. Our findings reveal that lower layers capture syntactic patterns, while higher layers encode abstract semantics. We further show that targeted concept edits within feedforward layers preserve model performance. These results provide insights toward interpretability, debugging, and controllability of code LMs.
