title: Research on Monitoring and Recognition Technology of Dangerous Behaviors at Construction Sites of Proxy Construction Projects Based on Convolutional Neural Networks and Long Short-Term Memory Networks
authors: Peng Mu, Juan Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Construction sites of agency-built projects are characterized by complexity and dynamism. This study constructs a behavior monitoring and recognition system based on deep learning. The proposed solution integrates the CNNLSTM neural network model with the YOLOv5 object detection framework to form a multimodal behavior analysis technology system. Experimental results show that this technological combination has significant advantages in the safety scenarios of agency-built project construction. The CNN-LSTM model achieves high recognition accuracy in hazardous behavior identification, and YOLOv5 achieves high recognition accuracy in personal protective safety detection. This technological integration has opened up new pathways for improving the safety management efficiency of construction sites in agency-built projects.

title: A Method for Characterizing the Process Immunity Time Curve of Industrial Processes for Sensitive Users
authors: Chenrui Long, Yinghu Luo, Hanzhang Xu, Yongqiu Lu, Xuejing Gong, Zusong Tan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The process immunity time(PIT) curve is a crucial analytical tool for evaluating the immunity capacity of sensitive industrial users to voltage sags. However, in actual production, due to the difficulty in monitoring process parameters and the limited cooperation of users, the accurate drawing of this curve often faces difficulties. Therefore, this paper proposes a method for constructing a process immunity time curve model for typical industrial processes under the condition of missing or incomplete monitoring data. Firstly, based on the system structure of typical industrial processes and the voltage sag immunity time constant of sensitive equipment, the process immunity time is derived. Then, through empirical formulas and quadratic Newton interpolation methods, the process immunity time curves under the conditions of no monitoring data and only a small amount of monitoring data are obtained. Finally, through the analysis of multiple actual industrial processes in a cigarette manufacturing factory, the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method are verified.

title: Bi-objective UAV Route Planning for Medicine Delivery: Balancing Profit and Risk
authors: Zeyang Li, Xuting Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: As urban medicine delivery demands surge, Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) has emerged as a pivotal logistics tool. This study investigates a bi-objective route-planning approach to harmonize economic profitability with operational safety. We propose a mathematical model that accounts for dedicated and standard delivery modes and enforces payload constraints, ensuring the physical feasibility of flight trajectories. One of our primary innovations is how we combine distinct risk dimensions into a unified safety index that serves as a foundational element of the routing logic. To address the intricate decision-making this entails, we developed an enhanced Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm III (ENSGA-III). When applied to the Shanghai geographic landscape, the model demonstrated its value by generating a diverse set of Pareto-optimal trajectories. These outcomes empower stakeholders with a reliable framework for evaluating trade-offs between profit and risk.

title: Dual-Stream Feature Fusion Network for Complex Surface Defect Detection
authors: Suyu Li, Ronghua Lu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Low contrast and complex texture backgrounds remain major obstacles for industrial defect detection algorithms. We propose a dual-stream architecture based on YOLO11 that processes texture and structural information through separate pathways with edge priors. Our Texture-Structure Block (TSBlock) uses large-kernel convolutions for texture capture and learnable edge-aware operations for boundary enhancement, with features combined through a dynamic gating mechanism. We also reweight the loss function to prioritize localization accuracy in industrial settings. On the NEU-DET dataset, our method achieves $77.4 \% \mathrm{mAP}$ @ 50 while maintaining real-time inference speed. The results demonstrate that the proposed dual-stream approach can substantially improve detection in low-contrast scenarios.

title: LCMP: Distributed Long-Haul Cost-Aware Multi-Path Routing for Inter-Datacenter RDMA Networks
authors: Dong-Yang Yu, Yuchao Zhang, Xiaodi Wang, Jun Wang, Wenfei Wu, Haipeng Yao, Wendong Wang, Ke Xu
publish: Proceedings of the 21st European Conference on Computer Systems
abstract: None

title: Disturbance Rejection Control Strategy for Suspension System of 12/8 Single Winding Bearingless Switched Reluctance Motor
authors: Kai Xie, Ye Yuan, Shihong Ding, Wei Xu, Fan Yang
abstract: In 12/8 single winding bearingless switched reluctance motor (SWBSRM), the performance of the suspension control system is reduced due to the disturbance caused by change of torque current since the torque system and suspension system share the same winding and mechanical structure. Hence, it is necessary to study a control strategy with strong disturbance rejection capability. This article proposes a disturbance rejection control strategy for the suspension system of 12/8 SWBSRM, which is based on the direct suspension force control strategy (DSFCS) and an adaptive sliding mode states observer (ASMSO). The DSFCS directly control the suspension forces, therefore the suspension system of 12/8 SWBSRM can be constructed to a second-order state equation. On the basis of the state equation, the ASMSO can be designed and used to observe the coupling disturbances and other unmodeled disturbances. In addition, the observed disturbances are compensated to the given suspension force through a feedforward channel. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy for suspension system is validated by simulations and experiments.

title: HSRNet: Lightweight Remote Sensing Small Object Detection for Multi Scale and Dense Scenes
authors: Boxiang Xie, Yao Zhang, Lingfeng Lin, Wei Zhang, Jie Zhang, Honggang Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing images often exhibit characteristics such as significant scale variation, dense distribution, and complex background textures, which lead to the obscuring of small target features and result in missed and false detections in occlusion scenarios. To address this, we propose a lightweight remote sensing dense small target detection network, HSRNet. This method introduces a Sparse Adaptive Attention Enhancement Module (SASR Transformer) in the deep features to strengthen effective context and suppress background interference. An Asymmetric Feature Aggregation Module (AFAM) is designed at the neck stage to improve multi-scale feature aggregation quality. Additionally, the detection head incorporates hypergraph relation modeling to construct higher-order semantic topology and better distinguish adjacent dense targets through gated distribution. Experimental results on the DOTA1.0, DIOR, and NWPU VHR-10 datasets demonstrate that HSRNet achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{50}$</tex-math></inline-formula> scores of 0.712, 0.864, and 0.936, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{50:95}$</tex-math></inline-formula> scores of 0.435, 0.662, and 0.611. Notably, in terms of small object detection (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$AP_{S}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), compared with other state-of-the-art models, our method achieves significant improvements of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.5\%--14.7\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.3\%--14.2\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6.6\%--27.0\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> on these datasets, respectively. In terms of efficiency, the model has approximately 3.5M parameters, a computational cost of about 9.0G FLOPs, and achieves real-time inference at 58.4 FPS on the Jetson Orin Nano, demonstrating its deployment potential in resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Closing the Proof-of-Stake Security Gap: A Signature-Based Defense Against Malicious Validators in Long-Range Attacks
authors: Zhanwen Chen, Yannan Li, Willy Susilo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Long-range attacks pose a significant threat to the integrity of Proof-of-Stake (PoS) blockchains by enabling adversaries to reconstruct an alternative chain history embedded with fraudulent transactions. These attacks can deceive honest participants into accepting a maliciously crafted branch as the canonical chain. While Key Evolving Signature (KES) schemes are widely adopted to mitigate such threats, they typically rely on the assumption that validators behave honestly. In this work, we challenge this assumption by demonstrating how a malicious validator can exploit inherent limitations in existing KES-based mechanisms to mount a successful long-range attack. To address this critical vulnerability, we introduce a novel cryptographic construction that combines one-time signatures with commitment schemes. Our approach imposes constraints on the signing capabilities of validators, thereby significantly reducing the feasibility of long-range attacks. We provide rigorous formal security proofs to substantiate the robustness of our scheme and conduct a comprehensive performance evaluation. The results show that our solution is both computationally and storage efficient, making it a practical and scalable defense mechanism for real-world PoS blockchain deployments.

title: Optimized Gene Selection Using Nomadic People and Salp Swarm Algorithms for Cancer Detection
authors: Sadyaa Fahad Jabar, M. A. Balafar, Ali Jameel Hashim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Accurately detecting cancer through gene expression analysis is crucial for early diagnosis and effective treatment. However, gene expression data's high dimensionality and redundancy pose significant challenges, such as overfitting and computational inefficiency. To address these issues, we propose a hybrid feature selection framework that integrates a filter-wrapper approach with swarm intelligence for optimized gene selection. The proposed method utilizes the Nomadic People Optimizer (NPO) in conjunction with Mutual Information (MI) to identify a relevant subset of genes from high-dimensional datasets. An optimized Support Vector Machine (SVM) is employed to further enhance classification accuracy, with its hyperparameters fine-tuned using an enhanced Salp Swarm Algorithm (SSA) incorporating a crossover operator. This hybrid approach not only reduces the search space but also mitigates overfitting by leveraging the exploration and exploitation capabilities of the NPO and SSA. Experimental results on five cancer gene expression datasets-lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD), breast cancer (GSE2034), glioblastoma multiforme (GBM), ovarian cancer (GSE2109), and colorectal adenocarcinoma (COAD)-demonstrate that the proposed NPO-SSVM achieves classification accuracies ranging from 91.25% to 97.02% with AUC-ROC values between 0.85 and 0.97. The framework achieves an average feature reduction of 51% while outperforming state-of-the-art methods (GA, PSO, GWO, SSA) by 3-12% in classification accuracy and 15% in computational efficiency. These findings confirm that NPO-SSVM provides a robust and efficient solution for gene selection in cancer detection, offering significant advancements for personalized medicine and early diagnosis.

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Classification for Consumer Electronics Using DenseNet and Grad-CAM++
authors: Hafsa Ahmad, Nauman Ali Khan, Nabeela Bibi, Mudassar Ali Khan, Ikram Ud Din, Kamran Ahmad Awan, Ahmad Almogren
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a progressive eye disorder that may lead to permanent blindness when it is not treated. Nevertheless, the issue of early recognition is a problem since the retinal assessment takes a lot of time, and ophthalmologists are in short supply in most places. The paper fulfills this urgent requirement by suggesting a light-weight deep learning architecture to classify DR, namely, in the consumer electronics domain. The model combines DenseNet models and Grad-CAM++ to enhance both interpretation and extraction of features. It involves three dense net models that are used to label retinal images, D121- [M1], D169- [M2], and D121- [M3] to classify APTOS-2019 retinal image samples, 44570 sample images of 5 levels of DR. The framework produces detailed heatmaps which indicate significant parts of the retina which affect classification through incorporation of Grad-CAM++. The design is made to comply with the requirements of consumer devices with a focus on efficiency and flexibility. The steps involved in preprocessing are image resizing to 224x224 pixels and data augmentation to enhance strength. D121-[M3] was the best performing of the models; it received 95.63 percent training accuracy, 0.9921 precision, 0.9930 recall, and a 0.9925 F1-score.

title: Hybrid Poisoning Attacks Leveraging Fake Identities in Cross-Device Federated Learning-Edge Consumer IoT Networks
authors: Xiong Xiao, Zhuo Tang, Yingjie Song, Jiawei Tan, Xin Wang, Lin Chen, Zhihong Liang, Kenli Li
abstract: In federated learning (FL)-enabled edge Consumer IoT (CIoT) networks, multiple participants collaboratively train a joint model by sharing local updates with the server while preserving raw data locality. Although this paradigm offers inherent privacy advantages, the lack of verifiable trust in cross-device communication renders FL systems highly susceptible to poisoning attacks. In such attacks, adversaries covertly inject poisoned samples into the training pipeline to manipulate the joint model’s behavior in a targeted manner. A more severe yet often impractical threat is collusion among multiple compromised honest participants, which incurs high cost and limited scalability in massive-scale FL deployments. Consequently, an adversary with the ability to exert significant poisoning influence at low cost remains a critical yet underexplored vulnerability. To address this gap, this work devises a novel hybrid poisoning attacks strategy leveraging fake identities, termed FIHA. Specifically, the attacker poisons local samples and implements covert hybrid attacks that integrate label flipping attacks and backdoor poisoning attacks to complete more misclassifications of target samples, while ensuring minimal disruption to non-target tasks. Additionally, due to the vulnerability that participants in the FL network are easily disconnected, attackers can simulate multiple fake identities to participate in the global update with a more significant aggregation effect. Notably, FIHA achieves broad poisoning coverage with significantly reduced adversarial resources, eliminating the need for costly collusion with honest participants. Extensive tests substantiate that FIHA surpasses state-of-the-art across various performance evaluation metrics, even with a robust defense strategy employed in the system.

title: Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Using ECG via A Time-Frequency Embedding Pre-trained Model
authors: Junhao Lin, Guanhua Ren, Changhong Jing, Hongyan Wu, Yanyan Shen, Bingcheng Zhao, Kexuan Liu, Baiying Lei, Yi Pan, Kim-Fung Tsang, Shuqiang Wang
abstract: Perioperative cardiovascular events are a major threat to patient safety and constitute one of the leading causes of mortality worldwide. Traditional preoperative risk assessment tools often fail to detect subclinical abnormalities, resulting in missed diagnoses and underestimated risk. Electrocardiography (ECG), a widely used non-invasive modality, offers valuable information for preoperative cardiovascular risk assessment. This study proposes a Time–Frequency Complementary Fusion Network (TFCFN) for ECG-based automated assessment of preoperative cardiovascular risk. The model employs a dual-branch architecture: the time branch utilizes a large pre-trained time series model to capture dynamic ECG features, while the frequency branch combines discrete Fourier transform (DFT) and dilated convolution to extract rhythm and spectral energy features, which are then adaptively integrated by a gated fusion mechanism for automated multi-label prediction of cardiovascular risk. Results on four public datasets and one real-world clinical dataset demonstrate that TFCFN achieves excellent generalization and robustness. In addition, an adversarial debiasing mechanism is introduced to mitigate potential fairness issues, effectively reducing gender bias with negligible impact on overall performance. These findings highlight the potential of TFCFN as a reliable tool for perioperative cardiovascular risk stratification in clinical practice.

title: Suppressing Gradient Conflict for Generalizable Deepfake Detection
authors: Ming-Hui Liu, Harry Cheng, Xin Luo, Xin-Shun Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Robust deepfake detection models must be capable of generalizing to ever-evolving manipulation techniques beyond training data. A promising strategy is to augment the training data with online synthesized fake images containing broadly generalizable artifacts. However, in the context of deepfake detection, it is surprising that jointly training on both original and online synthesized forgeries may result in degraded performance. This contradicts the common belief that incorporating more source-domain data should enhance detection accuracy. Through empirical analysis, we trace this degradation to gradient conflicts during backpropagation which force a trade-off between source domain accuracy and target domain generalization. To overcome this issue, we propose a Conflict-Suppressed Deepfake Detection (CS-DFD) framework that explicitly mitigates the gradient conflict via two synergistic modules. First, an Update Vector Search (UVS) module searches for an alternative update vector near the initial gradient vector to reconcile the disparities of the original and online synthesized forgeries. By further transforming the search process into an extremum optimization problem, UVS yields the uniquely update vector, which maximizes the simultaneous loss reductions for each data type. Second, a Conflict Gradient Reduction (CGR) module enforces a low-conflict feature embedding space through a novel Conflict Descent Loss. This loss penalizes misaligned gradient directions and guides the learning of representations with aligned, non-conflicting gradients. The synergy of UVS and CGR alleviates gradient interference in both parameter optimization and representation learning. Experiments on multiple deepfake benchmarks demonstrate that CS-DFD achieves state-of-the-art performance in both in-domain detection accuracy and cross-domain generalization.

title: Electroless-Plated Gold Nanolayer for Low Temperature Copper Hybrid Bonding
authors: Byeongchan Go, Hoogwan Lee, Keiyu Komamura, Takanori Kishida, Sarah Eunkyung Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: This study investigates the deposition and characterization of electroless-plated gold (Au) nanolayers for low-temperature copper (Cu) hybrid bonding applications. A 0.7 μm-thick thermally grown SiO₂ layer on 8-inch Si wafers was coated with a 50 nm Ti adhesion layer and 1 μm Cu layer, followed by electroless Au plating to form ~7 nm nanolayers. Prior to plating, samples underwent wet pretreatment, and post-deposition analyses included X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction (GIXRD), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), and atomic force microscopy (AFM). The electroless-plated Au exhibited predominantly metallic characteristics with minimal Cu oxidation and conformally covered the underlying Cu, achieving an average surface roughness (Rq) of 3.21 nm. Die bonding at 200 °C and 15 MPa demonstrated successful Cu–Au interface formation, and shear strength of 14.9 MPa showed mechanical integrity. Furthermore, selective deposition of Au on patterned Cu pads surrounded by SiO₂ was achieved, and its potential as a cost-effective solution for metal nanolayer formation in Cu hybrid bonding technologies.

title: Quantum key recovery attack on post-quantum cryptosystems
authors: Yaru Wang, Jianmei Liu
abstract: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC), designed to resist quantum attacks, has received significant attention. Băaetu et al. (EUROCRYPT 2019) found that most lattice-based PKEs follow the same design called meta-cryptosystem, and analyzed the security of meta-cryptosystems under key reuse by mounting a quantum key recovery under chosen-ciphertext attacks (KR-CCA). However, Băaetu et al.’s quantum KR-CCA requires the meta-cryptosystem without ciphertext compression, which is used by CRYSTALS-Kyber to reduce the size of ciphertexts. Developing a quantum KR-CCA against lattice-based PKEs with ciphertext compression, e.g., CRYSTALS-Kyber, is left as a problem by Băaetu et al. (EUROCRYPT 2019). This paper presents an efficient quantum KR-CCA against RLWE/MLWE-based PKEs with ciphertext compression. In particular, we first translate the key recovery problem into a quantum LWE problem, and consider the case of the errors follow centered binomial distribution for quantum LWE problem. Next, we define the meta-cryptosystems with the compression/decompression function under key reuse. Equipped with the aforementioned new algorithm for the quantum LWE problem with centered binomial distribution, we give a quantum KR-CCA for the redefined meta-cryptosystems. Finally, we demonstrate the application of our quantum KR-CCA to NewHope and Kyber and analyze their time complexity.

title: Fluid Antennas Meet Intelligent Surfaces: Security Analysis of NOMA Systems Under Hardware Impairments
authors: Tuo Wu, Jianchao Zheng, Xiazhi Lai, Maged Elkashlan, Hyundong Shin, Naofal Al-Dhahir
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The revolutionary convergence of fluid antenna systems (FAS) and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) creates unprecedented opportunities for secure wireless communications, yet the practical implications of hardware impairments on this promising combination remain largely unexplored. This paper investigates the security performance of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) systems when fluid antennas (FAs) meet intelligent surfaces under realistic hardware constraints. We develop a comprehensive analytical framework that captures the complex interplay between adaptive spatial diversity, intelligent signal reflection, and hardware-induced distortions in short-packet communications. Through novel piecewise linear approximations and block-correlation models, we derive tractable expressions for average secure block error rate (BLER) that reveal fundamental performance limits imposed by hardware impairments. Our analysis demonstrates that while the synergy between FAs and intelligent surfaces offers remarkable degrees of freedom for security enhancement, practical hardware imperfections create performance ceilings that persist regardless of spatial diversity gains. The theoretical framework exposes critical design trade-offs between system complexity and achievable security performance, showing that hardware quality becomes a decisive factor in realizing the full potential of FAS-RIS architectures. Extensive simulations validate our analytical insights and provide practical design guidelines for implementing secure NOMA systems that effectively balance the benefits of fluid-intelligent cooperation against the constraints of realistic hardware limitations.

title: Constitutional Challenges in AI-Driven Public Health Emergency Management in India
authors: Deval Vashishtha, Bhupnesh Kumar
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The speed at which AI is being applied for managing public health emergencies has been studied, particularly for surveillance, predictive modelling, and automatic triage. There were many examples of AI based emergency responses developed and implemented in India for the purpose of responding to the COVID-19 pandemic under emergency laws. These emergency responses allowed states to quickly respond to an emergency situation by providing them with greater capability, but also raised numerous constitutional issues related to privacy, equity, due process, and democracy. The purpose of this article is to provide a conceptual analysis of the role of AI in public health governance, using the framework of the constitution. This study will be done through the lens of Indian case law, international standards for human rights, and comparative regulations used in other countries to identify deficiencies in the legislative protection, institutional oversight, and public participation in the development and implementation of AI systems. This study provides a rights-based approach to governance grounded in the constitutional principles of legality, proportionality, and accountability to ensure that the application of AI during emergencies does not compromise the value of democracy.

title: Stridesync: A Multisensory Framework for Movement Monitoring and Response
authors: Smritti Bala R, Deborah, Pushpa B
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Accurate monitoring of biomechanical parameters with precision is essential in rehabilitation and mobility training to facilitate effective recovery, prevent reinjuries. Conventional walkers primarily provide mechanical support and lack the ability to yield quantify posture, grip stability, or gait coordination. Therefore, the development of the smart walker system, Stridesync, is designed to enhance rehabilitation through real-time sensing and feedback. The system integrates force sensors mounted on the walker handles, with an accelerometer module to monitor variations in motion and assess the stability of an individual's locomotion. A haptic unit provides feedback in the form of vibration to the user when walking assistance is active and deviation in posture or an imbalance occurs. Governed by a microcontroller that processes sensor data and transmits the output both visually and tactually via a digital display interface for therapist's evaluation, this prototype continuously monitors biomechanical changes, effectively corrects posture, and facilitates safe movement training. Therefore, the Smart Walker constitutes an economical, sensor-enabled rehabilitative assistive device.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Human Embryo Developmental Stages from Time-Lapse Imaging
authors: Carolina Jennifer. S, Saranya S, Chandana Mohanty, Remya Raj
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Although IVF success rates have remained inconsistent and largely reliant on subjective morphological embryo evaluation, time-lapse imaging technology emerged with prospects for objective morphokinetic analysis based on embryo developmental stage evaluation. Analyzing morphokinetics with time-lapse imaging technology in this study aims to automate embryo development stage assessment into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}$</tex> distinct morphokinetic phases using computer vision algorithms on time-lapse imaging videos. The proposed deep learning model is based on a ResNet-50 neural network serving as a vision embedding model pre-trained on an image recognition dataset called ImageNet, focusing on spatial image representation based on individual image frames rather than vision based solely on image elements. The model accuracy in classifying embryo developmental phases using a transform model focused on self-attention analysis is tested with a classweighted focal loss function to cope with class imbalance in a standard IVF embryo developmental stage assessment dataset with a total of 704 embryo developmental phases labeled in a publicly accessible online competition. The individual model performance accuracy in assessing embryo developmental phases can be measured by accuracy of 0.6359 and balanced accuracy of 0.5359. The individual model performance can be measured by macro-average metrics including precision of 0.5315, recall of 0.5359, and F1-score of 0.5273 with Cohen's Kappa statistic of 0.5955 with a level of being in moderate agreement. The model performance in assessing embryo developmental phases in early frequently observed phases such as tPNa with F1-score of 0.9004 and receiver operating characteristic curve accuracy of 0.9924, showing better performance in frequently observed developmental phases.

title: Soft Computing Techniques for Real-Time Financial Fraud Detection in Online Transactions
authors: Sanjay Padav, Turki Meften Saad, Taha Raad Al-Shaikhli, Iyad Waad, Sadiq Juma, Ahmad Fadhil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of digital payment systems and online financial services, the risk of financial fraud has considerably increased. Traditional rule-based systems are often not sufficient for detecting detailed and evolving patterns of fraud in real-time. To deal with such problems, this paper will suggest the application of soft computing methods - fuzzy logic, artificial neural networks (ANNs), support vector machines (SVM), and genetic algorithms (GA) - to come up with intelligent and strong systems of fraud detection in electronic transactions. The suggested system is based on the idea that learnability, flexibility, and tolerance to imprecision of soft computing methods are used to process transaction records and detect outliers of fraudulent action. The hybrid system is created by incorporating the neural networks to perform the pattern recognition, the use of fuzzy logic to manage the uncertainty, and tuning the parameters by genetic algorithms. The given system is trained and tested on real-life sets of financial transactions with much consideration to the unbalanced distribution of classes and the aspects of performance needs in real-time. Results of the experiments show that the model developed works better than the traditional machine learning models in terms of detection accuracy, precision, recall, and response time. The hybrid model has been effective in identifying the known and new types of fraud, which indicates that the model can be used as an efficient solution in preventing financial fraud in real-time. This study highlights the significance of soft computing in developing intelligent, scalable, and adaptive fraud detection systems capable of evolving with changing threat landscapes in the fintech ecosystem.

title: Evolutionary Neural Networks for Mental Health Monitoring in Digital Therapeutics Platforms
authors: Koushik Chakraborty, Hussana Johar R B, Saleh Naji Sreseh, Jamal K. Abbas, Liwaa Ali Hussein, Anurag Tewari
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Mental health disorders have become a growing global concern, necessitating the development of intelligent, scalable, and personalized monitoring systems. Digital therapeutics (DTx) platforms offer an innovative solution by delivering real-time, software-driven interventions through mobile and wearable devices. However, their effectiveness depends largely on accurate, adaptive analysis of complex, noisy behavioral data. This paper presents a novel approach utilizing Evolutionary Neural Networks (ENNs) for mental health monitoring within DTx platforms. ENNs combine the learning power of neural networks with the global optimization capabilities of evolutionary algorithms, enabling automatic adaptation to individual behavioral baselines and improving generalization across diverse populations. The proposed architecture processes multimodal data from sources such as speech, facial expressions, physiological signals, and mobile usage patterns. A simulated study and experimental validation using public mental health datasets demonstrate that ENNs outperform traditional deep learning models in classification accuracy, adaptability, and computational efficiency.

title: Impact of AI-Powered Recommendations on Consumer Satisfaction and Buying Behaviour in E-Commerce
authors: Karthick K K, Lakshmi K K, V. Ganesan, Arul Kumar Natarajan, M. Sivaraman
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has transformed digital commerce, with AI-powered recommendation systems significantly shaping consumer behaviour. These systems guide product discovery, influence purchasing decisions, and enhance overall consumer satisfaction, making them crucial for businesses aiming to improve engagement and remain competitive. This study aims to examine the relationship between brand and platform loyalty driven by AI recommendations, the influence of AI recommendations on impulse buying, and consumer satisfaction with AI-powered recommendation systems. By analysing these factors, the study provides insights into how AI personalization affects consumer responses in e-commerce. A quantitative research design was adopted, collecting data from 211 online consumers with prior experience using AI recommendations. A structured questionnaire with a five-point Likert scale captured information on loyalty, impulse buying, and satisfaction. Data were analysed using SPSS, and multiple linear regression tested the hypothesized relationships. The results indicate a statistically significant model, showing that both brand and platform loyalty and AI-driven impulse buying positively affect consumer satisfaction, with brand and platform loyalty being the strongest predictor. This highlights the importance of consistent, relevant, and reliable AI personalization in fostering trust and engagement. The study concludes that AI-powered recommendation systems not only stimulate immediate purchases but also strengthen long-term consumer satisfaction and loyalty. These findings offer practical guidance for e-commerce platforms and marketers to optimize AI-driven strategies while maintaining a consumer-focused approach.

title: Scalable Financial Strategy Validation using Distributed Data Architectures
authors: Pramath Parashar
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The highly competitive nature of financial markets necessitates robust methods for validating automated trading strategies. This paper presents an approach to build an efficient and extensible backtesting platform leveraging distributed data processing tools. Addressing the substantial computational demands of historical market data, the implemented system utilizes a big data framework for scalable storage and rapid processing of time series. Beyond validating trading strategies, the architecture supports potential extensions for real-time operations and enhanced functionalities.

title: Dielectric Performance and Reprocessability of Boronic-Ester Vitrimeric Epoxy with Nanoscale Phase Separation_supp1-3685648.docx
authors: Liang Zou
abstract: Epoxy resins are widely employed as insulating materials in ultra-high-voltage (UHV) power equipment thanks to their excellent electrical properties, yet their permanently cross-linked networks are insoluble, infusible and difficult to recycle, and the electrical behavior of their dynamic-bonded counterparts remains poorly understood. Here, Bisphenol A diglycidyl ether (BADGE) was co-cured with a boronic-ester diamine and a flexible monoamine to generate a vitrimer network (EP-NBN) featuring 10 nm hard/soft periodic domains. The electrical behavior of EP-NBN was systematically evaluated at 20 ℃ to 60 ℃: Dielectric analysis and Havriliak–Negami fitting show that increasing temperature weakens interfacial polarization while activating local boronic-ester–related relaxation. The fitted apparent dc conductivity (σdc) decreases with temperature, accompanied by reduced low-frequency dielectric loss, due to diminished electrical contrast between hard and soft phases. Space-charge peaks at both electrodes decrease in magnitude and broaden with temperature. The current density-electric field (J-E) curves evolve from a two-regime lnJ-E behavior consistent with field-assisted interfacial hopping to an approximately single-slope behavior at 60 ℃. EP-NBN shows high AC breakdown strength (38.32 kV/mm) and retains 69.2 % of this value after reprocessing, outperforming the non-dynamic EP-ref. EP-NBN can also self-heal surface scratches and breakdown channels at elevated temperature. DFT calculations indicate a bipolar localization tendency in boronic-ester units, where electrons preferentially localize near boron sites and holes near O/N sites, providing a microscopic basis for interfacial trapping behavior associated with boronic-ester units, while the wide band gap (7.3 eV) supports the high intrinsic insulation level of the network. Overall, the microphase separation + boronic-ester dynamic bonds strategy enables high insulation performance together with reprocessability and self-healing.

title: CORE: Common Random Reconstruction for Distributed Optimization with Provable Lower Total Communication Costs_supp1-3677732.pdf
authors: Cong Fang
abstract: In this paper, we propose a new compression technique named Common randOm REconstruction (CORE), which can be used to compress the information transmitted between agents to reduce the total communication costs (TCCs) without other strict conditions. Previous communication compression techniques cannot ensure the improvement of TCCs due to the additional communication rounds caused by information distortion. CORE projects the vector-valued information to a low dimensional one through common Gaussian random variables and reconstructs the information with the same random noises after communication. We design varieties of CORE-based algorithms in different problem settings, including linear models, general convex optimization, and non-convex optimization, achieving provably lossless communication rounds and extremely lower TCCs. Specifically, for linear models, the CORE-based algorithm can encode the gradient vector into a lower constant dimension without increasing the number of communication rounds. For general convex and non-convex optimization problems, we characterize the TCCs by the effective dimension and prove the improvement beyond previous methods.

title: DTBA for Smart Grid Optimization: A Case Study on Loss Reduction and Voltage Improvement in Distribution Networks
authors: Yam Krishna Poudel, Asmita Rijal, Rashik Kumar Badgami, Nava Raj Karki
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This research paper focus on novel Driving Training-Based Algorithm (DTBA) for optimal placement and sizing of distributed generation (DG) solar PV and shunt capacitors. DTBA is used in radial distribution networks to minimize power losses and improve voltage profiles. The increasing complexity of power distribution systems, coupled with rising electricity demand all over the world and renewable energy integration, necessitates advanced optimization techniques. Classical methods Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) have limitations in convergence speed and solution accuracy. This research applies DTBA, a human-inspired metaheuristic algorithm, to solve the nonlinear optimization problem of DG and capacitor placement. The proposed algorithm is tested on the IEEE 33-bus system and a real-world Thimi-Sallaghari 11 kV feeder in Nepal. Load flow analysis is performed using the backward/forward sweep (BFS) method in MATLAB, with validation in ETAP. Results shows that DTBA reduces active power losses by 85.98% in the combined DG-capacitor case, outperforming GA (72.91%), PSO (74.85%), and Reptile Search Algorithm (RSA) (75.27%). Voltage profiles improve by 5.89%, ensuring stable operation within standard limits (0.95–1.05 pu). The findings of this research show the DTBA’s superiority in convergence speed and solution quality, making it a promising tool for smart grid optimization in radial distribution system.

title: Secure and Explainable Generative AI and Consumer Trust in India: A Thematic Analysis and Systematic Review
authors: Subhadeep Ghosh, Loveleen Gaur, R Meenakshi, Ramakrishnan Raman
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Generative AI application is becoming one of the most effective technologies that attracts a large number of consumers and organization. The lack of research on explainable AI (XAI) created an influence on the author to initiate this study. The primary objective of this study is to identify the impact of secure and trustworthy XAI on Indian consumer purchase decisions, trust, and behavior. Prisma 2020 and the systematic literature review method have been used by the researcher to evaluate 45 peer-reviewed journal articles. The overall findings indicate consumer preference are being increased towards the Explainable AI and trustworthiness in the Generative AI. This study discusses different psychological factors along with the regulatory framework to derive the implications of this technology and the policies. This SLR highlights the emergency of contact-aware emergency responses and responsible approaches to foster sustainable consumer trust.

title: RideAgent: An LLM-Enhanced Optimization Framework for Automated Taxi Fleet Operations_supp1-3685621.pdf
authors: Wei Qi
abstract: Efficient management of electric ride-hailing fleets, particularly pre-allocation and pricing during peak periods to balance spatio-temporal supply and demand, is crucial for urban traffic efficiency. However, practical challenges include unpredictable demand and translating diverse, qualitative managerial objectives from non-expert operators into tractable optimization models. This paper introduces RideAgent, a Large Language Model (LLM)-powered agent framework that automates and enhances electric ride-hailing fleet management. First, an LLM interprets natural language queries from fleet operators to formulate corresponding mathematical objective functions. These user-defined objectives are then optimized within a Mixed-Integer Programming (MIP) framework, subject to the constraint of maintaining high operational profit. The profit itself is a primary objective, estimated by an embedded Random Forest (RF) model leveraging exogenous features. To accelerate the solution of this MIP, a prompt-guided LLM analyzes a small sample of historical optimal decision data to guide a variable fixing strategy. Experiments on real-world data show that the LLM-generated objectives achieve an 86% text similarity to standard formulations in a zero-shot setting. Following this, the LLM-guided variable fixing strategy reduces computation time by 60.22% compared to solving the full MIP with only a 2.96% average optimality gap. Moreover, this variable fixing strategy outperforms five cutting plane methods by 42.3% time reduction with minimal compromise to solution quality. RideAgent offers a reliable and scalable automated framework for objective modeling and accelerated optimization. This framework empowers non-expert fleet operators to personalize operations and improve urban transportation system performance.

title: Stress Detection Using Physiological Signals and Machine Learning
authors: V. Subha Ramya, Bethanney Janney. J
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Stress is a critical physiological and psychological condition that, if left undetected, could result in severe health complications, namely cardiovascular disorders, depression, cognitive impairment, reduced productivity. Advancements in wearable sensing technologies and artificial intelligence have enabled continuous monitoring of stressrelated physiological changes, making automated stress detection more practical and reliable. This paper offers comprehensive review of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) approaches utilized for stress prediction using biomarkers, namely Electrocardiogram (ECG), Galvanic Skin Response (GSR), Respiration Rate, Heart Rate Variability (HRV), multimodal sensor data. A generalised methodological pipeline is proposed, which includes physiological signal acquisition, feature extraction, preprocessing in time and frequency domains, classification utilizing intelligent models such as Random Forests (RF), Support Vector Machines (SVM), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long ShortTerm Memory (LSTM) networks. Literature studies demonstrate that performance of stress detection systems significantly relies on quality of physiological data, feature representation, and model architecture. The review also highlights recent advancements, including remote photoplethysmography-based stress analysis, transfer learning, and hybrid fusion models that improve robustness in realworld conditions. This work provides insights into state-of-theart (SOTA) techniques, existing limitations, upcoming research opportunities to facilitate development of efficient real-time stress monitoring systems for personalised healthcare and wellbeing enhancement.

title: Simulation Study of Data Skew in Federated Learning for Tuberculosis Detection
authors: Masoodhu Banu N. M, Jehosheba Margaret M, P Vanitha, Madhumithaa. S, Thiyam Deepa Beeta
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Deep learning (DL) plays a major role in the automation area, from robotics to the medical field. However, it suffers from two issues, one is the need for a huge amount of data, and the other is explainability. Though the DL models failed to give explanations, the recent explainable algorithms provide detailed explanations, and hence DL can be used for automation. In the medical field, it is impossible to get more data due to the restrictions imposed by regulatory standards. The objective of this paper is to reduce the dependency of the number of medical images required for the DL network training using Federated Learning in order to increase the accuracy. This work simulates collaborative work on the same DL model environment by generating multiple clients with data dissimilarity among them, and also investigates the impact of multiple data dissimilarities, such as the number of data available with each client and machine-dependent data dissimilarity. The results of sample size skew illustrate that the proposed FL framework achieves a global accuracy of up to 95.39% within 2 communication rounds, closely approaching the centralized model accuracy of 98.7%, while maintaining consistent performance across additional rounds. With an increasing number of clients with equal or unequal datasets, the accuracy is 97%, which is the same as independently using the dataset with the same model. As it increases the privacy of the dataset and the model developer owns their model, the FLDL technique will rule the future in the field of diagnostic tool automation.

title: Hybrid Multilevel Thresholding for Skull Base Tumor Segmentation and Classification Using an Improved YOLOv8s Model
authors: M Ranjith Kumar, Yadeesh T, Kishore A G
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The very early and precise identification of skull base tumors is key to better prognosis and treatment planning. In that complex anatomic area, tumors present diagnostic issues due to their variable presentation and proximity to critical neural and vascular structures. Current manual methods for segmentation and classification from MR and CT scans are time-intensive, subjective, and observer-dependent. Automated methods exist but still struggle with poor boundary definition, distinguishing tumor from adjacent healthy tissue, and boundary and performance discrepancies with different imaging and tumor types. These poor performance boundary discrepancies lead to diagnostic delays and poor patient outcomes. To overcome these obstacles, we introduce a fully automated, hybrid framework that integrates classical image analysis and deep learning. Using Multi-Otsu thresholding for accurate tumor region segmentation, we optimize precision before classification. The derived segments are then processed by a tuned medical-grade YOLOv8s augmented with attention layers and structural enhancements to better capture fine tumor details. On a large skull base tumor dataset, the model achieved strong results (mAP @ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50=0.8362$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 50-95=0.6499$</tex>, classification accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=99.41 \%$</tex>), proving efficient and clinically compatible. This work contributes an accurate, stand-alone, and computationally efficient tool to assist radiologists and neurosurgeons in rapid, reliable skull base tumor diagnosis, supporting timely and precise clinical management.

title: Design and Development of a Smart Wrist Band for Remote Rehabilitation and Vital Sign Monitoring
authors: Bethanney Janney. J, Jeffin Jose, Janne Chetan, Angeline. J, Manjari. S. V, Raghupathi Prahala
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: This work proposes the design and development of a smart rehabilitation band that integrates biosensors with advanced features, wireless communication, and therapeutic assistance. The proposed system includes a blood pressure sensor with the ability to measure systolic, diastolic, and pulse values in real time, therefore allowing for continuous monitoring of a patient's vital signs. A Global System for Mobile Communication module is used for sending automated alerts in cases of physiological abnormalities to the healthcare provider for immediate medical response. An OLED display on the wearable facilitates the live visualisation of health parameters directly by the user, therefore fostering better user engagement and motivating patients toward their rehabilitation process. The therapeutic mode in the smart band leads patients through exercises of wrist movements that are vital for physical recovery. The architecture includes an Arduino Nano (Atmega328P) that will handle the operation of sensors and motors, and the ESP32 module that will provide wireless connectivity for seamless wireless transmission of data. This architecture uses a two-controller system to enable efficient sharing of tasks and ensure reliable communication with external monitoring platforms. The proposed smart band combines continuous physiological monitoring, real-time alerts, and interactive rehabilitation assistance to offer a comprehensive solution for post-surgical recovery. It is particularly useful in remote or underserved areas where frequent clinical visits are difficult, thus enhancing access and efficiency of rehabilitation services. Monitoring blood pressure is a safety and risk-reduction feature, particularly for stroke patients and older adults undergoing unsupervised rehabilitation at home.

title: A Wearable Footstep Energy Harvester With Novel Dual-Clutch Mechanical Motion Rectification for Self-Powered Sensing_supp2-3675109.mp4
authors: Fang Deng
abstract: Wearable and autonomous sensing systems require efficient energy conversion under low-frequency, bidirectional mechanical excitations generated by daily human motion. This work presents a novel dual-clutch ratchet energy harvester (DREH) that converts bidirectional heel motions into unidirectional high-speed rotation, enabling direct DC power generation without conventional AC–DC rectification losses. The dual-clutch architecture ensures robust motion rectification under weak excitation and enables recovery of elastic potential energy during the liftoff phase, thereby improving effective energy utilization. A coupled dynamic–electromechanical model is developed to analyze the system behavior and predict electrical output. A compact prototype (86.4 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, 89 g) is fabricated and experimentally evaluated under pseudowalk and natural walking conditions. Experimental results show that elastic energy recovery increases output power by 59.5% at 0.3 Hz; at 6 km/h with a 75 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\Omega}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> load, the prototype delivers 82 mW peak and 28 mW average power with a driving force below 14 N. System-level validation demonstrates continuous operation of a wireless sensing node, confirming the DREH as an effective self-sustained power source for wearable and industrial sensing applications.

title: Adaptive Grouping-Based Offspring Generation for Sparse Large-Scale Multimodal Multi-objective Optimization_supp1-3685860.pdf
authors: Jinhua Zheng
abstract: Sparse large-scale multimodal multi-objective optimization problems (SLMMOPs) are characterized by high dimensionality, sparse effective variables, and multiple disconnected Pareto-optimal regions. These properties create vast and deceptive search spaces, where only a small subset of variables contributes meaningfully to optimal solutions. Existing algorithms often emphasize diversity preservation, but struggle to identify and exploit sparse structures effectively, thus leading to inefficient search and premature convergence. To address this challenge, we propose AGOEA, an Adaptive Grouping-based Offspring Generation Evolutionary Algorithm. AGOEA streamlines the search process through two core innovations. First, it introduces a dual-granularity decision variable grouping strategy. A coarse-grained grouping leverages long-term population statistics to identify globally important variables and differentiate between Pareto sets, while a fine-grained grouping utilizes local feedback to refine variable selection within subpopulations. These grouping insights subsequently guide customized reproduction operators that perform dimension-specific variation to balance exploration and exploitation. Second, AGOEA adopts a multi-population evolution framework, enabling subpopulations to explore distinct sparse structures in parallel. To coordinate these subpopulations and balance computational effort between discovering new Pareto sets and refining sparse variable structures, a dynamic monitoring and resource allocation mechanism is employed. This mechanism adaptively identifies stagnation and redistributes resources to under-explored regions, thereby enhancing global coverage and convergence. Extensive experiments on benchmark SMMOPs with 500 to 1000 decision variables show that AGOEA consistently achieves superior convergence and diversity compared to six state-of-the-art algorithms. (The source code is available at https://github.com/WP0102/AGOEA.).

title: Alzheimer’s Disease Detection using Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Technique
authors: Ashok Koujalagi, Saragadam Anuradha, B.Venkata Varma, P Chandra Sekhar Reddy, A. Lakshmanarao, Naladi Ram Babu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Early identification of Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) is crucial for timely intervention, yet traditional diagnostic pathways are costly, time-intensive, and prone to variability. This paper presents a hybrid learning framework that combines deep representation learning with ensemble classifiers for AD detection from structured, non-imaging clinical and lifestyle data. Using a Kaggle dataset of 2,149 records, the pipeline includes rigorous preprocessing, stratified splitting, and class-imbalance mitigation via Adaptive Synthetic Sampling (ADASYN) applied only to the training set. Three machine-learning baselines—XGBoost, CatBoost, and KNN to establish reference performance. Deep models (TabTransformer and Wide & Deep) are then trained as supervised classifiers, while an Autoencoder is employed strictly as an unsupervised feature extractor. Hybrid systems are formed by feeding deep embeddings into boosting classifiers. Results show consistent gains from deep models over ML baselines, with the hybrid configurations performing best. The proposed TabTransformer–CatBoost model attains 0.98 accuracy, 0.97 F1-score, and 0.97 ROC-AUC, indicating improved generalization and sensitivity to AD-positive cases.

title: Privacy-Preserving Coordinated Scheduling of Distributed Resources Using ADMM and Tianji’s Horse Racing Optimization
authors: S. Sankara Prasad, Kammari Tharunsai, Sampangi Sai Kumar, Vele Jaya Krishna
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The accelerating electrification of transport and integration of distributed renewables are reshaping modern distribution networks, where uncoordinated scheduling of electric vehicle (EV) aggregators, energy storage systems (ESS), and distributed generators (DG) can compromise voltage stability, increase network losses, and distort operational economics. This paper introduces a distributed scheduling framework that integrates the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) with Tianji’s Horse Racing Optimization (THRO) to achieve scalable and privacy-preserving coordination among multiple flexible resources. In the proposed approach, ADMM decomposes the global problem into independent quadratic sub-problems solved locally by EV, ESS, and DG agents, while a THRO-based projection at the distribution system operator enforces true AC power-flow feasibility without relying on linearized or convexified approximations. Battery degradation costs are explicitly modeled to ensure economically realistic scheduling decisions. Extensive simulations on a benchmark 15-bus feeder demonstrate that the ADMM–THRO framework eliminates voltage violations, reduces network losses significantly compared to convex-projection baselines, and delivers robust convergence under nonlinear operating conditions. These results confirm that coupling metaheuristic projection with distributed optimization provides a technically faithful and economically sound solution for coordinated scheduling in next-generation distribution grids.

title: ProLiBatt: An AI-Powered System for Predicting Lithium-Ion Battery Health in Electric Vehicles
authors: Vinay Kalluri, Nithik C Reddy, Diya Ponnarian, Sudhakiran Ponnuru
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The lithium-ion battery accurate estimation of State of Health (SoH) is critical for ensuring safety, reliability, and cost efficiency in electric vehicles and grid storage. In this work, we proposed a hybrid framework that integrates Random Forest– based feature selection with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) temporal predictor, designed to capture both nonlinear feature importance and long-term degradation trends. The method is evaluated on the NASA and Oxford datasets, covering multiple chemistries and usage profiles. Our model achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.7 % ± 0.2 and a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 3.1 % capacity under cross-cell validation, reducing error by up to 18 % compared with the best baseline (Gradient Boosting). Furthermore, we validate generalization by training on one chemistry (NMC) and testing on another (LFP), where the model retains an R2R^2R2 above 0.92. Runtime analysis shows inference latency of under 12 m/s on an ARM Cortex-A53 with less than 3 MB memory usage, demonstrating feasibility for embedded Battery Management Systems. These results highlight that hybrid machine learning approaches, when combined with careful feature engineering, can deliver accurate and deployable SoH estimation for next generation energy systems.

title: A Review of Deep Learning–based Malaria Disease Detection Techniques: Challenges and Gaps
authors: Nallagoni Rajitha, Akhila Ravi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Malaria remains one of the most life-threatening infectious diseases, particularly in developing and resource-limited regions. Early and accurate diagnosis plays a critical role in reducing mortality and preventing disease spread. In recent years, Deep Learning (DL) and computer vision techniques have been widely adopted for automated malaria detection using microscopic blood smear images. This work presents a comprehensive review of existing malaria disease detection models, focusing on convolutional neural networks, hybrid DL architectures, and object detection frameworks. The review identifies common limitations such as reliance on single datasets, loss of fine morphological features due to image resizing, lack of species and parasite stage classification, high computational complexity, and limited real-world validation. Based on these observations, key research gaps are highlighted, and sustainable solution directions are discussed to guide future research toward accurate, efficient, and clinically deployable malaria detection systems.

title: Federated Learning in Healthcare: A Survey of Privacy-Preserving Medical AI Solutions
authors: Ritu, Sakshi Koli, Nagendar Yamsani, Ankur Dumka
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Learning (FL) is a promising approach for collaborative Medical AI while maintaining patient privacy. This study surveys the use of FL in radiology, genomics, and electronic health records. The (More) effective methods for model aggregation (such as FedAvg and FedProx), differential privacy, and secure multiparty computation were surveyed. Cases from multi-hospital collaborations were used to demonstrate how FL facilitates learning without centralizing data sharing. Challenges of data heterogeneity, communication cost, and model convergence are discussed in the review. Comparative experiments demonstrate that FL can achieve close-to-centralized performance and preserve data protection requirements, such as HIPAA and GDPR. We focus on tools such as NVIDIA Clara, TensorFlow Federated, and PySyft. Future work will include federated reinforcement learning, personalization of FL models, and combination with blockchain to enable secure and auditable AI pipelines in clinical settings.

title: Hybrid K-Means–Logistic Regression Framework for Automatic Resume Short-Listing and Job Matching in HR Systems
authors: Afreen Bari, K Kalyani, Prakash Ranjan Parida, K. Anju Aravind, Anuradha, Naveen Nandal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Companies get thousands of applicants for a single position in today's competitive employment market, which makes manual short-listing laborious, irregular, and sometimes difficult for HR staff. We created a Hybrid K-Means–Logistic Regression Framework that functions as a recruiter's smart assistant in order to tackle this bigger problem. After reading each CV, the algorithm uses K-Means to group similar profiles together before applying Logistic Regression to precisely match each applicant with the best position. The model is better able to understand both broad trends and small shifts in expertise, experience, and domain relevance thanks to this layered approach. In comparison to current approaches, the system produced quicker decisions, more accuracy, and more reliable candidate-job suggestions when evaluated on actual resume collections. This hybrid model provides a practical, adaptable approach that adds intelligence, equity, and efficiency to contemporary HR recruiting systems by combining supervised prediction with unsupervised neural networks.

title: Design and Implementation of a Raspberry Pi–Based Multilingual Image-to-Speech System
authors: Nayana R, Gowthami H R, Likhitha Shree K R, Athul Ghosh P
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The paper deals with a Raspberry Pi based multilingual image to speech converter system, which can help the visually impaired people to speak out the meaningful words from images in different languages. The gadget takes pictures of printed text in any language using a camera. OCR technology will be applied to the received image. The text which is extracted is run through a multilingual TTS engine which converts the written text into natural human voice in the respective language. The implementation uses open-source software libraries are Tesseract OCR with multilingual language packs and e-Speak or Festival for speech synthesis, all operated locally on a low-cost portable Raspberry Pi device. Image pre-processing techniques enhance text recognition accuracy, and the blur detection mechanism ensures reliable input quality. This system doesn’t require internet to function, making it usable in any setting for real-time application. The solution can recognize and produce multilingual text and speech. This solution aids in the inclusion of assistive technologies for the disabled. It enables users to gain access to printed information in various languages using human-like speech output.

title: Greener Web: Minimizing the Carbon Footprint of Web Applications
authors: Kavinmathi V, A. Shanthakumari
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Web applications have become an emerging issue in relation to the environment as digital services continue to grow at an alarming rate. Despite websites and online platforms are not seen as big obstacle, they draw a lot of consumption of energy by means of data centers, communication networks and the devices people use. In this paper, the carbon footprint of the current web development has been discussed and suggests a design that is sustainable in order to cut down emissions. By analyzing and improving massive web performance features, including page size, resource loading, server hosting, and caching plans, this paper gives a presentation of how energy consumption can be empirically compared and cleared out on a user-by-user basis. A case study comparison is carried out through audit tools to examine the amount of carbon emitted by a standard and an optimized version of a sample site. The results indicate the use of environmentally friendly design. Not only these practices reduce the environmental impact, but also delivers a more user-friendly experience by making web pages more mobile efficient. This paper offers useful advice to developers, designers, and organizations that intend on developing more sustainable digital systems that are compliant with world environmental objectives.

title: Utilizing Multi Layer Perceptron (MLP) to Enhance Stability Analysis of Geotechnical Engineering
authors: Lixia Li, Peng Jin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In the study of geotechnical mechanics, slope stability is always a key problem, which is directly related to the design and construction of the whole project. Conventional methods of stability calculation have some shortcomings, such as low precision, long time consuming, unable to deal with the complex relationship between complex models, etc. In this project, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) is taken as the research object, and the corresponding geotechnical parameters and characteristics are introduced to model and predict the MLP model. This project will make use of the relation and nonlinear mapping function of multi-layer neural network to describe the complex relation and rule of rock mass structure better, and improve the calculation precision and efficiency. Through the training and testing of MLP, it is found that MLP has higher accuracy and predictability in stability study, and can evaluate the stability, deformation and bearing capacity of soil (rock) more accurately. Compared with the conventional numerical simulation results, the MLP numerical simulation results can reach 95.9%. The results show that it is feasible to use multi-layer sensors for slope stability analysis. MLP model is used to analyze the stability of slope, which makes it more scientific and accurate, and provides a new and efficient analysis method for safety problems in geotechnical engineering. The research of this project will play an active role in promoting the development of geotechnical mechanics in China.
