title: An IoT and Machine Learning-based Digital Framework for Smart Agriculture: Crop and Action Recommendations
authors: Sathya J, V. Asha, A. Kalaivani, Sanuth Bibin B, Sharfaraj Kamalsab Gumnalli
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This paper presents a smart agriculture framework that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) sensing with machine learning techniques to support precision farming and sustainable agricultural practices. Environmental parameters such as temperature, humidity, rainfall, soil moisture, and soil pH are continuously captured using field-deployed sensors. Soil pH estimation is performed through image-based analysis using a Support Vector Machine (SVM) model. The collected data are processed using Decision Tree, Random Forest, and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithms to identify suitable crops and generate actionable recommendations, including irrigation scheduling, fertilizer management, and soil treatment. By functioning as a digital representation of the farm environment, the proposed framework enables real-time, data-driven decision-making that enhances crop productivity and efficient resource utilization.

title: Artificial Intelligence Driven Congestion Aware Routing Framework for Next Generation Communication Networks
authors: T. Saravanan, Yousuf Hazaimeh, S.Agnes Shifani, Jayashree N, V. Manasa, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: With the exponential rise in connected devices and data-heavy applications, traditional routing protocols struggle to efficiently manage congestion in next-generation communication networks. In this paper I introduce a congestion-aware routing framework (AI-CARF) that was developed based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) to proactively control the impact of congestion through a multi-layered intelligence framework. The framework includes Graph Signal Processing to encode topology, Topological Data Analysis to identify anomalies using persistence, and Neuroevolution to develop context-based routing capabilities. Moreover, Fuzzy Reinforcement Learning model is used to interpret quality-of-service measures, whereas Multi-Agent Deep Q-Learning helps to make decentralized and real-time decisions. Knowledge distillation is done in a simulated digital twin environment, which enables lightweight deployment of the model in edge devices. The model was experimented with various synthetic and real-world traffic topologies and demonstrated a congestion-aware routing quality of 96.42 which is better than the conventional AODV and DSR protocols in terms of packet delivery ratio, delay, and jitter. Findings verify the strength of AI-CARF when a load is varied, a node failed and topology changed dynamically. The framework is a breakthrough towards autonomous, scalable and adaptive routing in ultra-dense communication networks like 6G, IoT mesh networks, VANETs and satellite communications, which will enable intelligent infrastructure optimization in network generations to come.

title: From Text to Ticker: A Comprehensive Survey and Methodological Guide to Named Entity Recognition in Finance
authors: Nilesh Vidhani, Gobi Ramasamy, Syam Mohan E
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The financial industry generates vast volumes of unstructured textual data from sources such as regulatory filings, news articles, social media, and earnings call transcripts. Extracting structured and actionable intelligence from this data remains a significant challenge. Named Entity Recognition (NER) is a fundamental task in natural language processing that supports this process by identifying and categorizing key information within text. However, the linguistic complexity, contextual ambiguity, and domain-specific terminology of financial text require specialized approaches that extend beyond general-purpose NER models. This paper presents a comprehensive survey and methodological guide to Financial Named Entity Recognition (Fin-NER). It begins by introducing the core concepts of NER while highlighting the unique challenges posed by financial text. The paper then reviews the evolution of Fin-NER approaches, ranging from rule-based systems and classical machine learning techniques to modern deep learning architectures. Furthermore, it analyzes the distinction between fine-tuned transformer-based models and general-purpose large language models in the current research. The study also examines commonly used datasets and evaluation metrics for benchmarking Fin-NER systems. Finally, it discusses key findings, existing methodological limitations, and future research directions, including hybrid modeling strategies, cross-lingual datasets, and the development of more reliable and explainable systems. Overall, this work serves both as a scholarly review of the Fin-NER field and as a practical guide for researchers and practitioners seeking to transform unstructured financial text into structured and informative representations.

title: IoT-based Secure Health Monitoring System in Machine Learning using Deep Neural Network
authors: R. Satheesh Kumar, M S. Sachika, A P. Priyavarshini
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The proposed method for remote healthcare monitoring is based on the Internet of Things, which allows for a secure method of collecting, analyzing, managing, and storing physiological data through machine learning and deep neural network algorithms. The IoT will allow for real-time measurements of vital signs as opposed to relying on periodic clinical evaluations. Using wearable devices equipped with sensors, real time values will be captured and securely transmitted to a cloud-based server. A deep learning neural network model will be created and trained to determine the complex, non-linear correlations between multiple biomedical signals and identify whether a health condition is normal or abnormal. This deep learning model will provide an early prediction of a potential medical risk. The proposed method will allow for continued monitoring of patients, and the data will remain secure and confidential using cryptographic hashes and the blockchain-based logging of immutable and tamper resistant records. The combination of IoT sensing, deep learning-based inference, and decentralized security will provide a means for continuous health evaluation while still protecting the patient’s right to privacy. The proposed method will also produce alerts to caregivers and/or clinicians during critical events that require immediate intervention and will decrease the need for patients to return to the hospital for evaluation. By linking intelligent prediction with secure data governance, the proposed method provides a scalable and trustworthy solution to healthcare delivered remotely, in a clinically significant manner.

title: Personalized E-Commerce Recommendations through Hybrid Social Media-Graph Neural Networks
authors: Amarthaluri Tirupathaiah, Mulamraju Sai Sumanth, T. Seshasai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The use of recommender systems to direct consumers towards certain products has grown in tandem with the popularity of online marketplaces. Despite their initial naiveté, these systems have grown into an invaluable asset for businesses, making it easier for customers to discover things they love. Although there are still major obstacles in the form of research gaps and privacy concerns, recommender systems do use data mined from user activity or hand-coded product knowledge to analyse databases. To tackle these difficulties, this paper presents a platform for E-Commerce Recommendation that is built on GNN. After tokenisation, lemmatisation, and snowball stemming were used for data preprocessing, dimensionality reduction, and feature extraction, PCA was applied. The next step was to build prediction models using GNN techniques and compare them to more traditional machine learning methods. The results showed that the proposed GNN model outperformed competing models in e-commerce recommendation tasks and related domains like cardiovascular illness prediction, reaching an accuracy of 96.35%. Although user privacy must be carefully considered in recommender system design, these results show that GNN-based architectures have the ability to improve recommendation accuracy, fill in research gaps, and make online shopping more personalised.

title: Real Time Agriculture Monitoring and Communication System using Video Streams and IoT Networking
authors: A. Prasanth, E. Rajini, E. Meghana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The need for optimal resource consumption and real-time monitoring in the field has significantly led to the growth of smart agriculture technologies. The Internet of Things (IoT) provides an environmental parameter to monitor and support the concept of data-driven agricultural-related decisions. This paper describes the design of a real-time agriculture monitoring and communication system. It combines environmental parameter monitoring through IoT technology and data processing via cloud technology to emphasize the concept of precision agriculture. The proposed design uses the concept of sensor technology to monitor the critical parameters of the environment such as the moisture content, the temperature, and the humidity levels. The parameters are then sent to the cloud platform for real-time monitoring. In addition to the sensor-based monitoring, the system allows for real-time observation of the field conditions for enhancing the situational awareness that helps in making timely decisions.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Battery Exchange System for Electric Two-Wheelers
authors: R. Indhumathi, P. V. Adhini, S. Aravind, S. Agilesh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Two-wheelers powered by electricity are gradually taking over as a choice of urban transport because of their low emissions and low prices. Nevertheless, the issue of a small number of charging stations, hours to charge, and reliance on grid power make their mass adoption difficult. In this paper, an IoT enabled smart battery change system of two-wheeler electric vehicles is introduced, which incorporates solar energy harvesting, automatic battery changeover and real-time monitoring to sustain uninterrupted functioning. The system uses voltage sensors to check on the main battery and an auxiliary battery which is solar powered. Upon the main battery being low in power, an ATmega328 microcontroller will trigger a relay to turn the power on to the solar battery and the vehicle will keep running just as usual. The vehicle is propelled by a DC motor and an LCD display gives the riders real time data about the battery condition, voltages and power source used. Moreover, an IoT cloud application will allow remote monitoring, collecting and analyzing data and will help preventative maintenance and efficient energy consumption. The proposed system will minimize the use of conventional charge infrastructure by integrating renewable energy as well as smart battery control and improving the efficiency of the entire energy network and optimizing the energy-saving use of transport, thereby supporting an environmentally friendly movement. The practical aspect of the approach is proved by its implementation, which is effective and feasible, and it has the potential to provide sustainable urban mobility.

title: Crop Recommendation System using Confidence-Weighted Ensemble Optimization: A Comprehensive Multi-Objective Approach for Sustainable Precision Agriculture
authors: Pranav Ramachandra Hegde, V. Srinivasan, Bharath M V, Prajapati Darshan Jayeshbhai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This study presents a novel crop recommendation system for precision agriculture, addressing limitations of existing machine learning approaches. The proposed system employs a Confidence-Weighted Ensemble of five models: Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and Gaussian Naive Bayes. It integrates a Multi-Objective Optimization framework balancing yield, water efficiency, and profitability. Tested on 2,200 agricultural samples across 22 crops from diverse Indian agro-climatic zones, the model achieves 98.86% accuracy with high stability (0.83% std dev). The ensemble outperforms the best single model by 0.45% and engineered meta-features improve accuracy by 1.36%. With an inference time of 0.20 seconds and a 403 MB footprint, the system is suitable for resource-constrained settings. It facilitates informed decisions, potentially increasing yields by 15-25% and reducing water use by 20-30%.

title: Enhancing Agricultural Pest Monitoring with AEC-YOLOv8n and EfficientNetV2-B0: A Hybrid Approach
authors: Rajasekaran Arunachalam, J Mohana
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: In precision farming, identification of agricultural pests is essential and directly affects crop output, food security and sustainable agricultural practices. Pest monitoring procedures that have been traditionally used are often labor-intensive, inefficient and unsuitable in real-time applications, especially in low resource settings. The advanced pest detection system described in this paper was developed to eliminate these challenges through joint application of deep learning and adaptive optimization methods in an innovative manner. Having added the AFEM_SIE and Concat Weighting modules to enhance the spatial attention and feature fusion, the proposed model combines the AEC-YOLOv8n detector with EfficientNetV2-B0 as a feature extractor. The model, using the IP102 data and a selected sample of the three principal rice pests (stem borer, rice leaf caterpillar, and brown planthopper), has an accuracy of 99.01 and F1-score of 91.27, surpassing the existing benchmarking models. The system is optimized with an Improved Harris Hawks Optimization (IHHO) method to tune the hyperparameters of the models to enhance the generalization and faster convergence. The lightweight design of this framework and its low computational footprint allow it to be deployed on the edge devices with IoT capabilities, and hence permit real time detection of pests and response in intelligent agriculture environment. This research is a significant contribution to the efforts of precision agriculture in search of practical methods of pest management that can be used on large scale.

title: Learning to Discern Fine-Grained Cues Across Domains: Generalizing Re-ID via Multi-Level Feature Propagation
authors: Xu Zhang, Shulin Wu, Siji Chen, Zuyu Zhang
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Contrastive Dual-Prototypical Learning for Efficient Multi-Label Ophthalmic Diagnosis
authors: R.S.Naga Kumar Ganugula, Sri Phani Krishna karri
abstract: Early screening for ophthalmic diseases is a public health imperative for preventing irreversible vision loss. However, real-world fundus datasets often exhibit multi-label comorbidities and long-tailed distributions, inducing diagnostic bias in conventional deep learning models. This letter proposes the Contrastive Dual-Prototypical Learning (CDPL) framework to address these challenges. CDPL utilizes a two-stage curriculum: Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) pre-training to extract invariant features, followed by orthogonality-regularized dual-prototypical fine-tuning that enforces angular separation to decouple overlapping pathological signals. Evaluated on the ODIR dataset using a ResNet-18 backbone, CDPL achieves a 9.7% Macro-F1 improvement over standard benchmarks. Qualitative t-SNE and Grad-CAM analyses confirm the structural refinement of the metric space and superior sensitivity to rare tail-class pathologies. The framework is designed for extensibility to multi-label medical imaging domains characterised by class imbalance, with theoretical generalizability supported by the dataset-agnostic construction of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {L}_{\rm{ortho}}$</tex-math></inline-formula>.

title: Reliable and Energy-Efficient MAC Protocols in Industrial IoT Networks via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Luciano Miuccio, Daniela Panno, Salvatore Riolo, Anita Schilirò
publish: IEEE Transactions on Machine Learning in Communications and Networking
abstract: The transition from wired to wireless communications in industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks introduces stringent challenges in terms of reliability and energy efficiency, aggravated by harsh propagation conditions and contention for a shared radio medium. These constraints require advanced medium access control (MAC) protocols capable of jointly managing channel access, packet retransmissions, and buffer operations while accounting for the battery limitations of IoT devices (IoTDs). This paper proposes a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework for the autonomous design of energy-efficient and reliable MAC protocols in uplink wireless IIoT networks supporting time–frequency multiplexing. Moving away from conventional decentralized partially observable Markov decision process (Dec-POMDP)-based MARL designs, the framework adopts a partially observable Markov game (POMG), thereby enabling per-device policy learning. A novel reward mechanism is introduced, in which the base station broadcasts a resource-level feedback, and each device constructs a local reward based solely on its own observations and past actions, ensuring feasibility in real deployments. Simulation results show that the proposed framework achieves maximum reliability, whereas state-of-the-art MARL benchmarks based on global rewards fail to meet the required target, highlighting the importance of POMG modeling and local reward structures for reliable wireless IIoT networks. Furthermore, a comparison with conventional grant-based protocols, which inherently achieve maximum reliability, demonstrates that the proposed solution significantly reduces the active-mode duration, thereby improving overall energy efficiency.

title: Covert Knowledge Poisoning Attacks in Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
authors: Yu Zhang, Miao Chen, Xinlei He, Tianshuo Cong, Ke Xu, Qi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation (RACG) systems enhance code generation by dynamically integrating examples retrieved from open knowledge bases, but their reliance on external sources exposes them to knowledge poisoning. While prior attacks inject explicit vulnerable code, such payloads are easily detected, limiting their real-world impact and failing to probe the full attack surface. To overcome this limitation, we propose Arachne, a covert knowledge poisoning attack that, for the first time, achieves fine-grained control over vulnerability types while evading detection. Arachne relies on two mechanisms: (1) Benign-Appearing Fragment Construction, where vulnerable code is decomposed into benign-appearing fragments that pre serve compilability and contextual cues, rendering them effectively undetectable by vulnerability analyzers; and (2) Retrieval Driven Knowledge Completion, in which retrieved fragments activate the large language model's (LLM) contextual reasoning to autonomously generate complete vulnerable code. We evaluate Arachne on eight mainstream LLMs and four retrievers across four common vulnerability types. On GPT-4, Qwen2.5, Gemini 3-flash, Claude-4-sonnet and DeepSeekCoder, Arachne achieves attack success rates exceeding 70% in most settings for each vulnerability type across CWE-78, CWE-295, CWE-367, and CWE-614, peaking at 100% for CWE-367. Arachne demonstrates up to a 37% improvement in attack success rate over existing poisoning attacks. In two real-world applications, the attack achieves 87% success rate with 70% benign utility, indicating that the generated code often satisfies user requirements while introducing vulnerable code. Critically, our poisoned samples, which are designed without explicit malicious patterns, fully bypass rule-based analyzers and challenge state-of-the-art LLM based detectors, exemplifying their stealth among multiple failed defense paradigms. Our findings expose critical limitations in existing defense frameworks, highlighting the urgent need for novel defense mechanisms specifically designed for RACG systems.

title: Strategies to Improve Efficiency of Select-Gate Voltage Floating During GIDL-Assisted Erase in 3-D NAND Flash Memory
authors: Sungju Kim, Jaeyeop Jung, Hyungcheol Shin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: This study addresses the issue in 3-D NAND flash memory where, during GIDL (Gate-Induced Drain Leakage)-assisted erase, the floating (FT) select-gate cell (SG) approach induces uncertainty in SG voltage (VSG) rise due to the parasitic capacitive suppression (PCS), degrading SG reliability. First, Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) simulations were performed to elucidate the mechanism of VSG rise in the FT approach (VSG,FT), and a physics-based compact model capable of quantitatively predicting VSG,FT variations was developed. Strategies to mitigate the degradation of VSG,FT rise were proposed, ultimately achieving both SG reliability and GIDL erase efficiency while offering a scalable approach. The validity of the proposed strategies was confirmed through a Simulation Program with Integrated Circuit Emphasis (SPICE)-based Monte Carlo simulations using the developed compact model, evaluated from the perspective of threshold voltage distribution.

title: Stabilizing Affective Representations with CLAIRE: A Cognitive-Linguistic Architecture for Interpretable Reasoning and Emotion
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Dylan M. Diaz, Divya Thakar, Mira Kim, Yunfei Hou, Soo Dong Kim, Jennifer Jin
abstract: This study presents CLAIRE (Cognitive-Linguistic Architecture for Interpretable Reasoning and Emotion), a unified framework that integrates cognitively motivated regularization, transformer-based language modeling, and post-hoc neuro-symbolic reasoning to advance affective text classification. CLAIRE incorporates cognitive priors expressed through entropy-based attention regularization to promote stable, psychologically plausible focus patterns during affective representation learning. These priors guide a transformer architecture that combines pretrained encoder fine-tuning with multi-task learning, enabling both coarse-grained and fine-grained emotion recognition under a shared representation. A post-hoc symbolic reasoning layer further provides transparent, rule-based explanations without influencing neural prediction. The framework is evaluated under full-dataset conditions on the GoEmotions and SemEval-2018 Affect in Tweets benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate that entropy regularization consistently improves classification performance over standard transformer baselines, increasing accuracy from 0.765 to 0.796 on GoEmotions and from 0.703 to 0.728 on SemEval-2018 for coarse-grained emotion classification, with comparable gains observed for fine-grained emotion recognition. These findings indicate that cognitively inspired regularization supports stable performance improvements across multiple affective datasets under in-domain evaluation, while preserving interpretability.

title: A Deep Feature Extraction Method Based on Variational Inference and Self-Attention Mechanism of Graph Neural Networks
authors: Rui Feng, Qingsen Cai, Aruhan Bao, Hu Li
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Complex hierarchical dependencies and upstream/downstream mappings exist among the items in equipment procurement projects, making anomaly identification difficult to achieve using traditional rule-based or independent sample-based methods. To address this issue, this paper abstracts equipment procurement data into a graph structure and proposes a graph neural network feature extraction method (VGAE-AttnGNN) that integrates variational inference and self-attention mechanisms to model the structural relationships and potential uncertainties among equipment procurement items. Experimental results on a real equipment procurement dataset show that this method achieves $90.6 \%$ accuracy and $84.3 \%$ Macro-F1 scores, respectively, representing a stable improvement over comparative methods.

title: Multistage Constant Current Wireless Charging Strategy Based on Multiharmonic Power Transmisson
authors: Wenzhi Zheng, Wei Deng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Due to its safety and convenience, wireless charging technology has been widely adopted in electric vehicle chargers. However, in the typical constant voltage(CV) following constant current(CC) battery charging process, the output current is not adjustable with high flexibility, and the temperature rise is fast, which leads to the reduction of charging speed. In order to solve the above problems, a multistage constant current(MSCC) wireless charging strategy based on multiharmonic power transfer is proposed in this paper for charging lithium batteries in electric vehicles. The charging efficiency is above 92% in the second to sixth stages of the dominant battery charging. In addition, it provides a pre-charging stage for deeply discharged batteries to increase their lifetime.

title: An Algorithm-Centric Computational Framework for Near-Scope 3 Carbon Diagnostics: Fixed-Effects Learning, PSO-NN Ensemble Forecasting, and Uncertainty-Aware Scenario Analytics
authors: Ziyuan Jia, Kaiyuan Bai
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Definition, sample protocol, equations, and robustness design were revised. This paper proposes an algorithm-centric framework for macro near-Scope 3 diagnostics. NearScope 3 is explicitly defined as a macro trade-embedded proxy, $G_{c, t}=E_{c, t}^{{cons }}-E_{c, t}^{{prod }}$, rather than firm-level Scope 3 accounting. We reconcile descriptive, estimation, and forecasting samples, then combine small-sample panel inference (HC3, leave-one-entity-out, year-block bootstrap) with PSO-NN ensemble fore-casting and chronology-consistent validation. Mathematically, the PSO constriction factor, learning-rate schedule, and sigmoid price path are corrected. Uncertainty propagation uses moving-block residual resampling, and scenario analysis separates signed accounting burden from non-negative exposure burden. The revised manuscript is reproducible, computationally explicit, and methodologically defensible for engineering-oriented review.

title: Effective Missing Data Imputation Techniques Utilizing Regression Methods
authors: Xiaomei Feng
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of the information age, vast amounts of data are being generated, often leading to the common occurrence of missing values. This study presents a statistical imputation method using regression to effectively fill in these gaps. Two real-world public datasets with $\mathbf{5 0 \%}$ missing values, were analyzed, comparing the performance results to both the original datasets and existing mode imputation methods. The findings indicate that the regression imputation method significantly enhances accuracy, with an improvement of up to 8.95% in prediction accuracy. These results have important implications for data-driven decision-making across various fields.

title: Personalized Federated Learning for Sovereign Personal AI Agents: A Review
authors: Adil Akhmetov, Batyr Sharimbayev, Mohammed Alaa Ala'anzy
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is at an inflection point, shifting from the “One Model Fits All” philosophy represented by centralized models like ChatGPT to “Hyper-Personalized AI” that honors user data sovereignty while retaining the collaborative learning advantages. This review examines the convergence of Personalized Federated Learning (PFL), Large Language Models (LLMs), and privacy-preserving technologies to create truly sovereign personal AI agents. We review over 39 peer-reviewed articles (2020-2025) on these topics, with particular emphasis on three major areas: architectural innovations in personalization using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) adapters and knowledge graphs, privacy-preserving technologies like Differential Privacy (DP) and secure aggregation, and frameworks that allow users' personal data to be used to train personal agents locally without requiring cloud access. Our review demonstrates that PFL is at the core of creating the “Sovereign Data Ecosystem” where users can retain personal data like their calendar, email, and health records locally while contributing to global model improvements through aggregated gradient sharing. We also outline future research directions towards Federated Agent Swarms and how PFL redefines the relationship between user data ownership, model personalization, and algorithmic transparency.

title: Effect of Rotation on MHD Peristaltic Flow With Heat Transfer in a Tapered Wall
authors: Mohammed Salim Ramadhan, Khalid Khaleefah Jassim
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study examines how rotation affects magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) peristaltic movement in a canal with a tapering. The equations that govern the system describing the thermal flow are first formulated. Then, by adopting the lengthy-wavelength and minimal Reynolds number Estimate, the solution is acquired. Expressions the velocity field, temperature distribution, stream function are derived. The results are not left at that. The effects of various physical parameters on the system are examined through numerical methods and graphical representations. The core of this analysis investigates the interplay of rotation, wall tapering, and a transverse magnetic field in determining the peristaltic motion of an electrically conducting Newtonian fluid. Specifically, the model is applied to a two-dimensional asymmetric channel. The momentum and energy equations are constructed while accounting for key effects such as the Lorentz force, the Coriolis force, and viscous dissipation. The problem is simplified and explicit analytical solutions for the temperature and velocity distributions, stream function are obtained by applying the long-wavelength and low Reynolds number computations. The research provides important new information about the flow and heat transmission control mechanisms in these kinds of structures.

title: A Novel Low-PAPR Waveform for Integrated Communication and Navigation in LEO Satellite Systems
authors: Zhaoxian Yang, Xinkun Zheng, Miaoran Peng, Yu Zhang, Jiaxi Zhou, Tao Jiang
abstract: This paper proposes a novel integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) waveform, termed low-PAPR binary offset carrier multiplexed orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (LPB-OFDM), designed for low-earth orbit (LEO) satellite systems to simultaneously support reliable broadband communication and high-precision continuous navigation, while accommodating multiple navigation services. First, we develop a vectorized representation for the multi-carrier navigation signal and theoretically characterize its inherent peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) properties. Second, we present the time-frequency structure of LPB-OFDM, which exploits the navigation component on the lowest-frequency subcarrier to implicitly convey the phase sequence index required to minimize the PAPR of the integrated waveform. Furthermore, a comprehensive theoretical analysis of the mutual interference between the two components is conducted, and closed-form expressions for the communication bit error rate (BER) and navigation ranging accuracy are derived. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed waveform achieves significant PAPR reduction and enables high-precision positioning, all while maintaining robust communication performance with negligible degradation.

title: Research on Optimization of Logistics Network Inspection Path Based on Genetic Algorithm
authors: Lixin Xue, Shuang Zhang, Haoyang Liu, Chengyan Liu, Yongyi Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the continuous development of the e-commerce industry, logistics hubs have become deeply intertwined with people’s daily lives. Consumers can purchase goods from thousands of miles away with just a few taps on their smartphones. However, behind this convenience and efficiency, we must also recognize that regulatory oversights at some logistics hubs have been exploited by criminals, enabling the circulation of illicit drugs. Therefore, inspecting logistics hubs is a critical task in public security patrols. To enhance inspection efficiency and overcome the limitations of individual experience in route optimization, this paper introduces genetic algorithms to optimize inspection routes for logistics hubs. Experimental results demonstrate that genetic algorithms effectively address route planning challenges. The optimized routes achieve time savings and shorter distances, highlighting the effectiveness and practicality of genetic algorithms in logistics anti-drug inspection scenarios.

title: Research on the Design of Information Network Security Defense System Based on GABP Neural Network Algorithm
authors: Qingqing Ren, Kai Yun, Haomiao Tian, Wanpeng Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of information network, network security has become a key issue. This paper presents the design and research of information network security defense system based on genetic algorithm back propagation (GABP) neural network algorithm. The system integrates data preprocessing, feature extraction, intrusion detection and defense strategy generation through modular architecture. The two-stage feature extraction method of information gain and PCA is adopted, and the BP neural network is optimized by genetic algorithm to improve the convergence speed and detection performance. The results show that the accuracy rate of GABP neural network is 97.9%, the accuracy rate is 98.1%, the recall rate is 98.2%, and the F1 score is 98.0%, which are better than the traditional model. The system can adaptively generate defense strategies, with a high average interception rate against four types of attacks and a normal traffic passing rate of more than 99.3%. Improve the efficiency and accuracy of network intrusion detection and defense, and provide new methods for information network security defense.

title: AIGC Enhanced Anomaly Detection in Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Kamilla Abdullayeva, Diyorbek Gaffarov, Xasan Zikiryoyrv, Jafar Abbas, Wesley O'Quinn
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: With the advent of Industry 4.0, the ubiquity of sensors in large scale industrial equipment increased exponentially. This flood of cheap and reliable sensors allowed monitoring of critical equipment with real-time cloud access to the readings. With most industries squarely in the middle of this paradigm, the ability to utilize this sensor data is at the forefront in many industries. The methodology utilized to accomplish predictions typically revolves around semi-supervised learning, where models are only trained on good data. The reason this methodology is needed is due to the lack of anomalous data to train models on. This is not ideal in some cases, as supervised learning can be a powerful tool, particularly when there is a desire to predict Remaining Useful Life (RUL). Recent advances in generative artificial intelligence–such as diffusion learning–potentially enables the ability to increase the training set in such a way that supervised learning can be used in this anomaly detection framework. This work investigates the use of Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) for the purpose of providing augmented training samples. We demonstrate that current methods can generate extremely high quality training samples in this context and improve predictions metrics.

title: Quantitative Evaluation Method for Well integrity Risk in Heavy Oil Steam Stimulation Wells
authors: Xueqiang Wang, Haiqing Long, Hongliang Yi, Jian Ding, Jinlong Wang, Chuanlei Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: After multiple high-temperature cyclic steam stimulation (CSS) operations, the barrier performance of heavy oil wells gradually deteriorates, leading to potential risks of high-temperature steam leakage. To address the well integrity risk associated with heavy oil CSS wells, a barrier risk evaluation framework and design criteria were developed based on relevant well integrity standards and the specific characteristics of CSS injection–production processes. The proposed method considers the effects of key operating parameters—including injection temperature, injection pressure, injection rate, and the number of steam stimulation cycles—under high-temperature and multi-cycle working conditions. A barrier damage coefficient was introduced to quantify barrier degradation, while the combined effects of high-temperature steam leakage, thermal diffusion, and overpressure injury were analyzed to evaluate barrier failure risk. On this basis, a quantitative assessment method for well integrity risk in heavy oil CSS wells was established and validated through a field case study. The results show that the proposed model can quantitatively determine the wellbore integrity risk level and identify dominant risk factors. Considering the degradation of barrier performance under high-temperature service conditions, the calculated failure probability increases from 3.523 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup> to 10.774 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−5</sup>. The introduction of the barrier damage coefficient significantly enhances the objectivity of the evaluation. When the injection temperature rises from 200 °C to 400 °C, the steam leakage influence radius increases from 10.61 m to 11.49 m, indicating that injection temperature is the primary factor influencing wellbore integrity failure risk. Moreover, the proposed method establishes a quantitative and interpretable integrity risk representation, which provides a foundation for foundation-model-driven risk inference, blockchain-based integrity data management, and intelligent monitoring systems for thermal recovery wells.

title: ICECCO 2026 Reviewers
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)

title: Research on Evaluating the Operational and Maintenance Development Level of Smart Grids under the Generation-Grid-Load-Storage Environment
authors: Guanglin Hu, Chen Yang, Jiao Yan, Yueping Zhong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: The phenomenon of global warming has drawn significant attention from countries around the world. As the highest proportion of carbon emissions, the power industry’s low-carbon transformation has become the focus of concern. New electricity system, with the integration of generation-grid-load-storage (GGLS), provides an important means for addressing the generation of renewable and clean energy. The quality and safety of power supply are directly related to the effectiveness of intelligent operation and maintenance management of GGLS. Therefore, evaluating its intelligent operation and maintenance management level is of great significance for improving the clean and stable power supply. The paper firstly establishes an indicator system for intelligent operation and maintenance management. Then, based on the indicator system, the fuzzy analytic hierarchy process, capable of scientifically and conveniently handling both qualitative and quantitative data, is selected as the evaluation method. Finally, we conduct empirical analysis in combination with the intelligent operation and maintenance management of project D under GGLS environment. The research results confirm the effectiveness of the indicator system and method proposed, and provide relevant suggestions through examples. They can provide theoretical support and practical guidance for the intelligent operation and maintenance management of GGLS and also offer references and guidance for the intelligent operation and maintenance management of other similar power enterprises.

title: Research on Missile-Borne Micro-Inertial/BeiDou Scalar Depth Integrated Navigation Technology
authors: Fangjin Deng, Xin Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Precision strikes are the foundation for future information warfare. Adding guidance modules to artillery shells can provide a benchmark for trajectory correction and effectively improve the accuracy of shell hits. Currently, the most commonly used integrated navigation system is the inertial navigation/BeiDou-Songhua combination mode, which combines the position and velocity outputs of the two subsystems. However, high-speed flying vehicles such as airplanes and rockets have a Doppler frequency shift of ±50 to 100 kHz, which causes the receiver carrier tracking loop to lose lock and is difficult to re-acquire quickly. At this time, the inertial navigation/BeiDou Song combined navigation mode will switch to single inertial navigation mode. This paper studies the micro-inertial/BeiDou scalar depth integration technology, which combines measurement information with the inertial navigation system using pseudorange and pseudorange rate. The combined velocity and acceleration information is fed back to correct the acquisition and tracking loop of the receiver, which can effectively improve the loop tracking and control capability of the integrated navigation system under high dynamic conditions.

title: Teleoperating Autonomous Vehicles over Commercial 5G Networks: Are We There Yet?
authors: Rostand A. K. Fezeu, Jason Carpenter, Rushikesh Zende, Sree Ganesh Lalitaditya Divakarla, Nitin Varyani, Faaiq Bilal, Steven Sleder, Nanditha Naik, Duncan Joly, Eman Ramadan, Ajay Kumar Gurumadaiah, Zhi-Li Zhang
abstract: Remote driving, or teleoperating Autonomous Vehicles (AVs), is a key application that emerging 5G networks aim to support. In this paper, we conduct a systematic feasibility study of AV teleoperation over commercial 5G networks from both cross-layer and end-to-end (E2E) perspectives. Given the critical importance of timely delivery of sensor data, such as camera and LiDAR data, for AV teleoperation, we focus in particular on the performance of uplink sensor data delivery. We analyze the impact of Physical Layer (PHY layer) 5G radio network factors, including channel conditions, radio resource allocation, and Handovers (HOs), on E2E latency performance. We also examine the impact of 5G networks on the performance of upper-layer protocols and E2E application Quality-of-Experience (QoE) of the adaptation mechanisms used for real-time sensor data delivery, such as Real-Time Streaming Protocol (RTSP) and Web Real Time Communication (WebRTC). Our study reveals the challenges posed by today’s 5G networks and the limitations of existing sensor data streaming mechanisms. The insights gained will help inform the co-design of future-generation wireless networks, edge cloud systems, and applications to overcome the low-latency barriers in AV teleoperation.

title: MetaFlex: A Flexible Architecture for Efficient Packet Scheduling and Memory Allocation
authors: Anthony Dalleggio, Peixuan Gao, Yongbo Gao, Hao Wang, Yang Xu, H. Jonathan Chao
abstract: Packet schedulers are essential for managing packet transmission order in high-speed networks, where scheduling metadata must be processed at line rate under bursty traffic conditions. In such systems, packet scheduling and traffic management operate on compact packet descriptors rather than on full packets. FIFO-based schedulers are attractive for their simplicity and throughput, but implementations that statically partition descriptor memory across queues must provision for worst-case occupancy, leading to inefficient memory utilization. This paper presents MetaFlex, a scalable architecture for efficient implementation of calendar-queue–based packet scheduling using shared descriptor memory. MetaFlex dynamically allocates descriptor storage to scheduling queues on demand, allowing memory to be effectively shared across a large number of rank bins while maintaining constant-time enqueue and dequeue operations. Rather than introducing a new scheduling algorithm, MetaFlex focuses on the architectural realization of shared-memory descriptor queues that scale to large queue counts with predictable timing and modest hardware cost. We evaluate MetaFlex using NS2 simulations of weighted fair scheduling and a hardware prototype implemented in VHDL on an AMD/Xilinx Alveo U250 FPGA board. Simulation results show that MetaFlex achieves comparable or lower packet loss than fixed-memory calendar queues under identical descriptor-memory budgets, while using substantially less descriptor storage under typical traffic conditions. The FPGA prototype operates at 322 MHz, sustains 100 Gb/s line rate for packets larger than 370 bytes, and uses less than 1% of logic resources and less than 5% of on-chip memory, demonstrating the practicality of MetaFlex for high-speed hardware datapaths.

title: Consolidated Fe/FeSi Mixtures for Applications Requiring Increased Frequency Stability of Magnetic Properties_supp1-3686591.pdf
authors: Zuzana Bircakova
abstract: The study investigates the effect of the ratio of Fe/FeSi, as well as the different process additives and compaction parameters, on the magnetic properties of Fe-6.5Si/Fe@phosphate soft magnetic composites, obtained by a method of mixing of different commercially used powder materials. With the worlds´ rapid advancement in 5G, and the development of 6G mobile communications, the robotics, the emerging new applications as electrical vehicles, or the renewable energy industries as photovoltaic technology, the soft magnetic materials components for power and electronic devices operating at high frequencies towards megahertz range and beyond, are urgently required. The goal of improving the high frequency characteristics of the resulting composites with concurrently maintained their cost-effective mass production capability was fulfilled, and the compositions with a significantly enhanced frequency stability of magnetic permeability were found (e.g. for 50/50 wt. % ratio: stable real part of complex relative permeability of ~65, the resonant frequency of ~1 MHz). The relatively low core energy losses (~80 J/m3 at 1 kHz, 0.2 T) is ensured by a sufficiently high specific resistivity (~0.1 Ω.cm), the values comparable with the current literature ones. The assessment of magnetic and electrical properties indicates the effective insulation of ferromagnetic particles, and the inorganic insulation furthermore enables the Fe-6.5Si powder to be used as a ferromagnetic component for composites intended also to higher temperature applications.

title: A Multi-Strategy Enhanced Zebra Optimization Algorithm and Its Application
authors: Jiatong Shang, Shengli Xie, Wei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Constrained engineering optimization problems are often characterized by high dimensionality, strong nonlinearity, and multiple local optima, posing significant challenges for traditional methods. This paper aims to improve the performance of the zebra optimization algorithm (ZOA) for such complex problems. However, there is an imbalance between exploration and exploitation, a loss of population diversity, and poor local exploitation in the original ZOA. To address these issues, an improved ZOA (IZOA) is proposed by integrating differential evolution (DE), lens imaging opposition-based learning (LIOBL), Lévy flight, and a dynamic scaling factor. These strategies are integrated into many algorithmic phases to create a synergistic framework that improves both local exploitation and global exploration, boosting convergence robustness and accuracy Experimental results on CEC benchmark functions and a compression spring design problem show that IZOA outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms. In particular, it achieves the best mean objective value of 0.01270956, demonstrating higher accuracy and stability for constrained engineering optimization.

title: RLMToT: A Thought Tree-Based Model for Sentiment Triplet Extraction from MOOC Reviews
authors: Huaibo Chen, Huibing Zhang, Lei Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Accurately identifying aspect terms and their implicit sentiments in MOOC reviews provides valuable insights for improving course quality. This paper proposes a Thought Tree-based sentiment triplet extraction model for MOOC reviews, aiming to precisely detect aspect terms, opinion expressions, and sentiment polarities. First, a MOOC-specific knowledge base is constructed using retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) techniques based on course metadata and lecture transcripts, enhancing the model’s ability to identify aspect terms. Then, a Tree of Thought (ToT) reasoning mechanism is introduced to guide the large language model (LLM) in step-by-step inference of aspect terms, opinion expressions, and sentiment polarities. At each stage, the model evaluates and refines intermediate outputs to ensure the accuracy and completeness of sentiment triplet extraction. Experimental results on the constructed MOOC dataset show that the proposed model achieves an F1-score of $62.71 \%$. Furthermore, comparative experiments on four benchmark datasets-Lap14, Res14, Res15, and Res16demonstrate that the model outperforms baseline methods, with F1-score improvements of $6.03 \%, 4.84 \%, 9.08 \%$, and $5.69 \%$, respectively.

title: PPSplitFed: Privacy-Preserving Splitfed Learning via Functional Encryption
authors: Jing Ma, Xixiang Lyu, Yong Yu, Stephan Sigg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Federated learning (FL) and split learning (SL) are two emerging distributed learning mechanisms that enable model training without sharing the local data. The integration of FL and SL, called splitfed learning (SFL), reduces the training burden on the client side and parallelizes the model training to achieve efficient distributed model optimization without sharing the local data and the whole global model. Currently, concerns about the privacy leakage risks of splitfed learning have garnered much attention, but effective solutions have not yet been fully explored. We propose a novel privacy-preserving splitfed learning framework, named PPSplitFed, that guarantees the confidentiality of the intermediate parameters to protect data and model privacy. By designing a secure model training algorithm based on single-input functional encryption and a secure model aggregation algorithm based on multi-input func tional encryption, PPSplitFed achieves secure model optimization. We describe the implementations of PPSplitFed on Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models and compare the performance with centralized learning (CL), FL, SL, SFL, and the state-of-the-art privacy-preserving SFL scheme, as well as privacy-preserving model training scheme on multi-sourced datasets. The experimental results show that PPSplitFed is effective and efficient with reduced computation and communication costs while maintaining the model accuracy.

title: An INS/DVL/PS Integrated Underwater System Based on NLS-Transformer-LSTM Velocity Prediction Model
authors: Xinhui Zhang, Wenfeng Nie, Yangfan Liu, Yubo Li, Tianhe Xu
abstract: High-precision underwater navigation is a prerequisite for underwater vehicles to explore the marine environment and resources. Inertial navigation system (INS), Doppler velocity logger (DVL) and Pressure sensor (PS) onboard the underwater vehicles are favored for integrate due to their effectiveness and user-friendly configuration. Specifically, the velocity measurement from the DVL is important to reduce the accumulated error of the INS. Different from the traditional filter-based fusion solution, we firstly introduce a Factor graph optimization (FGO) framework to integrate the INS/DVL/PS navigation system. Since the DVL observations often experience outliers and outage due to the harsh sea condition and range limitation, it can lead to rapid position drift and error accumulation in the navigation system. Thus, we develop an outlier detection scheme based on the improved interquartile range method, which combines sliding window and dynamic threshold adjustment of the propeller revolutions per minute (RPM). Then, we propose a DVL velocity prediction method based on the nonlinear least squares (NLS)-Transformer-LSTM model. We refine the RPM by NLS and use it as one of the key features in the prediction model. Finally, the constructed system is compared with several classical Kalman filters through both simulated and measured experiments. The performance of the NLS-Transformer-LSTM model is comprehensively evaluated. The results indicate that the system can provide more accurate pseudo-DVL velocity estimates. This capability ensures more stable and reliable underwater navigation accuracy during DVL outages. Consequently, it offers strong support for autonomous underwater vehicle applications in complex marine environments.

title: Development of a Low-Cost Flex-Sensor and Machine Learning Based Sign Language Interpreter
authors: Adeyinka Ajao Adewale, Wou Onn Choo, Ebubechukwu I. Onwudiegwu, John S. Wejin, Aitimbetov Maksetbay, Stephen Aderibigbe
abstract: Over the years, sign language has been a paramount approach for bridging the communication gap between people with normal hearing and those with hearing challenges, thereby achieving inclusive education and social integration. With the diversity of modern-day gestures and body positions during communication, developing automated Signed Language Interpreters (SLIs) and recognition systems is complex and challenging. Thus, scientists and researchers worldwide are developing simpler and more affordable SLI systems. This research addresses the issue of complex, unaffordable, and ineffective communication between hearing- and hearing-impaired communities by developing a wearable SLI using a flex-sensor-based glove and a machine learning algorithm. The primary aim is to assist individuals with hearing impairments by converting American Sign Language (ASL) finger spelling into text. The study focused on ASL due to its extensive research base, global reach, and historical ties with Nigerian sign language. The methodology involved designing and integrating flex sensors into a glove to capture hand gestures, which were then processed using a machine learning algorithm to interpret the ASL finger spelling. The hardware components included flex sensors, a NodeMCU-32S LUA development board (based on the ESP32 microcontroller), and an MPU6050 accelerometer. At the same time, the software implementation utilised Python and the Arduino Integrated Development Environment (IDE) for data acquisition and model development. The results demonstrated the system's effectiveness in accurately interpreting ASL finger spelling, with significant improvements in training and test accuracy over multiple epochs. The system achieved satisfactory performance, indicating its potential for real-world applications in assisting communication for people who are deaf or hard of hearing. The implications of an accuracy of 92% indicate that a low-cost wearable device can interpret sign language correctly, bridging communication gaps for deaf people, thereby enhancing SLI availability and enabling hearing-challenged individuals to interact more freely and confidently across various social settings.

title: Influences of bending degree on non-contact gap and pressure measurements by using eddyingized conductive polymer composite
authors: Luheng Wang, Zhen Gui
abstract: The advancement of flexible sensing technology has created a strong demand for reliable operation on curved surfaces, but characterizing and predicting sensor performance under bending remains a fundamental challenge. This study systematically investigates the influence of bending deformation on the impedance response of a flexible eddyingized sensor made from a carbon nanotube/silicone rubber composite. The research reveals that curvature significantly modulates the electromagnetic-structure interplay, altering both the magnetic coupling and the internal conductive network response across non-contact, loading, and relaxation stages. A larger radius of curvature enhances impedance magnitude variation but reduces impedance angle change, while also decreasing the critical impedance and DC resistance. By integrating these curvature-dependent features with an interpolation algorithm, a sensing methodology is established to decouple gap and pressure under arbitrary curvature conditions accurately. This work provides both mechanistic insight and a practical framework for curvature-adaptive flexible sensing, laying the foundation for high-performance soft robotics and bio-integrated devices operating on curved surfaces.

title: Fringe projection profilometry method based on dual-epipolar and phase constraint
authors: Limei Song, Zongyang Zhang, Haozhen Huang
abstract: This letter proposes a stereo matching method based on dual-epipolar constraint (DEC) and phase neighborhood constraint (PNC). Epipolar constraints are established according to the spatial relationship among the left camera, projector, and right camera, which narrows the search range between left and right images with low computational cost. Fine matching is achieved using phase neighborhood constraint to quickly obtain high-precision 3D data. This method enables an average improvement of over 40% in the overall computational speed of stereo matching. In the reconstruction experiments of the standard sphere and checkerboard, the obtained root mean square errors (RMSE) are 0.023 mm and 0.018 mm, respectively. The PNC method reduces the RMSE induced by the DEC method by 99.19% and 98.10%, respectively.

title: DRL-Assisted Null Space Aware (DNSA) Framework for MU-MISO ISAC Systems
authors: Amit Agarwal
abstract: This paper presents a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-assisted framework for joint communication and sensing in next-generation 6G systems. A novel DRL-assisted Null Space Aware (DNSA) approach is proposed for a multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) setup under practical power constraints. The proposed two-stage design employs Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to optimize beamforming for communication in the first stage. In the second stage, a radar sensing beam is constructed within the null space of the effective communication channel, ensuring interference-free sensing. A closed form solution for radar beamformer is derived. A dynamic power-splitting mechanism, controlled via a factor α, enables flexible trade-offs between sensing accuracy and communication throughput. Simulation results confirm that the DNSA framework achieves significant sensing gains (up to 18.6 dB) and high communication rates (up to 5.09 bps/Hz). A comparison with conventional techniques like Maximum Ratio Transmission (MRT) and Zero-Forcing (ZF) is also presented as baseline. Moreover, the learned beamforming policy adapts efficiently to spatial channel variations, and also could be easily modified for fairness based rate allocation offering intelligent resource allocation suited for 6G-enabled IoT environments. The simulation results further corroborate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Scheduling Training-Inference Co-Location in Demand Response for Sustainable Edge AI
authors: Konglin Zhu, Siyuan Wei, Xuan'er Wu, Lei Jiao, Jin Dong, Lin Zhang
abstract: In the pursuit of data privacy and reduced latency, the adoption of edge intelligence has surged. Meanwhile, the enormous increase in AI has resulted in significant energy consumption. Edge intelligence plays a crucial role in Energy Demand Response (EDR). However, existing edge intelligence falls short of meeting the demands of co-locating training and inference tasks while satisfying EDR. Specifically, the intertwinement between balancing energy consumption, system delay and model accuracy, and uncertain future inputs adds to the challenge of designing an online sustainable system for co-located training and inference tasks. To address these challenges, we propose a novel two-timescale system for co-locating training and inference EDR. Our approach satisfies EDR by strategically planning training schedules on macro-timescales and migrating inference requests between heterogeneous edges on micro-timescales while minimizing long-term cost. We introduce a novel online polynomial time algorithm that first breaks down the problem into two subproblems, which are subsequently solved using an online-learning-based fractional algorithm and a randomized roun ding algorithm, respectively. Rigorous analysis demonstrates that our approach achieves both sublinear dynamic regret and sublinear dynamic fit. Extensive trace-driven evaluations validate the practical superiority of our approach over multiple existing methods, highlighting its effectiveness in real-world scenarios.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Bio-Inspired Control for Stability Enhancement in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: T. Venkatamuni, B Pavani Srikavya, S. Bathrinath, S. Gayathri Priya, M Pandikumar, Abhishek Kumar Jain
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The current paper proposes a support system to increasing the stability of autonomous vehicles by using the combination of reinforcement learning (RL) and bio-inspired control strategy. In particular, we suggest to utilize genetic algorithms to learn optimal control to RL so that the system is able to learn in changing road conditions and disturbances. The genetic algorithm aids in the search of the solution space which seeks the best performing policy to enhance the vehicle stability control. We use the CARLA simulator as an instrument to evaluate and verify the developed approach in a range of driving situations, in sharp turns and slippery roads. The stability aspect of our approach is much better as the response times and change of control was smoother than in the traditional methods. The findings suggest that RL coupled with bio-inspired optimization procedures provides a resilient adaptive solution to obtaining stability improvement in autonomous vehicles, and thus it is suitable to real life situations in autonomous driving systems.

title: Estimation of Skin Surface Voltage Distribution Due to Stenosed Brachial Artery Using Numerical Technique
authors: Sankar Kandasamy, Kalpana Ramakrishnan
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Electrical bioimpedance is a non-invasive diagnostic technique that exploits the electrical properties of biological tissues to an applied sinusoidal current. Various tissue composition contributes to different impedance distribution inside tissues. This method is used to detect changes in tissues due to pathological conditions. Atherosclerosis is a vascular disease that causes stenosis due to plaque deposition and impedes blood flow. This causes ischemia and induces stroke. In this study, impedance changes in the upper arm caused by the plaque formation in the brachial artery are investigated. A numerical model of the upper arm with appropriate tissue electrical properties is developed, and a physiologically safe sinusoidal current is applied. The resulting surface voltage and impedance distributions are analyzed and compared with a healthy model. The results indicate that plaque presence produces change in the voltage distribution near artery and distinct surface voltage change in the stenosis region when compared with non-stenosis region, thus demonstrating the potential of bioimpedance analysis for peripheral artery assessment.

title: Early Detection of Dyslexia Based on the Analysis of Handwritten Images
authors: Jebisha Sweetylin A M, Abiraame A J, Dheepa M, Janani Arivudaiyanambi
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Learning disabilities often go undetected during the early developmental stages making it complex for treatment procedures thereby impacting academic performance and selfesteem. The objective of this work is to design and develop a handwriting-based tool for early detection of learning disabilities by leveraging machine learning techniques and also to classify the condition based on the severity levels. As a first step, the character-level detection is done using a dataset consisting of characters with the help of techniques namely, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and MobileNetV2. Secondly, sentence-level detection was performed using a different dataset based on a hybrid model of MobileNetV2+Bi-LSTM architecture. The classification showed promising results on character level recognition wherein Random Forest and SVM gave accuracies of 90 % and 82 %, respectively, for 2 classes, Normal and Reversal characters, and the MobileNetV2 for 3 classes, Normal, Reversal, Corrected gave an accuracy of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 6 \%}$</tex>. The classification of dyslexic and non-dyslexic categories based on handwritten sentences using the hybrid model of MobileNetV2+Bi-LSTM architecture gave an accuracy of 94 %. As an additional step, the developed algorithm classifies the dyslexic condition based on severity levels as low, mild, moderate, and severe. The models have been tested on real data obtained from dyslexic children. This research brings together a solution to fill the lacuna in early dyslexia screening by focusing on handwritten images' analysis thereby providing an easy way for meaningful assessment.

title: RegEase: Simplifying Insurance Compliance
authors: Samhitha Poreddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Regulatory compliance is not just necessary, but a transformative journey for organizations in highly regulated industries such as insurance, finance, and healthcare. The traditional approach to compliance reviews, often manual, can hinder progress, leading to inefficiencies and increased risk. In this paper, we introduce RegEase, an innovative AI-driven solution that revolutionizes the regulatory compliance landscape. Our framework empowers users by automatically extracting text from regulatory documents, intelligent summarization of compliance obligations, and semantic retrieval of regulations using customizable metadata. This tool frees compliance officers at insurance companies from laborintensive tasks, enabling them to focus their energy and creativity on shaping and implementing effective compliance policies.

title: Deep Learning Based Vegetable Price Forecasting for Nepalese Markets: An STL Attention LSTM Approach
authors: Pranaya Nakarmi, Nabin Lamichhane, Nabaraj Subedi, Janak Kumar Lal, Pravin Poudel, Bikrant Poudel
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Agricultural price volatility poses significant challenges to food security in developing economies. This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines Seasonal-Trend decomposition using local Estimated Scatterplot Smoothing (STL) with attention-enhanced Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for daily vegetable price forecasting. The STL component explicitly decomposes price series into interpretable trend, seasonal, and residual patterns, while the attention mechanism automatically identifies temporally important observations. The model integrates 11.5 years of historical price data from Kalimati market with meteorological variables and cultural event indicators. Evaluation on five major vegetables demonstrates strong predictive capability with mean coefficient of determination of 0.833 and mean absolute percentage error of 15.67%. Analysis of the attention weights further demonstrated that the vegetable price forecasts made by the LSTM and Attention model rely heavily upon data from vegetables traded on the market approximately 18-22 days prior to the forecast day, which confirms that the LSTM+Attention model is best suited to detect relatively short-term trends in the agricultural market.

title: AI-Powered Health Monitoring Using IoT Devices and Wearable Technologies for Continuous Patient Assessment
authors: Sanjay Kurkute, Jai Singh W., R Sujitha, Ajitesh Kumar, Keerthika J, Ravi Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Continuous remote patient monitoring is becoming critical with the increasing burden of chronic diseases, but existing wearable-based patient monitoring systems are limited in terms of accuracy and false alarms and face challenges with heterogeneous sensor data integration within the real world settings. To overcome these difficulties, an AI-based health monitoring system based on multimodal IoT and wearable signals is studied in this paper for continuous cardiac monitoring. The goal is to create a powerful and low-latency privacy-aware system that is able to reliably detect events in ambulatory environments. The methodology takes a combination of edge preprocessing to happen, uncertainty aware multimodal fusion, personalized model-adaptation and a hybrid clinician-AI labeling pipeline. Experiments using big data (600 subjects, 9 months follow up) demonstrate substantial performance improvements over 5 state of the art baselines for 0.92 sensitivity, 0.96 specificity, 0.89 PPV and 0.12 false alarms for patients per patient-day with clinically feasible latency. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the combination of signal quality awareness fusion and adaptive personalization, which contributes to the robustness and clinical relevance in the real world. Overall, the study demonstrates evidence that AI-driven multimodal monitoring can help to move forward the standard of care for patients through proactive care and help support early detection of clinically actionable events.

title: Multi-Modal Fusion Approach Combining LSTM and CNN Models for Parkinson’s Detection from Speech and Handwriting Data
authors: Guntumadugu Venkata Sravan, Guntumadugu Revanth, Gangireddy Vishnu Vardhan Reddy, Ajay Sharma, Kamalpreet Kaur, Rachit Garg
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Parkinson’s disease is a degenerative neurological condition that progressively interferes with normal nervous system activity, leading to deterioration in motor and speech capabilities, which makes early identification crucial for prompt medical intervention. In this study, two deep learning approaches were independently applied to handwriting and speech datasets to identify the presence of Parkinson’s disease. Handwriting spiral images were pre-processed using Sobel edge detection to emphasise stroke irregularities caused by shock, and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was trained to classify the images. Speech recordings were converted to Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficient (MFCC) spectrograms to reveal vocal characteristics affected by PD. After that, a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model was trained to capture temporal changes. The effectiveness of both models was assessed through accuracy, loss progression, confusion matrix, and receiver operating characteristic (ROC) analysis. The findings indicate that both CNN and LSTM models can successfully distinguish Parkinson patients from healthy individuals, with gradually increasing accuracy across epochs. The results confirm that handwriting irregularities and speech pattern abnormalities are effective non-invasive digital biomarkers for PD assessment. This research highlights the relevance of deep learning in medical decision support systems for the prompt identification of Parkinson’s disease.

title: A Parallel Hardware Architecture for the Twiddless Fast Fourier Transform (TFFT)
authors: Rajesh Kumar Mahato, Madhav Prasad Pandey, Sushant Adhikari
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: The Twiddless Fast Fourier Transform (TFFT) is a non-interpolated and non-decimated DFT algorithm for integer multiples of a power of two signal lengths. This paper presents a customized and parallel hardware architecture for the TFFT algorithm and compares the computational complexity of the proposed design with direct DFT and radix-2 FFT algorithms. The design have been implemented on a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) using Verilog and follows the IEEE-754 single-precision floating-point standard. DSP58 primitives have been utilized to perform the required arithmetic operations, and the design is verified through simulation using the Vivado software environment. This paper also evaluates resource utilization and latency for different signal lengths, verifying that while the arithmetic operation requirements are lower than those of the direct DFT, the proposed approach achieves better memory optimization than the radix-2 FFT due to its support for a wider range of valid signal lengths.

title: Modeling Adoption Intention of Generative AI Chatbots in Higher Education: A Quantitative Decision-Oriented Analysis
authors: Shubham Puranik, Loveleen Gaur, Ramakrishnan Raman
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) chatbots application in educational settings is influenced by measurable decisions and utility features rather than understanding or perceived risk. We propose a measurable adoption intention model utilizing survey data collected from 131 university students. Multiple constructs demonstrating perceived benefits, awareness, concerns, and institutional challenges were analyzed using multiple linear regression. The model explained 30.0% of the variance in adoption intention (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.300, F = 13.47, p < 0.001). Perceived benefits emerged as the dominant predictor (β = 0.428, p < 0.001), while awareness (p = 0.906), concerns (p = 0.574), and challenges (p = 0.103) did not demonstrate self-sufficient statistical significance. The results imply that adoption intention is predominantly utility-driven rather than controlled by familiarity or perceived risk. This study contributes a quantitative, system-level viewpoint on the implementation of GenAI chatbots in higher education.

title: The Limits of Macroscopic Analysis in Global Protest Dynamics: Evaluating Static Clustering vs. Microscopic Complexity
authors: Krishnanand Badu, Sumitra Gyawali, Shailesh Pandey, Nischal Regmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: In the quantitative analysis of political instability, a fundamental question remains: can global protest dynamics be adequately understood through a macroscopic, static lens, or do they strictly require microscopic, context-specific time series analysis? This study investigates this dichotomy using the Integrated Crisis Early Warning System (ICEWS) dataset (1995–2022). Since protest data is inherently sequential, we argue that standard static methods are insufficient. We employ four clustering methodologies designed to handle temporal data: Standard K-means (baseline), DTW K-means, DFT-based K-means, and ARIMA-based K-means. While internal validation metrics consistently suggest a two-cluster solution, further inspection of the underlying ARIMA parameters reveals that these clusters differ primarily in scale rather than in temporal dynamics. This negative result provides empirical evidence that macroscopic clustering approaches fail to capture the heterogeneity of protest behavior, supporting the necessity of microscopic, country-specific time-series analysis to understand the evolution and drivers of political unrest.

title: Remarks on the Relevance of Privacy Expectations for Default Opt-out Settings
authors: Sebastian Zimmeck
abstract: Over the past few years an increasing number of states in the US have adopted new privacy laws. The majority of these laws require compliance with universal opt-out mechanisms (UOOMs), which allow consumers to send legally binding opt-out signals. However, a number of laws generally do not allow UOOMs to be enabled by default. While some laws exempt privacy-protective software from this prohibition, the exemption does not apply to pre-installed software, e.g., a privacy-protective web browser bundled with an operating system. The reason for not allowing default opt-out settings for pre-installed software is to ensure that settings reflect consumers’ “affirmative, freely given, and unambiguous choice,” as, for example, the Colorado Privacy Act (CPA) is putting it. However, prohibiting vendors of privacy-protective software from turning on UOOMs by default can force them into committing unfair or deceptive acts or practices under the FTC Act and equivalent state laws. Thus, whether UOOMs can be turned on by default on pre-installed software should depend on consumers’ privacy expectations. For pre-installed software that is creating a reasonable expectation for consumers that their privacy will be protected, the simple use of such software should be considered a valid choice for enabling UOOMs. In such software a turned-on UOOM is not a “default setting” but rather the software’s inherent behavior that a consumer expects and chooses through its use. This interpretation of consumer choice is preferable under the CPA and similar laws as it grounds the notice and choice principle in the privacy expectations of consumers and enables companies to compete on better privacy for consumers.

title: Introduction
authors: N/A

title: Discrete Tokenization for Multimodal LLMs: A Comprehensive Survey_supp1-3676982.pdf
authors: Menglin Yang
