abstract: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has intensified the need for effective mechanisms to transform continuous multimodal data into discrete representations suitable for language-based processing. Discrete tokenization, with vector quantization (VQ) as a central approach, offers both computational efficiency and compatibility with LLM architectures. Despite its growing importance, there is a lack of a comprehensive survey that systematically examines VQ techniques in the context of LLM-based systems. This work fills this gap by presenting the first structured taxonomy and analysis of discrete tokenization methods designed for LLMs. We categorize 8 representative VQ variants that span classical and modern paradigms and analyze their algorithmic principles, training dynamics, and integration challenges with LLM pipelines. Beyond algorithm-level investigation, we discuss existing research in terms of classical applications without LLMs, LLM-based single-modality systems, and LLM-based multimodal systems, highlighting how quantization strategies influence alignment, reasoning, and generation performance. In addition, we identify key challenges including codebook collapse, unstable gradient estimation, and modality-specific encoding constraints. Finally, we discuss emerging research directions such as dynamic and task-adaptive quantization, unified tokenization frameworks, and biologically inspired codebook learning. This survey bridges the gap between traditional vector quantization and modern LLM applications, serving as a foundational reference for the development of efficient and generalizable multimodal systems. A continuously updated version is available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/jindongli-Ai/LLM-Discrete-Tokenization-Survey</uri>.

title: Efficient Algorithm for Early Stage Detection of Breast Cancer for Microwave Imaging
authors: Saranya N, Raja P, Nithyalakshmi S, Rubika Sakthi B, Sahithya T S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Timely identification of breast cancer is crucial for enhancing survival rates and the efficacy of treatment. Conventional imaging modalities like mammography and MRI, while effective, are constrained by factors such as radiation exposure, elevated costs, and diminished sensitivity in dense breast tissues. Microwave imaging (MWI) has emerged as a viable alternative owing to its non-ionizing, cost-effective, and safe characteristics. This study evaluates three reconstruction methods: Delay and Sum (DAS), Delay Multiply and Sum (DMAS), and a Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR)-based algorithm, utilising pre-scanned breast phantom datasets. The results indicate that the DAS algorithm outperforms DMAS and GPR-based methods, delivering precise tumour localisation and enhanced image quality. The research establishes DAS as the most effective method for reconstructing early-stage breast tumour detection.

title: Analysis of Discord-Based Remote Access Threats: Detection and Prevention
authors: Rakshit Dogra, Sonika Vasesi, Harish Kumar Mittal, Swati Juneja
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This research study looks at an emerging cybersecurity threat, malicious software disguised as legitimate applications that uses Discord's bot API to establish covert command and control channels. These attacks can take screenshots, collect keystrokes, access webcams, and exfiltrate sensitive data while escaping typical antivirus detection. Through controlled laboratory investigation, to demonstrate how these attacks work, why they frequently go undetected, and provide both technical and user-oriented solutions. The findings emphasize the substantial security implications of messaging platform APIs, as well as the necessity for improved detection systems. This study intends to increase awareness of these vulnerabilities among security professionals, platform developers, and end users in order to support more effective prevention solutions.

title: AI-Augmented Threat Intelligence Framework for Proactive Vulnerability Assessment in Evolving Enterprise Networks
authors: Kismat Chhillar, Saurabh Shrivastava, Deepak Tomar, Alok Verma
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The rapid evolution of cyber threats demands proactive security strategies beyond conventional methods that heavily depend on human expertise and static models. This paper presents an advanced AI-driven threat intelligence framework designed to detect vulnerabilities and improve situational awareness in dynamic enterprise networks. By integrating machine learning, natural language processing, and contextual analytics, the framework automates threat identification, analysis, and response, enhancing detection accuracy while reducing false positives and incident response times. It empowers security teams to anticipate and mitigate emerging vulnerabilities across complex digital infrastructures. Comprehensive architectural and methodological details are provided alongside case studies demonstrating performance improvements across industries. The paper also discusses challenges faced during implementation and proposes strategies to embed ethical considerations and privacy protections. Ultimately, AI’s integration marks a paradigm shift in enterprise cybersecurity, essential for addressing an increasingly hostile digital landscape.

title: Energy-Aware Dynamic TDD Scheduling in 5G RAN Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Amit Anand, Prabhat Thakur, Valluri Murthy, Priyanka Anand, Neelam Gupta, Ghanshyam Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The rapid expansion of 5G networks has increased Radio Access Network (RAN) energy consumption, leading to higher costs and environmental concerns. Static Time Division Duplex (TDD) configurations fail to adapt to fluctuating traffic demands, resulting in spectrum and power wastage. This paper proposes an AI-driven D-TDD framework utilizing Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) within an O-RAN architecture for real-time, energy-aware slot adaptation. The proposed system automatically adjusts uplink and downlink slot ratios, predicts idle transmission opportunities, and activates O-RU smart sleep modes to conserve power while maintaining Quality of Service (QoS). FlexRIC integration, OpenAirInterface (OAI), and USRP B210 hardware testing were employed to validate the framework. Long-term energy savings of up to 65 kWh were observed in realistic macrocell simulations, particularly under low traffic conditions (0–40% load), without compromising throughput. The results demonstrate that AI-driven dynamic TDD scheduling is scalable and robust for energy-efficient 5G and beyond-6G RAN deployments.

title: Automated Kidney Disease Classification Using Transfer Learning with VGG19 on Ultrasound Images
authors: M Sravan Kumar Babu, Panja Aparna, Puppala Mishritha, Paidipala Bhavani, Punem Hasini, Puli Deepika
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Kidney disease is one of the leading causes of chronic illness, and imaging is important for early detection, making its diagnosis and management challenging. In this study, we present an optimized deep learning model by using VGG19 architecture for automatically classifying CKD kidney ultrasound images into four categories: Cyst, Normal, Stone and Tumor. We used a pretrained VGG19 model that was fine-tuned by making the last convolutional blocks trainable again, and added several Batch Normalization and Dropout layers to increase generalization and decrease overfitting. Multiple preprocessing and data augmentation strategies were performed to make sure the system is robust to differences in the ultrasound images. We provide the highest ever reported test accuracy of 99.68% on this dataset with our proposed model, with significant improvements against traditional CNN models in precision, recall and F1-score. This deep transfer learning model for kidney disease classification represents another step forward for high-performance classification framework development to aid and support radiologists in diagnosing kidney disease for improved clinical decision support systems.

title: Bulk-Driven high gain OTA using Quasi-Floating Gate Method for Low Frequency Application
authors: Sagarika Tripura, Anil Kumar Gautam, Tripurari Sharan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This work presents a low power, high gain bulk driven-quasi floating gate (BD-QFG) operational transconductance amplifier (OTA). Implemented in 0.18 μm CMOS technology using Tanner EDA tools, the OTA achieves a high open loop gain of 74 dB, a unity gain frequency (UGF) of 12.6 kHz, and a phase margin (PM) of 66.4° , with a −41.5 dB total harmonic distortion (THD) when driving a 10 pF capacitive load, while consuming a low power of 115 nW under a ± 0.3 V dual supply voltage. A MISO G<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>-C biquadratic filter is used to demonstrate practical applicability, capable of producing various filter responses without changing the circuit core. These findings support the proposed OTA suitability for biomedical systems.

title: AI-Powered Rockfall Prediction for Open-Pit Mines
authors: Karpagavadivu K, Divakar E, Kowsihash P, Sethusakthivel G
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Rockfall incidents in open-pit mines are a major risk to workforce safety, long-term industrial operations, and the stability of landforms and ecosystems. Current prediction methods utilize manual field inspections and empirical formulas, which may be time-consuming to deploy and inaccurate in highly dynamic geological environments. This project proposes an AI Rockfall Prediction and Risk analysis System to provide location-relevant and real-time predictions for rockfall hazards in Indian open-pit mining locations. The prediction system evaluates the likelihood of rockfalls by utilizing a Random Forest classifier that was trained on environmental and geological variables that have proven for a variety of rockfalls to be critical to rockfall at open pit mining locations that includes rainfall, slope angle, soil moisture, temperature, seismic vibrations and deformation. The implementation of the model in a Flask based Web Application provides geospatial interactive mapping (Leaflet.js), chart trends (Chart.js), and pdf, excel and word automated reports. The full system has secure user authentication, a hidden admin dashboard for user management and analytics, and a conversational rule-based AI computer chatbot that provides safety recommendations, statistics and prevention control measures. The system demonstrates a reliable and interpretable approach for data-driven rockfall risk assessment, supporting timely decision-making in open-pit mining environments. Our findings have demonstrated that machine learning and spatial intelligence when combined provide a valuable decision support tool to promote worker safety in open-pit mining regions. Overall, this research has demonstrated the value of combining spatiotemporal data sets, machine learning tools and AI for rockfall research to improve safety and decision-making in open-pit mining regions.

title: COVID-19 Detection from CT Scans Using Hybrid CNN-SNN Deep Learning
authors: Sriman G, Kamatchi K S
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Although RT-PCR determines COVID-19, its sensitivity variability and slow turnaround time made many scientists seek alternative methods to scan the body, which is CT. We provide an effective hybrid framework to detection of COVID 19 in thoracic CT scans by combining a CNN with a Spiking Neural Network (SNN). Our hybrid system, with CNNs used as a contentious feature extractor and the SNN used as an event-driven compute unit, was found to yield 97.2, 96.9 and 97.6 percent accuracy, sensitivity and specificity, respectively on the SARS-CoV-2 CT-Scan dataset. More to the point, it lowered the computational costs (in FLOPs) by 28 percent and inference latency by 27 percent in comparison to conventional models. Such efficiency jump is fundamental in implementing AI in the workflow in practice where the power resources of the computer can be finite. As shown in our work, a combination of CNNs and SNNs creates a strong, realistic avenue of AI-aided diagnosis, which can be applied to other respiratory conditions as well.

title: A Feature-Optimized Machine Learning Approach for Accurate Heart Disease Prognosis
authors: Shobha Rajak, Vasuja Midasal, S. Nagakishore
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In concert with heart attacks, heart diseases (HD) are the leading origin of death worldwide. Doctors cannot contact patients all the time. This work strives to embed several methods of feature selection to evolve an accurate ML approach for heart disease. Machine learning (ML) can carry diagnosis by laying a more accurate basis for predictions and decisions based on the data given by healthcare systems around the world. This work focuses on developing accurate ML techniques for predicting heart disease at the earliest stage using multiple methods feature selection. Features selection was executed using four different methods: chi-square test, analysis of variance (ANOVA), mutual information (MI), forward adaptation.SET-1, SET-2, SET-3, SET-4 four working groups Ultimately selected formed. The Thirteen different ensemble ML classifiers were used to regulate the optimal method and the most suitable set. These ML classifiers like KNN, Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting, Decision Tree, support vector classifier (SVC), GaussianNB, AdaBoost, Bagging, Extra Tree, MLP, Voting and Stacking. Included and named (M1, M2, ---, M13). To find a classifier that provides the precise cardiac disease prediction rate, we have applied synthetic minority oversampling technology (SMOTE) to address issues of imbalanced datasets. To understand how a system predicts the outcome, an explanatory approach to artificial intelligence is developed using the SHAP method. The bring forward technique has been shown to be promising for the medical field as it can predict early stages of heart disease at low cost and at a minimum time. At long last a web app was created that uses the best ML techniques to allow users to quickly get predictive of heart disease simply by entering HD symptoms.

title: Optimization of 3D Reconstruction Algorithm for Virtual Reality Environment Based on Computer Vision
authors: Shensen Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: When applying 3D reconstruction technology in virtual reality field, it is find that the real-time performance of 3D reconstruction algorithm is poor, and the details reconstruction is not very accurate in the dynamic scenes. In this paper, a hybrid 3D reconstruction algorithm is proposed based on computer vision. Firstly, the multi-scale feature fusion mechanism is proposed. Secondly, an iterative optimization strategy is designed to use the non-rigid deformation model to handle the motion estimation of dynamic objects and improve the temporal continuity. Finally, a parallel computing framework based on GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) is constructed. Finally, a semantic segmentation module is introduced to optimize the topological structure of the reconstruction results based on context. The experimental results show that the average Chamfer Distance of the static scene is decreased to 0.696mm, the reconstruction error of the dynamic scene is controlled within 1.52mm, and the frame rate of the processing is 26.4 FPS. The conclusion of this research is that the proposed algorithm improves the performance of 3D reconstruction in virtual reality environment through multi-scale feature fusion and dynamic optimization.

title: Enhancing U-Net with Attention-Guided Decoders and Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling for Tomato Plant Disease Segmentation
authors: E. Jansi, Kavitha BR
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In this paper, we propose AGAD-U-Net, a novel U-Net variant designed for precise plant leaf disease segmentation through an Attention-Guided Aggregation Decoder (AGAD). While traditional U-Net architectures rely on direct skip connections and standard upsampling operations, the AGAD module enhances the decoder by selectively integrating multi-scale attention-refined features, aiming to improve spatial consistency and feature fusion. To evaluate the effectiveness of AGAD-U-Net, we conducted a comparative analysis with baseline U-Net, Dilated U-Net, and ASPP-U-Net on a plant disease segmentation dataset. Experimental results show that while Dilated U-Net achieved the highest overall performance, AGAD-U-Net demonstrated competitive precision (0.6758) and accuracy (0.9431), and outperformed the baseline U-Net across multiple metrics. Although its IoU and F1 score were comparatively lower, AGAD-U-Net exhibited strong boundary refinement in qualitative observations. The findings suggest that the AGAD module offers a promising foundation for future decoder-level attention frameworks and motivates further optimization to fully exploit its capabilities in real-world agricultural applications.

title: Hybrid Model for Enhanced Crypto Currency Forecasting: Polygon (POL) Price Prediction with XGBoost GRU and BiLSTM
authors: A. Kalaivani, V.Asha, J.Sathya, Sahil Rajesh, Ranjita Nigale, Puneeth Kumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Crypto currency price prediction has become a critical area of financial research because of the rapid growth and changes in the financial market, decentralized structure, and high susceptibility to external factors such as investor sentiment and macroeconomic trends. Existing traditional models are not capable of handling the complex and time-dependent patterns within such data. This research paper proposes a hybrid model which integrates XGBoost, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) models to predict the forecasting of crypto currency. The dataset, sourced from the Coin Gecko API, includes 90 days of historical market data enriched with key technical indicators such as SMA-14, SMA-50, and RSI-14. XGBoost was employed for feature-level learning, while GRU and BiLSTM captured temporal dependencies and bidirectional context. Among all models, GRU exhibited the best performance with an RMSE of 0.2060, and the value of MAE with 0.1618, and MAPE with 0.71%, outperforming both XGBoost and BiLSTM.

title: Artificial Intelligence based Behavior Adaptive Authentication Model in Solving Security Problems of Cloud Computing Systems
authors: Rashid Alakbarov, Mammad Hashimov, Oqtay Alakbarov
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Cloud computing technologies provide flexible and efficient solutions for storing, processing and distributing large amounts of data in modern digital infrastructure. At the same time, their open and distributed structure leads to the emergence of new security risks. Recently, artificial intelligence technologies offer effective approaches to detecting and preventing these problems. The article analyzes the applications of artificial intelligence in the security of cloud systems and presents an adaptive defense method that detects anomalies by monitoring user behavior in real time.

title: NDVI-Driven Forest Depletion Analysis: A Remote Sensing Framework for Evaluating Cement Industry Impacts on Vegetation
authors: T.Poovizhi, S.Christy, Veerasundari.R, G.S.Annie Grace Vimala, Arul Raj A.M, Moorthy Agoramoorthy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Areas where limestone is mined and cement is produced at large scale have undergone much environmental change over the past few years. This paper quantifies the extent of forest loss in the industrial landscapes on the basis of the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) based on the MODIS satellite imagery. Two time periods, pre-2010 (2000-2009) and post-2015 (2016- 2025) periods were contrasted to identify the long term vegetation trends. The NDVI thresholding was used to differentiate between forested and non-forested areas to be able to quantify the changes in the green cover. The interpretation shows that vegetation degradation significantly decreases and most of the major losses have been centered in the areas of cement production centers and mining areas. These results point to the mounting unease between the industrialization and the ecological equilibrium, and the necessity to plan the land-use in a more ecologically conscious way and to approach the long-term management of resources more ecologically responsible.

title: Smart Video Surveillance System with Intruder Identification and Behavioural Anomaly Detection using YOLOv8
authors: R. Anto Pravin, S. Akalya, V. Harinipriya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The smart video surveillance system with intruder identification and behavioural anomaly detection using YOLOv8 aims to enhance security monitoring through intelligent automation. Traditional surveillance systems require manual observation, making them inefficient and prone to human error. Existing methods often fail to accurately detect intruders or analyse unusual behaviour in real time. To overcome these limitations, this project integrates YOLOv8, a state-of-the-art deep learning object detection model, for precise and rapid identification of people and intruders. The system will also include behavioural anomaly detection using motion tracking and pattern analysis to recognize suspicious activities. Implementation involves real-time video streaming, object tracking and alert generation using Python and OpenCV. The model will be trained on security datasets to improve detection accuracy. The expected outcome is an automated, high-accuracy surveillance system that minimizes false alarms and enhances situational awareness in security-sensitive environments.

title: Privacy-Preserving Cross-Chain Federated Blockchain for Carbon-Credit Management
authors: C.Madhusudhana Rao, Praveen Kumar Naidu Rayanki, Polepalli Rajeev Meenon, Salapakshi Jai Kumar, Ganga Kaveri Nimmakayala
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The rapid growth of the carbon-credit markets in China has revealed serious flaws in the transparency, security and interoperability across sectors, especially in the automotive PCFN regulatory, ecosystem. The currently deployed blockchain-IoT systems enhance traceability but do not have privacy-protective insight, scalable verification, and interledger dialogue needed to facilitate multi-sector carbon governance. The paper will offer a Privacy-Preserving Cross-Chain Federated Blockchain-IoT Framework, which will combine RFID-enabled real-time capture of emissions, cross-chain interoperability via IBC (Inter-Blockchain Communication) and a differential-privacy-enhanced federated learning (DP-FL) engine to detect fraud and make dynamic predictions of carbon-credit prices. The system uses the hybrid DPoS-sidechain architecture to enable it to achieve high-throughput validation and ensure high-level data sovereignty assurances across the heterogeneous enterprises. The experimental analysis based on simulated national automotive and energy-sector datasets indicates that the suggested framework can help lower inter-chain verification latency by 74.8 percent, increase the cross-sector audit transparency by 41.3 percent, and increase the rate of fraud/anomaly detection by 93.5 percent to 96.8 percent in the case of integrating DP-FL. Moreover, advanced transactions through smart contracts are cross-chain automated settlement which results into overhead of transaction decreased by 52.4 percent and market liquidity enhanced by 35.6 percent in multi-sector simulations. The findings affirm that the suggested system would bolster security, scalability, and intelligence in the carbon-credit ecosystem of China to a considerable extent, providing a scalable base to support countrywide carbon-neutrality goals.

title: An Adaptive Hybrid Disk Scheduling Algorithm for Fair and Efficient Storage Systems
authors: Ninad Mahajan, Marimuthu M, Arnav Parekar, Nikhil Parkar, Riya Joshi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The performance of disk I/O operations remains a significant problem in contemporary computing systems due to mechanical components of hard disk drives. Although there have been significant improvements in the processor speed of the CPU and the speed of memory, seek time and rotation in hard disk drives still create bottleneck limitations for the efficiency of computing systems. In this study, we suggest a hybrid algorithm for disk I/O operations based on the concept of Fuzzy Logic combined with the Sort Mid Current Comparison (SMCC) algorithm that optimizes the efficiency of disk I/O operations in the computing system. Compared to traditional algorithms in which the sole aim is the minimization of seek time, our approach incorporates the Fuzzy Inference System (FIS) which optimizes both seek time in hard disk drives and rotation time. Furthermore, the proposed algorithm takes into account the dynamic calculation of the current midpoint of the disk I/O queue with the intention of optimizing the movement of the disk head for efficiency. Experimental results show that the hybrid algorithm results in a substantial decrease in the number of head movements compared with the SSTF, LOOK and C-LOOK algorithms. Furthermore, the algorithm takes into consideration the rotation delay of the disk, which optimizes its performance in terms of throughput and delay while also considering practical concerns such as power consumption cooling in the implementation of high-speed systems. This Hybrid SMCC–Fuzzy Disk Scheduling Algorithm (FuzzySMCC) provides a balanced solution that incorporates adaptability and efficiency of fuzzy logic and SMCC respectively. It not only minimizes delays but also has adaptations in real-time disk conditions that ensure an adaptive solution for existing disk scheduling techniques.

title: VAE-Based Feature Extraction for Lung Nodule Diagnosis
authors: Zhihan Liu, Debora Gil, Guillermo Torres
publish: Proceedings of the International Workshop on Automotive and Medical Multimedia: Bridging the Gap Between Mobility and Healthcare
abstract: None

title: Generative AI and Ethical Integration in Higher Education: Navigating Innovation and Responsibility
authors: Umar Adam Ibrahim, AbdulAzeez Omeiza Salawu, Zhangl Abilbek, Nurbol Aidarbayev
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The recent proliferation of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) technology among institutions within the education sector has opened doors to personalize learning processes beyond imagination while also fueling daunting ethics issues pertaining to academic integrity, algorithm biases, transparency, and equal opportunities. This research project aims to explore the diverse dimensions and challenges arising from the adoption and use of GenAI technology, including the use of ChatGPT, within learning institutions while using a final review of 25 peer-reviewed articles to explore and demonstrate how different learning institutions, frameworks, and mechanisms address these diverse challenges arising from GenAI technology adoptions and use. Findings from the research clearly show that despite 94 % of top institutions within the learning sector having guidelines to use GenAI technology among faculty members, a daunting deficit still exists within various learning sectors pertaining to privacy, biases within GenAI technology, and the digital divide within learning institutions. This research work argues that to effectively integrate GenAI within learning institutions, collective stakeholders must come into play to ensure that GenAI technology does not undermine academic integrity within the learning sector.

title: Research on Cooperative Sensing of Multi-Modal Signals and Structural Health Identification Algorithm for Power Transmission and Distribution Equipment Foundation
authors: Xiaoqiong Huang, Huayong Lai, Tao Wang, Xiaoqing Su, Junyu Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Based on the urgent demand of the new power system for the safe operation of power transmission and distribution equipment, this paper puts forward a multi-modal signal collaborative sensing and structural health identification algorithm guided by physical mechanism to solve the problems of poor robustness and poor interpretability of single modal monitoring under complex working conditions. The algorithm constructs a “perception-fusion-prediction” three-layer hybrid framework: firstly, based on the first principles of electro-thermal-mechanical coupling and acoustic-thermal correlation, a cross-modal physical rule base is established to realize feature screening and causal constraint; Secondly, a two-way modal attention mechanism is designed to dynamically allocate the weights of multi-modal features such as temperature, voiceprint and stress, so as to realize information complementarity and adaptive fusion; Finally, a graph convolutional network (GCN) and a long short-term memory network (LSTM) were integrated to capture the spatial dependence and temporal evolution of the device, achieving 72 hour fault probability prediction. The experiment relies on the multimodal data set of 110 kV real transmission scene, and the results show that the accuracy of health state identification of the algorithm is 95.8%, and the F1-Score is 0.955, which is significantly better than the single-mode and conventional fusion methods. The visualization of attention weight verifies the consistency between its decision-making process and physical mechanism, and provides an interpretable and highprecision technical scheme for predictive maintenance of power transmission and distribution equipment.

title: Application of Machine Vision Technology in Tobacco Quality Inspection System
authors: Wenfeng Guan, Jiamin Lei, Ye Zhang, Wanwen He, Zhenye Chen, Xianyun Zhong, Chunqiu Li, Lulu Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: In cigarette manufacturing processes, precision management is critical for quality control. However, traditional manual inspection methods suffer from significant limitations such as low efficiency and high subjectivity, failing to meet the modern tobacco industry’s stringent demands for parameter accuracy and real-time performance. To overcome this bottleneck, this study focuses on machine vision technology and meticulously designs an online detection system for tobacco quality parameters. Leveraging core technologies in machine vision—including high-precision imaging, multispectral analysis, and deep learning algorithms—the system achieves automated and accurate detection of key indicators, such as the color, extension degree, and blending uniformity of leaf and tobacco shreds. System validation results demonstrate that machine vision technology achieves a 96.6% accuracy rate in insect spot detection, with temperature measurement errors controlled within $2^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, and blending uniformity analysis errors of less than 20%. This provides robust data support for optimizing cigarette production processes.

title: The Integration of Big Data Technology in Transformer Condition Monitoring and Detection
authors: Xiaoying Guo, Tian Gao, Dandan Li, Ye Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: As power systems become increasingly intelligent and reliable, the continuous monitoring of transformers-critical assets for transmission and distribution-becomes essential for grid security. Conventional monitoring relies on periodic offline tests and manual inspections, yielding low-real-time fidelity, unidimensional data, and belated fault alerts that are incompatible with the fine-grained asset management required by contemporary grids. Big-data analytics-owing to its capacity for high-volume processing, multidimensional analysis, and real-time pattern discovery-offers a transformative approach to transformer monitoring. Drawing on empirical cases, the study underscores the strategic value of transformer monitoring, delineates the advantages of big-data technologies, and examines their deployment in data acquisition, cleansing, and condition assessment. The findings furnish actionable insights for whole-life transformer stewardship and grid resilience.

title: Research on modeling and optimization of front-end orchestration tool based on low code engine in power system service convergence
authors: Li Chen, Hao Yang, Qijuan Sun, Juntai Shi, Xiaoxia Kou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the popularization of new energy and the deepening of power market reform, the power system is facing problems such as data islands, difficulties in business process coordination and disconnection between IT and business. In this paper, a modeling and optimization method of front-end scheduling tool based on low code engine is proposed to solve the above challenges and promote the digital transformation of power system. The system architecture design adopts fourlayer decoupling architecture, including business scene layer, visual layout layer, low code engine core layer and power data access layer, which realizes two-way data flow mechanism and dynamic rule injection and improves the flexibility and responsiveness of the system. A power business component model is proposed, which abstracts complex business functions into standardized component meta-models, and integrates rule engines and multi-source data fusion models to realize modular encapsulation and reuse of business functions and efficient integration and processing of data. Strategies such as component loading on demand, rule engine performance optimization and hierarchical data flow update are adopted to improve application performance and resource utilization efficiency and ensure efficient real-time synchronization of key information. In the upgrading project of spot trading platform of a provincial power grid company, the tool scheme effectively improves the development efficiency and flexibility of operation and maintenance, reduces the time-consuming of interface function iteration, rule update response and crossbusiness scenario construction, and significantly improves the real-time data processing performance. The research results show that the low code engine front-end orchestration tool can effectively solve the challenges of power system business integration, and provide theoretical support and practical guidance for digital transformation of power system.

title: Automatic Generation Technology for Distribution Network Protection Settings Based on Integrated Graph Data Model Approach
authors: Cheng Guo, Qiang Wu, Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Using a method of independent local information calculation, protection setting generation for distribution networks can only be simply configured based on limited conditions, resulting in low accuracy. Therefore, this study investigates an automated generation technique for distribution network protection settings based on integrated Graph Data Model technology. Noise data is processed using filtering algorithms, missing values are filled via linear interpolation, and data is categorized and stored to complete the processing of distribution network operational data. Graphical modeling defines equipment symbol attributes, integrating graphical representations with equipment parameters and operational condition models for validation. This establishes a unified Graph Data Model integrated distribution network model. Key parameters are extracted, protection settings for various protection types are calculated, and validation/optimization is performed to achieve automated generation of distribution network protection settings. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed technology achieves over 90% correct operation rates in most scenarios. The measured sensitivity coefficients for all protection types exhibit minimal deviation from theoretical values. Compared to alternative methods, it exhibits slower growth and lower overall setting errors across varying load levels, thereby enhancing the precision of distribution network protection setting generation.

title: Mechanical Design of Auto Feeder for Poultry and Livestock in Smart Agriculture
authors: Yingke Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of smart agriculture, the demand for automated and precise feeding equipment in poultry farming is increasing day by day. In view of feeding requirements of poultry grains, an efficient and precise automatic feeding device has been designed. The device adopts a quantitative screw mechanism, which can achieve accurate quantitative feeding according to the scale the poultry group, ensuring “enough but not excessive” feeding, effectively avoiding feed waste. The feeding system covers core functional modules such as bin storage, conveying transmission, quantitative, and regular feeding. Before and after the improvement: the traditional device feeds about 1 kg each time, which is independent of the number of poultry; the optimized system can feed with a minimum common tolerance of $\mathbf{1 0 ~ g}$. This improvement has significantly reduced the waste of grain feed, with an average of 50 g of feed saved per poultry per, effectively reducing the breeding cost. Through reasonable selection and parameter optimization of each module, the system has achieved stable storage, precise delivery, and uniform regular feeding of grain feed, not improving the automation level and feeding efficiency of poultry farming, but also significantly reducing the input of labor. This provides important equipment support for the deep application of smart agricultural technology in the of poultry farming.

title: Learning Adaptive Routing Policies Under Stochastic Service Times
authors: Yelbek Abdumajitov, Oybek Abdukhalimov, Azamkhon Azamov, Shirali Kadyrov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Field service routing is strongly affected by uncertainty in on-site service durations, where deviations from nominal times propagate through the schedule and induce tardiness. We study the Multi-Vehicle Routing Problem with Stochastic Service Times (MVRP-SST), in which customer service times are revealed only upon arrival and follow customer-specific Gamma distributions. We formulate the problem as a cooperative Dec-POMDP and propose a Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) approach with centralized training and decentralized execution to learn adaptive routing policies that react to realized service times. The objective minimizes a weighted cost comprising travel distance and tardiness (with an additional penalty for unserved customers). Experiments on synthetic instances with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$K=3$</tex> vehicles and problem sizes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N \in\{30,50,100\}$</tex> show that MAPPO consistently improves the overall objective compared with classical constructive heuristics and simulated annealing, with particularly strong gains at larger scales. Statistical testing confirms significant differences across methods via the Friedman test, and post-hoc Wilcoxon signedrank tests with Holm correction indicate that MAPPO significantly outperforms all baselines for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=50$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=100$</tex>, and all except simulated annealing for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N=30$</tex>.

title: Ontology Evolution via Renormalization in Latent Semantic Space
authors: C. M. Wyss
abstract: Crucially, data integration involves reconciling schemas by means of mapping ontologies onto each other. Generally, such mappings are labor-intensive to create and require extensive expert intervention. Here, we present a mathematically principled framework for ontology evolution that can assist at each stage of data integration, from evolving a comprehensive centralized ontology to integrating member ontologies. To do this, we view an ontology as a geometric structure within semantic space, then employ standard results from spectral theory and dynamical systems to define the iterative transformation of a seed ontology into a global domain topology. Empirical results on a large-scale mathematics corpus demonstrate that the method reduces a noisy pool of over 130,000 candidate concepts to a high-quality, connected ontology of roughly 2,200 concepts—without manual feature engineering or domain heuristics. This suggests Renormalization-based Semantic Filtering (RSF) is a scalable, theoretically grounded approach for integrating and evolving reliable ontologies from large and heterogeneous corpora.

title: A Multi-View Deep Representation Learning Approach for Drug Discovery and Target Affinity Estimation
authors: Xi Qin, Hongjing Yang, Weijie Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Drug discovery relies on effective representation learning to capture the complex structural and semantic characteristics of molecules and biological targets. However, existing deep learning methods often focus on single-view representations and fail to fully exploit complementary information from heterogeneous molecular descriptors and structural views. In this paper, we propose a multi-view deep representation learning framework for computational drug discovery that integrates multiple molecular and target representations into a unified embedding space. The proposed approach jointly learns view-specific encoders and a shared latent representation through cross-view consistency regularization, enabling the model to capture both local structural patterns and global biochemical semantics. Extensive experiments on benchmark drug discovery datasets demonstrate that the learned representations exhibit strong discriminative capability and generalization performance in downstream screening and similarity retrieval tasks. The proposed framework provides an effective and scalable representation learning paradigm for large-scale drug discovery and molecular analysis.

title: Construction of a Retrieval-Enhanced and Dynamically Updated Collocation Knowledge Base System for Second Language Learning
authors: Jiyan Liu, Nan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To address the limitations of static collocation knowledge bases in Intelligent Systems for second language learning, an L2 collocation domain Adaptation framework PL2CDAF is proposed. The framework introduces a hierarchical two-stage adaptation process, combining error-aware pretraining and reinforcement to transform a Transformer-based model into a dynamic collocation knowledge base. It integrates a multi-head neural memory network for storing and updating collocation patterns, enabling real-time error detection and contextually appropriate feedback generation. Experimental validation demonstrates its effectiveness in enhancing collocation awareness and corrective feedback, offering a scalable and integrable solution for personalized language learning.

title: Fault-Tolerant Operation of Micro-Hydro and PV-BSS-based Standalone Microgrid using Predictive Control
authors: Astha Bharat Patel, Rajasekharareddy Chilipi
abstract: This paper presents the fault-tolerant operation of a self-excited induction generator (SEIG) coupled with a micro hydro and solar photovoltaic (PV) based standalone microgrid (SMG). The intermittent nature of these renewable sources is mitigated using a battery storage system (BSS) to ensure an uninterrupted power supply to consumer loads. A three-phase voltage source inverter (VSI) is utilized to integrate the PV–BSS into the SMG. Afixedswitching frequency-based model predictive control (FSF-MPC) is developed for the control of VSI. The control achieves multiple objectives, such as active power injection, regulation of the point of common coupling (PCC) voltage profile, and suppression of load harmonics. Fixed-frequency operation of the VSI is ensured by applying predefined vector combinations with only one state change at any instant. Six fixed vector combinations are assigned to each of the six sectors. The optimal sector is selected based on the minimum cost-function value, which is computed using the duty cycle and the cost-function values of the vectors within that sector. The control is further designed to switch operating modes during faults occurring near the interconnection of the SEIG-based micro-hydro system and the PCC of the SMG. The performance of the proposed control is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations under various dynamic operating conditions. In addition, a hardware prototype of the SMG is developed. A dSPACE-based MicroLabBox is used as the controller to verify the real-time performance of the proposed control. Finally, a comparative study with conventional MPC is presented to showcase the merits of the proposed FSF MPC.

title: Learning Disentangled Representations for Generalized Multi-view Clustering
authors: Xin Zou, Ruimeng Liu, Chang Tang, Zhenglai Li, Xinwang Liu, Kunlun He, Wanqing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Multi-View Clustering (MVC) has gained significant attention for its ability to leverage complementary information across diverse views. However, existing deep MVC methods often struggle with view-distribution entanglement during cross-view fusion, which hampers the quality of the shared latent space and leads to suboptimal clustering performance. To address this issue, we propose the Generalized Multi-view Auto-Encoder (GMAE), a framework designed to preserve cross-view complementarity through disentangled representation learning. Specifically, GMAE employs dual-path autoencoders to decouple source features into view-specific and view-common embeddings, facilitating the discovery of clearer clustering structures. We further construct cross-view adversarial discriminators to guide view-specific encoders in capturing more discriminative features. By strategically modulating mutual information, GMAE effectively aligns distributions and prevents representation collapse, ensuring the generation of robust, non-trivial embeddings. Comprehensive experiments on 13 benchmark datasets demonstrate that GMAE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both complete and incomplete MVC tasks. Our code implementation is available at the repository: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/obananas/GMAE</uri>.

title: Mapping Banana Plantation Distribution and Their Establishment Years: A Phenology-Based Approach Using Multi-sensor Satellite Imagery
authors: Zirong Ruan, Guizhen Wang, Hongyan Lai, Yingdui He, Kai Jia, Zhixiang Wu, Weili Kou, Jiantang Xie, Li Yu, Ting Yun, Bangqian Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Banana is a perennial crop cultivated in tropical and subtropical regions with substantial economic importance worldwide. Accurate mapping of banana plantations and their established years is essential for monitoring banana cultivation patterns, managing production, and assessing environmental impacts. Complex banana phenology, and the lack of methods to determine plantation establishment timing. This article developed a phenology-based approach for mapping banana distribution and establishment year in Hainan Island, China, using time-series Landsat and Sentinel-2 satellite imagery (LS2) within Google earth engine platform. The method exploits the distinct phenological characteristics of banana growth cycles, incorporating three temporal windows (growing period; harvest period; and preplanting phase) and multiple spectral-temporal metrics to distinguish banana from other crops. A decision tree classification framework achieved an overall accuracy of 93.61%, outperforming random forest (91.34%) and support vector machine (85.15%) approaches. The estimated banana area in 2023 was 32,239 hectares, showing strong agreement with official agricultural statistics (R2 = 0.81). Analysis of historical time series enabled determination of plantation establishment year with high accuracy (R2 = 0.81), revealing that 66.0% of banana plantations were established in 2022 (one cropping), while 16.0%, 13.7%, and 4.3% were established in 2021 (two cropping), 2020 (three cropping), and 2019 or earlier (four cropping), respectively. Spatial analysis demonstrated concentration of banana cultivation in northern and western regions, predominantly in areas below 100 meters elevation (91.98%) with slopes less than 5° (92.65%). Providing an effective framework for monitoring banana cultivation dynamics in tropical regions, with applications for agricultural management.

title: Derivation of Three-Dimensional Surface Displacements in Mining Areas Using Farneback Optical Flow Method with LuTan-1 Ascending and Descending SAR Imagery
authors: Hai Huang, Xiang Zhang, Hongdong Fan, Tao Li, Jian Li, Qiqiang Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This study addresses the challenges in monitoring large-gradient, three-dimensional (3-D) surface deformation in mining areas, where conventional InSAR often suffers from coherence loss and pixel offset tracking (POT) methods struggle to balance accuracy and efficiency. We propose a novel 3-D displacement retrieval algorithm that integrates the Farneback optical flow (FOF) method with coordinated ascending and descending observations from China's independently developed LuTan-1 (LT-1) synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellites. The approach first employs the FOF algorithm to efficiently and accurately extract two-dimensional displacement fields along the line-of-sight and azimuth directions from single-track SAR imagery. Leveraging the quasi-synchronous ascending–descending observation capability of the LT-1 dual-satellite system, a rigorous geometric model for 3-D deformation retrieval is then established. Thus, the ill-posed problem for 3-D deformation monitoring is overcome in combination with the multi-dimensional independent observations. By solving an overdetermined system of equations, vertical, east–west, and north–south displacement components are effectively separated and robustly estimated. Tests conducted in a coal mine in Inner Mongolia demonstrate that the vertical displacements derived by this method agree well with leveling measurements, achieving a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.224 m and a coefficient of determination (R²) of 0.949. Compared with the adaptive cross-correlation window POT (ACC-POT) algorithm, the proposed method improves RMSE and mean absolute error by 7.82% and 4.83%, respectively, while reducing computation time from several hours to approximately 10 seconds. Based on nearly one year of long-term SAR image sequences, the dynamic evolution of the 3-D displacement field in the mining area was successfully reconstructed, with maximum displacements exceeding 2.5 m, clearly revealing the spatiotemporal propagation patterns of deformation induced by mining activities. The integrated “FOF + multi-geometry SAR cooperative observation” algorithm developed in this study effectively addresses key challenges in large-gradient 3-D deformation monitoring in mining areas, including loss of coherence, limited accuracy, computational complexity, and ill-posed inversion problems. It provides a reliable technical pathway for obtaining fully vectorized, high spatiotemporal-resolution deformation fields and highlights the application potential of the LT-1 satellites in high-precision engineering deformation monitoring.

title: Autonomous Fixed-Time Model Predictive Control for Decentralized Coordination and Stabilization of Hybrid Fuel Cell/Battery/Supercapacitor Systems
authors: Zehua Zhang, Panbao Wang, Sibao Ding, Wei Wang, Dianguo Xu
abstract: For the fuel cell/battery/supercapacitor hybrid energy storage system (HESS) with constant power loads (CPLs), its controller needs to meet desired control goals, including decentralized dynamic power sharing, fast voltage regulation, large signal stabilization, reliability, and scalability. To this end, this paper proposes an autonomous fixed-time model predictive control (FxTMPC), which aims to achieve the above objectives with less system prior knowledge and sampling cost. First, the fixed-time disturbance observer (FxTDO) is constructed to estimate and feed-forward compensate for unknown and time-varying source-load side information, which not only reduces controller design complexity but also significantly improves robustness against disturbances. Second, the receding horizon optimization problem is solved offline based on source-load estimation variables, and FxTMPC is obtained by embedding fixed-time algorithms into the framework to correct the reference trajectory. Then, a decentralized hybrid droop control (DHDC) based on FxTMPC is developed, which achieves virtual impedance/resistance/capacitance at output terminals by modifying voltage reference values, not only enables different types of sources to respond to low/mid/high-frequency components of the load respectively, but also to reduce sensor usage with the assistance of FxTDO, thus achieving above the desired objectives with low cost. Finally, simulation and experimental results under multiple scenarios demonstrate that the proposed control strategy achieves HESS objectives with less prior knowledge and sensors, while comparative studies confirm its superiority.

title: Integrating Aesthetic-Driven Hierarchical Semantics for Image Quality Assessment
authors: Rui Xiong, Li Chen, Zhida Feng, Jiaxiang Liu, Shikun Feng
abstract: Image quality assessment (IQA) has traditionally relied on task-specific models and overlooked the perception of aesthetic attributes, often limiting their adaptability and generalization to diverse image content. To address this limitation, we introduce AH-IQA, a method for integrating aesthetic-driven hierarchical semantics for image quality assessment. It introduces aesthetic-driven context optimization (ADCO), which extracts aesthetic labels from each input image and embeds them into hierarchical semantic content templates. These hierarchical semantic content features are then utilized by the hierarchical visual grounding alignment (HVGA), which aligns visual grounding features such as segmentation masks, bounding boxes, and overall layouts with the hierarchical semantics of local details, area structures, and global concepts through a hierarchical attention mechanism. This resolves the issue of inadequate integration of multi-level semantic information, thus promoting a deeper understanding of images. Experimental results demonstrate that AH-IQA achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple datasets. Moreover, it exhibits improved generalization capabilities and robustness compared to existing methods.

title: Health-aware Economic Model Predictive Control of Wave Energy Converters Based on a Reliability Metric
authors: Amin Ziaei, Hafiz Ahsan Said, John V. Ringwood
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Conventional energy-maximising controllers for wave energy converters (WECs) often exaggerate device motion, which can shorten device lifetime and increase operational costs (OpEx), by reducing maintenance intervals and system reliability. This paper proposes a novel health-aware WEC controller to achieve a suitable trade-off between captured energy and device lifetime, ultimately leading to a lower levelised cost of energy (LCoE). Using a reliability metric, the proposed controller incorporates power take-off degradation into the performance function of an economic model predictive controller (EMPC). The proposed performance function does not require additional (regularisation) terms to convexify the optimisation problem, as is often necessary for typical energy-maximising WEC EMPCs. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed controller in achieving an optimal trade off between captured energy and device longevity. Furthermore, this study examines potential challenges in the implementation of health-aware control for WECs and outlines key directions for future research in the health-aware WEC control domain.

title: Differentially Private Reward Functions in Markov Decision Processes: Policy Synthesis and Tradeoffs
authors: Alexander Benvenuti, Calvin Hawkins, Brandon Fallin, Bo Chen, Brendan Bialy, Miriam Dennis, Matthew T. Hale
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Control Systems
abstract: Policy synthesis in Markov decision processes uses a known reward function to compute a policy that maximizes it. However, onlookers may infer reward functions by observing agents, which can reveal sensitive information. Therefore, in this paper we introduce and compare two methods for privatizing reward functions in policy synthesis for multi-agent Markov decision processes, which generalize Markov decision processes. Reward functions are privatized using differential privacy, a statistical framework for protecting sensitive data. The methods we develop perturb (i) each agent's individual reward function or (ii) the joint reward function shared by all agents. We prove that both of these methods are differentially private and show approach (i) provides better performance. We then develop guidelines for selecting reward functions to achieve high performance while remaining private, and we quantify the increase in computation required to compute policies with privatized rewards. Numerical simulations are performed on several examples, and these examples show that even relatively strong differential privacy (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\epsilon =1.3$</tex-math></inline-formula>) induces as little as a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> decrease in performance and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.016\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> increase in computation time.

title: Enhancing Integrity Verification of Convolutional Neural Network Predictions in a Malicious Model
authors: Lingling Wang, Yitong Sun, Zhongkai Lu, Meng Li, Jingjing Wang, Huaqun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The widespread deployment of neural networks has raised significant concerns regarding the integrity and privacy of model predictions, especially in malicious environments. Current approaches have explored zero-knowledge proofs for integrity verification. However, they suffer from inefficiency in proving runtime and a lack of rigorous integrity verification for non linear operations. To address these issues, we present a trustwor thy framework for Enhancing Integrity Verification of Convolutional Neural Network predictions (EIV-CNN) in a malicious model, whose key contributions are an efficient optimized sum check protocol and a robust enhanced verification mechanism. Specifically, we first propose an algorithm that enables efficient proving of both batch and collaborative CNN predictions by com bining sumcheck claims of multiple matrix multiplications into one. Moreover, we introduce a non-interactive sumcheck protocol with malicious security (NM-Sumcheck) to serve as a building block for publicly verifying matrix multiplication operations. Furthermore, we introduce a verifiable method for transforming nonlinear operations into matrix operations, enabling their sub sequent evaluation with the NM-Sumcheck protocol. Our EIV CNN provides malicious security, guarantees public verifiability, and preserves model privacy. Empirical results demonstrate that our sumcheck framework achieves constant prover time, verifier time, and proof size. Compared to the state-of-the-art, it achieves up to a 128.56× reduction in prover time, along with significant reductions in communication overhead and enhanced scalability.

title: DCGFI: Robust and Interpretable Failure Identification Using Dual Causal Graph for Microservices on Multimodal Observability Data
authors: Xiangbo Tian, Shi Ying, Tiangang Li, Ning Li, Chuan Shi, Ding Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Accurate failure identification is crucial for ensuring the reliability of microservice systems. However, existing failure identification approaches are usually based on data-driven correlation learning strategies. These approaches focus on statistical correlation in observability data and ignore the causal mechanism in microservice systems, which leads to insufficient robustness and interpretability. In this study, we propose DCGFI, a robust and interpretable failure identification approach based on multimodal observability data, which uses dual causal graphs to integrate causal modeling, data-driven strategy and knowledge driven strategy to achieve effective failure identification for microservice systems. DCGFI first constructs data causal graphs and knowledge causal graphs based on multimodal observability data. Then, DCGFI uses a causal graph convolutional network to learn data causal graphs and update knowledge causal graphs. Finally, DCGFI combines data causal graphs with knowledge causal graphs to jointly optimize the failure identification process. Experimental results on two datasets demonstrate that DCGFI outperforms all baselines on both Macro-F1 and Micro-F1 and exhibits good robustness across different datasets.

title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation based Cross-Type Deepfake Image Detection
authors: Qin Wang, Xiaofeng Wang, Zinian Liu, Ningning Bai, Minghua Zhao, Shanmin Pang
abstract: In practical application of social media and the Internet, deepfake face images involves a plethora of unlabeled samples. To effectively identify unlabeled deepfake images, the domain adaptation technique has gained significant attention. It applies the knowledge learned from labeled samples (source domain) to unlabeled samples (target domain) in a cross-domain manner. However, the existing domain adaptation-based deepfake detection methods primarily focus on intra-type cross-domain scenarios. In this study, we propose an unsupervised domain adaptation-based deepfake face image detection method for extra-type cross-domain scenarios. The core idea of our approach lies in the development of a domain adaptation model that consists of Domain Tag Adversarial (DTA) and Domain Feature Alignment (DFA) algorithms, called DTA-DFA, which empowers the proposed method with strong cross-domain capability. The DTA is utilized to weaken the specificity within each domain, while DFA aligns the distribution between the source and target domains. Compared with the existing deepfake detection methods, the experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method dramatically enhances the extra-type cross-domain detection performance. Moreover, the DTA-DFA model also exhibits a remarkable ability to perform cross-domain detection from large-shot labeled samples to few-shot labeled samples, further verifying its powerful cross-domain capability. Code is released at https://github.com/QinQin741/DTA-DFA-DA-model.

title: Risk-Assessment and Visual Perception of Autonomous Microrobots using Model-free Reinforcement Learning to Cope with Multi-challenging Environments
authors: Trirat Radomngam, Xiuzhen Tang, Laliphat Manamanchaiyaporn
abstract: Autonomous microrobotic navigation in life sciences is highly challenging due to environmental disturbances, unpredictable phenomena, diverse obstacles and dynamic changes. These significantly limit motion of microrobots manipulated by remote magnetic fields. While conventional control including trajectory planning strategy is feasible when dynamic models of environments are known, they lack robustness in highly complex scenarios. Herein, we present a modern control based on model-free reinforcement learning (MFRL: SAC algorithm) combined with a visually augmented perception. This is implemented into Electromagnetic Actuation System to autonomously manipulate microrobots in dynamic challenges. As physical sensors cannot be embedded within the robots, sensing is enhanced via virtual tentacles that mimic proximity sensors to detect in-range obstacles. Meanwhile, the system processes environmental feedback—such as angular deviation, motion cues, and distances—as feedback to perform diverse actions, including trajectory planning, obstacle avoidance, and self-navigation. Experiments were conducted across three levels of unpredictable environments featuring moving and chasing obstacles These tests evaluate the microrobot’s real-time decision-making and its ability to reach a target despite restricted motion. A key advantage of this control technique is that although the RL policy is trained in simulation, seamlessly transferred to the physical control system without parameter tuning. The results demonstrate that the microrobot can effectively cope with unseen environments, providing a practical and robust approach for operating in uncertain biological conditions.

title: Multispectral Demosaicking using Bilinear Decomposition with Multichannel Structural Regularization
authors: Sanghoon Kim, Jinook Lee, Moon Gi Kang
abstract: In this paper, a multispectral demosaicking algorithm that builds upon bilinear decomposition of a color image is proposed. The overall structure of the proposed algorithm can be reduced into three parts, where the first part constructs a graph-based measure that confines the structure of neighboring pixels. The second part decomposes the raw observation into panchromatic image and chromaticity component, while the third part reconstructs the multispectral data using the decomposed component. Contrary to previous methods that identify the panchromatic image before chromaticity or spectral difference, the proposed method regularizes the mosaicked chromaticity with bilateral weights that operate as an interpolation stencil to promote similarity between the two, thereby suppressing pattern artifacts. Then, the weights formerly utilized in the identification of the mosaicked chromaticity are applied to interpolate and refine the two components, thus reconstructing chromaticity that has concurrent edges with the panchromatic image. The proposed method demonstrated results comparable to and superior to those of other state-of-the-art algorithms in both quantitative and qualitative evaluations, showing less aliasing and pattern artifacts with an accurate visual representation of the trichromatic rendition for the reconstructed multispectral data.

title: Time-Scale-Adaptable Spectrum Sharing for Hybrid Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Yanmin Wang, Wei Feng, Yunfei Chen, Yongxu Zhu, Shidong Zhou, Cheng-Xiang Wang
abstract: Cooperation between satellite and terrestrial wireless networks promises great potential in meeting fast-growing demands for ubiquitous communications coverage. To tackle spectrum scarcity, spectrum sharing is studied for a hybrid satellite-terrestrial network where satellite links share the same group of time-slotted subcarriers with terrestrial links opportunistically. In particular, with coarse network-wide time synchronization, a time-scale-adaptable spectrum sharing framework is proposed based on a satellite-terrestrial cooperation time scale that can be flexibly adjusted according to practical requirements. For generality, it is assumed that both full and partial frequency reuse could be adopted among the base stations (BSs) and satellite selection is supported when multiple satellites are available. Relying on only statistical channel state information (CSI), joint link scheduling and power control are explored to maximize the average sum rate of the network while ensuring quality of service (QoS) for users. To solve the complicated mixed integer programming (MIP) problem, a low-complexity spectrum sharing scheme is presented based on link-feature-sketching-aided hierarchical link clustering and Monte-Carlo-and-successive-approximation-aided transmit power optimization. Simulation results demonstrate that by link feature sketching, diversity of the links brought by the spatial distribution of the users could be well utilized. The proposed scheme promises a significant performance gain even under strict inter-link interference constraints.

title: Optimization Design and Mechanical Analysis of a 5 T Iron-Based Superconducting Insert Coil for High Field Application
authors: Zhipeng Jiang, Hangwei Ding, Pengcheng Huang, Zhiyou Chen, Xianping Zhang, Dongliang Wang, Wenge Chen, Yanwei Ma
abstract: Iron-based superconductors (IBS) have emerged as a promising candidate for high-field magnets, owing to their extraordinarily high upper critical fields and the relatively simple fabrication processes. This study presents the design and numerical evaluation of a 5 T IBS insert coil, intended to operate within a 28 T background field, generating a total central field of 33 T. A genetic algorithm was employed to minimize the coil volume while ensuring the generation of a requisite central magnetic field. This volume-minimization strategy is pivotal for reducing conductor cost and conserving critical cryogenic space within the magnet bore, thereby enhancing the feasibility of high field magnet systems. Building upon this foundation, the mechanical structure for the IBS coil, including its support and suspension systems, was meticulously designed. A comprehensive analysis of the mechanical stability throughout the entire operational process of the iron-based superconducting insert coil was subsequently performed. This research primarily aims to explore the practical application of IBS coils under even higher magnetic fields, thereby accelerating the advancement of IBS technology towards practical.

title: Dual Event-Triggered Remote Information-based State Estimation with Measurement Outliers
authors: Tao Cui, Peng Dong, Zhongliang Jing, Kai Shen, Wujun Chen, Yuanbo Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper examines event-triggered remote state estimation in scenarios with measurement outliers. To address the issue of trigger failures caused by outliers, a dual event-triggered criterion is proposed to ensure the transmission of informative and reliable data. Considering that feedback values in closed-loop structures may be corrupted by outliers, the trigger vector is constructed using local sensor information, rather than feedback from the estimator. For state estimation, a minimum-mean-square-error (MMSE) estimation method is first deduced within the information filter framework. To address the exponential growth in posterior Gaussian components induced by the dual trigger mechanism, a mode dependent time-varying transition matrix is introduced, enabling an interacting multiple model (IMM) approximation approach based on conditional transition probabilities. Moreover, the boundedness of error covariance is theoretically established. Simulations validate that the proposed algorithm exhibits strong robustness under measurement outliers and achieves good performance.

title: Multi-Priority Reactive Motion Control for Safe and Coordinated Dual-Arm Manipulation in Dynamic Environments_supp1-3686598.mp4
authors: Ze Wang
abstract: Reactive motion generation for dual-arm robotic systems is challenging due to their high degrees of freedom, nonlinear characteristics as well as the presence of multiple constraints, including kinematic limits, collision avoidance, dual-arm coordination, and other task-specific requirements. These constraints may become incompatible especially in dynamic operational scenarios. This paper presents a multi-priority reactive motion control framework to address the above challenges. First, a novel time-varying control barrier function leveraging multi-body distance blending is proposed to formulate dynamic whole-body collision avoidance constraint. Then, a constraint prioritization mechanism is introduced to incorporate multiple task objectives into a single optimization-based controller, where the constraints are resolved in strict order of priority using hierarchical quadratic programming. The proposed control framework is extensively validated in both simulations and real-world experiments, with results consistently demonstrating its ability to generate both safe and coordinated reactive motions across various dual-arm collaboration tasks.

title: Low-Light Salient Object Detection via Representation Decoupling
authors: Nana Yu, Jie Wang, Yahong Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In low-light scenes, images often suffer low contrast, poor distinction between the target and the background, and loss of regional information. This makes it challenging for Salient Object Detection (SOD) algorithms to identify and locate the targets. Most existing methods address low-light SOD by first enhancing low-light images and then performing saliency detection. However, splitting these into two sub-tasks may result in negative transfer effects. Additionally, some methods attempt to integrate these two sub-tasks into an end-to-end framework. However, conflicts may arise during the training process due to the differing features required by low-light enhancement and saliency detection. To address this conflict, We propose a decoupled representation network called DRNet, whose core is the construction of a Visual Center Decoupler (VCD). The VCD decouples the learned representations into enhancement-specific and SOD-specific embeddings. This module provides an implicit way to balance the specific requirements of the two subtasks. On the one hand, the enhancement-specific embeddings use RGB illumination constraints and Local Binary Patterns (LBP) feature aggregation to constrain illumination and maintain texture feature stability. On the other hand, the SOD-specific embeddings utilize dynamic multi-scale convolutions to integrate fine-grained details required at multiple scales. Finally, to validate the performance advantages of DRNet, we conduct extensive comparative experiments between the proposed DRNet and existing single-modal methods. Comparative experiments with some representative bi-modal methods further demonstrate the merits of our proposed method.

title: Wiener-Deconvolution-Driven Event-Based Deblurring for Low-Light Imaging
authors: Jie Zhu, Zeyu Xiao, Jianlong Jin, Feng Xue, Yu Liu, Zhao Zhang, Wei Jia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: We address event-based deblurring for low-light imaging, where conventional frames suffer severe blur, noise and saturation, while events capture sharp high-frequency contrast changes with microsecond latency that can guide the recovery of lost structures. Existing event-based reconstruction methods neither explicitly model low-light noise and saturation nor enforce precise alignment between events and frames, which limits cross-modal fusion and deblurring quality. We propose the Wiener-Deconvolution-Driven Event-Based Deblurring Network (WiED-Net), which embeds the Wiener deconvolution into a deep architecture so that the physical imaging model and noise statistics are encoded in the frequency domain and high-frequency recovery is stabilized on noise dominated night data. WiED-Net adopts a two stage design. The first stage applies Wiener deconvolution in both image and feature spaces to suppress noise, recover saturated regions and reduce ringing, assisted by an eventguided cross-modal feature fusion (ECFF) module for accurate alignment. The second stage uses a multi-scale fusion module to integrate the complementary event and image branches. Training is constrained by a set of losses, including a tailored blur kernel loss that provides closed-loop regularization from physical priors. Together, these designs enable WiED-Net to recover fine details while robustly suppressing artifacts and noise, and to achieve superior quantitative and qualitative performance, achieving superior quantitative and qualitative performance with a notable improvement of 1.97 dB in PSNR and 5% in SSIM over the previous state-of-the-art methods in low-light deblurring. Code will be available at https://github.com/zhuzifeng38/WiED-Net.
