title: Prediction of Yogurt Quality Attributes from Hyperspectral Images using Deep Learning
authors: Kartha Veerya Reddy Jammuladinne, Ayesha Sarker, Anirudhaa Maiti
abstract: Food quality attributes such as pH, lactic acid concentration, and viscosity are important in evaluating the stability, safety, and textural properties of refrigerated dairy products. This study uses yogurt as a test case for predicting these attributes from hyperspectral images. The proposed method uses raw spectral data collected from yogurt samples made using two different milk sources: cow milk and coconut milk. We developed an automated pipeline for predicting seven food quality attributes of yogurt, including pH, lactic acid concentration, viscosity, and color-based quality values. The pipeline uses a stepwise segmentation method to select the yogurt region, which is then divided into fixed-size spectral patches. Each patch contains pixels from only the yogurt region. We designed a convolutional neural network which is trained using these patches along with temporal information. The network includes a shared encoder and separate prediction heads for the two different milk sources. Our evaluation shows high test-set $R^{2}$ values (above 0.85) for $\mathbf{p H}$ and color attributes ($\mathrm{l}, \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$), and moderate values for titratable acidity (0.43) and viscosity (0.68). This result indicates that non-invasive hyperspectral imaging provides reliable prediction for key yogurt quality attributes, especially when combined with temporal information.

title: Research on Key Technology of High-quality Scientific and Technological Achievements Screening and Evaluation Based on Deep Learning
authors: Jing Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This study investigates the challenges in screening and evaluating scientific and technological achievements. With the dramatic surge in research output in recent years, high-quality and innovative achievements now require rigorous multi-stage screening, which currently relies heavily on manual processes. We propose a deep learning-based key technology for high-quality achievement screening and evaluation in academic institutions. The approach first analyzes the computational advantages of deep learning and the challenges in achievement screening, then introduces the DLSAQEM model to streamline the process. Experimental results demonstrate that the DLSAQEM model achieves high screening accuracy, significantly reduces response time, and offers excellent flexibility and practicality.

title: Uncovering Research Trends in Machine Learning Applications for $\text{CO}_{2}$-Equivalent Emissions
authors: Samuel-Soma M. Ajibade, Evans Oteng, Anthonia O. Adediran, Adedotun O. Adetunla, Muhammed Basheer Jasser, Ghassan Saleh ALdharhani
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study examines the application of machine learning (ML) to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>-equivalent emissions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex>) through a comprehensive bibliometric analysis, focusing on publication trends, key contributors, and emerging research themes. Bibliographic data were retrieved from the Scopus database covering the period 2010-2024. Bibliometric mapping techniques were employed to analyse publication output, institutional and country contributions, collaboration patterns, and keyword cooccurrence networks. The results reveal a rapid expansion of ML<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> research, driven largely by conference papers and highimpact journal publications. China and the United States emerge as leading contributors, supported by major funding bodies such as the U.S. Department of Energy and the Chinese Academy of Sciences. Thematic evolution indicates a shift from general climate-related analyses toward advanced ML-driven applications, including carbon capture optimisation, emissions forecasting, and real-time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> monitoring. While the analysis relies exclusively on Scopusindexed publications potentially omitting studies from other databases, the findings nonetheless provide valuable insights into the intellectual structure and global dynamics of the field. Overall, this study offers one of the earliest structured bibliometric assessments of ML applications in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2} \mathrm{e}$</tex> mitigation, highlighting key research trajectories, influential stakeholders, and opportunities for collaboration to advance data-driven climate action.

title: Zero-Shot Autonomous Dialogue Agent for Efficient Electric Power Data Inquiry
authors: Yiliang Wu, Fengchan Guo, Xuliang Luo, Fangfang Zhou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Electric power dialogue systems utilize natural language processing for service interactions and decision support. Traditional methods include pipeline and end-to-end designs, which face challenges such as error accumulation and hallucinations. This paper introduces a novel design that abandons modular frameworks in favor of an instruction following guide for large language models to generate relevant responses. We conduct intent analysis on user requests to autonomously determine actions in each dialogue round, such as retrieving grid information, querying databases, and correcting errors. Additionally, we established a simulation-based evaluation mechanism for dialogue assessment. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms traditional rule-based methods and pure large language models, with ablation studies showing that intent analysis significantly improves dialogue effectiveness.

title: Evaluating CNN Architectures in Multi-Agent PPO for Cooperative Snake Gameplay
authors: Janak Kumar Lal, Pravesh Chapagain, Arun Kumar Timalsina, Pranaya Nakarmi, Rajesh Maharjan, Pesal Rai
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Neural network architecture profoundly influences multi-agent reinforcement learning performance, yet the role of convolutional neural network design in cooperative spatial reasoning remains understudied. We present the first systematic comparison of four CNN architectures within Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization: single-layer baseline, standard three-layer sequential, dilated convolution, and multi-scale parallel networks. Through rigorous controlled experiments on a two-agent cooperative Snake environment with five independent runs per architecture, we isolate purely architectural effects on learning dynamics and final performance. Our results establish three key findings. First, multi-scale parallel architectures dramatically outperform conventional designs, demonstrating superior parameter efficiency for cooperative tasks. Second, expanded receptive fields alone provide negligible benefit, challenging assumptions that maximum spatial coverage optimizes performance. Third, we reveal a critical tradeoff between sample efficiency and asymptotic performance. These findings provide empirical foundations for architecture selection in cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning, establishing that simultaneous multi-resolution feature extraction constitutes the essential architectural innovation for grid-based coordination tasks.

title: Hardware-Implementable Tunable Synaptic Weight for Neural Networks
authors: Logan Larsh, Jonas D. Manchester, Sarah Sharif, Mike Banad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: This paper presents a hardware-implementable tunable synaptic weight circuit for neuromorphic computing applications based on a modified LIF Neuron design. The proposed circuit implements a digitally controlled analog weight with n-bit binary control using a capacitor-based tunability circuit offering a novel method of handing synaptic weights. We demonstrate successful operation across a voltage sweep ranging from 0.10.9 V and three capacitor configurations (4-8-16 fF, 8-16-32 fF, and 16-32-64 fF), achieving spiking frequencies from 22.5 MHz to 97.25 MHz with energy efficiency ranging from $242 \mathrm{aJ} /$ spike to $601 \mathrm{aJ} /$ spike at 0.4 V. The circuit is implemented in 16 nm TSMC FinFET technology, demonstrating ultra-low power operation suitable for energy-constrained neuromorphic hardware. Comprehensive SPICE-level simulations validate the circuit’s ability to provide tunable synaptic weights with predictable behavioral characteristics across different operating conditions.

title: DKT based AR framework for predictive color learning assessment in children with autism in Nepal
authors: Sachin Shrestha, Amit Shah, Anup Rai, Kiran Shrestha, Binod Shrestha, Anup Thapa, Ram Kaji Budhathoki
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: To address the lack of adaptive AR learning tools available in Nepal for autism, this paper conceptualized, developed, and tested an AR based architecture incorporating pinch-gesture and a DKT-driven color learning perception model suitable for children with autism. The goal of this paper was to develop adaptive learning tool with dynamic learning pathways targeting individual learning. The application projected colored balloons on a live interface using a webcam with color recognition logging accuracy, color selection accuracy based on responses given to these targets, and used a model DKT mastery score model for adaptive task recommendations based on color expertise mastery. The pilot project conducted in an autism center showed active participation in learning. The results demonstrate effective knowledge modeling, with overall global accuracy of 0.84 and PR-AUC of 0.82, indicating strong predictive performance. The framework highlights the potential of AR-based adaptive learning systems for personalized educational support in neurodiverse populations.

title: Optimizing Muller C Element Behavior for Robust Bayesian Inference
authors: Gourab Datta, Sarah Safura Sharif, Yaser Mike Banad
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Although its traditional implementation is computationally demanding, Bayesian inference offers a principled statistical framework for updating beliefs under uncertainty. For energy-constrained domains like edge devices and neuromorphic systems, Bayesian inference is appealing because stochastic computing with Muller C-elements provides a hardware-efficient substitute. However, because of autocorrelation effects in Muller C-element circuits, this method’s efficacy is constrained by the requirement for incredibly lengthy input bitstreams, especially at extreme probabilities. In order to improve inference accuracy with shorter bitstreams, this work systematically examines and compares a variety of decorrelation techniques, such as burnin, block averaging, random sampling, Monte Carlo simulation, and downsampling. We show that Monte Carlo based decorrelation consistently produces robust inference results, even at significantly reduced bitstream lengths, outperforming alternative methods in terms of accuracy and stability, using Kullback Leibler divergence as a performance metric. These results pave the way for scalable stochastic hardware design and lay the groundwork for implementing Muller C-element based Bayesian inference in realistic low-power systems.

title: Real-Time Super-Resolution Ultrasound Imaging using the Erythrocytes_supp2-3683326.pdf
authors: Sebastian Kazmarek Praesius
abstract: Super-resolution ultrasound imaging using the erythrocytes (SURE) visualizes microvasculature in a few seconds without contrast agents. However, it is computationally intensive because it uses processing steps similar to those in ultrasound localization microscopy (ULM). The hypothesis is that a graphics processing unit (GPU) can process SURE in real time, allowing the resulting images to be displayed immediately at the patient’s bedside. The processing included beamforming, motion correction, tissue signal removal through singular value decomposition (SVD)-based filtering, and peak detection. All calculations were performed on the GPU, where batches of 32 frames were processed in parallel to improve hardware utilization. The image quality was compared with that of the original SURE implementation for in vivo rat kidney data acquired at 417 frames/s, and the processing rate was measured on an NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 GPU. The processing rate was 517 images/s, demonstrating real-time SURE imaging with GPU acceleration. Furthermore, the image quality was improved, with more blood vessels being clearly visualized. In conclusion, SURE imaging can be performed in real time, similar to power Doppler, enabling non-invasive super-resolution ultrasound imaging at the bedside.

title: Optimizing Electric Vehicle Scheduling in Smart Grids: Strategies for Cost Savings, Alleviating Congestion, and Promoting Sustainable Car-to-Grid Integration
authors: Md Sahil Ahammed, Debjani Bhattacharya, Sawan Sen
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The incorporation of Electric Vehicles (EVs) in the Smart Grids has its opportunities and challenges in the provision of cost-effective, reliable, and sustainable power delivery. EV charging with the grid engages either traditional unidirectional charging (G2V) or advanced bidirectional Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) skill, which allows EVs to not only consume power from grid but also deliver power back to the grid. This paper develops an EV charging scheduling framework aimed at minimizing total charging costs while maintaining grid stability under uncertain circumstances such as variable vehicle arrival times and time-of-charging of EV, type of charging and mostly significant, electricity pricing. The proposed methodology combines optimization and heuristic strategies to balance robustness, flexibility, and computational efficiency. To prove the efficacy of the proposed scheduling model in a parking lot, modified IEEE 33-bus distribution network has been considered, clustering was performed to classify Load Distribution Centres (LDCs) and a fleet of total 100 EVs with a total capacity of 5 MW have been considered, and three different charging scenarios were analysed under different time-of-charging and variable pricing conditions. Evidence indicates that the slow charging policies are less expensive than the fast charging, which is subject to the location of the LDCs that play a significant role in influencing the power grid outputs and cost-efficiency. The results of the analysis reveal the significant importance of co-arrangement of EV scheduling in cost-reduction, grid congestion prevention, and facilitating successful Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) operations in the context of the sustainable involvement of EVs in the contemporary Smart Grids.

title: Mitigating Optimism Bias in Fast Antimicrobial Susceptibility Testing via Imbalance-Aware Kernel-KNN and SMOTE-NC
authors: Santosh Kumar Singh, Tripti Swarnkar
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The fast emergence of multidrug resistance (MDR) Pseudomonas aeruginosa imposes a serious challenge to medical outcomes universally, necessitating a move away from traditional antimicrobial susceptibility testing (AST) system that normally take 48-72 hours to complete. While ML offers an inspiring alternative for a very fast susceptibility prediction most current models suffer from “optimism bias” due to their inefficiency to effectively handle severe class imbalance and missed type medical data. In this research we propose an imbalance-aware diagnostic pipeline utilizing SMOTE-NC based synthetic oversampling for minority classes and a kernel-based K-Nearest Neighbor (KKNN) model with nonlinear BRF weighting. Also including advanced feature engineering calculations such as age stratification and a composite infection severity score, the model efficiently captures drug-specific resistance patterns for five very high priority antibiotics available in medical. Experimental outcomes demonstrate the model's durable performance, achieving a maximum accuracy of 77.59 % and a Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) of 0.5518 for gentamicin. Specifically, this framework reveals an average inference time of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> milliseconds. Setting up its clear appropriateness for real-time, point-of-care applications in resource-constrained medical settings.

title: Real-Time Prosthetic Hand Control Using Forearm EMG Signals
authors: K.V. Karthikeyan, A. Aswin Kumar, G. Hari Prasath
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: The current project is aimed at creating a prosthetic arm that can be controlled using electromyography (EMG) signals generated by the muscular activity of the user's forearm. The system employs a Muscle Sensor V3 to acquire EMG signals, which are processed using an Arduino microcontroller. In addition to EMG sensing, an ADXL accelerometer is used to capture wrist motion and orientation, while a force-sensitive resistor (FSR) is incorporated to measure grip pressure during object interaction. These multimodal sensor signals are interpreted by the Arduino and translated into appropriate actuation commands for servo motors that drive a 3D-printed robotic hand. The prosthetic mimics natural hand movements, such that muscle contraction results in hand closing, while muscle relaxation leads to hand opening, enabling an intuitive and mirrored control mechanism. The system provides a non-invasive and userfriendly interface for individuals with upper-limb amputations. By integrating EMG, motion, and pressure sensing with opensource hardware and 3D printing, the proposed solution remains low-cost and suitable for low-resource environments and educational applications. Overall, the project demonstrates the feasibility of developing an effective, affordable, and real-time myoelectric prosthetic hand using embedded systems to enhance assistive technology for physically disabled individuals. It highlights how embedded systems have the potential to transform the real-world to assist physically disabled people.

title: An fNIRS - Enabled Memory Game Device
authors: Arun Karthick S, Yashwanth Kumar S, Ranjani K, Sindhuja Mary S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Memory and attention related issues are becoming a widespread problem among students and older adults, which prompts the necessity to find convenient and efficient methods of contributing to cognitive wellness. In this paper, a Memory Game Device is proposed, and this device combines interactive gameplay with real time monitoring of brain activity. The device will make memory exercises more interesting at varying degrees of difficulty using visual, audio and touch cues such as colored LEDs, a small display and a buzzer. The system displays the brain activity by monitoring alterations in blood oxygen in the brain using fNIRS to reveal the extent to which the brain is engaged in play. The game runs on ESP32 microcontroller, where data is collected and transmitted on wireless. The scores and fNIRS data are stored in a database and represented on a web dashboard, where the users can analyze their performance, time, and get feedback dedicated to their health. This system is cheap and portable compared to traditional brain monitoring such as EEG and MRI and as such, this system can be used on a daily or classroom basis. This device assists in cognitive enhancement and educational research by utilizing memory training, actual brain monitoring, and personalized feedback.

title: Facial EMG Controlled Wheelchair - A Simulated Mobility Assistance for Quadriplegia
authors: Thamizhvani TR, Chandrasekaran R, Yazhini S, Deepika P
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Quadriplegia is a severe form of paralysis that affects the motor functions, causing problems in ability to move independently. Traditional manual and electric wheel chair failed to meet the specific need of people with quadriplegia, requiring developments for quadriplegia patient. Facial EMGbased mobilization device for quadriplegia that helps the individual with mobility assistance on their own, highlighting the importance of finding a new solution for the patient. This paper focuses on Facial EMG acquisition, signal processing and feature extraction using wavelet. Facial EMG signals are acquired and given as input for filtering process. These processed signals are then used as input functions for the simulation in Simulink, which include components such as PID controller, torque, torque source, shaft and common gear assembly. This paper aims to create a simulated model for a fully operational and independent control wheel chair that could improve the quality of life for quadriplegic patient using facial EMG as input. This paper aims to provide a simpler and more effective method for controlling movement and to demonstrate the capacity to transform the use of technological advancement in restoration.

title: Enhanced Video Surveillance Framework Using Improved Faster R-CNN ResNet101 and LP-SIFT for Robust Object Detection and Tracking
authors: Balamurugan M, Vibinchandar S, Rajesh Rao K, Narasimha Murthy G K
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Video surveillance systems are security tools designed to observe, capture, and evaluate unauthorized visual data activities. The methodology in video surveillance systems involves real-time video capture, processing, and analysis using AI, whereas the research gap lies in improving accuracy, privacy preservation, and adaptability to complex environments. This study proposed a new lightweight filtering method called L-Filter, which offers accurate predictions of video frames without objects of interest through hybrid time- series analysis. Subsequently, traditional segmentation methods in video surveillance lack contextual understanding and temporal consistency, which Modulated Memory Networks (MMN) aim to address using adaptive memory-driven feature modulation. Faster R-CNN is the regional popular model for object detection, which is used for the classification and segmentation. The objective of this work is to discover objects in images or videos. To optimize the accuracy and efficiency of video classification by fine-tuning the model parameters, the Gradient Sine Linear (GSL) optimizer has greatly improved the performance over a range of effectiveness and practicality of neural network architectures, and datasets in deep learning are well demonstrated.

title: Securing Industry 5.0 Financial Workflows with Blockchain-AI Convergence: A Framework for Resilient Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Kaiss Redouane, Praneel Kumar Mukherjee, Kush Singh, Kiran Veernapu, Shahneela Jamal Kidwai, Saanvi R Prabhu
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The industrial and financial processes are being redefined by Industry 5.0 environments by incorporating human-machine collaboration, intelligent automation, and intelligent networks. But with the rising level of digitalization of systems, cyber risks and vulnerability to fraud have increased, especially in the transmission of financial data and supply chain transactions. This paper presents empirical research on the hybrid structure of Blockchain and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to boost financial traceability and cyber-resilience in Industry 5.0 systems. Based on the information comprising 187 manufacturing-based fintech operations in worldwide industrial centers, the offered model assesses the transparency of transactions, the accuracy of fraud detection mechanisms, the minimization of latency, and the sturdiness of systems. Anomaly detection through a hybrid CNN-LSTM model is the AI component, and immutable logging, distributed security are provided with the help of Blockchain. Findings indicate that efficiency in traceability increased by 34 percent, anomaly detection accuracy by 92 percent, and the number of attempted cyber intrusions dropped by 41 percent. The results verify that Blockchain and AI constitute a strong enabler of secure industrial finance, which can facilitate resilient digital transformation and real-time decisions in Industry 5.0 operations.

title: Green Innovation and Technology Readiness: Sustainable Development in the Global South
authors: Najla Shafighi, Sara Ravan Ramzani, Behrooz Gharleghi, Khaled Nawaser
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The aim of this paper is to empirically examine the impacts of green innovation and technological readiness on the level of CO2 emission in the Global South. The data spans from 2008 to 2021 for 23 developing countries in Latin America, Africa, Asia, and East Asia. Pooled Mean Group is applied to find such impacts. The empirical findings reveal that in the long run green innovation has a significant negative impact and ICT trade has a positive impact on CO2 emissions in developing countries. In order to observe the impact of technological readiness and green innovation, four interaction terms are examined: technological readiness and "high technology export", technological readiness and "trade in low carbon technology products", green innovation and "high technology export", and green innovation and "trade in low carbon technology products". The results show that the interaction terms associated with technological readiness have a significant and negative impact on CO2 emissions while the green innovation interactions found to have a positive impact. Such long run impacts show different patterns in developed nations, i.e., only technological readiness and ICT trade are found to be significant and negative, and none of the interaction terms were significant. The policy implication of our findings implies that Southern countries should integrate more of technological related factors for environmental protection for long run. The findings further suggest that developed nations must slow down the emission pace or boost activities which are related to green innovation.

title: Future Trends of Design Management in the Hospitality Industry: An AI-Driven Perspective on Intelligence, Sustainability, and Interdisciplinary Integration
authors: Yingli Sun, Runbo Zhang, Sitian Feng
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: With the deep integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and the hospitality industry, hotel design management is undergoing a profound transformation. Based on the development of China’s hospitality industry (2014-2023), the disciplinary evolution of design management, and the industry’s 2026 development trends, this study draws on UNESCO’s 2025 AI Ethics Principles, Deloitte’s industry reports, and relevant theoretical achievements to explore the full-process application framework of AI-empowered design management in hotels from intelligent upgrading, functional restructuring and industrial linkage dimensions. It proposes the Technology-Humanity-Ecology-Virtual (THEV) Four-Dimensional Integrated Model and verifies its feasibility through case studies of Huazhu Group, Marriott International and other leading enterprises. The study identifies the interdisciplinary characteristics of AI-supported hotel design management and its core development trends (intelligence, greening, interdisciplinary integration) in 2026, and puts forward practical paths and countermeasures to address existing challenges. The research shows that AI is the core driving force for breaking the industry’s homogeneous dilemma and promoting high-quality development, which can restructure the full-process value of hotel design management, promote in-depth green development, and construct a multi-domain integration ecosystem. This study provides a theoretical basis and practical reference for the AI-driven transformation of hotel design management and the high-quality development of the global hospitality industry.

title: Temperature-Aware Modelling of MOSFET Characteristics for Sustainable Circuit Design
authors: Abinaya S M, V Sheeja Kumari
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor field-effect Transistors (Power MOSFETs) are also a necessity in modern day industry applications, in since they enable efficient conversion and switching of power and energy management. However, the step-by-step adherence to the right methods of control solution to their performance is critical especially when they are subjected to dynamic load conditions and high frequency switching environment. The paper explores and demonstrates smart gate drive techniques by harnessing adaptive control, soft-switching techniques and feedback as well as real-time to further enhance switching efficiency, electromagnetic interference (EMI) and to reduce power losses in industrial applications. The arrangement of machine learning algorithm and sensor based feedback programs in the suggested intelligent gate drive system will also render the system dynamic in accordance to load and temperature products leading to enhanced stability and trustworthiness of the system. The validity of the suggested method has been discussed as being valid through the results of a simulation and an experiment, proving to have great improvements in switching ability, thermal behaviour and lifetime of a total system. The findings introduce the new paths in the question about how to design the power electronic smart, robust, and energy efficient systems that would be the perfect next generation industrial automation.

title: Gen-AI Assisted Machine Learning Framework for Early Heart Disease Risk Prediction
authors: Koby Smith-McCallister, Sarthak Aitha, Monika Bk, Dinesh Jackson Samuel Ravindran
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Heart disease is a major life-threatening global health condition that often remains undetected in its early stages due to the absence of noticeable symptoms. Consequently, early risk analysis is essential for effective prevention and timely intervention. This study presents a real-world heart disease prediction system that analyzes user vitals, lifestyle and behavioral factors, and medical history to assess individual risk. The proposed system integrates machine learning with generative AI to deliver risk predictions along with personalized health insights and recommendations. The model was developed using the Heart Disease Health Indicators Dataset, comprising 253,680 records derived from over 400,000 survey responses. Five machine learning algorithms Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, Random Forest, and CatBoost were evaluated, with CatBoost achieving the highest performance, attaining a ROC-AUC score of 0.92. The application presents confidence scores from all five models to support informed decision-making. Following prediction, the results and contextual prompts are passed to a large language model (LLM) to generate actionable, personalized health recommendations. The proposed system enables real-time health assessment and decision support, particularly in emergency situations, and has the potential to facilitate early detections.

title: Optically Navigated Surgical Robotic System for Automated In Situ Bioprinting in Confined Anatomical Spaces_supp3-3680970.mp4
authors: Jinwu Wang
abstract: Robot-assisted in situ bioprinting allows for the direct deposition of biomaterials at defect sites, offering a minimally invasive alternative to traditional scaffold implantation. Yet, its clinical translation remains hindered by limited accessibility in anatomically constrained regions and the lack of precise control. To address these challenges, this study introduces optical navigation from computer-assisted surgery into in situ bioprinting to achieve explicit image–patient registration and translate anatomical localization into accurate robot motion. An optically navigated active remote center of motion (RCM) strategy is further developed to support constrained motion in confined anatomical spaces. The system was validated through autonomous in situ printing of a two-layer scaffold on a femoral defect phantom using a six-degree-of-freedom robotic arm. Quantitative evaluation demonstrated a target registration error of 0.583 mm and a maximum RCM distance error of 0.052 mm, indicating accurate localization and constrained motion. The printed scaffold exhibited high geometric fidelity based on multiple structural metrics. These results highlight the precision and reliability of the proposed framework and its potential for autonomous in situ bioprinting in deep or subdermal environments.

title: Technical Perspective: Diva: Dynamic Range Filter for Var-Length Keys and Queries
authors: Viktor Leis
abstract: None

title: Mlops Model Deployment System for Multi-Cloud Environments and Improvement of Commercial AI Service Availability
authors: Huisheng Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Background: Commercial AI deployed on a single cloud is vulnerable to regional failures and neighboring interference, resulting in service level agreement violations and rollback delays. MLOps platform for AWS us-east-1, GCP us-central1, and Azure eastus2 regions in the United States, integrated with the multi-cluster service mesh KServe, implemented CI/CD/CT automation, enforced SRE error budget constraints, and implemented canary/blue-green releases and chaos testing. Conducted online assessments on Yelp 2024, UCI Adult, and LISA. Results: Architecture optimization activities effectively reduced mean repair time by 41% to 58%, and the 30-day service target achievement rate increased by 2.7 to 4.9 percentage points. Based on the improvement in parallel performance, throughput was close to a linear trend, and the tail latency of eastus2 was optimized using network weights and retry/timeout control measures. Conclusion: The implementation of multi-cloud MLOps has significantly improved the availability and release security of commercial AI, and the control plane costs have been reasonably controlled. It is independent of frameworks and models, compatible with different tasks such as natural language processing, visual recognition and table processing, and enables scalability for larger-scale models and cost-service level agreement joint optimization.

title: Renewable Energy Integration with Battery Energy Storage System for Electric Vehicle Charging Stations (EVCS) using HOMER Software at Ambedkar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, India
authors: Sonu Kumar, Ruchi Agarwal, Sanjay Agrawal, Govind Kumar, Sahitya Verma, Shruti Upadhyay
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The rapid adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) in India has necessitated the development of reliable and sustainable electric vehicle charging infrastructure (EVCS) along major expressways. This study focuses on the techno-economic assessment of a hybrid renewable energy-based EVCS at Ambedkar Nagar on the Purvanchal Expressway, integrating solar photovoltaic (PV) and battery energy storage systems (BESS) with a diesel generator (DG) as an emergency backup during extended grid outages. Using HOMER Grid software, a detailed simulation and optimization of the system is conducted by considering site-specific parameters such as solar irradiance, EV charging demand profiles, and grid interaction patterns. The analysis evaluates energy dispatch strategies, system performance, and overall economic viability. The results reveal that the integration of PV and BESS with EVCS significantly reduces the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE), enhances energy efficiency, lowers operational costs, and maximizes renewable energy utilization. Furthermore, the proposed hybrid configuration demonstrates a substantial increase in annual bill savings and improved sustainability of EV charging operations.

title: Performance Analysis of IRS-Enabled Cognitive Radio Networks over Composite Fading Channels
authors: Bhawna Trivedi, Himanshu Katiyar, Pappu Kumar Verma, Vijay Kumar Verma, Kuldeep Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive performance analysis of intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-enabled cognitive radio (CR) networks operating over composite Nakagami-m/Gamma fading channels, which capture both multipath and shadowing effects. The IRS is employed to enhance spectrum sensing reliability and secondary user communication efficiency in dynamically varying spectrum environments. The closed-form analytical expressions are derived for key performance matrices, including outage probability (OP), average bit error rate (BER), and ergodic channel capacity (ECC), under the composite fading model. The effects of IRS phase optimization, channel state information (CSI) accuracy, and fading severity parameters are thoroughly investigated. Simulation results validated through Monte Carlo analysis confirm that IRS deployment dramatically improves link reliability, reduces error rates, and enhances spectral efficiency. The findings demonstrate the potential of IRS technology as a cornerstone for robust, energy-efficient, and high-capacity cognitive radio networks in future 6G and beyond wireless systems.

title: Retrievable Data Perturbation and Third-Party Auditing Frameworks: An Analytical Review for Secure Public Cloud Environments
authors: Anik Acharjee, Lalit Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Cloud computing’s speedy approval introduces determined obstacles for public data safety and security. It mainly includes isolation, privacy, and authenticate access control. This literature review mainly investigates the "Design of Retrievable Data Perturbation Approach and TPA for Public Cloud Data Security," that initiates a recoverable general additive data perturbation(RGADP) principles/mechanism to protect delicate and sensitive cloud information that enables secure, safe and obtain recovery on demand. The solution merges an enhanced cloud drops randomly noise creator or generator with a proxy re-encryption protocol. It tries to enhance key privacy and permits authorized data recovery. It is mainly accomplished all under the inspection of a third-party auditor (TPA) for rectitude affirmation. After examining state-of-the-art strategy, the review investigates RGADP alongside with the privacy-preserving models which re-distribute encoded data, common examining solutions, and delicate cryptography for limited resources environments. Significance is set down on the equity executed between multiple parameters like security, recovery correctness, computational coherence, and service quality and standards.

title: Smart Grid Framework Leveraging Advanced Semiconductor Nodes and RISC Nodes with Agentic AI, Machine Learning, Blockchain, Quantum Computing, and Next-Generation 6G/7G Communication Architectures for Sustainable and Autonomous Energy Systems
authors: Seshua Sai M, Adinath M, Sivaraman T, Ithihas Thiruvenkata Durai, Sharan S, Suryaprakasam B, Shamili E
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This paper presents a novel smart grid architecture that integrates next-generation semiconductor technologies, RISC-V edge nodes, agentic artificial intelligence, blockchain authentication, quantum assisted optimization, and future 6G/7G communications to enable sustainable, autonomous energy distribution. We propose specialized hardware designs (e.g. Qualcomm 5G/6G IoT chipsets and RISC-V SoCs) coupled with an agentic AI layer for real-time grid decision-making. Transactions are secured using a Hyperledger Fabric blockchain, while IBM Qiskit–based quantum algorithms optimize grid operations. We simulate the framework using Synopsys/Cadence for VLSI design, TensorFlow for AI models, Qiskit for quantum optimization, and Hyperledger for blockchain. Results show significant improvements in energy efficiency and latency: energy efficiency increases from 65% to 92% (+41%), and latency drops from 18 ms to 5 ms (–72%). The integrated system ensures high data integrity and low communication delay, supporting the move toward carbon-neutral, self-healing grids. Detailed comparisons with traditional grids are presented, showing clear gains across key performance metrics

title: AI in LegalTech: Automating Contract Analysis and Fraud Detection
authors: Preeti, Pankaj Kumar. Varshney, Sunaina
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: In this paper, we introduce the transformative power of artificial intelligence (AI) in legal technology, that is, contract analysis automation and fraud detection. Traditional contract review is normally laborious and error-prone, while fraudulent activities in legal documents pose a severe threat. The primary objectives of this research are to examine the efficacy of AI-powered solutions—specifically those that employ machine learning, natural language processing, and anomaly detection— to enhance contract analysis accuracy and efficiency and the detection of fraud. This is through a mixed-methods study that includes an extensive literature review and real-case studies to analyze prevailing trends and challenges. The outcomes of the study establish that AI-based solutions greatly ease contract management, reduce the cost of doing business, and improve fraud detection through automated identification of anomalies which could be missed by human analysts. However, there are real issues in the form of data privacy, algorithmic bias, and compliance with legal requirements. The article then proposes the way AI can be integrated in the legal system as a supplementary tool to complement human abilities, in the hope of improving overall provision of legal services without any compromise on high ethical and compliance standards.

title: Environmental Factors Influencing Noise in Voice Signals
authors: Jyoti Vijay Sabarad, Hemant Kumar Upadhyay, Rocky Kumar, Neha Shrinivas Joshi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This study examines different environmental factors affect voice signal quality and intelligibility. It analyzes the impact of these variables on signal-to-noise ratio and spectral characteristics using advanced signal processing techniques. The present work aims to analyze the environmental factor influencing noise in voice signals with the help of application of widely used multi criteria decision making technique fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process. Nine environmental factor influencing noise in voice signals have been categorized into three different categories as ‘Natural category’, ‘Acoustic & Spatial category Factors’, ‘Human & Technological category’ which are further compared to determine a priority ranking. The study highlights that Environmental factors affecting noise in speech signals in the ‘Human & Technological category’ were found to be the most important in this study; in contrast, factors in the ‘Acoustic & Spatial category’ were ranked somewhat lower. ‘Natural category’ elements have less of an effect. The research underscores the importance of clarity in voice communication systems, particularly for speech recognition and telecommunication quality.

title: TinyML for Edge Devices: Current State, Technological Advancements, and Future Opportunities
authors: Anil Kumar Lamba, Dharmendra Pal, Sakshi Koli, Nagendar Yamsani, Amit Saini
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: TinyML makes machine learning possible on ultralow-power devices. In other words, it was designed for applications with relatively limited computational resources. The TinyML survey describes the burgeoning discipline of ML on edge devices. TinyML supports lower-power hardware development, processing limitations, toolchains, models, and applications. The entire process was defined as TinyML, which includes the TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers, Edge Impulse, and TVM frameworks. Some examples of applications include monitoring health with wearables, sensors, and monitoring the environment increase in industrial manufacturing. A comparison of model optimisation such as quantisation or pruning and neural architecture to search for TinyML is being made. Deployment is promising, but it challenges existence, including energy constraints, model robustness, and real-time inferring or continued existence. TinyML may be mentioned via AI and IoT in short, which probably suggests that TinyML bridges AI and IoT. In terms of future work, federated TinyML can be mentioned, so Federated TinyML offers the potential for combining learning experiences from vast populations end-to-end, bringing the edge even nearer to consumers. The integration of neuromorphic computing may be helpful. As an edge device that uses intelligence to protect data privacy, TinyML can fully implement it.

title: Optimizing Quantum-Inspired Machine Learning Models for Energy-Efficient Computing
authors: Randeep Singh, Neeraj Kumar, Abhishilpa Nandini, Aman Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The accelerated pace of machine learning application has been accompanied by an equally high increase in energy consumption, which casts grave doubts of sustainability and scalability. Traditional optimization tools only emphasize on accuracy and convergence and do not consider the veil cost of calculation, which is power consumption. The presented quantum-inspired and hybrid quantum-classical models provide relief to some extent but many of them rely on specialized devices or fixed setups, which restricts them to the energy demands found in the real world. This paper presents the QiML-Opt framework, an energy-conscious quantum-inspired machine learning model which jointly employs hybrid cost-scheduling, compressional compression, and adaptive inference scheduling to reduce the computational energy usage without reducing accuracy. The framework inserts a direct energy cost into the learning task, where the models are motivated to self assemble into thin representations. Experiments on typical benchmarks show up to 39 percent energy consumption and a 35 percent higher inference rate and comparable accuracy to state-of-the-art hybrid quantum systems. The given strategy offers a hardware-agnostic, scalable path to sustainable AI and demonstrates that the energy efficiency can be achieved with the help of an algorithmic intelligence instead of hardware-design-intensive hardware.

title: Intelligent Healthcare Security Architecture Using Deep Learning and Predictive Analytics
authors: Sowmini Bandaru, Purvi Pareek, Raviteja Guntupalli, Gurpreet Kaur, Anup Hanamantrao Shinde, Priti Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The fast growth of digital healthcare systems has also brought exposure to advanced types of cyber threats necessitating dynamic and purposeful security systems. This paper introduces a new Intelligent Healthcare Security Architecture (IHSA) combines multi-layered deep learning based on predictive analytics on a real-time operating and protects clinical network, electronic health records, and medical IoT exchanges. The architecture presents a Progressive Threat Anticipation Engine (PTAE) that trains off of temporal patterns of threat evolution and proactively raises an alarm against abnormal patterns of internal behavior prior to system compromise. There exists a custom Hybrid Deep Sequential Encoder (HDSE) which is meant to combine heterogeneous healthcare data streams allowing high-fidelity anomaly representation with low latency. Also, a Dynamic Risk Prioritization Module (DRPM) calculates situational risk scores through dynamic probabilistic models, which can be quickly used to support intrusion mitigation decisions. The suggested architecture is entirely compatible with deployment to cloud-edge healthcare applications and can preserve privacy in data flowing with federated updates. The experimental confirmation confirms that IHSA can provide resilient and proactive defense appropriate to the contemporary healthcare facilities.

title: Adaptive Duty-Cycled Cooperative Clustering Model for Energy-Harvesting Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are critical for real-time monitoring applications, yet their operational sustainability is severely constrained by limited energy availability in sensor nodes. Energy harvesting provides a solution, yet inconsistency in energy arrival presents instability. To deal with this issue, we introduce a new cooperative clustering scheme that uses adaptive duty cycling of energy-harvested WSNs. The methodology incorporates RF-based energy harvesting models to include supercapacitor storage, dynamic duty cycle scheduling, and cooperative cluster-head (CH) selection. CH election is based on a hybrid measure of residual energy, energy predicted to be harvested and node centrality which provides a fair distribution of load on nodes. Duty cycle synchronization reduces idle listening and cooperative relaying promotes the low-energy nodes, which prolongs network life. Simulation of NS-3 shows major improvement over base protocols. The proposed scheme had a mean accuracy of 95.1 % in the delivery of the packet as opposed to 90.4 % in HEED and 87.2 % in LEACH in moderate traffic. It increased network lifetime by 35 and 45 % over HEED and LEACH respectively and shortened latency by as much as 25 %. Also, the fairness index of energy consumption was more than 0.90, which provided the balanced consumption of energy over the network. This work proves the efficacy of cooperative clustering integrating with duty cycling with energy-harvesting limitations. The results point to the way of sustainable WSN implementations in smart cities, health care and environmental surveillance where energy-saving and dependability hold the key.

title: A Comparative Analysis of BERT and LLM Models for Advanced Phishing Detection
authors: Justin Wilson John, Mohankumar B
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Phishing attacks remain one of the most persistent and rapidly evolving threats in cybersecurity, increasingly fueled by AI-generated malicious content. This paper presents a focused comparative analysis of two advanced language model-based techniques for phishing email detection: a fine-tuned transformer model—RoBERTa, a variant of BERT— and a locally deployed Large Language Model (Llama 3.2 1B) utilizing prompt engineering. To ensure fairness and consistency, the study employs a curated, balanced subset of a comprehensive phishing email dataset comprising 1,000 emails (624 legitimate, 376 phishing). The transformer based approach fine-tunes a pre-trained RoBERTa model for binary classification, while the LLM operates in a zero-shot setting using structured prompts, requiring no additional training. Performance evaluation using standard classification metrics reveals that the RoBERTa-based model significantly outperforms the LLM, achieving 95.0% accuracy and a phishing F1-score of 92.9%. In contrast, the LLM attains only 67.3% accuracy and a 57.4% F1-score. RoBERTa also demonstrates stronger discrimination (ROC-AUC 0.9380 vs. 0.6594) and near-zero false positives, reinforcing its reliability for practical deployment.

title: Automated Eye Disease Diagnosis Using Random Forest Classifier for Clinical Decision Support
authors: Mahitha Dilli, Shagala Niharika, Shaik Sumaya, S. Devi, V. Arundhathi, V. Sravya
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Timely and accurate identification of eye disease is essential to avoid permanent vision loss and to begin immediate treatment. Methods: We examined a Random Forest classifier that determines eye-disease status given clinical patient records. Confusion-matrix results detected 1,834 diseased and 1,743 non-diseased subjects in diagnostic classification. The model is highly accurate with extremely low false positive (just 4) meaning that there are rare false alarms. However, the 419 false negatives demonstrate a relatively low sensitivity for early or subtle disease. The ROC curve gives some more insight into this, and the classifier is 0.74 AUC, which is moderate discriminative ability, and also shows that the model is performing consistently above random guessing across thresholds. The above further supported by the classification report shows that this model has an overall accuracy of ~0.89 with pretty high F1-scores of 0.89 for No Eye Disease class and 0.90 for Eye Disease class. In conclusion, the Random Forest model performs very accurately and consistently, but still requires additional optimization with respect to sensitivity and early detection of the disease.

title: Symbiotic Minds: HyperFused Emotion Recognition Models for Adaptive Human–AI Interaction
authors: S. Ponmaniraj, Balajee Maram, Rudra Kalyan Nayak
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Symbiotic Minds introduces a HyperFused framework for adaptive human–AI interaction, capable of concurrently recognizing emotions across multiple modalities and agents. The system integrates facial, audio, gestural, and contextual cues to decode nuanced emotional states in real time, effectively overcoming the limitations of single-modality and sequential fusion approaches. Through a dedicated multi-agent learning module, the framework models inter-agent emotional dependencies, enabling precise tracking of group-level emotional dynamics in complex social settings. Furthermore, a reinforcement learning component dynamically refines AI responses based on observed emotions, ensuring context-aware and socially intelligent interactions. Experiments conducted using the EmotiW Group Videos dataset reveal that the HyperFused model outperforms conventional baselines, demonstrating significant gains in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The adaptive AI response module exhibits high interaction appropriateness with minimal response latency, validating the framework’s real-time applicability. By merging multi-modal fusion, multi-agent reasoning, and reinforcement learning, the proposed system establishes a deployable solution for symbiotic human–AI ecosystems, capable of perceiving and responding intelligently to collective emotional cues.

title: Machine Learning Models Employing Data Implications for Diabetes Prediction
authors: Nitya Priya Agarwal, Parul Awasthi, Amit Katiyar, Anand Gupta
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Diabetes, Healthcare is a vital part of life. Unfortunately, the progressively aging population and the related increase in prolonged illness are placing substantial pressure on current healthcare systems. One of the major health problems that are spreading quickly and alarmingly around the world is diabetes. It significantly affects a nation's social, economic, and human elements. Diabetes mellitus can have a negative impact on a variety of human organs, including the brain, nerves, heart, kidneys, eyes, skin, and more. It is crucial to address the accompanying perplexities if we are to decrease the occurrence of this sickness. Excessive thirst, excessive weariness, weight gain, wooziness, skin discoloration, sexual problem, fungus infection, high blood sugar, frequent urination, etc. are some of the prevalent early symptoms and indicators of diabetes. Because of the under resourced healthcare system, people all over the world are uninformed of the signs, symptoms, and difficulties of this syndrome. The need for resources from hospital beds to doctors and nurses is exceptionally high. The Machine learning has been broadly recognized as a prospective solution to relieve the burdens on healthcare systems and has thus been the focus of much recent research. There are about 40 diverse forms of diabetes, some of which include Type 1, Type 2, gestational, and pre-diabetes. Of all diabetes forms, Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) affects 90% of the population. This will enable us to decide which of these approaches is the most successful. Some of the performance measures that are looked at include accuracy, F-measure, recall, and precision. Other performance measures are also available. The best accuracy score is 76% for the Random Forest model, which also has the highest overall score of 0.76 and the highest f-measure value. Getting the highest score possible is how all of these honours came to be. Additionally, with a precision value of 0.63, it has the greatest f-measure and f-measure precision values. It also has the greatest worth. Out of all the methods, the Random Forest won, obtaining the highest recall score of 0.74

title: Effect of Ground Plane Variation and Defected Ground Structures on the Multiband Response of a Fork-Shaped Microstrip Antenna
authors: D Ramesh Varma, P Bhargavi, Y Keerthi, B Rohitha, M Venkata Subbarao, G Challa Ram
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: A compact fork-shaped microstrip antenna is developed and investigated to study how variations in ground-plane geometry and the use of defected ground structures (DGS) influence multiband behavior. The prototype is realized on an FR-4 printed-circuit board with dielectric constant 4.4 and thickness 1.6 mm. To quantify the impact of the ground region, the ground length is swept from 4 mm to 10 mm and the corresponding changes in input impedance and resonant response are examined. Several DGS arrangements (DG1–DG4), including an extended configuration, are also introduced to refine impedance matching and improve radiation stability over multiple operating bands. The extended DGS reshapes the current flow beneath the radiating element, enabling tighter control of the excited modes and a wider achievable bandwidth than with the basic DGS layouts. Full-wave simulations performed from 2 to 12 GHz demonstrate that suitable ground-plane sizing combined with DGS integration markedly modifies the return-loss profile and resonance locations. With optimized dimensions, the antenna attains return-loss values better than −30 dB at several frequencies, indicating efficient multi-band operation. The obtained responses confirm that the proposed structure is well suited to WLAN, WiMAX, and C-band wireless links and underscore the role of careful ground-plane and extended-DGS design in compact antenna development.

title: Deep Learning based Automated Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Bindu Priya Makala, Manoj Kumar D, B L Sirisha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: One of the serious side effects of diabetes is diabetic retinopathy (DR), which causes blindness and progressive vision loss. early and precise detection and assessment of DR severity is critical for treatment. Retinal scans are frequently used for subjective analysis by the medical experts, which is time-consuming, and error-prone manual diagnosis. To overcome these challenges, this study proposed an automated DR classification framework by employing advanced image processing techniques and combined with NASNet-Large model, which is an effective convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture discovered via neural architecture search. The implemented methodology includes green-channel extraction, and enhancement by contrast enhancement (CLAHE) and followed by segmentation for retinal features based on morphological transformations. The NASNet-Large model is then trained and validated on standardized retinal datasets (Messidor-1), achieving accurate classification across multiple DR severity levels. The Experimental results obtained shows good performance with an accuracy of 92.2%, and highlighting the NASNet-Large’s capability to in effectively recognizing the retinal features. The proposed system significantly enhances the automated DR screening, and offers robust diagnostic assistance by reducing the ophthalmologists' workload and impacts better patient outcomes.

title: Environmentally Resilient Crop Prediction Framework using Digital Twin and XGBoost and Remote Sensing Data
authors: A. Kalaivani, V. Asha, J. Sathya, Ranjitha, R Manogna, Ravishankar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Agriculture is essential to food security and economic development. Obtaining reliable crop yield estimates and making appropriate crop choices are necessary actions to improve productivity and resource efficiency. Current guidelines are established based on grower’s practical experience, which typically publicly recognizes estimates that depend on manual estimates, limitations, slow response time, and their sensitivity to the environment. With expansion as an effective methodology to overcome these limitations. This research introduces the Extreme Gradient Boosting(XGBoost) algorithm and applies it for regression and classification problems. The XGBoost Regressor was used to predict results with an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.96 and a low Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 1.71. The XGBoost Classifier was employed to recommend crops and produced an accuracy of 95.27% in classifying potential crops based on soil and climate features including amounts of rainfall, temperature, pH, and moisture. Feature importance was analyzed to teach the most significant feature on yield outcomes. The results show that using data science to support farmer’s decision-making process improves management and environmental sustainability is likely to support sustainable outcomes.

title: Emoticare+: A Proactive and Personalized Emotion-Aware Conversational Agent for Mental Health Support
authors: Mary Valentina Janet A, Alexander R, G. Brindha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The emerging prevalence of mental health challenges calls for intelligent, accessible, yet privacy-preserving support systems. Although conversational AI agents have shown potential for emotional support, most existing solutions remain reactive and provide static responses based solely on instantaneous emotion recognition. This work presents EmotiCare+, a proactive and personalized emotion-aware conversation agent to offer effective support for mental well-being by predictive modelling, adaptive intervention, and transparent decision-making. The proposed system incorporates a hybrid deep learning–based emotion recognition module with temporal emotion transition modelling to predict the time series emotional drift. An adaptive coping strategy engine tailors therapeutic interventions based on user feedback and historical effectiveness, while personalized emotional baseline calibration decreases inter-user variability in improving alert precision. Furthermore, a cognitive load and emotional fatigue index allows early detection of burnout, while an explainable emotion and strategy framework enhances user trust and ethical compliance. EmotiCare+ is implemented on generic CPU-based hardware with encrypted local storage, ensuring privacy and compliance with relevant regulations. An experimental evaluation of anonymised conversational data highlights proactive early risk detection, reduced false alerts, improved emotional recovery, and very high user satisfaction as key benefits that facilitate its development into an ethical mental health support platform.

title: Analysis and Comparison of Cloud Data Storage Services with a Practical Study on Google Drive
authors: Priyanka V, Lekshmi S. Raveendran, R. Kesavan, Sandhya.U, Anuradha. T, Jose Anand A.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The work evaluates thoroughly the most popular cloud storage solutions with the aim to compare their performance, security and cost-effectiveness. The study was done on five major suppliers, i.e., Google Drive, MEGA, iCloud, Dropbox and OneDrive through a combination of quantitative and qualitative methods. The qualitative evaluation focused on concealment of input and output, throughput, reliability index in supervised networks, encryption strength in qualitative appraisal, authentication, data redundancy, privacy transparency, and accessibility. The experimental findings pinpointed Google Drive and OneDrive as the winners in the race of speed, security and reliability, with high availability and reliability indices. MEGA, on the other hand, offered the best in terms of encryption and privacy control but at a slightly slower pace due to the last encryption step. The case of Dropbox is somewhat paradoxical as it has only moderate privacy transparency but at the same time it has the highest cost per gigabyte because of its low price. The results are important not only for individual users but also for companies as they point out the criteria for choosing cloud storage solutions with regard to performance, security, and economic factors

title: When Bodies Resonate in Sound: Sonifying Interpersonal Movement Dynamics in Dance

publish: Proceedings of the 10th International Conference on Movement and Computing

authors: Lingxi Cui, Huan Li, Ke Chen, Lidan Shou, Gang Chen

title: Graph of Agentic Thoughts: Bridging Agentic Reasoning and Explainability through Graphs
authors: Moushumi Mahato, Javaid Nabi
publish: Proceedings of the 13th ACM IKDD International Conference on Data Science
