title: LoRaWAN and Arduino based Smart Campus Energy Monitoring
authors: Leo Raju, Bhuvanesh N P, Dhanushram K, Prasanth V A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: This study presents a smart campus energy monitoring system leveraging the LoRaWAN and Arduino technologies to reduce excessive energy use and advance sustainability. As compared to existing short-range communication technologies such as Wi-Fi and ZigBee, LoRaWAN offers long-range low-power data transmission capabilities, which are useful for campus-wide monitoring. The smart campus energy monitoring platform design utilizes PZEM-004T sensors and Arduino Mega 2560 at the transmitting nodes to collect electrical parameters such as voltage, current, power, and energy consumption and transmits this data to a receiver node via a LoRa module. The receiver node transmits data to a cloud platform via LoRa Gateway and ESP8266 and provides real-time data accessible on a web-based interface. The framework for a smart campus energy monitoring platform provided is a way to promote energy awareness, enables remote monitoring, and supports data-driven decision making for increased energy efficiency.

title: Monitoring and Mitigating Data Drift in Batch-based Machine Learning Systems using Adaptive Retraining
authors: G.Bharath Kumar Reddy, G.Charan Kumar Reddy, P.J.Beslin Pajila
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Machine learning models deployed often exhibit a loss of performance when they are used in non-stationary settings, where data distributions change with time. This is referred to as data drift, which is a major issue with batch-based systems, where delayed feedback may cause early indicators of a poorer model to be obscured. In this paper, a batch-based adaptive learning system will be introduced, and it will incorporate statistical drift detection and performance-based monitoring to detect both covariate and concept drift. The selective sliding-window retraining approach is adopted to retrain models using recent batches of data but preventing future data leakage. The proposed framework is tested on an actual credit-risk dataset and compared to the strategies of retraining the baseline and the other drift-adaptation methods. The effectiveness of the proposed approach in terms of performance stability during a drift, the absence of excessive retraining overhead, and the achievable deployment latency is proved by experimental results. The paper also examines sensitivity to window-size, overhead of deployment, and limitations intrinsic to it, and the paper notes the applicability of this framework into application in the real-world setting of batch-processing.

title: KisanConnect: A Unified Smart Service Platform for Farmers and Rythu Seva Kedram
authors: Mamidi Tuhi, K. Sree Vijayalakshmi, Inala Jaswanth Naidu, T.V.N.G. Swetha
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Agriculture remains the backbone of rural India, yet farmers continue to face challenges in accessing timely information, essential agricultural inputs, and government welfare benefits. The Government of Andhra Pradesh has launched Rythu Seva Kendrams (RSKs) for farmer welfare. Rythu Seva Kendram is a place where farmers can get the necessary seeds, fertilizers, and other assistance. Here, all the requirements of farmers can be fulfilled. Farmers visit Rythu Seva Kendram located in their respective area. Until farmers physically visit the RSKs office, there is no means of knowing whether the required fertilizers are available or whether the office is open. There is no platform to connect RSK staff and farmers. So, this paper presents progressive web app (app) to reduce the gap between RSK staff and farmers. This system reduces the need for frequent visits to RSKs, as all communication takes place through the application. The app simplifies the work of both RSK staff and farmers. Farmers can access stock details, scheme information, request required fertilizers, pesticides. we included a caching technique for providing offline accessibility. So, the data is available to users in offline mode. The RSK staff can make use of this app to monitor stock and sales data digitally, replacing the need for maintaining hard copies of records. The objective of proposed paper is reduce the communication gap between farmers and RSK officials through mobile app. Our aim is to make all necessary services available to farmers in a few clicks. Additionally, our innovative approach reduces the workload of the officials also.

title: Smart Coastal Erosion Monitoring using IoT
authors: Rishant Kumar Singh, Bishal Swain, Bharti
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Coastal erosion is a significant menace to ecological balance, infrastructure and lives of the people on the coast in the world. Though the conventional systems of tracking and monitoring are of help, they tend not to be adequately placed to provide real time, constant information which can be acted on to act on time. To resolve this dilemma, this paper would propose a Smart Coastal Erosion Monitoring System wherein Internet of Things (IoT) technology is used as the driving force. The system relies on a network of IoT-based sensors that are connected to collect valuable data on linear changes, waves, soil humidity, as well as weather conditions. These data flows are transported to a cloud cloud computing system to be intelligently processed to anticipate early signs of erosion, conduct predictive estimates using data-driven models. The proposed framework is concerned with scalability, low costs of implementation and also capabilities of integrating with geographic information systems (GIS) to enhance visualization. The results that it is expected to achieve include high degree of accuracy in detecting erosion, proactive assessment of risks, and policy implications to the policy makers and the environmental agencies. Lastly, the solution would be beneficial in securing sensitive coastlines, increasing the disaster preparedness, and assisting in sustainable management of coastal lands using IoT.

title: Predictive Maintenance of Power MOSFETs with a Baseline RUL Prototype
authors: Nayan Kathait, Shivani Sharma, Rishibrind Kumar Upadhyay
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Power MOSFETs are integral to a wide range of critical systems, including consumer electronics, automotive, and aerospace applications, where their failure can result in significant operational risks. Accurate estimation of Remaining Useful Life (RUL) is essential for enabling Predictive Maintenance (PdM) strategies to enhance system reliability and safety. In this study, we present a data-driven approach utilizing time-series analysis of the NASA Prognostics Centre of Excellence (PCoE) MOSFET Ageing Dataset. Key features such as temperature-normalized ON-state resistance degradation (ΔRDS(ON)) are extracted to characterize the device's ageing behavior. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network is employed to model temporal degradation patterns and predict RUL. Our results indicate that ΔRDS(ON) is a strong indicator of MOSFET health, and the proposed LSTM-based model achieves competitive accuracy when benchmarked against existing methods on a subset of the dataset. This work contributes a comparative survey of machine learning techniques for MOSFET prognostics and a baseline LSTM prototype implementation.

title: Evaluation of Deep Learning Architectures for Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Priya Arora, Soham Biren Katlariwala, Mitali Raj, Mahdisa Moradi, Sunita Sahu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet-connected systems has significantly expanded the surface of potential cyber threats, necessitating efficient Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) capable of handling complex and high-volume network traffic. In this paper, we provide a detailed comparative evaluation of deep learning-based IDS models tested on two popular benchmark datasets: KDD Cup 99 and UNSW-NB15. Both datasets represent distinct eras and characteristics of network traffic, with KDD focusing on classical attack types and UNSW-NB15 incorporating modern, real-world traffic diversity. Our proposed framework integrates preprocessing, feature selection, and multiple neural architectures including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Deep Neural Networks (DNN), and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to evaluate their performance under unified experimental conditions. The study systematically examines accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics, revealing that CNN-based models outperform other architectures on both datasets due to their hierarchical feature extraction capabilities. Furthermore, feature correlation analysis highlights dataset-specific dependencies that influence model generalization. This work provides a unified empirical benchmark and insights into how deep learning models adapt across legacy and modern intrusion datasets, contributing to the ongoing advancement of data-driven network security research.

title: Weed Recognition Using Deep Learning Techniques: A Review
authors: Nisha Choudhary, Om Prakash Sangwan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Weed infestation continues to be a thorn in agriculture, reducing crop yield and increasing production costs. Deep learning has proved to be a robust alternative to traditional and chemical-based weed management, with automatic, precise, and scalable outputs. This review compares three prominent strategies: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Vision Transformers (ViTs), and Hybrid (CNN-Transformer) models on various datasets and studies. Experiments demonstrate that CNNs are effective but perform poorly under actual field conditions, ViTs excel through global context capture but require extensive datasets and a great deal of computational resources, while hybrids share both the strengths to provide the best balance among accuracy, energy efficiency, and scalability. In spite of these developments, challenges like class imbalance, limited interpretability, and generalization are still present, calling for future research on lightweight, interpretable, and field-deployable solutions to sustainable weed control. These advancements can improve food security and reduce chemical usage in farming.

title: Optimizing Supervised Learning via Entropy Regularization
authors: Ruchi Garg, Sumit Kumar
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Entropy, a measure of uncertainty in information theory, plays a critical role in understanding the behavior of probabilistic models. In supervised machine learning, optimization techniques often neglect entropy dynamics, leading to overconfident predictions, poor calibration, and reduced robustness. This paper introduces a principled framework for entropy-aware optimization, integrating entropy metrics into loss functions, regularization, and training dynamics. We propose entropy-adaptive mechanisms that dynamically respond to prediction uncertainty, benchmark them across diverse datasets, and demonstrate improvements in accuracy, calibration, and resilience to noisy labels. Our theoretical analysis connects entropy-aware learning to information bottleneck principles and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, offering a robust foundation for future research.

title: Vulnerability Discovery Modeling in multi-version software using Weibull- Logistic distribution
authors: Anchal Dhillon, Vijay Kumar, Yoshinobu Tamura, Sunil K. Khatri
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: Unresolved security weaknesses raised serious concerns since they have the potential to allow serious breaches and threaten software system. A vulnerability is a loophole or flaw in a security system that an attacker could take advantage of and cause harm or loss. Since significant portions of code are carried over into subsequent releases, the vulnerabilities may remain and resurface in later versions. These vulnerabilities are often introduced during development as a result of ongoing upgrades and the addition of new features. It is crucial to identify not only when vulnerabilities are introduced but how they are discovered over time because they remain across software generations. A systematic framework for investigating vulnerability discovery trends and forecasting the accumulation of defects has been made available by Vulnerability Discovery Modeling (VDM). However, a large amount of the work that has been done is limited to single-release software, ignoring the impact of shared code and inherited vulnerabilities. In this work, we prefer a multi-version VDM derived from generalized Weibull–logistic distribution. The proposed work intends to examine the impact of vulnerabilities in previous releases and their recurrence in upgraded systems via code inheritance. The model assesses the potential for unfixed vulnerabilities from previous iterations to reappear during new code testing and impact the current release. Real datasets from three distinct Windows Server releases are used to test and validate the model. The prediction performance shows that the newly proposed generalized Weibull-Logistic VDM performs better than existing multi-release VDM. The proposed model offers a more practical framework for evaluating persistent security threats in extensive software ecosystems by incorporating vulnerability inheritance into the discovery process.

title: IoT-Driven Innovations in Food Processing for Sustainable Industry 4.0 Development
authors: Ashish Avasthi, Ranu Burad, Prerna Srivastava, Vijay Singh, Suresh Kadwa, Udit Mamodiya
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is fundamentally transforming the food processing industry by enabling real-time monitoring, enhanced traceability, and automation across complex supply chains. As food safety and quality demand continuous improvement amid rising consumer awareness and regulatory pressures, IoT facilitates data-driven decision-making from farm to fork. This paper explores the integration of IoT technologies within food processing systems and their synergy with Industry 4.0 principles, encompassing cyber-physical systems, connectivity, and automation. Key applications include environmental monitoring, predictive maintenance, smart packaging, and supply chain transparency. We discuss how IoT drives efficiency gains, reduces waste, and supports sustainability goals. Furthermore, this study addresses challenges including data security, privacy concerns, and implementation barriers. Experimental examples of sensor deployment and analytical models for process optimization are presented. By advancing IoT adoption within Industry 4.0 frameworks, the food processing sector can enhance safety standards, operational resilience, and consumer trust, ensuring sustainable and innovative food production for future demands.

title: Optimized YOLOv8 with Spatial Path Guidance for Reliable Outdoor Navigation Assistance for Visually Impaired Individuals
authors: V. Murali Bhaskaran, Nanditha N, Neela A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Vision-based outdoor navigation systems for visually impaired individuals often struggle with detection reliability, limited spatial guidance, and a lack of real-world testing. This paper presents a better assistive navigation framework that improves YOLOv8 object detection. It uses optimized training methods with a specially selected outdoor dataset that includes 35 important object classes. The system has a spatial path guidance module that looks at obstacle distribution and distance to offer real-time direction advice for safer navigation. A calibrated monocular distance estimation method is used to provide reliable awareness of nearby objects. An intelligent SOS mechanism supports emergency alerts and live location sharing. The framework is tested by comparing it with standard object detection models and through real-world outdoor experiments in various scenarios. The results show improved detection accuracy, fewer false alerts, and better navigation reliability, demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach for outdoor mobility assistance.

title: Hybrid GRU-Attention Network for Multi-Dimensional Mental Health Risk Prediction: A Comparative Deep Learning Analysis
authors: A.Akilandeswari
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Mental health risk assessment is the most critical issue with healthcare analytics because of the multifaceted interaction between psychological, behavioral, and time aspects. Last generation machine learning methods have difficulty in long-range dependencies and cannot dynamically rank features in terms of their predictive value. We present Hybrid GRU-Attention Network, which is a combination of Gated Recurrent Units and multi-head attention models to predict mental health risks holistically. Extensive testing on real-world clinical data proves that our model could achieve a high level of performance with 98.70 percent accuracy and 0.0307 loss which is better than Standard GRU (97.70 percent accuracy), LSTM (96.75 percent accuracy) and BidirectionalGRU (96.90 percent accuracy). The model has good convergent characteristics, a small amount of overfitting and a great deal of confidence calibration and therefore may be used to implement the model in clinical decision support systems. Our work contributes to computational psychiatry since it offers a robust, interpretable and correct mental health risk assessment framework.

title: An Efficient Underwater Acoustic Sensor Network with Delay-Aware Error Analysis and CNN-based Energy Management
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: An Underwater Acoustic Sensor Network (UASN) comprises acoustic communication-based sensors to collect data underwater. We developed the EUDECE model of underwater acoustic communication and data classification, integrating physical-layer modelling and machine learning. The signal propagation by Urick, including path loss and absorption, nodes estimate ambient noise to determine the Signal-To-Noise Ratio (SNR) for transmissions. The application of an RTS/CTS-based MAC scheme ensures optimal energy for maintaining SINR. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) performs signal processing for proper feature extraction, assisted by the use of dropout layers for the prevention of overfitting. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) data classifier for accurate data classification, the two models are utilised to make better-informed decisions by the network. The performance analysis of throughput, average delay, average traffic time, channel utilisation, Q-value, packet delivery ratio, and per packet is compared by the existing model CADDR, ATODI, APERU, with the EUDECE model.

title: Real-Time Image Processing Technology for Robotic Vision Systems in Precision Agriculture
authors: Lingdong Mei, Shuo Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: To address the challenges of poor robustness and real-time performance of robotic vision systems in precision agriculture field operations due to drastic changes in lighting, visual and weather diversity, and other factors, this paper constructs a lightweight and improved YOLOv8n model. The original backbone is replaced with HGNetV2 to significantly reduce the convolutional computational burden and floating-point operations. LSKA attention is embedded in SPPF to achieve selective aggregation of multi-scale context and channel recalibration. BiFPN is used for top-down and bottom-up bidirectional weighted feature fusion to enhance multi-scale information interaction. Finally, DSC is used to reconstruct the prediction head to compress parameters and reduce inference computation. An end-to-end evaluation was conducted using a public field weed dataset and an embedded platform. The improved YOLOv8n model achieved 92.13%/95.88%/93.2% accuracy in the morning, midday, and evening on the test set. The model achieved an FPS of 68.50 and an average inference latency of 14.60 ms, improving weed recognition accuracy in complex environments and real-time processing capabilities for edge deployment. This model provides an engineered, lightweight path for real-time image processing and inference latency constraints in robotic vision systems.

title: Facial Expression Recognition Using Fine-Tuned ResNet-50 Model
authors: Priyal Bhusate, Pranav Chinthala, Aditya Ulangwar, Shivam Aher, Kanak Bansal, Urvee Bhagwat, Ajay Chhajed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) explores facial expressions recognition for various applications such as mental health assessment and human-computer interaction, by fine-tuning a ResNet-50 model using Low-Rank Adaptation methods on the FER20113 dataset. The system processes facial images, detects faces, and predicts emotional status using deep learning techniques and optimized training strategies. While the approach achieves notable accuracy for several expression’s categories, there are challenges such as class imbalance, resource needed for training, and issues with misclassification and noisy data. The work aims to provide clinicians and researchers with improved tools for emotional assessment but recognizes current limitations and areas for future scope enhancement.

title: Chemical Potential Driven Transition from Single Band to Dual band Resonance in Graphene Hybrid Reconfigurable THz Antenna for 6G Applications
authors: Arpita Raj, Kajal Pandey, Ravi Gupta, Ravinder Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: A technique has been designed to transition into dual band graphene metallic hybrid THz antenna to cover the entire 2.4–6 THz frequency range. The tunability of surface plasmonic resonance through the variation of chemical potential plays a significant role in modern nano electronic and terahertz devices. In this it demonstrate the conversion of a single band to dual band response by increasing the chemical potential upto to 0.9 eV. The variation of chemical potential of graphene shift the fermi energy level, concentrating carrier density and enhancing the plasmon coupling. This modifies the resonance condition, leading to the splitting the fundamental mode, where the single band response evolves into dual band response. The Dual band of THz antenna is suitable for the applications of multifunctional terahertz antenna, for sensors and the modulators.

title: Airspeed Sensor Reliability Assessment for Robust UAV Control
authors: Atharva Phand, Javed Sayyad, Manoj Kumar Shukla, Manoj Singh Adhikari, Swapnil Narkhede
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) stability, control, and navigation depend on precise airspeed measurement. Airspeed sensors, which are usually based on dynamic pressure, can introduce significant measurement errors due to their sensitivity to environmental and flight-related factors, such as temperature changes, wind disturbances, altitude variations, and UAV orientation. A high-fidelity MATLAB-based simulation framework is presented in this study to assess sensor performance during takeoff, cruise, and landing. It also includes significant parameters such as pitch, roll, yaw, altitude, and weather. Controlled flight tests, aside from the simulation, also enable evaluation of calibration methods and verification of sensor accuracy. For enhanced measurement reliability, the study also examines adaptive solutions, including real-time error compensation and sensor location. The findings give insights into sensor responses under dynamic flight conditions, informing the design of reliable airspeed sensing and smart flight control systems. The outcomes have relevance to the application of UAVs in search and rescue missions, environmental monitoring, precision delivery, and autonomous aerial missions.

title: Automated Vulnerability Scanning and Bug Detection for Secure Web Applications
authors: Atharva Kekare, Vedaant Chaudhari, Vidhya R.
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Automated vulnerability identification is essential for protecting digital systems since web applications are increasingly vulnerable to cybersecurity threats. This paper introduces a web security assessment method that uses automated URL-based scanning to find vulnerabilities that match the OWASP Top 10. In addition to identifying software flaws that could compromise dependability, the method combines rule-based detection with structured logging to identify important threats including SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting (XSS), resource-intensive Dynamic Application Security Testing platforms, is especially well-suited for early-stage security assessments and instructional use because it is built for quick analysis with minimal computational overhead. Live bug detection words on HTTP traffic to encourage responsible testing, and non-intrusive scanning techniques are used to evaluate HTTPS destinations. To preserve report integrity, scan findings are kept as safe, timestamped documents. This study enhances web application resilience against changing cyberthreats and fortifies automated auditing procedures.

title: An Adaptive Edge-Cloud Task Offloading Framework using Reinforcement Learning for Latency-Sensitive AI Applications
authors: Anshu Vashisth, Gagandeep Kaur, Bhupinder Kaur, Gaurav Sethi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Applications of artificial intelligence (AI) and real-time video analytics, autonomous perception, augmented reality, and intelligent healthcare are latency-constrained and cannot be supported by cloud-centric architectures alone. Although edge computing can reduce the latency by executing tasks nearer to the data sources, the scarcity of edge resources and dynamic networks render static or heuristic-based offloading schemes of tasks not optimal. Current methods are usually dependent on fixed thresholds, decision by rules, or offline optimization, and thus provide no flexibility to the changes in workload, network traffic, and service level demands at runtime. The paper presents a dynamically task placement-based adaptive edge-cloud task offloading framework, constructed based on reinforcement learning or the use of reinforcing learning (RL), to address latency-sensitive AI task workloads dynamically. The model of the offloading decision process is the Markov Decision Process (MDP), in which the agent will learn an optimal policy by taking into account the characteristics of different tasks, availability of edge resources, network latency, and cloud processing delay. A reward mechanism is constructed to ensure a reduction in end-to-end latency and energy efficiency, as well as reliability in task completion. Larger-scale simulations also show that the proposed framework lowers the average latency by 18-25% relative to both the static and heuristic baselines, converges faster on dynamically changing workloads, and remains stable to network changes. The findings show that adaptive offloading that is RL-based offers a high-quality and scalable solution to next-generation edge-cloud AI systems.

title: OpenStack Release Evolution: Enabling Practical Upgrade Choices for Cloud Operators Through Benchmarking
authors: Rekha Jain, Vansh Agarwal, Narendra Kumar Meena
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: OpenStack is a widely adopted open-source cloud computing platform, where rapid release cycles and evolving architecture introduce significant challenges for operators planning system upgrades. In this work, we systematically analyze the evolution of OpenStack from Austin (2010) to Epoxy (2025), focusing on the practical implications of upgrading across major releases. We conduct empirical benchmarking of instance lifecycle operations using Rally, and employ an M/M/1 queueing model to estimate provisioning delay and headroom under increasing load. Our results reveal that while recent releases such as Caracal exhibit modest performance overheads with instance boot times increasing from 10.71 s (Queens) to 12.57 s (17% increase) and modeled system response times rising from 23.05 s to 33.80 s, they introduce stronger security and automation compared to earlier versions. Notably, Rocky demonstrates the highest operational overhead (16.14 s boot time, 83.52 s modeled system time under λ = 0.05 req/s), while Caracal achieves a balance between performance and advanced functionality. The study provides actionable insights to guide practitioners in making informed upgrade decisions, balancing new feature adoption with operational risks and system complexity.

title: Large Language Models for Explainable and Self-Service Business Intelligence in Large Enterprises
authors: Ajith Suresh
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Business intelligence (BI) in large enterprises refers to the systematic process of collecting, integrating, analyzing, and interpreting organizational data to support strategic and operational decision-making. Traditional enterprise BI systems rely heavily on predefined dashboards, static reports, and manually authored Structured Query Language (SQL) queries, which ensure governance and reliability but require technical expertise and limit flexibility for non-technical users. As data volumes and analytical demands increase, these approaches struggle to provide accessible, interactive, and explainable analytics at scale. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have introduced new possibilities for natural language-driven analytics; however, existing LLM-based BI solutions often lack semantic grounding, governance enforcement, and explanation fidelity, rendering them unsuitable for deployment in large enterprise environments. This study proposes a semantic-constrained LLM architecture leveraging Generative Pre-trained Transformer 4 (GPT-4) to enables explainable and self-service BI while preserving enterprise analytical correctness and control. The approach integrates GPT-4-based natural language understanding and explanation generation with an enterprise semantic layer and symbolic query planning, ensuring alignment with enterprise-defined metrics, schemas, and aggregation rules. The proposed system is evaluated using the AdventureWorks enterprise data warehouse. Experimental results demonstrate a query correctness of 94.6% and semantic validity of 97.8%, indicating reliable interpretation of natural language analytical requests. Explanation faithfulness reaches 95.2%, and explanation quality achieves an average score of 4.5 out of 5, supporting transparent and interpretable analytics. A self-service success rate of 92.1% further confirms effective support for non-technical users with interactive response times. This study paves the way for the adoption of LLM-enabled, explainable, and governance-aware BI systems in large enterprises, enabling scalable and trustworthy natural language analytics.

title: Research on 3D Reconstruction of Pick-and-Hang Robot Workspace Based on Binocular Vision and IMU Fusion
authors: Yuqiang He, Shiqiang Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Monocular vision 3D reconstruction mainly relies on the image information acquired by a single camera to infer the 3D structure of the scene, and the acquired image is one-sided, which leads to the low quality of 3D reconstruction, in order to study the 3D reconstruction method of pick-and-hang robot workspace based on binocular vision and IMU fusion. The left and right eye images are synchronously acquired through the checkerboard calibration board, and the precise calibration of the binocular system is completed to obtain the camera parameters. The IMU data are combined in image processing to compensate the position error and correct the boundary of the reachable space. Based on the calibration parameters and fusion data, a dense 3D model of the workspace is constructed to provide a decision basis for robot grasping and obstacle avoidance. The experimental results show that the errors of the method in the $X, Y$, and Z directions are $-0.15 \sim 0.22 \mathrm{~mm}$, $-0.02 \sim 0.06 \mathrm{~mm}$, and $0.95 \sim 1.42 \mathrm{~mm}$, respectively, and the completeness reaches $0.91 \sim 0.95$, and the success rate of collision detection is stabilized at $96 \% \sim 99 \%$, and all the indexes are superior to those of the comparison method, which verifies that the method is of high precision and practicability, and it can effectively support the pick-and-hang robot’s practical operational needs.

title: A Dual-Branch Feature Enhancement Approach for Unsupervised Person Re-identification
authors: Guanghui Deng, Xiaoyong Song, Xuehong Sun, Liping Liu, Yingdong Li, Xiaolong Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: In addressing the challenges of insufficient globallocal joint modeling and limited discriminative feature representation in most existing unsupervised person reidentification methods, this paper proposes a pure Transformerbased dual-branch feature enhancement approach for unsupervised person re-identification. A deep-shallow dualbranch network architecture is designed to improve the discriminability of feature representations, while average fusion is employed to enhance feature diversity. Additionally, a dual attention mechanism is introduced, consisting of two stages: feature aggregation and feature redistribution. This mechanism dynamically enhances informative features and suppresses noise, thereby improving both the recognition accuracy and robustness of the model. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated on two benchmark datasets: Market-1501 and DukeMTMC-reID.

title: Medical Image Volumetric Arbitrary Scale Super-Resolution Via Learnable Adaptive Upsampling and Weight Dynamic Balancing
authors: Shulin Li, Jiapeng Qi, Chaolu Feng, Yang Hu
abstract: Volumetric Computed Tomography (CT and Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) are crucial in clinical diagnosis. However, practical limitations often result in anisotropic volumes with high in-plane but low through plane resolution. Volumetric SR therefore focuses on recovering missing through-plane details. Stronger anatomical variation along the through-plane makes direct adaptation of 2D restoration components prone to inefficient context aggregation and limited robustness in volumetric settings. In addition, although recent arbitrary-scale SR methods enable flexible scaling, they often face a trade-off between scale flexibility and parameter efficiency. To overcome these challenges, we propose a Learnable Adaptive Upsampling-based Volumetric SR network (LAUVSR). The first key innovation is a volumetric-anisotropy-driven upsampling core, which integrates a learnable weight generation module employing in-plane weight sharing to capture fine details and through-plane independent generation to model anatomical variations, augmented by a volume specific criss-cross attention mechanism through mapping attention to in-plane and through-plane interactions, ensuring both high-fidelity and parameter-efficient SR at arbitrary integer and decimal query scales. Furthermore, we in corporate a reinforcement-learning-guided weight dynamic balancing strategy to improve training robustness across datasets, modalities, and target scales. Extensive image quality assessments were conducted on four datasets across two modalities (CT and MRI) using synthetically downsampled LR–HR pairs. These assessments demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method in achieving high-quality SR results across a dense range of integer and decimal upsampling scales.Additionally, evaluations at unseen upsampling scales and downstream task analyses further support its robustness and practical potential.

title: FAST-RTFM: Fast RGB–Thermal Fusion for Point Cloud Mapping in Low-Light Environments
authors: Jiaming Xu, Tianyong Ye, Shikuan Shi, Man He, Chunran Zheng, Tingwen Huang, Yukang Cui
abstract: Thermal cameras, capable of operating independently of ambient light while acquiring extra-spectral radiation data, present distinct advantages over conventional RGB cameras that require adequate illumination in low-light environments. However, traditional visual simultaneous localization and mapping methods struggle with thermal imagery due to limitations, such as lower image resolution, constrained dynamic range, and imaging interruptions during nonuniformity correction. Although existing deep learning-based approaches have partially improved the performance of thermal imaging in localization and mapping tasks, their generalization capability across diverse environments remains weaker than traditional geometric methods. Integrating thermal information as complementary data with other sensors, such as LiDAR and RGB cameras, has proven to be an effective approach to address this issue. This article introduces a pointwise RGB–thermal pixel fusion strategy for efficient real-time point cloud coloring based on LiDAR–inertial–visual odometry frameworks. By effectively combining the complementary strengths of thermal cameras (illumination robustness), RGB sensors (textural details), and LiDAR's high-precision geometric structures, we successfully implement an efficient mapping system for target detection and nighttime reconnaissance. The system achieves both high real-time performance and accuracy, demonstrating broad application potential in security operations and emergency response scenarios.

title: Development of a Unified Resilience Metric for Microgrids and Its Improvement Through Building Energy Management Systems
authors: Mohammadreza Fathizadan, Mahesh S. Illindala
abstract: The increasing frequency and severity of natural disasters driven by climate changes have caused widespread power disruptions and prolonged outages globally. As a result, improving power system resilience has become a major research focus. This paper introduces a Unified Resilience Metric (URM) that provides a consistent and intuitive quantification of system resilience, independent of event type. Additionally, a microgrid Energy Management System (EMS) using Building Energy Management System (BEMS)-informed demand profiles is developed to enhance operational performance during major disruptions. Using a modified IEEE 13-bus system as a case study, the study evaluates the proposed framework based on established reliability indices and the introduced resilience metric. Simulation results demonstrate that integrating BEMS significantly improves microgrid performance by enabling dynamic load shedding, reducing total curtailed load, and enhancing system recovery. Specifically, the proposed framework reduces load shedding by up to 6.5 MWh and lowers operating costs by as much as $27,000 over a 24-hour period under contingency scenarios, while achieving a 3.74% improvement in the proposed resilience metric and notable reductions in standard reliability indices. These improvements not only strengthen technical resilience and reliability but also contribute to the overall social welfare of the community.

title: Effects of Displacement Damage on the Structural and Electrical Properties of Ferroelectric Hafnium Zirconium Oxide Capacitors
authors: Samantha T. Jaszewski, Colin McKay, David R. Hughart, John M. Cain, Zachary T. Piontkowski, Toai Ton-That, Anthony McDonald, Megan K. Lenox, Jon F. Ihlefeld, M. David Henry
abstract: This study investigates the effects of displacement damage induced by heavy ion irradiation on the structural and electrical properties of ferroelectric hafnium zirconium oxide (HZO) capacitors and thin films, establishing displacement-damage limits and decoupling the electrical degradation from irradiation-induced phase transformation by comparing films with different starting phase constitution. Using 6 and 8 MeV Au<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2+</sup> ions, the radiation tolerance of HZO capacitors was assessed through polarization-voltage and capacitance-voltage measurements. The findings reveal that significant losses in remanent polarization do not occur until high displacements per atoms (DPA), around 10-2 DPA. While lower DPA values allow for partial recovery of polarization through voltage cycling, the highest levels result in irreversible degradation. Structural characterization via grazing-incidence X-ray diffraction and Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy indicates that the HZO films containing only the metastable phases maintain their phase stability, with no significant amorphization or phase transformations, even at the highest DPA levels. However, films containing the equilibrium monoclinic phase experience phase transformations, likely due to increased oxygen vacancies which destabilize this phase. Photoluminescence spectroscopy provides evidence of a significant change of the defect populations in the irradiated samples. The introduction of oxygen vacancies during irradiation is identified as a primary factor contributing to the degradation of ferroelectric properties due to their ability to pin ferroelectric domains. This work underscores the critical role of phase composition in determining the radiation tolerance and performance of HZO capacitors and demonstrates that these devices are promising candidates for radiation environments due to their high intrinsic radiation hardness.

title: Cybersecurity in eHealth: A Scoping Review of Current Research and Trends
authors: Michael Winter, Robin Kraft, Pascal Leber, Manfred Reichert, Helmut Greger, Johannes Mühr, Rüdiger Pryss
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Healthcare ranks among the most vulnerable sectors to cybersecurity threats, experiencing widespread security incidents and substantial data breach costs. Despite growing global cybersecurity expenditures, health care systems remain distinctively vulnerable. This paper presents a scoping review of research on cybersecurity in eHealth. Following PRISMA-ScR guidelines, we searched and analyzed literature across four major databases: ACM Digital Library, IEEE Xplore, PubMed, and Web of Science. From an initial 3,146 identified papers, 567 met inclusion criteria and underwent detailed analysis. The analysis iden tifies blockchain as the most researched technical solution, with system and communication protection dominating cybersecurity categories. Geographically, research output has shifted significantly toward India and China. However, the study highlights critical gaps in real-world implemen tations, user-centric perspectives, and evaluation studies. This comprehensive review offers insights for researchers, healthcare professionals, and policy makers to develop targeted, evidence-based cybersecurity strategies that protect sensitive health data and ensure the integrity of digital health systems.

title: Post-Disaster Mobile Network Resilience Through Variance-Aware Framework with Population Weighting
authors: Merve Güllü, Sümeye Nur Karahan, Sedat Çimen, Ahmet Yazar, Mustafa Serdar Osmanca
abstract: Disaster events significantly affect communication network performance by causing spatially localized degradations that may not be captured by conventional monitoring approaches. Traditional network assessment relies primarily on average Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), which may obscure localized anomalies and fail to reveal region-specific disruptions. To address this limitation, this study proposes a multi-stage statistical and spatial analysis framework that integrates non-parametric statistical testing with density-based spatial clustering to detect localized performance degradation patterns under disaster conditions. The proposed framework combines statistical significance testing and Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN) to identify abnormal regions and quantify performance variations across network infrastructure. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively detects spatially localized anomalies and provides improved insight into network behavior during abnormal operational conditions. The findings indicate that the proposed framework enables more reliable identification of performance degradation patterns and supports more effective network monitoring and decision-making processes in disaster scenarios.

title: Context-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination Framework for Intelligent Electric Vehicle Charging Optimization
authors: Muddsair Sharif, Huseyin Seker, Yasir Javed
abstract: The accelerating transition toward electric mobility presents unprecedented challenges in coordinating distributed charging infrastructure across multi-stakeholder ecosystems. This paper introduces a novel Context-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Dynamic Resource Allocation (CAMAC-DRA) framework that addresses these challenges through intelligent integration of Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL). Our framework enables autonomous coordination of 250 electric vehicles (EVs) across 45 charging stations while dynamically adapting to real-time environmental conditions including weather patterns, traffic dynamics, grid fluctuations, and electricity pricing mechanisms. The proposed system processes 20 contextual features through sophisticated attention mechanisms, implementing hierarchical coordination protocols that balance competing objectives across five key stakeholder groups: EV users (25% weight), grid operators (20%), charging station operators (20%), fleet operators (20%), and environmental entities (15%). Through comprehensive validation using real-world datasets encompassing 441,077 charging transactions from diverse operational scenarios, the CAMAC-DRA framework demonstrates substantial improvements over state-of-the-art baseline algorithms including Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG), Asynchronous Advantage Actor-Critic (A3C), Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), and conventional GNN approaches. Experimental results reveal exceptional performance metrics: 92% multi-agent coordination success rate, 15% energy efficiency improvement, 10% operational cost reduction, 20% grid strain mitigation, and 2.3× faster convergence compared to existing methods, while maintaining 88% training stability and 85% sample efficiency. Real-world validation confirms commercial viability with a Net Present Cost of -$122,962 and 69% cost reduction through strategic renewable energy integration. The framework’s unique contribution lies in synthesizing context-aware adaptation with multi-stakeholder optimization, achieving unprecedented balance between competing objectives while maintaining real-time responsiveness. These advances position the Smart2Charge application as a transformative solution for sustainable transportation electrification, addressing critical scalability and coordination challenges in next-generation smart grid ecosystems. The demonstrated capabilities establish new benchmarks for intelligent EV charging coordination, with significant implications for urban energy management, Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology deployment valued at $25.5 billion by 2029, and the broader transition toward carbon-neutral transportation infrastructure.

title: Efficient Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption with Dual Privacy Protection_supp1-3686439.pdf
authors: Meiyan Xiao
abstract: With the rapid evolution of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, privacy-sensitive data is exploding, creating significant obstacles in the sharing and retrieval of information. To enable high-precision data retrieval and fine-grained sharing, only the integration of Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption (ABSE) with cloud storage can fulfill these requirements while ensuring security. However, existing ABSE schemes face major limitations in IoT scenarios. Publicly accessible access policies and unprotected private key attribute sets pose risks of information leakage. In addition, the limited expressiveness of access policies and high computational overhead hinder their practical deployment in IoT environments. In this paper, we propose an Efficient Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption with Dual Privacy Protection (EASDP). By fully hiding access policies and attributes, our scheme ensures strong privacy against cloud inference attacks.We enhance the expressiveness of access control through sub-policy division and improve efficiency for resource-constrained IoT devices using techniques such as offline encryption, outsourced decryption, and keyword–attribute matching. The scheme is formally proven secure. The experimental results demonstrate its high performance in encryption and decryption at the user side and its suitability for various IoT devices, confirming its effectiveness in secure and flexible data sharing across IoT healthcare applications.

title: A Comparative Study of Lightweight Convolutional Models for Visual Surface Material Classification
authors: Jeremy Sean Tan, Aaron Raymond See, Aaron Benjmin Alcuitas
abstract: Surface material classification is essential for robotic perception, enabling grasp adaptation, slip prediction, and safe manipulation. This work presents a systematic analysis of accuracy-efficiency trade-offs among lightweight convolutional neural networks for visual-only surface material classification on the LMT-108 database. The evaluation spans both classical and compact models, including AlexNet, ResNet-18, ShuffleNetV2 (x0.5, x1.0, x1.5), MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3 (Small and Large), and EfficientNet (B0–B2), all trained under an identical pipeline and assessed in terms of accuracy, parameter count, floating-point operations (FLOPs), and inference latency. We apply a lightweight augmentation and regularization strategy to enhance generalization on texture-rich visual data. Across all architectures, EfficientNet-B2 achieved the highest mean Top-1 accuracy (97.8%). However, multi-objective analysis reveals that ShuffleNetV2 and MobileNet architectures provided the best accuracy–efficiency balance, attaining ≈97% accuracy while reducing parameters and latency by more than 70% relative to larger models. These results highlight the suitability of modern lightweight CNNs for computationally efficient surface material classification. A supplementary haptic fusion experiment further demonstrates that top lightweight visual backbones extend naturally to compact multimodal perception systems.

title: Introducing Graph Attention Network to Improve Wang Zhengfei Semi-Empirical Model for Accurately Simulating Forest Fire Spread in Western Guizhou Province
authors: Yuying Gong, Jingyi Wang, Mingshi Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate simulation of forest fire spread is crucial for enhancing emergency response. Currently coupling the Wang Zhengfei model with Cellular Automata (CA) is regarded as an effective method for the simulation, but the rigid neighborhood rules inherent to CA often limit the ability to flexibly adapt to non-linear fire propagation. To overcome this defect, this paper utilized six major forest fires in western Guizhou province taking place on February 20, 2024, as a case study. We developed an improved model by training a Graph Attention Network (GAT) on historical Guizhou fire incidents to adaptively adjust the CA's neighborhood rules, utilizing the Wang Zhengfei spread rate as a key input feature. The fires were simulated and validated against the Sentinel-2 derived 20 meter resolution burned patches via calculating a spatial agreement (SA) measure. Additionally, the largest fire underwent dynamic assessment using the vegetation damage patterns (dNDVI) derived from high-frequency Gaofen-4 (GF-4) satellite observations. Results showed that SA measures from the GAT-improved model ranged from 0.713 to 0.990, much higher than those from the original Wang Zhengfei-CA model ranging from 0.095 to 0.773. And the dynamic simulation of the largest fire among the six was relatively consistent with the evolutionary patterns of vegetation damage characterized by GF-4 satellite observations derived NDVI changes. Overall, the GAT-improved model is a potentially effective framework for depicting the spatio-temporal evolution of forest fires in Guizhou province, providing a reliable tool for targeted forest fire prevention and suppression.

title: Temporal Evolution Prediction of TCM Syndromes in Anorexic Children Based on LSTM-CNN Hybrid Network
authors: Shuya Tang, Kun Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the difficulty in predicting the temporal evolution of traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) syndromes in anorexic children, a hybrid network combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is proposed. This study simplifies the background and related work, and focuses on the detailed design of the prediction model and experimental verification. First, the clinical data of anorexic children are preprocessed to construct a temporal sequence dataset of TCM syndromes. Then, the CNN layer is used to extract local correlation features of syndromic symptoms, and the LSTM layer is employed to capture the long-term temporal dependence of syndrome evolution. Finally, the effectiveness of the model is verified on the self-built dataset. Experimental results show that the proposed LSTM-CNN hybrid model outperforms single LSTM, CNN and other baseline models in terms of prediction accuracy, precision, recall and F 1 -score. It can accurately predict the temporal evolution trend of TCM syndromes in anorexic children, providing a reliable decision-making basis for clinical TCM diagnosis and treatment. The proposed method enriches the technical means of TCM syndrome prediction and has important clinical application value.

title: Intelligent Parameter Optimization for Degradable Cassava-Chitosan Liquid Mulch Film Driven by a Genetic Algorithm
authors: Zhi Li, Huixin Yang, Shangyun Yang, Xun Lan, Tiancheng Gao, Sitong Meng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Conventional preparation processes for degradable cassava-chitosan liquid mulch film rely on single-factor gradient experimental schemes, a method plagued by low parameter exploration efficiency, difficulties in balancing multiple performance metrics, and inadequate control precision-all of which fails to meet the requirements of large-scale intelligent production. As a pivotal technology in computerized intelligent optimization, genetic algorithms can tackle these bottlenecks by virtue of their global optimization capability and iterative evolutionary properties. Based on experimental data from cassava-chitosan liquid mulch film fabrication, this study establishes a full-process genetic algorithm optimization system. Covering experimental data preprocessing for constructing the algorithm input dataset, designing core algorithm modules (including decision variable quantification, fitness function modeling, and constraint condition formulation), and conducting full-linkage iterative optimization and result validation, the system ultimately replaces the traditional empirical control mode. The results demonstrate that after 200 generations of iteration, the genetic algorithm accurately outputs the optimal preparation parameter combination. This research confirms that genetic algorithms are the core driver for realizing the “data-algorithm dual-driven” mode of mulch film preparation, and the constructed optimization framework can provide a technical paradigm for the intelligent fabrication of analogous agricultural materials.

title: Bridging Classic and Modern AI for Analyzing Disagreement Between Lexical and Neural Code Retrieval
authors: Vishwa Bhatt, Jerry Cervantes-Fernandez, Zhixin Yu, Bryan Vera, Yunfei Hou, Jennifer Kim Jin
abstract: Aligning natural-language documentation with source code remains a core challenge in semantic code search, particularly when documentation varies in abstraction and lexical grounding. This work evaluates lexical, neural, and hybrid retrieval approaches under a global code search setting using the Java subset of the CodeSearchNet benchmark. A symbolic BM25 baseline is compared with a neural semantic encoder and two integration strategies: hybrid reranking and disagreement-aware routing. Neural retrieval consistently outperforms lexical retrieval, improving mean reciprocal rank from 0.271 to 0.318, while hybrid reranking further increases performance $(M R R=0.341)$. A disagreement-aware routing strategy achieves the best results (MRR $=0.345$), demonstrating that principled integration of symbolic and neural representations improves robustness. Analysis further shows that lexical–semantic rank disagreement systematically correlates with semantic abstraction, indicating that such disagreement reflects meaningful alignment differences rather than retrieval noise.

title: AI-Based Detection of Evasive Malware Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Mohammad Imtiyaz Gulbarga, Ruslan Isaev, Ruslan Amanov
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Modern malware increasingly employs sophisticated evasion techniques, including obfuscation, polymorphism, and anti-analysis mechanisms, to bypass traditional detection systems. Memory analysis captures runtime behavioral characteristics that persist regardless of code-level alterations, limiting practical deployment. To address this crucial gap, we propose a framework that combines memory forensics with ensemble machine learning. We extended the Advanced-VolMemLyzer tool from 75 to over 250 behavioral features, targeting evasive malware indicators. A balanced dataset of 58,596 memory samples covering three malware families Ransomware, Spyware, and Trojan Horse was assembled from real-world specimens. The two-layer stacked ensemble reached 99% detection accuracy at 0.23 seconds per sample, outperforming traditional signature-based approaches (78.4%) and remaining feasible for real-time deployment.

title: Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)

title: Day-Ahead PV Power Profile Forecasting Using Wavelet-BiLSTM Driven by Numerical Weather Prediction
authors: Yanguo Huang, Weifeng Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: Accurate day-ahead photovoltaic (PV) power profile forecasting is crucial for grid scheduling but remains challenging due to nonstationary weather and seasonal shifts. We propose a wavelet-enhanced bidirectional LSTM (WT-BiLSTM) that augments NWP inputs with multi-scale wavelet components to forecast a 96-point PV power curve. Experimental results under a rolling-origin evaluation framework demonstrate that the proposed model consistently outperforms the BiLSTM baseline across four seasons. Moreover, the proposed approach demonstrates improved robustness to seasonal distribution shifts, supporting its effectiveness for day-ahead PV power profile forecasting.

title: Optimizing Memory Usage in Streaming Pipelines Through Information Lifecycle Management (ILM)
authors: Alua Onayeva
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Streaming systems often struggle with memory overload when large amounts of data are processed in real time. This paper presents a lightweight Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) strategy designed to control hot-tier memory usage in Redis by dynamically archiving stale records to cold storage. The proposed method combines time-based expiration with an asynchronous sweeper thread that migrates inactive keys after a configurable threshold. The approach was implemented and tested within a Kafka-Spark-Redis pipeline over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0}$</tex>-minute workloads. The results show that Redis memory usage was reduced by 83 %, falling from 8.1 GB to just 1.4 GB, with only a 1 % decrease in Kafka throughput and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 \%}$</tex> increase in Spark latency. These findings demonstrate that effective memory control can be achieved without compromising real-time performance. The architecture remains fully containerized and uses only native TTL and file operations, making it easily deployable in resourceconstrained environments.

title: Faithful or Plausible? A Counterfactual Analysis of LLM-Generated Explanations for Machine Learning Models
authors: Shaun Colegado, Jennifer Jin, Yunfei Hou
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate natural language explanations of predictive machine learning models, yet their outputs often emphasize plausibility over faithfulness to the underlying model logic. This study applies counterfactual testing to evaluate the fidelity of LLM explanations in a structured setting: decision tree models predicting customer churn (i.e., when customers stop using a company’s product or service-a classic benchmark problem in predictive modeling research). We design two counterfactual tests across four prompting strategies that vary in whether dataset and/or model information are provided. Results from 1,200 generated explanations show that supplying model context substantially improves faithfulness whereas dataset context provides little benefit, and LLMs often broaden explanations beyond the model’s true decision path. Notably, even when given full model logic, LLMs remain predisposed toward generating plausible rather than strictly faithful explanations. These findings highlight both the promise and the limits of LLMs as interpreters of predictive models and introduce counterfactual testing as a potential framework for evaluating explanation faithfulness in data-heavy contexts.

title: Feature Extraction and Abnormal Pattern Recognition Technology for Fault Diagnosis of Electrical Automation Systems
authors: Wenhao Xu, Yaoyao Luo, Ziheng Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the widespread application of electrical automation systems in industrial production, the reliability and fault diagnosis capabilities of the system have become a focus of attention. To improve the precision of fault diagnosis in electrical automation systems, this paper designs a feature extraction and anomaly pattern recognition model that combines deep learning (DL) and Bayesian neural networks (BNN). Firstly, the relevant variables are evaluated by distance correlation coefficient, and the modeling variables are screened using minimum redundancy maximum correlation (mRMR) algorithm, followed by feature extraction using convolutional neural network (CNN). Secondly, this article uses Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) networks to explore the correlation characteristics of multiple parameters and the temporal characteristics of historical data, and establishes a system operation state prediction model. In response to the diversity of abnormal patterns, this paper combines LSTM and BNN for recognition, and uses LSTM to achieve efficient classification of known fault categories; Introducing BNN to quantify uncertainty in model predictions, effectively distinguishing between known faults and unknown anomalies. The analysis of experimental results shows that the model proposed in this paper can improve the precision of fault diagnosis, effectively support intelligent operation and maintenance in industrial production, and enhance the efficiency of fault handling.

title: Software-Hardware Co-Design of a Distributed Energy Access System Based on a Unified Sensing-Computing-Communication Chip
authors: Xiaolong Lyu, Qin Ma, Jie Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Confronting the challenges of poor compatibility, complex communication protocols, and insufficient operational reliability in existing distributed energy resource (DER) integration systems, this paper proposes a hardware-software codesign method based on an integrated sensing-computingcommunication chip. Centered around this chip, the method establishes a modular hardware platform for multi-source energy integration. At the software level, a lightweight embedded operating system is designed, which, alongside unified driver interfaces and a multi-core task scheduling strategy, enables synergistic hardware-software optimization. Furthermore, this paper introduces a protocol adaptation mechanism and a firmware self-recovery mechanism to address communication protocol switching and system fault restoration in complex environments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves improvements in energy efficiency utilization, task response time, and operational stability compared to conventional solutions.

title: Cross-Dataset Speech Emotion Recognition Based on Resampling Normalization and Attentional LSTM Networks
authors: Bo Zhu, Lu Cao, Zhanbo Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study proposes a cross-dataset speech emotion recognition framework using resampling normalization and attentional LSTM to address domain variability. The method standardizes utterance length and applies spectral normalization, then uses a bidirectional LSTM with selfattention to process 1582-dimensional acoustic features. Experiments on CREMA-D and IEMOCAP for four emotions achieve 66.5% average Unweighted Accuracy across Leave-One-Dataset-Out protocol, outperforming the best baseline BiLSTM by 3.1 percentage points. Ablation shows resampling and spectral normalization provide 7.4% and 6.1% gains respectively. Limitations include reliance on acted datasets and computational overhead. Future work will evaluate on spontaneous speech and explore efficient attention mechanisms.

title: Multi-Strategy Improved Crested Porcupine Optimizer and Its Application
authors: Songye Yang, Shengli Xie, Wei Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: This study develops a multi-strategy improved Crested Porcupine Optimizer (MCPO), to improve the search behavior of the original Crested Porcupine Optimizer (CPO). The proposed algorithm introduces three modifications. First, Sobol Sequence combined with opposition-based learning is adopted to construct a more representative initial population. Second, a nonlinear inertia-weight mechanism is embedded into the fourth defense stage so that the search step can change adaptively during evolution. Third, a t-distribution mutation operator is applied to selected individuals to reduce stagnation and strengthen the ability to escape poor local regions. To examine its engineering applicability, MCPO is used to solve the speed reducer design problem. Over 30 independent runs, MCPO obtains Best = 2994.473, Mean = 2994.474, and Std = 1.60×10−5, outperforming the original CPO and four related improved CPO variants. These results indicate that MCPO provides a stable and accurate optimizer for constrained mechanical design and offers a practical reference for related engineering optimization problems within the conference scope.

title: Improved White Shark Optimizer and Its Application in Path Planning
authors: Haolong Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: White Shark Optimizer (WSO) is an efficient metaheuristic algorithm, yet it suffers from issues like slow convergence, unbalanced exploration and exploitation, insufficient local search capability, and parameter sensitivity, often resulting in premature convergence. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper proposes an Improved White Shark Optimizer (IWSO). It adopts uniform initialization to enhance population diversity, introduces an adaptive step size to balance global and local search, and employs perturbation with a greedy selection mechanism. Validated on the path planning task, experimental results demonstrate that IWSO outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: An Adaptive Gmapping Algorithm Integrating IMU Observations and Lightweight Loopback
authors: Xinyu Ye, Zhenyu Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To address odometer drift, particle degeneracy, and cumulative error during long-term operation of the traditional GMapping algorithm, this paper proposes a triple-improved simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) method. First, an Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) is introduced to fuse inertial measurement unit (IMU) data with odometry data, providing a more robust motion prior for particle propagation. Second, an adaptive particle filter based on the Kullback–Leibler divergence (KLD) criterion is designed to dynamically adjust the particle number according to environmental complexity, thereby balancing estimation accuracy and computational cost. Third, a lightweight loop closure detection strategy based on Scan Context is employed to suppress long-term accumulated drift. Simulation experiments conducted on the Robot Operating System (ROS) platform show that the proposed method increases the average effective particle ratio from 0.53 to 0.72 and the minimum effective particle ratio from 0.24 to 0.38, while improving map IoU from 80.2% to 87.6%. In the ablation study, the complete model achieves the highest map IoU of 88.7% with an average Neff/M of 0.71, demonstrating the collaborative effectiveness of the three modules. These results indicate that the proposed method provides a lightweight yet effective enhancement for filter-based 2D LiDAR SLAM, and it is of practical significance for mobile robot mapping tasks requiring robustness, efficiency, and long-term consistency.

title: Spatiotemporal Deformation Prediction in Permafrost Regions Based on CNN-LSTM Multi-Factor Fusion and SBAS-InSAR
authors: Yongwei Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: To address the low accuracy of traditional methods in predicting spatiotemporal deformation of permafrost regions affected by multiple factors, this paper proposes a prediction method combining SBAS-InSAR technology, CNN-LSTM network and multi-factor fusion. First, SBAS-InSAR technology is used to process Sentinel-1 SAR images to obtain the historical spatiotemporal deformation sequence of the study area. Then, environmental and anthropogenic factors affecting permafrost deformation are selected to construct a multi-factor dataset, which is fused with InSAR deformation features through a feature normalization and weighting strategy. The CNN-LSTM hybrid model is designed to extract spatial features of deformation data via CNN and capture long-term temporal dependencies via LSTM, realizing end-to-end spatiotemporal deformation prediction. Taking the Wudaoliang permafrost region on the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau as the study area, comparative experiments are carried out with single CNN, LSTM, SVR and MLP models. The results show that the proposed method achieves the optimal performance in evaluation indicators such as MAE ($\mathbf{2. 1 3 ~ m m}$), RMSE ($\mathbf{2. 8 7 ~ m m}$) and SSIM (0.942), which is significantly superior to traditional models and single deep learning models. It can accurately capture the nonlinear spatiotemporal deformation law of permafrost regions and provide technical support for geological disaster early warning and permafrost stability evaluation.

title: Enterprise Financial Data Analysis and Decision Support System Based on Algorithm
authors: Jianlin Li, Wanli Liu, Cong Meng, Xingguang Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: Enterprise financial management faces many challenges in the era of big data. This article aims to build an algorithm-based enterprise financial data analysis and decision support system to improve the level of enterprise financial management. Based on the in-depth analysis of the relevant theories and current situation of financial data in enterprises, this article designs the system architecture, functional modules and data processing flow by using regression algorithm and clustering algorithm. In an application case of a large-scale enterprise, the average error rate of budget forecast is reduced to 4%, the accuracy of financial risk assessment is 92%, the data processing time is greatly shortened, and the single complex analysis is reduced from 120 minutes to 25 minutes. The performance of the system is significantly improved, and it meets the financial management needs of enterprises in terms of accuracy, efficiency and stability. This provides strong support for enterprises to rationally plan funds, prevent financial risks and optimize resource allocation, and promotes the modernization and transformation of financial management in enterprises.
