title: Design and Application of a Railway Infrastructure Fault Early Warning System Based on Deep Learning Object Detection Technology
authors: Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: To improve the accuracy of railway infrastructure fault warnings, this study investigates the design and application of a railway infrastructure fault warning system based on deep learning object detection technology. The system hardware design adheres to modular principles, encompassing seven functional subsystems, including a core processing unit and power management. Low-power chips and protective circuits are selected to ensure stable operation in complex environments. Multi-parameter monitoring is performed using track inspection instruments, strain sensors, high-definition cameras, and other equipment, tailored to the specific characteristics of railway infrastructure. Targeted deployment is planned based on scientific formulas, resulting in a monitoring network covering tracks, bridges, tunnels, and overhead lines. An improved FLA-GSFPN deep learning model is constructed. The FLA attention module filters multi-scale features, and the anchor frame selection attention guidance module optimizes detection. The loss function is adjusted to improve railway infrastructure detection accuracy. Based on the fault probability output by the detection model, a status value is calculated. Dynamic warning thresholds are set, and health values are categorized into four warning levels. Corresponding operation and maintenance strategies are developed to achieve real-time, graded warnings for railway infrastructure faults. System testing results demonstrate that the designed system accurately captures infrastructure status changes at different locations along the railway line and issues timely warnings. Across 11 data sets, the average MAE (Massive Error Efficiency) was 0.6284, the average MSE (Meanwhile Average Sequential Error) was 0.2501, and the average RMSE (Residual Value Error) was 0.5001. The model prediction residuals were mostly within the range of [-2.0, 3.5], with very few outliers. The test results demonstrate that the designed system improves the accuracy of railway infrastructure fault warning by leveraging deep learning object detection technology.

title: Research on High-Precision INS Gravity Information Measurement and Compensation Technology
authors: Wenxia Ma, Xin Zhao, Yuanming Wang, Wangyang Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This paper focuses on the research of gravity information measurement and compensation technology in high-precision inertial navigation systems (INS). The correlation between the gravity field and the geomagnetic field is analyzed, providing a theoretical basis for underwater integrated navigation. The characteristics, error sources, and real-time compensation methods of underwater gravity measurement are discussed, with emphasis on key technologies for motion acceleration compensation, Eötvös correction, horizontal acceleration correction, depth correction, and zero drift correction in GNSS-denied environments, based on INS, Doppler Velocity Log (DVL), and depth sensors. An overall design scheme for an underwater gravity measurement system combining azimuth strapdown resolution and dual-axis stabilized platform control is proposed, aiming to improve the real-time performance and accuracy of gravity anomaly measurement, assist inertial navigation, and enhance comprehensive underwater navigation capabilities.

title: Charge-Storage Interlayer Strategy for High-Performance Droplet Energy Harvesting via Liquid–Solid Triboelectrification
authors: Xiaobo Meng, Zhe Huang, Kailin Li, Zong Li, Bin Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Harvesting electrical energy from water droplets via triboelectrification is a promising strategy, but conventional droplet electricity generators (DEGs) usually exhibit limited output. Here, we propose an interlayer-modified DEG (IML-DEG) by introducing a polyethylene terephthalate (PET) film between the polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) triboelectric layer and the bottom electrode. Under optimized conditions, the open-circuit voltage of the IML-DEG is approximately 2.9 times that of the conventional DEG. The enhanced output is interpreted as being consistent with interfacial-polarization-assisted field regulation and trap-assisted suppression of charge dissipation, based on Maxwell–Wagner–Sillars interfacial polarization and the charge-trapping characteristics of PET. The effects of droplet impact height, impact frequency, PTFE thickness, PET thickness, electrode stiffness, and tilt angle are systematically investigated to clarify the roles of key geometric and dynamic parameters in device performance. Humidity-dependent, cycling-stability, load-dependent, capacitor-charging, and liquid-chemistry measurements further show that the PET interlayer design improves practical output behavior, maintains higher output than the conventional DEG over the investigated humidity range and under repeated droplet impacts, and is sensitive to liquid ionic content.

title: Intelligent Diagnosis Method for Open Circuit Faults in On-Board Chargers Based on Adaptive Attention Convolutional Residual Network
authors: Jingzheng Li, Xidong Zheng, Zhiwen Zhao, Tao Jin, Mohamed A. Mohamed
abstract: The rapid deployment of electric vehicles (EV) is accelerating the electrification of transportation. During peak demand periods, EV can feed power back to the grid through onboard chargers (OBC), enabling vehicle-to-grid (V2G) interactions and enhancing grid flexibility. Consequently, accurate diagnosis of open-circuit (OC) faults in OBC is essential to ensure the reliable operation of EV and the safe functioning of the power grid. To address the complex and nonstationary characteristics of OC fault signals in OBC, this paper proposes an intelligent diagnostic model based on an adaptive attention convolutional residual network (AACRN). The proposed model integrates depthwise separable convolution (DSC) for efficient local feature extraction and residual network (ResNet) structures for deep hierarchical representation learning. Furthermore, a convolutional block attention module (CBAM) is incorporated to adaptively emphasize fault-relevant features while suppressing redundant information, thereby improving feature discrimination and enabling accurate fault localization. In addition, the model's interpretability is enhanced through a unified framework that integrates t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) visualization, attention heatmaps, and shapley additive explanations (SHAP) analysis. This framework highlights fault-relevant regions and quantifies key feature contributions, thereby enhancing the transparency and reliability of fault diagnosis. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a diagnostic accuracy of 96.54% under noise-free conditions and maintains an accuracy of 93.21% under real electromagnetic interference (EMI) in physical tests, confirming its strong anti-interference robustness in practical engineering scenarios.

title: TAAformer: Transposed Angular Attention for Channel Estimation With Fluid Antennas
authors: Weibiao Zhao, Yongyi Miao, Mengya Liu, Xin Liang, Die Hu, Yu Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Fluid antenna systems (FASs) can enhance communication system performance by flexibly switching antenna positions to ports with favorable channel conditions. To fully realize this benefit, acquiring accurate channel state information is essential. Existing Transformer-based FAS channel estimation methods mainly rely on conventional self-attention (SA), whose quadratic complexity poses challenges to network efficiency, especially when the number of switchable ports is large. To address this issue, we propose a novel transposed angular attention (TAA) that reduces complexity to linear levels while retaining global modeling capabilities. Unlike conventional SA directly modeling relationships between ports, TAA first disentangles the combined contributions of multi-path components (MPCs) at each port through an angular-domain transformation, and then, via a transposed operation, shifts the attention focus to model the relationships between angular features underlying all disentangled MPCs. We further extend TAA into transposed angular self- and cross-attention mechanisms. Building on these, we develop an efficient TAAformer network for FAS channel estimation, a UNet-like encoder-decoder architecture with multi-scale feature capabilities. Simulation results show that the proposed TAAformer can achieve higher channel estimation accuracy compared to existing methods.

title: SwitchFS: Asynchronous Metadata Updates for Distributed Filesystems with In-Network Coordination

title: MFS: An Efficient Model Family Serving System for LLMs

title: RSA-Enhanced Adaptive Noise Cancellation with Multi-Verse Optimizer for Robust ECG Signal Denoising and Classification
authors: Aravinth J, Sidhiksha T S
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) signals are widely used in medical diagnosis to study electrical activity of the heart. However, ECG data are sensitive to a variety of noise sources during acquisition, including baseline wander, white Gaussian noise (WGN), artifacts of muscle activity, and power line interference (PLI). These noises distorts the original signal and decrease the effectiveness of later analysis. Noise cancellation methods will improve the quality of the signal by removing unnecessary parts and increasing signal to noise ratio. This facilitates more precise analysis and enhances the performance of algorithms for anomaly detection and classification. This study provides a hybrid approach of combining Multi-Verse Optimizer (MVO)-based feature selection with an RSA-enhanced Adaptive Noise Cancellation (RSA-ANC) method. Multi-Objective Optimization (MVO) is applied to select features retaining key ECG characteristics while simultaneously suppressing noise levels, whereas the RSA-ANC system is applied for efficient denoising functions. Experimental results shows that the proposed system outperforms existing state-of-the-art ECG noise reduction methods as well as standalone MVO. After the denoising step, the system efficiently classifies arrhythmias (ARR) and Normal Sinus Rhythm (NSR) signals, and it shows potential for reliable clinical decision-making assistance.

title: Design of a Sensor-Assisted Five-Bar Finger Rehabilitation Device
authors: J. Sofia Bobby, Madhu Sri K, Lilly P
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Finger rehabilitation plays an important role in restoring hand function following neurological and musculoskeletal impairments, yet conventional therapy methods often lack repeatability and objective performance feedback. This paper presents the design and experimental evaluation of a compact finger rehabilitation device that supports adaptive active-assist therapy. The system is based on a five-bar linkage mechanism fabricated using thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) to achieve physiologically aligned finger flexion—extension while maintaining comfort and mechanical simplicity. The device integrates multiple sensing modalities, including inertial measurement, surface electromyography, force sensing, and galvanic skin response monitoring, to enable closed-loop control and safe operation. Experimental validation was conducted on healthy volunteers using a structured rehabilitation protocol. The results demonstrate stable motion repeatability, controlled finger movement within a physiological range, and reliable sensor-driven adjustment of assistance based on user effort and comfort. Compared to manual finger exercises, the proposed device provides improved motion consistency and quantitative control while maintaining comparable comfort levels. The use of widely available components and standard fabrication methods enables easy replication and integration by other researchers, making the system a practical platform for future rehabilitation studies and clinical evaluation.

title: Design and Implementation of a Low-Noise Signal Acquisition System for Small Animal Physiological Monitoring
authors: L Suganthi, D Gayathri, S Arun Karthick
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Accurate acquisition of physiological signals such as ECG, blood pressure, and temperature is critical in small animal biomedical studies. Analog monitor circuits are normally prone to problems such as signal degradation, motion artifacts, and environmental noise, which affect the quality of the acquired data. The objective of this research work is to develop a wearable signal acquisition circuit that ensures accurate and trustworthy physiological acquisition of small animals. The proposed circuit includes an instrumentation amplifier designed using INA333 with high common-mode rejection and low noise. The amplifier output is followed by high-pass and low-pass filters for efficient signal processing. The processed signals are then converted to digital form using an analog-to-digital converter (ADC) for analysis. The circuit is simulated and developed using Multisim software with a focus on gain maximization, power optimization, and noise reduction. This design will help improve the clarity of signals with integrity and address significant problems in biomedical signal acquisition and therefore will be suitable for continuous monitoring.

title: An Efficient Low Power Design of Multiplierless Discrete Cosine Transform
authors: Kavitha S, Swetha M, Swetha M, Thanishka K
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: An effective low-power multiplierless Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) architecture by using merely addition and subtraction instead of power-hungry multiplication processes is presented in this paper. The suggested architecture drastically lowers computing complexity. The DCT design uses optimized arithmetic units to achieve low power consumption and area efficiency. Because it uses fewer adders and subtractors, the multiplierless 8-point DCT structure is appropriate for errortolerant image processing applications. Power consumption, delay, and power-delay product (PDP) are used to assess the architecture. Using an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> block-based method, the suggested DCT is applied for image compression, and simulation results demonstrate significant reduction in power and area while maintaining acceptable performance. The suggested design is ideal for low-power multimedia and medical image processing applications because of these properties.

title: Hybrid Posture Monitoring System Using Computer Vision and Sensor-Based Analysis
authors: R. Prem Kumar, Abinaya Ramesh, Dharshini Padmanaban, Divya Dharshini Veeraraghavan
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Prolonged improper sitting posture is one of the leading ergonomic issues associated with musculoskeletal disorders and improper spinal alignment. Early identification and constant correction are critical towards ensuring proper spinal alignment and alleviating fatigue. For this purpose, this paper proposes a hybrid posture analysis system that utilizes both force-based sensing and computer vision. The proposed work will make use of force-sensitive resistor sensors connected to an ESP32 microcontroller for identifying pressure variations in seating positions, in addition to a vision module utilizing a webcam and OpenCV with MediaPipe Pose for identifying improper upper limb sitting posture positions such as slouching and neck bending. The solutions derived from these approaches will be processed using a Python programming language setup. The future scope of this work will incorporate an intelligent posture predictor with cloud analysis support.

title: Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting Platform for Low-Power Wearable Vital Signal Monitoring
authors: Kavyashri K, Priscilla A, Santhiya Sri H, R. Parameshwari
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Wearable health monitoring systems enable continuous observation of physiological parameters but are often constrained by battery dependency, frequent recharging, and limited operational lifetime. To overcome these challenges, this paper presents a piezoelectric energy harvesting system for a selfpowered vital signal monitoring application. The proposed system utilizes piezoelectric materials to harvest electrical energy from human motion, enabling sustainable power generation during daily activities. The harvested energy is conditioned through rectification, voltage boosting, and energy storage circuits to deliver a regulated power supply suitable for low-power biomedical sensors. The system demonstrates reliable operation under motion-based energy input, reducing dependence on conventional batteries. Experimental and simulation results validate the feasibility of the proposed piezoelectric energy harvesting approach for sustainable and long-term wearable healthcare applications.

title: Vision-Based End-to-End Learning for UAV Traversal of Irregular Gaps Via Differentiable Simulation_supp1-3685922.mp4
authors: Danping Zou
abstract: Navigation through narrow and irregular gaps is an essential skill in autonomous drones for applications such as inspection, search-and-rescue, and disaster response. However, traditional planning and control methods rely on explicit gap extraction and measurement, while recent end-to-end approaches often assume regularly shaped gaps, leading to poor generalization and limited practicality. In this work, we present a fully vision-based, end-to-end framework that maps depth images directly to control commands, enabling drones to traverse complex gaps within unseen environments. Operating in the Special Euclidean group $SE(3)$, where position and orientation are tightly coupled, the framework leverages differentiable simulation, a Stop-Gradient operator, and a Bimodal Initialization Distribution to achieve stable traversal through consecutive gaps. Two auxiliary prediction modules—a gap-crossing success classifier and a traversability predictor—further enhance continuous navigation and safety. Extensive simulation and real-world experiments demonstrate the approach's effectiveness, generalization capability, and practical robustness.

title: Deep Learning-Based Process Control of Microrobot Swarms Guided by Phase Diagrams_supp3-3686187.mp4
authors: Jiangfan Yu
abstract: Microrobot swarms with locomotion dexterity and shape reconfigurability show immense potential in biomedical applications. Automatic control strategies are critical for the navigation of swarms in unstructured environments. Existing control methods mainly focus on initial and final states of swarms, and swarm process control designed to avoid undesired states throughout the control process is yet investigated. In this work, we develop a deep learning-based process control strategy for swarms guided by phase diagrams. Two deep neural networks are respectively built to model the swarm shape and kinematics. Control approaches based on precise swarm models are designed to automatically tune multiple swarm parameters. A phase diagram-based controller is proposed to guide the swarm reconfiguration while eliminating the coupling effects between swarm parameters. The swarm is enabled to accurately track predefined trajectories while performing continuous reconfiguration with desired states during the entire process. By integrating the process control of swarm pattern and locomotion, the swarm can dynamically adapt to constrained unstructured spaces and achieve robust collision avoidance.

title: High-performance near-infrared photodetector based on Ag NPs/MoS QDs&sol;SWCNTs&sol;AlO&sol;InP heterostructure for optical communication_supp2-3686231.mp4
authors: Jun Chen
abstract: Near-infrared (NIR) photodetectors have emerged as critical components in optoelectronic systems. However, conventional bulk semiconductor photodetectors are limited by lattice mismatch and inappropriate band alignment. This work proposes a near-infrared (NIR) photodetector based on Ag NPs/MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> QDs/SWCNTs/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/InP structure. The light absorption is enhanced by the localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) effect from Ag NPs and the quantum confinement effect from MoS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> QDs. An Al₂O₃ thin film serves as a passivation layer to suppress dark current. The device achieves a responsivity as high as 414.5 mA/W at 1 V and a detectivity of 4.07 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sup> Jones at 0.05 V. The device also responds rapidly, exhibiting a rise time of 2.4 μs and a fall time of 36 μs. Furthermore, successful optical transmission experiments have been conducted using this photodetector.

title: Lottery Preferences, Skewness, and Stock Returns in The Emerging Market
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: This study investigates whether skewness is a priced risk factor in the Malaysian equity market, an emerging economy characterized by volatile returns, limited market depth, and strong retail investor participation. While traditional asset-pricing models such as the Carhart four-factor (FF4) framework explain variation in returns through market, size, value, and momentum effects, they do not explicitly account for higher-order moments such as skewness, which capture asymmetry in return distributions and exposure to tail risk. Using daily stock returns from January 2004 to June 2025, this study constructs monthly measures of return skewness for all listed firms. Stocks are sorted each month into portfolios based on lagged skewness, and the performance of a long–short strategy (PLS = low-skew minus high-skew) is evaluated. The results reveal a strong and statistically significant negative relation between skewness and subsequent returns: low-skew portfolios outperform high-skew portfolios by over 1% per month. Importantly, the PLS portfolio maintains a significantly positive alpha after controlling for the Malaysian four-factor model, demonstrating that skewness is not subsumed by conventional risks. These findings provide novel evidence that skewness is a priced anomaly in Malaysia, highlighting the limitations of classical factor models and offering practical implications for investors and policymakers in emerging markets.

title: Enhancing the Fama–French Five-Factor Model with Tail Risk and Geopolitical Uncertainty
authors: Tran Trong Huynh, Bui Thanh Khoa
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The Fama–French five-factor (FF5) model provides a widely used framework for explaining cross-sectional stock returns but omits important risk dimensions, notably downside tail risk and geopolitical uncertainty. Tail risk captures the probability of extreme losses beyond volatility- based measures, while the Global Political Risk (GPR) index reflects uncertainty from wars, trade disputes, and political instability. These risks are particularly relevant in emerging markets, where return distributions are skewed and markets are highly sensitive to shocks. This study extends the FF5 model by incorporating firm-level Value-at-Risk (VaR) and changes in GPR to forecast monthly excess returns of all Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange (HOSE) stocks from January 2010 to December 2024. Three approaches are compared: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and a Random Walk (RW) benchmark. Out-of-sample tests show that both OLS and SVR reduce forecast errors by approximately 35% relative to RW, with VaR providing significant explanatory power, while GPR remains insignificant at the monthly horizon. The results highlight the value of incorporating tail-risk measures into asset-pricing models, the competitiveness of simple interpretable methods, and practical implications for investment strategies in emerging markets.

title: Trustworthy AI in Cloud Security: Explainability, Robustness, and Regulatory Compliance Frameworks
authors: Wong Nelson Zee Yang, Lau Jim Yung Cheng, Missie Phreoza Dayzency, Jahnavi Anilkumar Kachhia, Vishal Verma, Hussana Johar R B
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Cloud computing has established itself as the backbone of the contemporary digital infrastructure but poses complex cybersecurity problems because of its distributed and multi-tenant characteristics as well as dynamism. Intrusion detection, anomaly detection, as well as automated threat mitigation have been greatly enhanced by Artificial Intelligence (AI). Nonetheless, black-box AI systems have problems with transparency, strength, and regulation. In this paper, the author suggests a single Trustworthy AI model that combines Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), adversarial robustness methods, and governance with compliance in a cloud-native security architecture. The models include hierarchy in data processing, threat identification on the basis of AI, explainability on the basis of SHAP, adversarial defense techniques, and modules of compliance monitoring. The experimental analysis shows that model resilience is improved against FGSM, PGD and data poisoning attacks significantly and that it can be interpreted. The offered architecture will ensure the gap between transparency, robustness, and regulatory compliance and will provide a holistic approach to cloud cybersecurity.

title: Real-Time State Management and Data Visualization in Cloud-Based Financial Applications
authors: Naveen Kumar Vayyasi
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The latest digital world has created large amounts of information for detailed knowledge to be stored by financial institutions and businesses as a result of their customers’ online activities. The present study provides an overview of how data collected and analyzed through cloud technologies will help develop more effective methods of analyzing and personalizing data so that businesses can meet their customers’ needs. This is done by identifying customer preferences, patterns, and other behaviours by extracting useful information from this information using various cloud-based systems, which further enhances the business’s ability to promote loyalty among its customers. The exploration of how organizations utilize these resources for analysis will also improve customer engagement. Personalization has become one of the primary means by which organizations engage with customers on a more personal level. By utilizing all types of data from various sources through the use of cloud-based data management, organizations can create and provide their customers with customized products and services. These organizations utilize various ways to personalize their products and services through the use of machine learning and collaborative filtering algorithms. The proposed financial prediction framework shows that the Transformer produced the most accurate prediction at 96% followed closely by the LSTM with 94% and the GRU with 92%. The GRU has the lowest latency at 40 ms, the LSTM model with 47 ms and the Transformer with 62 ms.

title: A Secure Cloud–AI Collaborative Framework for Financially Intelligent and Autonomous Manufacturing Enterprises
authors: Bhanuprakash Madupati, Rama Krishna Cherukuri, Priyameet Kaur Keer, Nithesh Naik, Niranjan N Prabhu, Shahneela Jamal Kidwai
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The paper is a proposal of secure cloud-AI cooperative model to empower financially intelligent and autonomous manufacturing businesses. The architecture combines Internet of Things data of industries, financial data of enterprises, and machine learning models on the cloud system. It involves single security platform that can be able to support scalable and reliable decision-making. Deterministic ML strategies are used to forecast financial risk, cost, and profitability. It can be further used by an independent decision engine to maximize manufacturing process. The experimental analysis by classification and regression indicators illustrates the presence of a better forecasting level, lower operational price, and effective mitigation of financial risks. The security analysis provides better data protection at minimal overheads of the system, which renders the framework to apply to the real-life intelligent manufacturing system. The current research proposes a Cloud–AI secure cooperating model as the basis for integrating security-aware cost optimization with a unified framework of adaptive resource orchestration and real-time governance using AI. The new solution is different from other current solutions (like FinOps) which treat costs or automation separately, but instead combine security, operation intelligence, and cost efficiency in one cloud-enabled manufacturing system.

title: Solar-Biomass Waste Energy-Based Standalone System with IoT-ML Algorithms: A Comprehensive Study in a South Indian Remote Village
authors: Renugadevi P, Sudha Anbalagan, Srimathi R
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The low electrification of a remote village is a result of frequent setbacks through intermittent supply of the grid, high technical losses, and poor control of the system. This holistic research becomes acquainted with a solar-biomass standalone system (OFF-Grid) of a south Indian village (100 households) with the aid of IoT (Internet of Things) empowered real-time monitoring and machine learning (ML) algorithms for predictive load forecasting, optimal energy controls, and fault detection. Compared to the traditional systems that experience difficulties in the supply interruption and a higher loss of energy, this system increases the solar irradiation and agricultural biomass. A techno-economic analysis conducted by HOMER Pro and MATLAB reveals a competitive levelized cost of energy of Rs. 10.88/kWh, a system uptime of 99.7%, a renewable content of 97%, and a payback period of 4.4 years, which is superior in performance amongst past performances of solar-only and biomass-only models. Consequences outline 1 year of avoiding 82 tons of CO2 emissions and more than 16% improvement in efficiency as a result of ML-IoT integration. The architecture, adaptive control policies, and real-time analytics offer resilient operation, and the challenges associated with supply irregularity, fault management, and economic viability are reduced. This paper will deal with the shortcomings of the earlier standalone renewable system, including a lack of real-time control, a lack of synergy between resources, and a lack of means to detect failures. The work provides a dependable solar biomass system to electrify the rural areas, bridging between high-tech energy technology and IoT-ML.

title: DeepRiskCast-Predicting Financial Fraud Using Behavioral Sequence Learning
authors: Moghal Yaseen Pasha, Ravi Bajulal Bhandari, Md Ankushavali, Rahul Pradhan, Anandakumar Haldorai, Rayappan Lotus
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Financial institutions are faced with mounting difficulties in detecting sophisticated fraud as their opponents exploit growing irregularities in behavior and multi-channel transactional flows. Traditional rule-based and static machine learning systems have difficulties in capturing temporal pattern, high dimensional signals and evolving fraud tactics and results in high false positives and missed detections. This work addresses this problem by introducing DeepRiskCast, a behavioral sequence learning framework grounded on the fusion of micro temporal encoding, relational context and adaptive attention for effective fraud prediction. The goal is to develop a model that will be able to detect subtle and temporally diffused fraud signals and keep the operational noise low. The methodology incorporates the combination of temporal convolution modules, Transformer-based long-range attention and ephemeral graph augmentation, which is enhanced using contrastive pretraining and cost-sensitive optimization. Experiments done on BankSim-Extended dataset shows that DeepRiskCast is a better predictor than state-of-the-art baselines with a AUC of 0.937, AUPR of 0.68, F1-Score of 0.678 and a significant decrease in false positives to only 23 per 10k transactions. These results validate the efficacy of modeling user activity as enriched behavioral sequences and suggest that DeepRiskCast is a plausible, scalable, explainable and operationally feasible solution in the modern day fraud detection ecosystems.

title: The Low-Latency UCIe-Compliant Spiral Layouts of TSV Arrays Against the Clustered Faults
authors: Bowen Tan, Xiaole Cui, Zhaohong Lin
abstract: The chiplet based integrated system contains lots of inter-die interconnections. The through silicon vias (TSVs) and micro bumps are the common components of the vertical electrical interconnections in the $2.5 \mathrm{D} / 3 \mathrm{D}$ multi-die integration circuits. They are usually laid out as arrays to regulate the routing rules. Faults may occur in the TSVs, and the redundant resources based self-repair mechanisms are introduced to improve the yield of the entire integrated chip. In the Universal Chiplet Interconnect Express (UCIe) standard, each advanced package module contains 32 data lanes and 2 redundant lanes, and the shift chain based intra-module self-repair mechanism is defined. However, the faults are usually clustered in certain region of the TSV array. The intra-module self-repair mechanism of UCIe standard is not able to cope with the cases that more than 2 clustered faults occur simultaneously in one module. An effective idea to improve the repair rate is to distribute the faults into different modules by regrouping the layout of TSV array. This work reports some new spiral layouts of rectangular and hexagonal TSV arrays. The self-repair schemes based on the proposed layouts are UCIe-compliant. Simulation results present that the proposed self-repair schemes are able to repair the clustered faults in TSV array with the low repair latencies.

title: An AI-Driven CNN–LSTM Framework for Multi-Stage Driver Drowsiness Detection with Real-Time Mobile Alerts
authors: Rajiv Kumar Yadav, Prabhat Kumar Chaudhary, Gargi Jha, Rupesh Dahi Shrestha, Ramesh Kumar Pudasaini
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Driver fatigue is a prominent source of traffic accidents and requires the need for accurate real-time detection algorithms. In this paper, the authors propose the design of "DrowsyDriveNet," a hybrid deep learning architecture that is capable of classifying the driver alertness level into four stages: "Alert," "Slight," "Moderate," and "Severe." In this proposed architecture, authors combine a spatial network that uses a frozen MobileNetV2 feature extractor with a temporal network that uses CNN and Bidirectional LSTM networks. In this architecture, the critical facial parameters such as Eye Aspect Ratio (EAR), Mouth Aspect Ratio (MAR), and circularity of eyes are computed over a window of 60 frames and are capable of determining the instantaneous and long-term behavior of the driver. A rule-based late fusion of the spatial and temporal networks has been carried out, and the proposed architecture is capable of achieving a high level of classification accuracy of 99.89%.

title: Physics-Aware Diffusion Models for Cyclone Damage Assessment Using Remote Sensing Imagery
authors: Pathanjali Chowdaiah, Sharmila Chidravalli, Vimuktha E. Salis, Vineetha Edwina Jathanna
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Assessment for cyclone damage with remote sensing images is difficult due to the scarcity of post-event images and corresponding ground-truth data. In this paper, we present a physics-informed diffusion framework for cyclone damage assessment based on pre-cyclone Sentinel-2 images and physics-informed environmental priors. In our method, a lightweight diffusion network is coupled with a physics-informed loss term based on wind physics and geographical properties to spatially predict cyclone damage likelihood with zero post-event ground truth. Unlike photorealistic image restoration tasks, our method predicts interpretable cyclone damage segments overlayed onto pre-event images to spatially highlight likely affected areas. Furthermore, we use SSIM measure–based cyclone damage severity mapping to provide continuous and spatially detailed characterization of structural damage. Validation experiments on real Sentinel-2 images show that our method can spatially coherently and physically consistently localize cyclone damage with high internal consistency between our overlays and SSIM measure–based heatmaps. These outcomes indicate that physics-informed diffusion models have shown promise for very early cyclone damage assessment and risk-informed decision support on cyclone-related disaster management tasks based on remote sensing images.

title: Transforming Project Scheduling and Monitoring with MS Planner: Lessons from Real-World Case
authors: Praneel Kumar Mukherjee, Neha Surendranath, Priyam Ganguly
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: This article explains the steps required to manage a project and the events that occur as it evolves from a preliminary concept to operational implementation [1]. The project estimation method is analyzed as a problem-solving technique in which, a complex problem is decomposed into smaller problems. The features of Microsoft Planner (MS Planner) as an effective project management tool are explored through a case study approach, and the roles of PERT and CPM in project management are discussed. This paper also explains how to manage client expectations through a written agreement and the significance of risk and configuration management in project management.
