title: Smart Monitoring System for Rapid Forest Fire and Smoke Identification
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Muddisetti Pallavi, Molaka Prashamsha, Nallathimmagari Sharanya Reddy, Nandi Thrinath, Sm Rafi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Wildfires currently pose an existential threat to global ecosystems, demanding scalable and immediate detection capabilities which legacy satellite and manual observation systems fail to provide. This research presents a high-speed, dual-stage computer vision framework designed to overcome the latency and accuracy limitations of existing fire monitoring solutions. By integrating a YOLOv9-based object detector with a specialized ResNet50 classification filter, our system achieves a critical balance between real-time responsiveness and false-positive suppression. We evaluate this architecture against a diverse dataset of flame and smoke imagery, demonstrating how deep neural networks can be optimized for deployment in resource-constrained surveillance networks. The study further identifies key bottlenecks in adversarial weather robustness and proposes a roadmap for next-generation edge-AI fire perception.

title: Design of Energy-Efficient Ternary Logic Circuits Utilizing GNR Field-Effect Transistors
authors: Bogyam Saikumar, P. Surendra Babu, Shaik Javid Basha, Y. Mallikarjuna Rao, Erfan Abbasian, Shams Ul Haq
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Legacy silicon binary circuits are limited by two primary bottlenecks such as high static power leakage and the excessive physical footprint of interconnecting wires. To mitigate these limitations, the multiple-valued logic (MVL) and the adoption of emerging nano-electronic devices represent two promising paradigms for modern circuit design. A novel approach to designing high-performance digital ternary gates with graphene nanoribbon field effect transistors (GNRFETs) is introduced in this study. The GNRFET has unique properties such as modifying the width of graphene nanoribbons (GNRs) to obtain desired threshold voltage values. The novel circuit designs such as Buffer/NOT, NAND/AND and NOR/OR circuits presented in this work. The proposed design logic functions and its complement depends on the control signal (S). Additionally, in these gates, the higher impedance state is presented that helps to save the power while gates are not in use. The presented gates are implemented in HSPICE software to acquire the transient response and performances including delay, power and power delay product (PDP), respectively. Furthermore, the presented gates are compared to the conventional CNTFET based gates in terms of performance. It is noted that the GNRFET gates show an average performance improvement up to 75.40% than the conventional CNTFET gates.

title: Metamaterial-based Antenna Design for Compact IoT Devices
authors: D.Lingaraja, B. Yemanth Kumar, V.Sasidhar Reddy
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The ruthless proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) is the ecosystem, including smart cities and bracelet health watches, puts the hardware, and most notably the antennas, which facilitate wireless connectivity, under high expectations. Traditional antenna designs are gradually becoming insufficient, as they have a devastating trade-off between physical size, bandwidth of operation and radiation efficiency. This trade- off is particularly acute in the context of ultra- compact and low-cost form factors which modern IoT devices are commonly constrained by, frequently compelling engineers to tolerate the compromised performance which can become an obstacle to the successful transmission of data, inter-operability between devices utilizing different communication standards such as LoRaWAN, Zigbee, and 5G-NB-IoT. In order to overcome these intrinsic drawbacks, a ground breaking engineering solution has emerged in the form of metamaterial- based antennas (MAs), which exploits the artificially created materials properties, which would not have existed naturally, to radically reformulate the idea of an antenna design concept. These antennas are being used to realize unparalleled miniaturization through the use of a negative refractive index, composite right/left-handed (CRLH) transmission lines, and electromagnetic band-gap (EBG) antennas, which generate effectively at intra and subwavelength scales as well as increasing gain and directivity. In this paper, the recent advances in the MA design procedures are fully reviewed such as multi-band and frequency-agile reconfigurable design that best fits the crowded and heterogeneous RF spectrum of IoT. It also includes an in-depth account of performance metrics that are achieved and openly states the outstanding challenges to achieve a widespread commercialization, including the complexity of fabrics production, the bandwidth of some of these designs, and how they can be integrated with other electronic devices.

title: IoT Sensor Network and Ethereum-based Blockchain for Enhanced Pest Identification and Water Management in Vineyards
authors: Kiran Bharadwaj Vedula, Rajesh Arunachalam
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The emergent high-speed growth of precision agriculture requires solid frameworks that will improve sustainability, efficiency, and transparency in managing vineyards. The proposed research suggests a system that uses IoT sensor networks, machine learning models, and Ethereum-based blockchain to solve two important problems: pest identification and the optimization of water resources. The IoT layer was used to implement soil moisture, climate, and imaging sensors to gather real-time data about the vineyard. At the edge, preprocessing and deep learning algorithms were used with a blockchain-enabled Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to provide correct pest detection. At the same time, the timing of irrigation was automated by IoT-enabled soil moisture monitoring, which greatly decreased the amount of wasted water. This ensured integrity of data and trust in the farmers as the Ethereum blockchain layer offered immutable storage, smart contracts to make decisions, and secure events logging. The results of the experiments revealed that the proposed system had a 95.8% pest detection accuracy, which was higher than the traditional and baseline machine learning methods. The efficiency of water management increased too by reducing water consumption by 55.2 % and doubling the crop productivity by 26.8 %t. Moreover, the blockchain application has scored high security of 0.93, confirming that it is a reliable solution even with moderate latency overhead. In this study, the authors emphasize the opportunities of converging the IoT with blockchain and transforming viticulture through sustainable practices, data safety, and efficiency.

title: The Role of AES and DES in Securing Data Transmission
authors: Kusuma G S, P Nithya Sree, J R Mahesh, N Yuva Shree, Ankitha A
publish: 2025 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Information Technology, Electronics and Intelligent Communication Systems (ICITEICS)
abstract: A comprehensive comparative analysis among various symmetric encryption algorithms, mainly the Advanced Encryption Standard and Data Encryption Standard. The study meticulously dissects the operational mechanics of AES algorithm, shedding light on their key expansion, substitution, permutation, and mixing stages. By juxtaposing the performance, security, and computational overhead of these encryption standards across diverse platforms and scenarios, aiming to discern the relative strengths and potential areas for enhancement among the AES, DES, ASH and similar encryption algorithms. AES and DES serve as key milestones in the evolution of symmetric encryption, each with unique operational mechanisms and levels of resilience to cryptanalysis.

title: Machine Learning based Predictive Analytics for Student Dropout and Academic Success
authors: Kanmani K, Hari Kumar Palani, Loganayagi S, C. Sivasankar, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, G. Vasanthi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Student attrition in higher education is acknowledged as a major concern that influences both institutional sustainability and the long-term academic and professional paths of learners. In many institutions, rising dropout rates place pressure on academic planning, financial stability, and student support systems, which makes the early identification of vulnerable students increasingly important. In this context, a comprehensive machine learning framework is applied in the present study to predict student dropout and academic success using the well-established dataset from the UCI Machine Learning Repository.A wide range of classification algorithms is systematically examined, including Decision Trees, Random Forest, Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression, and several ensemble approaches. Each model is evaluated to understand its predictive capability, generalization potential, and suitability for real-world academic monitoring systems. The ensemble model that demonstrated the highest effectiveness achieved an accuracy of 89.7 percent, a precision of 87.3 percent, a recall of 91.2 percent, and an F1-score of 89.1 percent, indicating a strong balance between identifying true dropouts and minimizing misclassification. The feature correlation analysis further indicates that curricular units credited in the first semester at 0.68, admission grade at 0.54, and tuition fee payment status at 0.47 show the most influential predictive associations. These factors reflect academic preparedness, financial consistency, and early academic engagement, all of which contribute meaningfully to student persistence. The overall findings provide practical and data-driven insights that can support the development of early intervention systems. By applying these predictive indicators, institutions can design proactive measures, strengthen academic advising, and offer targeted assistance that helps reduce dropout rates and fosters sustained student success.

title: Robust Ensemble Decision Tree Model for Precise Fault Analysis in Transmission Lines Under Dynamic Conditions
authors: Sunihta Dharnsai, Sushil Kumar Chaturvedi, Nookala Venu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: In power system network, transmission line plays a vital role in providing the link between generating station and load centre. The power from generating station is transferred to the load centre through the transmission line spread over long distances. Depending on the geographical location and related climatic conditions transmission line undergoes several environmental abnormalities like high-speed wind, rain and snowfall. Thus, the probability of fault occurrence in transmission line is higher than the other components of power system network. The reliable operation of any power system network is dependent on the associated protection scheme. The protection schemes should provide necessary information regarding the state of the transmission line (healthy or faulty), so as to help the power system engineers for taking corrective action for ensuring the stable and secured operation of the power system. In addition to the fault detection, a reliable protection scheme should also provide the information regarding the type and location of fault, which further helps in the early restoration of supply post fault. The major challenges associated with distance relay involve lesser relay reach, slow response and higher chances of maloperation during load variation and power swings. The intrusion of harmonics in the current-voltage profile during nonlinear loading further complicates the protection task. In order to address above mentioned issue, the present work is focussed on the development of reliable protection scheme for transmission line protection with immunity against stressed conditions involving variation in loading conditions (linear and nonlinear loading). In this regard, bagging tree algorithm is exploited for the development of the proposed protection schemes for performing the detection and classification of faults in transmission line.

title: Flood Dynamics in the Ganga River Basin: A Multi-Temporal SAR-Based Change Detection Approach
authors: G Uma Prasana, M Ramesh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) is crucial for monitoring natural disasters and supporting effective disaster management, offering distinct advantages over multispectral optical imagery due to its long-wavelength interaction with the Earth's surface. This study utilizes Sentinel-1A C-band SAR data to identify flood-affected regions near the confluence of the Ganga and Ghaghara Rivers in Chhapra city, Saran district, Bihar. Two Ground Range Detected (GRD) SAR images are pre-processed using the Sentinel Application Platform (SNAP) to extract relevant data. Multi-temporal change detection is performed by stacking images captured before and during the flood event. To enhance flood visualization, a resampled, atmospheric corrected Sentinel-2A multispectral image is used to generate various False Colour Composites (FCC). The resulting flood extent from Sentinel-1A, Sentinel-2A, and meteorological observations are compared. SAR imagery clearly reveals inundated areas along the riverbanks and at the river confluence, whereas optical imagery, while showing water presence struggles to distinguish between floodwaters and the main river channel. Overall, this study contributes to a deeper understanding of flood dynamics, supports flood risk assessment, and aids in developing effective mitigation strategies for vulnerable regions.

title: Machine Learning Algorithms for Predictive Maintenance in Electronic Systems
authors: Anuja Singh, Ashwani Maurya, Pradeep Kumar Verma, Lalit Kumar, Pankaj Vishwakarma, Sarika Shrivastava
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: Predictive maintenance (PdM) has emerged as a transformative approach to maintenance of electronic systems by enabling the early detection of operational faults before they escalate into costly failures. Leveraging machine learning (ML) techniques, PdM models analyze sensor data capturing device operational states to identify subtle failure patterns. This study applies three prominent supervised ML models—Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and Neural Networks—to a dataset of sensor signals collected from electronic devices under normal and faulty conditions. Using comprehensive evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, with a sturdy 5-fold cross-validation framework, the performance of these models is rigorously compared. The experimental results demonstrate that ML-based PdM substantially improves fault detection reliability compared to traditional approaches and provides practical advantages that enable smarter maintenance scheduling, reducing downtime and operational costs significantly.

title: Privacy-Preserving Medical Chatbot: A Local Deployment Framework using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning and Quantized Inference
authors: Pranav Pratap Singh Bahri, Pawan Joshi, Rashmi Bhel
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into the healthcare sector is currently caught in a difficult balancing act: achieving high-performance conversational AI while meeting the rigorous privacy standards of HIPAA and GDPR. While cloud-based LLM APIs offer significant power, they necessitate the transfer of sensitive patient data to external servers, which introduces substantial security risks. This paper introduces a robust framework for building medical chatbots that operate entirely on local, consumer-grade hardware to eliminate these vulnerabilities. By utilizing the open-source Gemma 2B model, we applied Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA) for memory-efficient fine-tuning on medical datasets and deployed the system via the GGUF format with 4-bit (Q4_K_M) quantization. Our architecture ensures complete data sovereignty, meaning patient queries never leave the host device. To ensure the model remains professional and helpful, we implemented strict behavioral controls—including a 1.2 repetition penalty and specialized stopping markers—that result in a consistent, 5-point structured output. The final result is a compact 1.5 GB model that achieves a 2.4x size reduction without compromising its medical accuracy. By detailing our solutions for toolchain compilation and runtime stability, we demonstrate that specialized, private medical AI is a feasible reality for edge devices today.

title: A Modular Simulation Framework for Adaptive Routing in Fog-Cloud Architecture
authors: Neeraj Gupta
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The Internet of Things can be modeled as a three-layer user-fog-cloud architecture. The main requirements in such systems are low latency on fog subsystems and balanced load distribution on cloud subsystems. This article presents a modular simulation framework that evaluates various fog routing strategies and cloud backends that are modelled as Erlang C, Erlang A, and G/G/c formulations to capture various assumptions under dynamic traffic variability. Nine performance metrics, including fog and cloud waiting time, offloading percentage, abandonment rate, and delta comparisons, were tracked to evaluate the performance of the system. Contrary to theoretical beliefs, it was found that priority-aware and energy-aware routing strategies lead to higher abandonment of jobs due to aggressive offloading. Cloud wait times were almost the same across the strategy, pointing to the backend saturation. The work highlights the importance of simulation-based validation. The results suggest developing intelligent routing strategies and AI-based load balancing algorithms to balance latency, energy efficiency, and service continuity in next-generation distributed computing.

title: Detecting Common Injection-Based Vulnerabilities in Web-Applications
authors: Mohd Danish, Abhinav Sushil Varshney, Vivek Jangra, Sunil Kumar Chowdhary, Nidhi Sindhwani Anand, Ramnaresh Yadav
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The research paper showcases a general method of detecting injection based vulnerabilities in the web applications. These type of vulnerabilities allows the attackers to manipulate various parameters leading to theft of data, identity fraud as well as system compromise. The key types of injections include SQL Injection, Cross-Site Scripting XSS and Command Injection. The currently available tools often fail to detect all three of these or are too costly. The technique mentioned herein the paper detects all these injection flaws by comparing both the application’s response as well as the behaviour before and after sending a crafted payload. By conducting experiments on multiple vulnerable web apps it was seen that our method outperforms both open-source tools as well as rivals that of commercial ones and thus achieving broad detection coverage with very few false positives.

title: A Comprehensive Framework for Deepfake Detection: Fusing Visual and Audio Modalities with Advanced Deep Learning Models
authors: Surag Sunil, Raseek C, Jayakrushna Sahoo
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The emergence of deepfake technology presents considerable challenges to multimedia authentication. This study introduces an innovative detection framework that analyses both visual and audio data. The methodology combines Convolutional Neural Networks in conjunction with Generative Adversarial Networks. Performance remains robust against sophisticated manipulation techniques. Strategic frame extraction reduces computational demands without sacrificing accuracy. Validation in the Deepfake Detection Challenge Dataset achieved 93.9% accuracy. The dual-modality approach addresses falsified content that undermines digital trust.

title: A Self-Biased Hybrid Voltage Reference with Ultra-Low Power, Low Noise and High Temperature Stability Using DTMOS
authors: Sweety, Sanjana Verma, Komal Duggal, Kanchan Sharma, Vandana Niranjan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This paper presents a self-biased hybrid (CMOS+BJT) voltage reference with ultra-low power, low noise and high temperature stability using dynamic threshold MOSFET(DTMOS). Stacked diode-connected MOS transistors and a DTMOS architecture are used in the design to minimize power consumption, and a bipolar junction transistor is used to reduce the voltage variation. A self-biased architecture is used to reduce the start-up time without the need for external biasing sources. To reduce temperature-dependent changes in the reference voltage, a temperature compensation network is integrated. The circuit is simulated in 180-nm CMOS technology, requires a 1.14V supply and generates a reference voltage of 741.44mV. The suggested design delivers a temperature coefficient of 6.8 ppm/°C from −50°C to 85°C, a line sensitivity of 0.19%/V, power consumption of 8.8nW and low output noise of 17.92 µV/√Hz at 1 Hz.

title: Pull-Out Bearing Performance of Transmission Tower Pile Foundation Based on SVM Algorithm and Meter-Wave Radar
authors: Yanzhong Ju, Zheng Wei, Dehong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: The conventional techniques find it challenging to quantitatively determine the bearing capacity of the transmission tower piles foundations and the way that they interact with soil around them. According to the support vector machines (SVMs), this paper applies meter-wave radar to nondestructively detect the soil around the piles. Through processing and analysis of the radar echo signals, vital characteristic parameters, the distribution of dielectric constant of the soil, its stratification attributes and localities of possible defects are obtained. The nonlinear problem is reduced to a high-dimensional linear problem with the help of kernel function mapping, and complex mapping relationship between the radar properties of the soil around the piles and the pullout bearing capacity is established. The predictive performance of this method is excellent, and the correlation between predicted and measured values is high: the dielectric constant provides an additional 38.5 percent of prediction; the dielectric constant increases bearing capacity by 45.6 percent when it grows by 12.3 to 24.2. The findings show that the meter-wave radar features and SVMs model is able to provide the pile-soil interaction mechanism, which offers a nondestructive testing technology alternative to monitoring health and safety evaluation of transmission tower foundations.

title: Design and Analysis of Cylindrical GAAFET using HfO2 High-k Dielectricfor Low Leakage with Reduced Sub-Threshold Swing
authors: Ayushi Rajput, Ravi Rastogi, Anurag Govind Rao, Arjesh Jha, Devendra Kumar Tripathi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation-based analysis of a Cylindrical Gate-All-Around Field-Effect Transistor (GAAFET) utilizing Hafnium Dioxide (HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) as a high-k gate dielectric, modelled using Schrödinger–Poisson quantum confinement and mobility models in Silvaco ATLAS TCAD. The proposed device features a gate length of 20 nm and leverages the superior electrostatic control of the gate-all-around architecture compared to planar and FinFET structures. Prior studies on nanowire and nanosheet GAAFETs with high-k dielectrics have demonstrated improved short-channel control and leakage reduction; however, cylindrical geometries remain relatively underexplored. This gap motivates the present work, which combines cylindrical symmetry with HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> dielectric to achieve enhanced leakage suppression and scalability. Simulation results confirm significant improvements in sub-threshold swing (SS), drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL), drive current (Ion), and off-state leakage current (Ioff), yielding a high Ion/Ioff ratio and excellent switching efficiency. The novelty lies in demonstrating that cylindrical HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> GAAFETs at the 20 nm node offer promising leakage control and energy savings for future CMOS technologies beyond 5 nm.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Compressive Strength in High-Performance Concrete
authors: Rupesh Kumar Tipu, Owais Ahmad Shah
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: This study explores the application of machine learning models to predict the 28 days compressive strength of high-performance concrete (HPC). A dataset comprising 610 samples with 16 input features and 1 output label was utilized, collected through an extensive literature review. The input features represent crucial components of concrete mixtures, including water content, cement content, slag activity index, and more. The dataset underwent scaling between 0 and 1 to ensure uniformity. Five popular machine learning models including Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Regression (RFR), Gradient Boosting Regression (GBR), XGBoost, and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) were employed with 10-fold cross-validation. Performance metrics, including R-squared (R2), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), were used to assess the models. The results indicated that the Gradient Boosting Regressor and XGBoost outperformed other models, demonstrating high accuracy and reliability. These findings provide valuable insights for optimizing concrete mix designs and contribute to the advancement of data-driven approaches in concrete technology, fostering efficiency and sustainability in construction practices.

title: FFT and AI-Integrated Signal Analysis for Arrhythmia Detection from ECG Data
authors: K. M. Manikandan, D. Pradhaban, L. Haldurai, L Mirudhuba Jayne, S Vinusha
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Advancing Sustainable Solutions through Technologies (ICASST)
abstract: The temporal and frequency characteristics of the electrical activity of the heart need to be captured by employing cutting-edge signal processing to properly detect arrhythmias in electrocardiograms (ECGs). The proposed framework leverages the Convolutional-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Conv-BiLSTM) and Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) hybrid model to achieve frequency-conscientious temporal learning. The adaptive frequency attention mechanism of FFT makes it easier to break down electrocardiogram (ECG) signals into discriminative spectral coefficients. The next step is the combination of these coefficients with time-domain embeddings. The large MIT-BIH Arrhythmia dataset is used for validation purposes to ensure accuracy. During the experiments, the developed FFT-Conv-BiLSTM model performs better compared to current designs based on CNN, LSTM having an accuracy (99.6%). An integrated spectral-temporal framework could be a better strategy for handling morphological distortions, noise, and inter-patient variability.

title: Design of Notching and Mechanical Stress Relieving Grooves for High-Speed PM Machine in EVs
authors: Wentao Zhang, Zhongze Wu, Wei Hua
abstract: In this paper, the rotor surface notching and mechanical stress relieving grooves are analysed and designed for the high-speed permanent magnet (PM) machine used in EVs. The rotor surface notching could efficiently suppress the torque ripple and noise of the PM machine, whilst the high-speed mechanical stress issue is tackled by the stress relieving grooves. Firstly, the spatial-temporal characteristic of the airgap flux density is analysed for the high-speed PM machine. The contribution of the rotor surface spatial positions on both electromagnetic torque and key order radial force density is revealed. This paper utilizes the q-axis notching for suppressing the torque ripple and the noise related key order radial force density, which features significant suppression effect across wide working conditions of the machine. However, the q-axis notching will raise the local mechanical stress concentrations in the rotor lamination. Therefore, the stress relieving grooves are designed and optimized based on the analogy between stress distribution and the fluid flow. The maximum mechanical stress is reduced from 387 MPa to 369 MPa, ensuring the safe operation of the PM rotor. Finally, the high-speed flat wire winding PM machine is built and tested, including the line voltage, torque-current characteristic, output power characteristic, noise spectrogram and the 22500-rpm rotor mechanical overspeed tests. The measured electromagnetic performance of the prototype agrees well with the simulation prediction, and the mechanical overspeed test validates the high-speed operation capability.

title: Consensus-Driven Event-Based Graph Information Bottleneck for Integrated Communication and Control
authors: Ziqiong Wang, Xiaoxue Yu, Rongpeng Li, Zhifeng Zhao
abstract: The integration of communication and control presents a critical challenge for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms operating in the formation control scenario. This co-design problem is typically formulated within the Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework. However, partial observability limitations will impair collaboration effectiveness, and a potential solution is to establish consensus through well-calibrated latent variables obtained from neighboring agents. Nevertheless, the rigid transmission of less informative content can still result in redundant information exchanges. Therefore, we propose a Consensus-Driven Event-Based Graph Information Bottleneck (CDE-GIB) method, which integrates the communication graph and information flow through a GIB regularizer to extract more concise message representations while avoiding the high computational complexity of inner-loop operations. Meanwhile, we investigate the potential impact of consensus errors induced by noise arising from multi-agent interactions. To further minimize the communication volume required for establishing consensus during interactions, we also develop a variable-threshold event-triggering mechanism. By simultaneously considering historical data and current observations, this mechanism capably evaluates the importance of information to determine whether an event should be triggered. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of efficiency, adaptability, and noise robustness.

title: Anonymization, Not Elimination: Utility-Preserved Speech Anonymization
authors: Yunchong Xiao, Yuxiang Zhao, Ziyang Ma, Shuai Wang, Kai Yu, Jiachun Liao, Xie Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: The growing reliance on large-scale speech data has made privacy protection a critical concern. However, existing anonymization approaches often degrade data utility, for example by disrupting acoustic continuity or reducing vocal diversity, which compromises the value of speech data for downstream tasks such as Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Text-to- Speech (TTS), and Speech Emotion Recognition (SER). Current evaluation practices are also limited, as they mainly rely on direct testing of anonymized speech with pretrained models, providing only a partial view of utility. To address these issues, we propose a novel two-stage framework that protects both linguistic content and acoustic identity while maintaining usability. For content privacy, we employ a generative speech editing model to seamlessly replace personally identifiable information (PII), and for voice privacy, we introduce F3-VA, a flow-matchingbased anonymization framework with a three-stage design that produces diverse and distinct anonymized speakers. To enable a more comprehensive assessment, we evaluate privacy using both acoustic- and content-based speaker verification metrics, and assess utility by training ASR, TTS, and SER models from scratch. Experimental results show that our framework achieves stronger privacy protection with minimal utility degradation compared to baselines from the VoicePrivacy Challenge, while the proposed evaluation protocol provides a more realistic reflection of the utility of anonymized speech under privacy protection.

title: Winding-Factor-Based Analysis of Pole-Slot Combinations for a Reduction-Gear Magsplit
authors: Tae-Yun Ha, Yong-Jae Kim, Eui-Jong Park
abstract: Magnetic power-split devices (Magsplit) based on magnetic gearing can integrate power-splitting and motor functions. A Magsplit comprises a PM rotor, a pole-piece rotor, and a stator; accordingly, the air-gap magnetic field contains both the PM rotor pole pair component and pole-piece induced asynchronous space harmonics. Therefore, this study investigates a reduction-gear-based Magsplit, quantifies the coupling of stator windings to each component using a winding factor-based approach, and screens pole-slot and coil-pitch combinations. As a result, an optimal winding configuration is derived that enhances coupling to the target asynchronous harmonic while suppressing coupling to the PM component. The selected configurations are then assessed under locked-rotor (stall-torque) conditions using FEA, and the final prototype is validated through no-load back-EMF and stall-torque measurements.

title: Real-Time Path Planning for Multiple Autonomous Underwater Vehicles in Dense Obstacle Fields: The DA-IFDS Approach
authors: Xizhe Chen, Shihong Yin, Yujing Li, Zhengrong Xiang
abstract: Multiple autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) face dense static and dynamic obstacle threats during path planning in complex and dynamic subsea environments. Therefore, each AUV must achieve safe, reliable, and real-time path planning under limited local observations. This article proposes a 3-D path planning method for multiple AUVs, named the dynamically adaptive-interfered fluid dynamical system (DA-IFDS) algorithm. It employs a two-layer real-time decision-making framework: the high-level decision-making module outputs step-by-step parameters based on proximal policy optimization (PPO), while the low-level module determines the velocity direction of each step according to these parameters. Such a hierarchical design improves the learning efficiency and adaptability of multiple AUVs. This algorithm models obstacles as disturbances in the flow field and incorporates these parameters into the action space. It employs bidirectional gated recurrent units to process variable-length observation sequences, effectively addressing the observation inconsistency faced by traditional methods in dynamic obstacle scenarios. In addition, it designs a diversified reward function. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed DA-IFDS algorithm outperforms soft actor-critic-IFDS, deep deterministic policy gradient-IFDS, and conventional PPO algorithms in terms of success rate, obstacle-avoidance performance, and planning efficiency, validating its effectiveness and superiority in real-time path planning for multiple AUVs in dense static and dynamic obstacle scenarios.

title: AV-Unified: A Unified Framework for Audio-Visual Scene Understanding
authors: Guangyao Li, Xin Wang, Wenwu Zhu
abstract: When humans perceive the world, they naturally integrate multiple audio-visual tasks within dynamic, real-world scenes. However, current works such as event localization, parsing, segmentation and question answering are mostly explored individually, making it challenging to comprehensively understand complex audio-visual scenes and explore inter-task relationships. Hence, we propose AV-Unified, a unified framework that enables joint learning across a wide range of audio-visual scene understanding tasks. AV-Unified standardizes the diverse input-output formats of each task and incorporates a multi-scale spatiotemporal perception network to effectively capture audio-visual associations. Specifically, we unify the inputs and outputs of all supported tasks by converting them into sequences of discrete tokens, establishing a shared representation that allows a single architecture to be trained jointly across heterogeneous varied datasets. Considering the varying temporal granularity of audio-visual events, a multi-scale temporal perception module is designed to capture key cues. Meanwhile, to overcome the lack of auditory supervision in the visual domain, we design a cross-modal guidance-based spatial perception module that models spatial audio-visual associations. Furthermore, task-specific text prompts are employed to enhance the model's adaptability and task-awareness. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets (e.g., AVE, LLP, MUSIC-AVQA, VGG-SS and AVS) demonstrate the effectiveness of AV-Unified across temporal, spatial, and spatiotemporal tasks.

title: Layer 0 and Layer 1 Integrated Smart Contract Kill Switch for Cross-Chain Security in Decentralized Systems
authors: Soonhong Kwon, Wooyoung Son, Jong-Hyouk Lee
abstract: The revised EU Data Act requires the incorporation of smart contract kill switch mechanisms. Nevertheless, most existing designs are either confined to single chain architectures or dependent on centralized control, thereby conflicting with the decentralization principles of blockchain systems and limiting their applicability in cross-chain environments. This limitation becomes particularly critical in IoT-driven industrial contexts, where security incidents demand synchronized execution control across multiple chains. Under such circumstances, single chain oriented kill switch mechanisms inherently suffer from structural constraints. To address these challenges, we propose an integrated Layer 0 and Layer 1 smart contract kill switch framework. The proposed framework enables distributed decision-making across heterogeneous blockchain systems by supporting diverse consensus algorithms, including PoW, PoS, PBFT, Paxos, and Raft. Cross-chain propagation of kill switch activation signals is securely facilitated through interoperability solutions such as CCMP and LayerZero. Importantly, activation decisions made on one chain are not mandatorily enforced on others; instead, each chain independently determines whether to execute the kill switch via its own consensus process, thereby preserving both autonomy and decentralization. For performance evaluation, repeated experiments were conducted across 50 use cases derived from exhaustive combinations of two interoperability mechanisms and five consensus algorithms. The analysis considered average activation time, gas consumption, and message delay, alongside Time to Activation (TTA) and tail-latency metrics to assess real-time responsiveness. Network experiments using Linux tc-netem demonstrate that even under severely degraded network conditions, the cross-chain p99 TTA is bounded at 12.2596 s, establishing a predictable worst-case upper limit. These results highlight the structural robustness of the proposed framework by guaranteeing a bounded reaction time even in environments characterized by increased latency and consensus overhead, rather than merely improving average-case performance.

title: Bilevel Optimized Implicit Neural Representation for Scan-Specific Accelerated MRI Reconstruction_supp1-3686724.mp4
authors: Yun Jiang
abstract: Deep learning (DL) methods can reconstruct highly accelerated magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans, but they rely on application-specific large training datasets and often generalize poorly to out-of-distribution data. Self-supervised deep learning algorithms perform scan-specific reconstructions, but still require complicated hyperparameter tuning based on the acquisition and often offer limited acceleration. This work develops a bilevel-optimized implicit neural representation (INR) approach for scan-specific MRI reconstruction. The method explicitly formulates the undersampled MRI reconstruction problem as a bilevel optimization problem and automatically optimizes the multidimensional hyperparameters of the reconstruction method for a given acquisition protocol, enabling a tailored reconstruction without training data. The proposed algorithm uses Gaussian process regression to optimize INR hyperparameters, accommodating various acquisitions. The INR includes a trainable positional encoder for high-dimensional feature embedding and a small multilayer perceptron for decoding. The bilevel optimization is computationally efficient, requiring only a few minutes per typical 2D Cartesian scan. On the scanner hardware, the subsequent scan-specific reconstruction—using offline-optimized hyperparameters—is completed within seconds, while achieving comparable or improved image quality compared to previous model-based and self-supervised learning methods.

title: Intelligent Bionic Polarization Orientation Method Using Improved U-Net Full-sky Imaging Cloud Recognition model for Cloudy Conditions
authors: Qi Wu, Jiangtao Yang
abstract: Accurate and reliable navigation is critical for the Internet of Things (IoT). Polarization-based navigation has attracted extensive attention for autonomous heading estimation in small and unmanned platforms, particularly when satellite navigation is unavailable. However, conventional polarization navigation systems suffer from degraded heading accuracy under cloudy conditions, mainly due to unstable cloud identification and insufficient feature extraction in full-sky observations. To address these issues, this paper proposes a cloud layer identification method for full-sky images based on an improved U-Net. By integrating attention and residual learning, a novel Channel Weighting-Feature Fusion (CWFF) module is embedded into the U-Net structure to strengthen deep feature representation and cloud feature memorization. Furthermore, a feature-matrix-based cloud detection scheme is developed to skip explicit imaging and improve recognition efficiency. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves over 95% overall accuracy in cloud detection, with an average absolute error below 5%. Outdoor tests on an atmospheric polarization measurement system demonstrate that the root-mean-square error (RMSE) is reduced to 0.181°, effectively improving polarization-based heading accuracy under cloudy environments.

title: AI-Powered Medical Chatbot with Neural Network Symptom Analysis and Automated Appointment Scheduling
authors: Abdullayev Meirkhan, Praveen Kumar
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: The proposed research work describes an AIassisted medical chatbot system incorporating a topic-related large language model, convolutional neural networks to analyze medical images, and an automatic appointment system. The proposed system utilizes the Meditron medical language model to obtain patient symptoms and determine the level of urgency through intuitive chat interactions. A convolutional neural network, trained with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5, 0 0 0}$</tex> marked medical images of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2}$</tex> typical symptoms, enables visual symptoms to be determined with an average accuracy of 94.3 % on the hold-out set. The appointment system incorporates a rule-based priority algorithm, which takes into account symptoms, specialties, and availability of consulting physicians. The pilot test conducted with 250 users confirmed that average waiting time in consultations was reduced by 47 %, with response times of less than 5 seconds even in cases of simultaneous interaction of numerous users. The study confirms the effectiveness of combining topic-related language models and deep learning algorithms in implementing preliminary patient screening in telemedicine systems.

title: Dynamic Load Balancing Algorithms in Cloud Computing: A Review
authors: Raiymbek Zhanuzak
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Load balancing is a critical component of cloud computing that ensures efficient distribution of workloads across available resources. Dynamic load balancing algorithms adapt to real-time changes in workload and resource availability, making them more effective than static approaches in cloud environments. This review presents a systematic analysis of dynamic load balancing algorithms with a focus on key performance metrics such as makespan, response time, throughput, scalability, and fault tolerance. The algorithms are classified and compared based on their scheduling strategies and optimisation objectives, highlighting their impact on makespan reduction and system scalability. The paper also identifies existing challenges and research gaps, providing insights for the development of more efficient and scalable load balancing solutions in cloud computing.

title: Smart Deep Learning for Managing Renewable-Powered Microgrids: A Digital Twin Implementation
authors: Qinghui Li, Xingjun Wang, Ruixiang Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study introduces an innovative demand response program for microgrids dominated by tidal and solar energy. A multi-objective optimization model is established to concurrently minimize total operating costs and mitigate grid failure risks associated with component reliability. To efficiently solve this complex, nonlinear problem, an enhanced Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO) is proposed. The algorithm incorporates a novel Dynamic Three-Phase Correction (DPC) mechanism, which enhances population diversity and global search ability, leading to faster convergence and a reduced likelihood of local optima entrapment. Furthermore, to address the inherent uncertainty of renewable generation, a forecasting model based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) is developed to provide accurate hourly predictions for both tidal and solar power output. The adversarial training process enables the model to better capture the complex patterns of real generation data. The entire integrated framework is validated through comprehensive simulations on a modified IEEE 33-bus test system configured as an islanded microgrid. Results demonstrate that the proposed methodology significantly outperforms conventional benchmark algorithms, achieving superior economic efficiency and enhanced operational reliability for the scheduling of smart microgrids with high renewable penetration.

title: Autonomous Mobile Robot Navigation in Multi-Room Environments Using Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3)
authors: Ayaulym Parmash, Ingkar Baidaly, Yernar Akhmetbek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Autonomous navigation in complex indoor environments remains a significant challenge due to the sparsity of rewards and the high dimensionality of sensor data. In order to enable a Pioneer 3-DX mobile robot to navigate a six-room environment, this work provides a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) technique using the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm. The robot learns to accomplish randomly generated goals while avoiding obstacles like tables and chairs by combining visual input via a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with scalar state information via a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Our findings show how well the suggested reward function and architecture work to achieve reliable navigation in multi-room arrangements, as measured by Success weighted by Path Length (SPL) and Success Rate.

title: An Enhanced Artificial Bee Colony with Dual Search Strategies and Local Search
authors: Yang Cao, Yunpeng Jia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: To solve continuous optimization problems, this paper proposes a Dual-search based Localized Improvement Enhanced Artificial Bee Colony Algorithm (EDABC). This algorithm balances global search and local development using two complementary search strategies. A local search is performed following the scout bee phase to improve convergence. In addition, we implement a self-adaptation rule which changes the probability of choosing each strategy according to their current fitness during evolution. The proposed algorithm is evaluated on 22 benchmark functions and compared with several ABC and DE variants. Experimental results show that EDABC achieves the highest optimization accuracy on most test functions, obtains the best Friedman rank among all compared algorithms, and demonstrates improvements in convergence speed and solution quality. These results confirm its effectiveness for complex engineering optimization problems.

title: An Improved Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm with Sine-Based Parameter Adaptation
authors: Ya’nan Sun, Yang Cao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Blockchain Technology and Foundation Models (BTFM)
abstract: Linear Population Size Reduction Success-History based Adaptive Differential Evolution with Semi-Parameter Adaptation based Covariance Matrix Adaptation (LSHADE-SPACMA) is an evolutionary algorithm proposed in 2017 that combines Linear Population Size Reduction SHADE (LSHADE) and Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES), showing strong performance when tackling high-dimensional complex optimization problems. However, since it employs CMA-ES during computation, some issues arise, such as insufficient local exploitation capability and relatively high computational complexity. To address these problems, this paper proposes an improved algorithm called LSHADE-XSCMA (LSHADE with eXponential Simplified CMA-ES). In the proposed method, a CMA variant, Exponential Simplified Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (xSCMA-ES), is used to replace CMA-ES. Moreover, a sine-perturbation-based parameter self-adaptation mechanism is introduced to enhance the balance between exploration and exploitation. Finally, a new mutation strategy is designed to make full use of the elite individuals generated by xSCMA-ES, effectively improving interactions among components within the hybrid framework. The proposed algorithm is validated on the CEC2017 benchmark suite, and compared with several state-of-the-art algorithms. Experimental results on the CEC2017 benchmark suite show that LSHADE-XSCMA outperforms several state-of-the-art algorithms on most benchmark functions, achieving competitive Friedman rankings and improved accuracy and robustness. These results indicate that the proposed method provides an efficient and effective framework for solving high-dimensional complex optimization problems.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)

title: Research on the Algorithm Model of the Periodic Detection and Slip Early Warning System of the Belt Outlet Based on Visual Recognition
authors: Jiamin Lei, Yunting Liang, Ziyang Zeng, Huanchao Liu, Wenyu Liao, Long Zhao, Turui Ning, Yingxiang Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: This study presents a vision-based conveyor belt slot cycle detection and slip warning system designed to address production stability issues caused by belt slippage in tobacco production. The system relies on visual inspection devices at both the feeding and discharging ends of the conveyor belt, utilizing an optimized BKD-YOLOv8 algorithm to monitor slot positions in real-time. Through time-series analysis, it calculates slot movement velocity and cycle patterns, comparing them against preset parameters to achieve intelligent monitoring of conveyor belt operation. The system's core innovation lies in its dual verification mechanism: real-time displacement changes at the slot position detect velocity anomalies, while coordinated detection points validate cycle deviations. This significantly enhances detection accuracy. Rigorously designed warning triggers prevent false alarms, and the system maintains comprehensive operational data records for anomaly tracing and analysis, providing reliable maintenance documentation ${ }^{[1]}$. This technology effectively prevents production accidents caused by belt slippage, ensuring continuous and stable tobacco production with notable practical value. Its modular design enables adaptable implementation across conveyor belt systems of varying specifications ${ }^{[2]}$.

title: Anthropomorphized Interfaces and User Perception in Financial AI Assistants
authors: Moldir Akhmetova, Alina Gutoreva
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Financial decision-making is increasingly supported by AI-based assistants, yet user trust remains a critical challenge in high-stakes such as finance. Anthropomorphization, defined as the inclusion of human-like characteristics in artificial intelligence, has been proposed as a means of fostering trust in AI-human interaction; however, its effects in financial decisionmaking contexts remain insufficiently understood. This study investigates how varying levels of anthropomorphism in AI financial advisors influence user trust and intention to use, with a focus on the perceptual mechanisms underlying these effects. An experiment was conducted in which participants interacted with one of four AI interface conditions representing increasing degrees of anthropomorphism. The results indicate that anthropomorphic design does not directly increase trust, but instead exerts an indirect effect through user perceptions, particularly perceived empathy and interaction quality. Trust was found to strongly predict intention to use AI-based financial advice. These findings suggest that effective AI financial advisor design should prioritize meaningful, empathetic interaction over surface-level humanization to support appropriate and responsible trust.

title: Projection Neural Network for Solving Interval Quadratic Programming Problems
authors: Feng Zhou, Shenglan Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Symposium on Big Data and Applied Statistics (ISBDAS)
abstract: This paper presents a second-order projection neural network to solve interval quadratic programming problems. The existence and uniqueness of the solution trajectories of the neural network are analyzed. Relying on Lyapunov analysis, the generated trajectories converge asymptotically to the optimal solution of the quadratic programming problem. The exponential convergence of the trajectories is also guaranteed under strong convexity. Numerical experiment is conducted to demonstrate the effectiveness and applicability of the proposed neural network approach.

title: LLM-based Zero-shot Triple Extraction for Automated Ontology Generation from Software Engineering Standards
authors: Songhui Yue
abstract: Ontologies have supported knowledge representation and white-box reasoning for decades; thus, the automated ontology generation (AOG) plays a crucial role in scaling their use. Software engineering standards (SES) consist of long, unstructured text (with high noise) and paragraphs with domain-specific terms. In this setting, relation triple extraction (RTE), together with term extraction, constitutes the first stage toward AOG. This work proposes an open-source large language model (LLM)-assisted approach to RTE for SES. Instead of solely relying on prompt-engineering-based methods, this study promotes the use of LLMs as an aid in constructing ontologies and explores an effective AOG workflow that includes document segmentation, candidate term mining, LLM-based relation inference, term normalization, and cross-section alignment. Expert-annotated reference sets at three granularities are constructed and used to evaluate the ontology generated from the study. The results show that it is comparable and potentially superior to the OpenIE method of triple extraction.

title: Machine Learning-Based Barrier Gate System for Security and Monitoring
authors: Jerome Carl R. Dagle, James Randall B. Gira, Renyl Gione C. Medina, Rafael Caesar M. Sebastian, Juliet O. Niega, Adrian M. Manalo
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning-based automated barrier gate system for vehicle access control and security monitoring using real-time License Plate Recognition (LPR) integrated with Internet of Things (IoT) technologies. The system employs an IP camera to capture live vehicle images, where YOLOv8 performs real-time license plate localization and Optical Character Recognition (OCR) extracts alphanumeric characters from diverse Philippine license plate formats, including modern plates and older designs featuring the Rizal Monument. Through IoTenabled communication, recognition results are transmitted instantly to a centralized processing unit and cloud database, allowing immediate verification and automated barrier gate actuation without manual intervention]. Recognized license plate data are validated against a Firebase/Firestore database, while unregistered vehicles trigger system alerts that prompt security personnel to record driver information such as name, destination, and contact number. Vehicle entries are logged automatically, achieving 100% successful data synchronization across all test sessions, and corresponding video recordings are stored for each granted access to support real-time monitoring, auditing, and postincident review through a centralized dashboard. The system was trained using Philippine license plate datasets and evaluated under varying environmental conditions, including clean, dirty, and low-light plates, following ISO/IEC 25010 quality standards. Experimental results show a license plate detection accuracy of 96.67% across 180 test samples, reliable OCR performance, 100% access verification accuracy, fast system response suitable for realtime operation, and 100 % logging reliability. These results demonstrate that the proposed IoT-enabled system provides a practical, cost-effective, and scalable solution for modern vehicle access control applications.

title: Research on Particle Statistical Analysis of High-Voltage Switchgear
authors: MingMing Yao, XiangKe Wang, Yi Yang, LiuCheng Hao, ZhiJun Wang, Hao Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Electrical and Electronic Engineering (AI3E)
abstract: With the rapid development of China’s power grid, the scale of the grid is increasing day by day. Gas-insulated switchgear (GIS) is an important substation equipment and is widely used in the power system.Based on the operating experience over the past 40 years, insulation failures have always been one of the key factors affecting the reliability of GIS, and conductive particle contamination is the most significant cause of insulation failures. The particles generated under different voltage levels and different numbers of mechanical disconnection operations were statistically analyzed and studied.By using descriptive statistical analysis methods, different devices, shapes, sizes, materials and distribution locations were classified. Scanning electron microscopy was employed to collect and analyze the shapes of the particles.By defining the shape of the particles, the main particles generated under different voltage levels and their distribution locations can be obtained. This further provides practical statistical data support for the insulation reliability of high-voltage switchgear.

title: Establishing a Reliable FBCSP Baseline for Robust BCI Systems
authors: Aigerim Aitim, Zhuldyz Bagybek
publish: 2026 18th International Conference on Electronics, Computer, and Computation (ICECCO)
abstract: Filter Bank Common Spatial Patterns (FBCSP) is widely used as a classical baseline in motor imagery BCI research. Despite its popularity, many published implementations suffer from methodological shortcomings that limit both reproducibility and practical relevance. Typical issues include information leakage caused by feature selection on the full dataset, superficial use of ICA without consistent artifact removal, and evaluation protocols that ignore class imbalance or subject variability. In this work, we present a fully reproducible FBCSP pipeline designed to address these limitations. The approach employs nested stratified cross-validation to ensure unbiased performance estimation, integrates ICA with automatic component rejection to improve signal quality, and reports results using chance-corrected metrics such as Cohen's Kappa. All processing steps are explicitly defined and implemented using standard, open-source tools. Evaluation on the BCI Competition IV-2a dataset demonstrates a mean classification accuracy of 81.8 percent, corresponding to a Cohen's Kappa of 0.757. This represents an improvement of 7.5 percentage points over the original FBCSP baseline. The proposed pipeline provides a robust and transparent reference point for future studies, particularly in neurorehabilitationoriented BCI system development, and all code is released publicly to support independent verification and reuse.

title: Bridging Lexical and Contextual Semantics for Adolescents’ Health Sentiment Analysis: A Cross-Platform Study Using Large Language Models
authors: Amima Shifa, G. G. Md. Nawaz Ali, Dylan Henricks
abstract: To understand, how adolescents emotionally engage with online health-related content, we require both contextual reasoning and semantic interpretation. This study presents a hybrid semantic framework that bridges classic and modern AI techniques for sentiment analysis of obesity-related discourse on social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram. The approach integrates lexicon-based polarity estimation with large language model’s (e.g., GPT-4) contextual refinement to enhance the derivation and integration of affective semantics from user-generated text. The dataset comprises of approximately 21,000 public comments related to adolescence obesity, healthy diet etc. categorized across five different content source types, including, healthcare professionals, fitness coaches, influencers, food and beverage creators, and others. Comparative results show that Instagram content exhibits cohesive, positive sentiment, while TikTok demonstrates greater emotional polarization and variability. By combining symbolic sentiment modeling with generative AI reasoning, this work contributes to semantic computing for health communication and highlights pathways for bridging lexical semantics and contextual intelligence in real-world social media analysis. The results also underscore the value of integrating large language models in health sentiment research and highlight the need for platform-sensitive, credibility-aware communication strategies when addressing adolescent audiences online.

title: A CVaR-Constrained Safe Reinforcement Learning Framework with Action Repair for Practical Portfolio Optimization
authors: Himanshu Choudhary, Arishi Orra, Manoj Thakur, Xiao-Zhi Gao, Prabhat Kumar Sahu
abstract: Portfolio optimization refers to allocating capital across multiple assets to attain an optimal balance between potential returns and associated risks under dynamic market conditions. While Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has shown significant promise in learning adaptive allocation strategies through continuous interaction with market, most existing approaches struggle to incorporate practical investment constraints and robust risk measures. To address these limitations, this paper introduces a constraint-aware and risk-sensitive DRL framework for portfolio optimization that operates within a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP) settings. The proposed model embeds Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) as a hard safety constraint using an Augmented Lagrangian approach on the agent to prevent high-loss outcomes. Additionally, a repair mechanism is introduced to adjust the agent’s actions which ensures the resulting portfolio weights remain feasible and compliant with real-world investment constraints. Our approach is evaluated using real-world stock data from multiple global indices and consistently outperforms existing DRL-based and traditional portfolio optimization strategies, achieving superior returns while strictly adhering to defined risk constraints.

title: Terahertz Chip Interconnects Based on Beam-Lead Structures
authors: Binbin Ma, Shixiong Liang, Yi Wu, Yang Yu, Sheng Li, Wenwei Wang, Meng Zhang, Yang Zhao, Ming Yin, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work suggests using low-loss beam-lead technology instead of wire bonding to prevent performance degradation in THz chip interconnects. Full 3-D finite-element method (FEM) simulations and lumped-element analysis indicate the parasitic reduction process, proving this structure’s superiority. Comparative measurements were taken in four frequency bands: 170–220 GHz, 240–320 GHz, 420–470 GHz, and 500–700 GHz. Experimental results show that the beam-lead interconnection substantially reduces insertion loss by approximately 0.5 dB in the 170–320 GHz range. More crucially, the performance advantage increases as the frequency rises to higher THz frequencies. In the 500–700 GHz range, beam-lead technology outperforms wire bonding by about 3 dB due to reduced inductive degradation. This study shows that beam-lead technology can connect future terahertz (THz) integrated systems with high-performance and wideband connections.

title: Adaptive Residual Mamba Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Junyi Zhang, Renwen Chen, Fei Liu, Aocheng He, Boyu Zheng, Xintong Hou
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Although convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Transformer-based models have achieved considerable success in the remote sensing change detection (RSCD) field, they often struggle to balance local detail preservation and global dependency modeling. Recently, state-space models such as Mamba have shown promising potential for efficient long-range representation learning. However, most existing Mamba-based RSCD methods are constrained by fixed residual connection mechanisms and selective scanning strategies, resulting in limited feature adaptability in spatial dependency modeling. To address these challenges, we propose Adaptive Residual Mamba (AR-Mamba), a novel Mamba-based framework that introduces adaptive residual and cross-gated scanning mechanisms. Specifically, the adaptive residual state (ARS) block employs adaptive kernel convolver and learnable scaling to dynamically calibrate residual signals. The cross-gated bi-scanning (CGBS) strategy performs bidirectional spatial scanning and cross-directional gating. Experimental results on the LEVIR-CD, SYSU-CD and WHU-CD datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed AR-Mamba, which achieves F1-score improvements of 0.82%, 1.95% and 1.62% over ChangeMamba, respectively.

title: Multi-source Fusion Self-Calibrated U-shaped Network for Hydraulic System Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xiansong He, Yibo Song, Jinyuan Wang, Xiaoli Zhao, Wenxiang Deng, Weilin Zhu, Jian Hu, Gongning Li, Jianyong Yao
abstract: Reliable condition awareness is vital for modern aircraft and industrial equipment, yet fault diagnosis of hydraulic systems remains challenging due to hidden failure modes, nonlinear dynamics, and multi-source heterogeneity. To address this, an end-to-end intelligent diagnosis framework that integrates multi-source fusion self-calibrated U-shaped network (MFSCU-Net)is proposed. Displacement, pressure, and flow signals are fused across data and feature levels, while a U-shaped encoder-decoder with Self-Calibrated convolutions enables multi-scale representation, adaptive recalibration, and deep feature interaction. This design enhances weak fault signatures and improves sensitivity to incipient faults. Experiments on electrohydrostatic actuator and electrohydraulic proportional servo valve datasets demonstrate that MFSCU-Net achieves superior accuracy and robustness over benchmark methods.

title: TFS-Net: Towards Reliable and Interpretable Lower-Limb sEMG Recognition Under Non-Ideal Signal Conditions
authors: Qianchen Liu, Liuyi Ling, Weiyan Lu, Liyu Wei, Yiming Liu, Jixiang Jia
abstract: In practical applications, lower-limb surface electromyography (sEMG) signals are susceptible to disturbances such as electrode displacement and transient noise, leading to unstable recognition outcomes. To address this, this study develops a novel, lightweight, and robust Time-Frequency masking and multi-Scale convolutional Network (TFS-Net) for lower-limb gait recognition. First, multichannel sEMG signals are transformed into time-frequency images to capture dynamic features via a multi-scale CNN module. During training, a Fixed-Time Masking Aligned (FTMA) strategy is explicitly employed to simulate real-world disturbances, thereby enhancing the model’s noise robustness. Furthermore, interpretability is validated both visually via Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and quantitatively using a novel Biomechanical Alignment Score (BAS), ensuring the model’s decision logic aligns with neuromuscular mechanisms.Experiments on the ENABL3S dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a maximum intra-subject accuracy of 95.34% on ideal data and exhibits outstanding stability, retaining over 85% accuracy even under simulated random distortion where half of the channels are distorted. Moreover, the quantitative analysis confirms valid feature learning, with the BAS improving by up to 154.6% over random guessing. In summary, the proposed model effectively balances accuracy, stability, and interpretability, exhibiting significant potential for rehabilitation assistance and human-machine interaction.

title: Integrated Energy-Efficient Scheduling of Distributed Flow Shop and Distribution Using a Q-Learning-Based Multi-Objective Cooperation Evolutionary Algorithm
authors: Zhengpei Zhang, Yaoguang Hu, Lixiang Zhang, Yanzi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Production and distribution are indispensable in a supply chain. Practically, finding an overall optimization of the two processes has become an urgent problem. This article addresses an integrated energy-efficient scheduling problem of distributed flow shop and distribution in the context of economic globalization and environment sustainability. First, a mixed integer programing model is formulized to achieve minimal maximum completion time, minimal total energy consumption and minimal maximum workload of factories. Second, a Q-learning-based multi-objective cooperation evolutionary algorithm with problem-specific knowledge is specially developed. A cooperation strategy is devised to search a solution space using three populations. Three heuristic rules are proposed to initialize the populations. A knowledge-based local search is devised to refine better individuals. A Q-learning-based approach is designed to adaptively choose premium search strategies at each iteration. Finally, the designed method is compared with three meta-heuristics in prior research and a mathematical programming solver CPLEX. The comparison experiments are conducted by using a group of benchmark instances and a real-world case. The outcomes confirm outstanding capacities of the developed model and algorithm for dealing with the considered problems.

title: Antenna Coding Design for Multi-User Transmissions Using Pixel Antennas
authors: Hongyu Li, Shanpu Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This work investigates exploiting the potential of pixel antennas, which are a reconfigurable antenna technology that can flexibly adjust the antenna characteristics through antenna coding, in multi-user transmissions. To that end, we propose a multi-user multi-input single-output (MISO) pixel antenna system, which deploys the pixel antenna at users, and develop the system model including pixel antenna with antenna coding and multi-user beamspace channels. Aiming at maximizing the sum rate performance, we first propose an algorithm to alternatively design the precoding at the transmitter and the antenna coding at users, which explores the performance boundary for the proposed multi-user MISO pixel antenna system. To reduce the computational complexity, we propose a codebook-based antenna coding design algorithm, where the antenna coder is online optimized from an offline codebook. To further enhance the computation efficiency, we propose a hierarchical codebook-based antenna coding design that uses a multi-layer hierarchical search to achieve a better performance-complexity trade-off. Simulation results show that, adopting the proposed algorithms, the multi-user MISO pixel antenna system can always outperform conventional multi-user MISO systems with fixed antennas. More importantly, results validate that the proposed (hierarchical) codebook-based algorithms can significantly reduce the computational complexity while maintaining a satisfactory sum rate performance.
